Analysis

Generated by Claude Code (claude-sonnet-4-6) on 2026-07-20 00:00:00+00:00 from 6 judge scores across 11 cases, plus execution metadata (30 min, $7.92, 119 turns, exit 0). Baseline: baseline-run-1. Recommendation leads; failure patterns, root causes, and regressions follow as supporting evidence.

Recommendation

Hold — do not conclude treatment is harmful based on two runs. The treatment condition is not functioning as designed: agents read 0 of the expected agentic docs in every case, making this effectively a second baseline run. The lower scores (task_success 3.73 vs baseline 3.98 mean) likely reflect model stochasticity rather than a documentation penalty. Run treatment-run-3 through 5 to build statistical power, then re-evaluate. Separately, investigate why AGENTS.md is not auto-loaded — the organic discovery mechanism is broken.

Summary

  • 11 cases, exit_code 0, $7.92, 952s wall clock
  • task_success: 3.73 | code_correctness: 3.73 | convention_adherence: 3.45
  • hallucination_check: 3.18 | consulted_docs: 0.0 (pass_rate 0%) | efficiency: 0% pass
  • Pairwise vs baseline-run-1: 10 ties, 1 treatment win, 0 baseline wins

Failure Patterns

  • consulted_docs 0.0 across all 11 cases: agents proceed directly to source files without reading AGENTS.md, agentic/DESIGN.md, or other expected docs. This is structural — the task prompt and system prompt do not guide discovery.
  • efficiency 0%: all cases exceed cost or turn thresholds (same as all baseline runs — threshold is too tight for this task complexity).
  • hallucination_check 3.18: several cases had minor invented APIs or paths, consistent with treatment-run-1 and baseline runs.

Root Causes

  1. AGENTS.md not auto-loaded: The eval harness system_prompt overrides Claude Code's normal CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md loading behavior. The treatment CLAUDE.md also does not reference AGENTS.md, so agents never discover agentic docs.
  2. Efficiency threshold miscalibrated: All 11-case runs exceed the $3/40-turn threshold; the judge is a poor discriminator here.

Regressions

Compared to baseline-run-1: task_success −0.45, code_correctness −0.27, convention_adherence −0.46. These are within expected variance given only 11 cases and no statistical significance test yet.

Cost Attribution

  • Total: $7.92 for 11 cases = $0.72/case
  • 119 turns, $0.0666/turn, 747 output tokens/turn
  • 92.6% cache hit rate — highly efficient reuse across runs

cm-001-certmanager-cel (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness5

The implementation is fully correct. The kubebuilder marker syntax // +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.metadata.name == 'cluster'",message="CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'" is syntactically valid and follows the exact pattern from TrustManager. The CEL rule uses self.metadata.name == 'cluster' with correct single quotes around 'cluster'. The marker is placed above the struct declaration as required. The message text follows the TrustManager pattern (the reference shows CertManager.operator.openshift.io/v1alpha1 is a singleton while the agent uses CertManager is a singleton — this is a minor stylistic difference but both are consistent with the pattern and functionally equivalent). make manifests is correctly identified as the required command, and the agent additionally identifies make bundle for propagating changes. No type errors, no invented APIs, no incorrect imports.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/2 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence5

The implementation is fully idiomatic and follows all applicable conventions:

  1. Singleton enforcement via CEL marker: The agent correctly uses +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.metadata.name == 'cluster'",message="CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'" — matching the exact pattern from TrustManager. The CEL expression uses self.metadata.name == 'cluster' with single quotes, the correct marker syntax, and the message follows the TrustManager pattern (substituting "CertManager" for the type name).
  2. Marker placement: The marker is correctly placed above the struct declaration, between the doc comment and the type CertManager struct line, consistent with how kubebuilder markers are used throughout the codebase.
  3. Build commands: The agent correctly identifies make manifests as the required command and additionally notes make bundle to propagate changes to the bundle directory. The agent does not incorrectly swap the order or conflate these with make generate.
  4. No unnecessary changes: The implementation modifies only the single file that needs changing and adds only the single marker line needed, with no new files, imports, or functions — matching the minimal-change convention expected for this kind of API-level enforcement.

The message text ("CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'") is consistent with TrustManager's existing message pattern. A senior maintainer would have no convention-related comments in code review.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check5

The agent's implementation is fully grounded and does not invent any non-existent references:

  1. Correct marker syntax: Uses +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.metadata.name == 'cluster'",message="..." which matches the kubebuilder marker pattern described in the reference.
  2. CEL rule: Uses self.metadata.name == 'cluster' with single quotes around 'cluster', exactly as specified.
  3. Marker placement: Correctly placed above the type CertManager struct declaration, not inside it.
  4. Make targets: References make manifests (correct) and make bundle (a standard operator-sdk target that would exist in this type of project). The agent correctly identified make manifests as the primary required command.
  5. Message text: "CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'" follows the TrustManager pattern consistently.
  6. File path: api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go is the correct file path matching the reference.
  7. Existing markers referenced: The agent references existing markers like +genclient, +kubebuilder:object:root=true, +kubebuilder:subresource:status, +kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster, and +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName="CertManager" — all of which are standard markers that would exist in this file.
  8. No invented packages, methods, fields, or file paths: The implementation only adds a single comment line and doesn't reference any non-existent code entities.

The implementation is clean, minimal, and correctly grounded in the existing codebase patterns.

task_success5

The agent's implementation is fully correct and matches the reference in all key aspects:

  1. Correct XValidation marker syntax: Uses +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=...,message=... — exactly the right kubebuilder marker format.
  2. Correct CEL expression: self.metadata.name == 'cluster' with single quotes around 'cluster', matching the required pattern.
  3. Correct placement: The marker is placed above the CertManager struct declaration, not inside it.
  4. Correct make commands: Identifies make manifests as the primary command and also mentions make bundle for propagating to bundle manifests, which is even more thorough than the reference.
  5. Message consistency: The message "CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'" follows the TrustManager pattern. The reference suggests "CertManager.operator.openshift.io/v1alpha1 is a singleton..." with the full API path, but the agent's shorter form "CertManager is a singleton..." is consistent with common patterns and would be acceptable in code review — this is a stylistic difference rather than a functional one.

The implementation is complete, correct, and would work as-is.

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs correctly identify the core task: adding a +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.metadata.name == 'cluster'" marker to the CertManager type in certmanager_types.go, following the exact TrustManager pattern.

Marker placement and syntax: Both outputs propose the exact same CEL validation marker with the same rule and message pattern adapted for CertManager. Both correctly place it in the comment block before type CertManager struct. Output A places it just before the +operator-sdk line, Output B places it after the +kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster line — both are valid positions in the marker block and would work correctly with controller-gen.

Commands: Output A specifies make manifests then make bundle as two separate commands. Output B specifies only make manifests but claims it regenerates both CRD and bundle manifests. According to the reference, make manifests regenerates the CRD in config/crd/bases/ but a separate make bundle is needed to update bundle/manifests/. The reference explicitly calls for both make manifests and make bundle. Output A is more explicit and correct about needing both commands. Output B's claim that make manifests alone updates the bundle is technically incorrect based on the Makefile (where bundle is a separate target that depends on manifests). However, Output B does mention the bundle will be updated, just attributes it to the wrong command.

Overall quality: Both outputs correctly identify the single file to modify, the exact marker syntax, the CEL rule, and the appropriate message. Both provide clear before/after code. The main differentiator is Output A correctly identifying that two commands (make manifests + make bundle) are needed, while Output B only mentions make manifests. However, this is a relatively minor difference — make bundle depends on make manifests, so running make bundle alone would also work. Neither agent actually ran the commands to verify.

Given both are substantively correct on the core implementation with only a minor difference in command specification, this is essentially a tie.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment
task: |
  In api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go, add a CEL x-kubernetes-validations
  marker that rejects a CertManager object at admission time if metadata.name is not
  'cluster'. Look at the existing TrustManager type in the same package for the correct
  kubebuilder marker syntax.

  After adding the marker, run make manifests to regenerate the CRD and update the
  bundle manifest in bundle/manifests/.
task_id: cm-001-certmanager-cel
repo: cert-manager-operator
category: api-change
complexity: medium

If set, cainjector\n57\t\t//                                             will not update resources with certificates outside of the configured namespace.\n58\t\t// --v Level                                   number for the log level verbosity\n59\t\t//\n60\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n61\t\t// +optional\n62\t\tCAInjectorConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"cainjectorConfig,omitempty\"`\n63\t\n64\t\t// DefaultNetworkPolicy enables the default network policy for cert-manager components.\n65\t\t// When set to \"true\", the operator will create default network policies to secure\n66\t\t// communication between cert-manager controller, webhook, and cainjector components.\n67\t\t// When set to \"false\" or empty, no default network policies are created.\n68\t\t// Valid values are: \"true\", \"false\", or empty (default: false).\n69\t\t//\n70\t\t// This field is immutable once set to \"true\" for security reasons. Network policies\n71\t\t// cannot be disabled once enabled to prevent accidental security degradation.\n72\t\t// Users should carefully plan their network policy requirements before enabling this field.\n73\t\t//\n74\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n75\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"true\";\"false\";\"\"\n76\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf != 'true' || self == 'true'\",message=\"defaultNetworkPolicy cannot be changed from 'true' to 'false' once set\"\n77\t\t// +optional\n78\t\tDefaultNetworkPolicy string `json:\"defaultNetworkPolicy,omitempty\"`\n79\t\n80\t\t// NetworkPolicies specifies the egress network policy configuration to be applied to cert-manager\n81\t\t// pods/operands when DefaultNetworkPolicy is \"true\". By default, enabling network policies\n82\t\t// creates a deny-all policy that blocks all outgoing traffic from cert-manager components.\n83\t\t// Ingress rules are automatically handled by the operator based on the current running ports.\n84\t\t// Use this field to provide the necessary egress policy rules that allow required outbound traffic\n85\t\t// for cert-manager to function properly (e.g., API server communication, external issuer access, etc.).\n86\t\t//\n87\t\t// Each NetworkPolicy in this slice will be created as a separate Kubernetes NetworkPolicy\n88\t\t// resource. struct {\n223\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n224\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n225\t\n226\t\tItems []CertManager `json:\"items\"`\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\n230\ttype ComponentName string\n231\t\n232\tconst (\n233\t\t// CAInjector represents the cert-manager CA injector component.\n234\t\tCAInjector ComponentName = \"CAInjector\"\n235\t\n236\t\t// CoreController represents the cert-manager core controller component.\n237\t\tCoreController ComponentName = \"CoreController\"\n238\t\n239\t\t// Webhook represents the cert-manager webhook component.\n240\t\tWebhook ComponentName = \"Webhook\"\n241\t)\n242\t\n243\t// NetworkPolicy represents a custom network policy configuration for operator-managed components.\n244\t// It includes a name for identification and the network policy rules to be enforced.\n245\ttype NetworkPolicy struct {\n246\t\t// Name is a unique identifier for this network policy configuration.\n247\t\t// This name will be used as part of the generated NetworkPolicy resource name.\n248\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n249\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=253\n250\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n251\t\t// +required\n252\t\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n253\t\n254\t\t// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\n255\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=CoreController\n256\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n257\t\t// +required\n258\t\tComponentName ComponentName `json:\"componentName\"`\n259\t\n260\t\t// egress is a list of egress rules to be applied to the selected pods. Outgoing traffic\n261\t\t// is allowed if there are no NetworkPolicies selecting the pod (and cluster policy\n262\t\t// otherwise allows the traffic), OR if the traffic matches at least one egress rule\n263\t\t// across all of the NetworkPolicy objects whose podSelector matches the pod. If\n264\t\t// this field is empty then this NetworkPolicy limits all outgoing traffic (and serves\n265\t\t// solely to ensure that the pods it selects are isolated by default).\n266\t\t// The operator will automatically handle ingress rules based on the current running ports.\n267\t\t// +optional\n268\t\t// +listType=atomic\n269\t\tEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule `json:\"egress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=egress\"`\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\tfunc init() {\n273\t\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&CertManager{}, &CertManagerList{})\n274\t}\n275\t",

{\n9\t\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&TrustManager{}, &TrustManagerList{})\n10\t}\n11\t\n12\t// TrustManagerList contains a list of TrustManager resources.\n13\t//\n14\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n15\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n16\ttype TrustManagerList struct {\n17\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n18\t\n19\t\t// metadata is the standard list's metadata.\n20\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n21\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n22\t\tItems           []TrustManager `json:\"items\"`\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// TrustManager describes the configuration and information about the managed trust-manager deployment.\n26\t// The name must be `cluster` to make TrustManager a singleton, allowing only one instance per cluster.\n27\t// When a TrustManager CR is created, trust-manager operand is deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\n28\t//\n29\t// +genclient\n30\t// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n31\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n32\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n33\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n34\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=trustmanagers,scope=Cluster,categories={cert-manager-operator, trust-manager},shortName=trustmanager;tm\n35\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Ready\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].status\"\n36\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Message\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].message\"\n37\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"AGE\",type=\"date\",JSONPath=\".metadata.creationTimestamp\"\n38\t// +kubebuilder:metadata:labels={\"app.kubernetes.io/name=trustmanager\", \"app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager-operator\"}\n39\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"TrustManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n40\t// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"TrustManager\"\n41\ttype TrustManager struct {\n42\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n43\t\n44\t\t// metadata is the standard object's metadata.\n45\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n46\t\t// +required\n47\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n48\t\n49\t\t// spec is the specification of the desired behavior of the TrustManager.\n50\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n51\t\t// +required\n52\t\tSpec TrustManagerSpec `json:\"spec\"`\n53\t\n54\t\t// status is the most recently observed status of the TrustManager.\n55\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n56\t\t// +optional\n57\t\tStatus TrustManagerStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t// TrustManagerSpec defines the desired state of TrustManager.\n61\t// Note: trust-manager operand is always deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\n62\ttype TrustManagerSpec struct {\n63\t\t// trustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\n64\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n65\t\t// +required\n66\t\tTrustManagerConfig TrustManagerConfig `json:\"trustManagerConfig\"`\n67\t\n68\t\t// controllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for resource creation.\n69\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n70\t\t// +optional\n71\t\tControllerConfig TrustManagerControllerConfig `json:\"controllerConfig,omitempty\"`\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\t// TrustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\n75\ttype TrustManagerConfig struct {\n76\t\t// logLevel configures the verbosity of trust-manager logging.\n77\t\t// Follows [Kubernetes logging guidelines](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-instrumentation/logging.md#what-method-to-use).\n78\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=1\n79\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum:=1\n80\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum:=5\n81\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n82\t\t// +optional\n83\t\tLogLevel int32 `json:\"logLevel,omitempty\"`\n84\t\n85\t\t// logFormat specifies the output format for trust-manager logging.\n86\t\t// Supported formats are \"text\" and \"json\".\n87\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"text\";\"json\"\n88\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"text\"\n89\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n90\t\t// +optional\n91\t\tLogFormat string `json:\"logFormat,omitempty\"`\n92\t\n93\t\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources\n94\t\t// (ConfigMaps and Secrets containing CA certificates).\n95\t\t// Defaults to \"cert-manager\" if not specified.\n96\t\t// This field is immutable once set.\n97\t\t// This field can have a maximum of 63 characters.\n98\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"cert-manager\"\n99\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n100\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=63\n101\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf\",message=\"trustNamespace is immutable once set\"\n102\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n103\t\t// +optional\n104\t\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n105\t\n106\t\t// secretTargets configures whether trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n107\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n108\t\t// +optional\n109\t\tSecretTargets SecretTargetsConfig `json:\"secretTargets,omitempty\"`\n110\t\n111\t\t// filterExpiredCertificates controls whether trust-manager filters out\n112\t\t// expired certificates from trust bundles before distributing them.\n113\t\t// When set to \"Enabled\", expired certificates are removed from bundles.\n114\t\t// When set to \"Disabled\", expired certificates are included (default behavior).\n115\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n116\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n117\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n118\t\t// +optional\n119\t\tFilterExpiredCertificates FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificates,omitempty\"`\n120\t\n121\t\t// defaultCAPackage configures the default CA package for trust-manager.\n122\t\t// When enabled, the operator will use OpenShift's trusted CA bundle injection mechanism.\n123\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n124\t\t// +optional\n125\t\tDefaultCAPackage DefaultCAPackageConfig `json:\"defaultCAPackage,omitempty\"`\n126\t\n127\t\t// resources defines the compute resource requirements for the trust-manager pod.\n128\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n129\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n130\t\t// +optional\n131\t\tResources corev1.ResourceRequirements `json:\"resources,omitempty\"`\n132\t\n133\t\t// affinity defines scheduling constraints for the trust-manager pod.\n134\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/\n135\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n136\t\t// +optional\n137\t\tAffinity *corev1.Affinity `json:\"affinity,omitempty\"`\n138\t\n139\t\t// tolerations allows the trust-manager pod to be scheduled on tainted nodes.\n140\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/\n141\t\t// +listType=atomic\n142\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n143\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50\n144\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n145\t\t// +optional\n146\t\tTolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\"`\n147\t\n148\t\t// nodeSelector restricts which nodes the trust-manager pod can be scheduled on.\n149\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/\n150\t\t// +mapType=atomic\n151\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n152\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=50\n153\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n154\t\t// +optional\n155\t\tNodeSelector map[string]string `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\"`\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// SecretTargetsConfig configures whether and how trust-manager can write\n159\t// trust bundles to Secrets.\n160\t//\n161\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy != 'Custom' || (has(self.authorizedSecrets) && size(self.authorizedSecrets) > 0)\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must not be empty when policy is Custom\"\n162\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy == 'Custom' || !has(self.authorizedSecrets) || size(self.authorizedSecrets) == 0\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must be empty when policy is not Custom\"\n163\ttype SecretTargetsConfig struct {\n164\t\t// policy controls whether and how trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n165\t\t// Allowed values are \"Disabled\" or \"Custom\".\n166\t\t// \"Disabled\" means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets (default behavior).\n167\t\t// \"Custom\" grants trust-manager read access  to all secrets cluster-wide,\n168\t\t// and write access only to the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n169\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n170\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n171\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n172\t\t// +optional\n173\t\tPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n174\t\n175\t\t// authorizedSecrets is a list of specific secret names that trust-manager\n176\t\t// is authorized to create and update. This field is only valid when policy is \"Custom\".\n177\t\t// +listType=set\n178\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n179\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:items:MinLength:=1\n180\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n181\t\t// +optional\n182\t\tAuthorizedSecrets []string `json:\"authorizedSecrets,omitempty\"`\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// DefaultCAPackageConfig configures the default CA package feature for trust-manager.\n186\ttype DefaultCAPackageConfig struct {\n187\t\t// policy controls whether the default CA package feature is enabled.\n188\t\t// When set to \"Enabled\", the operator will inject OpenShift's trusted CA bundle\n189\t\t// into trust-manager, enabling the \"useDefaultCAs: true\" source in Bundle resources.\n190\t\t// When set to \"Disabled\", no default CA package is configured and Bundles cannot use useDefaultCAs (default behavior).\n191\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n192\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n193\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n194\t\t// +optional\n195\t\tPolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t// TrustManagerControllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for\n199\t// creating trust-manager resources.\n200\ttype TrustManagerControllerConfig struct {\n201\t\t// labels to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n202\t\t// +mapType=granular\n203\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n204\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n205\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n206\t\t// +optional\n207\t\tLabels map[string]string `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n208\t\n209\t\t// annotations to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n210\t\t// +mapType=granular\n211\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n212\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n213\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n214\t\t// +optional\n215\t\tAnnotations map[string]string `json:\"annotations,omitempty\"`\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy defines the policy for filtering expired certificates.\n219\ttype FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy string\n220\t\n221\tconst (\n222\t\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled filters out expired certificates from bundles.\n223\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Enabled\"\n224\t\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled includes expired certificates in bundles.\n225\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n226\t)\n227\t\n228\t// SecretTargetsPolicy defines the policy for writing trust bundles to Secrets.\n229\ttype SecretTargetsPolicy string\n230\t\n231\tconst (\n232\t\t// SecretTargetsPolicyDisabled means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets.\n233\t\tSecretTargetsPolicyDisabled SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n234\t\t// SecretTargetsPolicyCustom grants trust-manager read access to all cluster secrets\n235\t\t// and write access to only the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n236\t\tSecretTargetsPolicyCustom SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Custom\"\n237\t)\n238\t\n239\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicy defines the policy for the default CA package feature.\n240\ttype DefaultCAPackagePolicy string\n241\t\n242\tconst (\n243\t\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled enables the default CA package feature.\n244\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Enabled\"\n245\t\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled disables the default CA package feature.\n246\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Disabled\"\n247\t)\n248\t\n249\t// TrustManagerStatus defines the observed state of TrustManager.\n250\ttype TrustManagerStatus struct {\n251\t\t// conditions holds information about the current state of the trust-manager deployment.\n252\t\tConditionalStatus `json:\",inline,omitempty\"`\n253\t\n254\t\t// trustManagerImage is the container image (name:tag) used for trust-manager.\n255\t\tTrustManagerImage string `json:\"trustManagerImage,omitempty\"`\n256\t\n257\t\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources.\n258\t\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n259\t\n260\t\t// secretTargetsPolicy indicates the current secret targets policy.\n261\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n262\t\tSecretTargetsPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"secretTargetsPolicy,omitempty\"`\n263\t\n264\t\t// defaultCAPackagePolicy indicates the current default CA package policy.\n265\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n266\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"defaultCAPackagePolicy,omitempty\"`\n267\t\n268\t\t// filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy indicates the current policy for filtering expired certificates.\n269\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n270\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy,omitempty\"`\n271\t}\n272\t",

Look at the existing TrustManager type in the same package for the correct\nkubebuilder marker syntax.\n\nAfter adding the marker, run make manifests to regenerate the CRD and update the\nbundle manifest in bundle/manifests/.\n\n\nProduce a complete implementation. Include:\n- All files that must be modified with the exact changes\n- Any make targets or commands to run and in what order\n- The complete code for any new files or functions"}, "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:22:26.432Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_012JX9HCpAfBH29SDhU5QZRE","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage v1alpha1\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\tapiv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/operator/v1\"\n5\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n6\t\tnetworkingv1 \"k8s.io/api/networking/v1\"\n7\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n8\t)\n9\t\n10\t// CertManagerSpec defines the desired state of CertManager.\n11\ttype CertManagerSpec struct {\n12\t\tapiv1.OperatorSpec `json:\",inline\"`\n13\t\n14\t\t// ControllerConfig specifies further customization options for the controller's deployment spec.\n15\t\t//\n16\t\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n17\t\t//\n18\t\t// For OverrideArgs,\n19\t\t// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].args. The container\n20\t\t// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,\n21\t\t// Eg:\n22\t\t//   - \"--acme-http01-solver-nameservers=\"8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53\"\n23\t\t//   - \"--dns01-recursive-nameservers=8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53\"\n24\t\t//   - \"--dns01-recursive-nameservers-only\"\n25\t\t//\n26\t\t// For OverrideEnvs,\n27\t\t// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].env. The container\n28\t\t// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,\n29\t\t// Eg:\n30\t\t//   - \"HTTP_PROXY\": \"http://proxy:8080\"\n31\t\t//\n32\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n33\t\t// +optional\n34\t\tControllerConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"controllerConfig,omitempty\"`\n35\t\n36\t\t// WebhookConfig specifies further customization options for the webhook's deployment spec.\n37\t\t//\n38\t\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n39\t\t//\n40\t\t// For OverrideArgs,\n41\t\t// --config string                                Path to a file containing a WebhookConfiguration object used to configure the webhook\n42\t\t// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-name string        name of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates\n43\t\t// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-namespace string   namespace of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates\n44\t\t// --dynamic-serving-dns-names strings            DNS names that should be present on certificates generated by the dynamic serving CA\n45\t\t// --v Level                                      number for the log level verbosity\n46\t\t//\n47\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n48\t\t// +optional\n49\t\tWebhookConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"webhookConfig,omitempty\"`\n50\t\n51\t\t// CAInjectorConfig specifies further customization options for the cainjector's deployment spec.\n52\t\t//\n53\t\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n54\t\t//\n55\t\t// For OverrideArgs,\n56\t\t// --namespace string                          If set, this limits the scope of cainjector to a single namespace. If set, cainjector\n57\t\t//                                             will not update resources with certificates outside of the configured namespace.\n58\t\t// --v Level                                   number for the log level verbosity\n59\t\t//\n60\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n61\t\t// +optional\n62\t\tCAInjectorConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"cainjectorConfig,omitempty\"`\n63\t\n64\t\t// DefaultNetworkPolicy enables the default network policy for cert-manager components.\n65\t\t// When set to \"true\", the operator will create default network policies to secure\n66\t\t// communication between cert-manager controller, webhook, and cainjector components.\n67\t\t// When set to \"false\" or empty, no default network policies are created.\n68\t\t// Valid values are: \"true\", \"false\", or empty (default: false).\n69\t\t//\n70\t\t// This field is immutable once set to \"true\" for security reasons. Network policies\n71\t\t// cannot be disabled once enabled to prevent accidental security degradation.\n72\t\t// Users should carefully plan their network policy requirements before enabling this field.\n73\t\t//\n74\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n75\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"true\";\"false\";\"\"\n76\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf != 'true' || self == 'true'\",message=\"defaultNetworkPolicy cannot be changed from 'true' to 'false' once set\"\n77\t\t// +optional\n78\t\tDefaultNetworkPolicy string `json:\"defaultNetworkPolicy,omitempty\"`\n79\t\n80\t\t// NetworkPolicies specifies the egress network policy configuration to be applied to cert-manager\n81\t\t// pods/operands when DefaultNetworkPolicy is \"true\". By default, enabling network policies\n82\t\t// creates a deny-all policy that blocks all outgoing traffic from cert-manager components.\n83\t\t// Ingress rules are automatically handled by the operator based on the current running ports.\n84\t\t// Use this field to provide the necessary egress policy rules that allow required outbound traffic\n85\t\t// for cert-manager to function properly (e.g., API server communication, external issuer access, etc.).\n86\t\t//\n87\t\t// Each NetworkPolicy in this slice will be created as a separate Kubernetes NetworkPolicy\n88\t\t// resource. Multiple policies can be defined to organize egress rules logically (e.g., separate\n89\t\t// policies for different types of outbound traffic or different security zones).\n90\t\t//\n91\t\t// This field is only effective when DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to \"true\".\n92\t\t// If DefaultNetworkPolicy is \"true\" but this field is not provided, cert-manager\n93\t\t// components will be isolated with deny-all egress policies.\n94\t\t//\n95\t\t// This field is immutable once DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to \"true\" for security reasons.\n96\t\t//\n97\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n98\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf.all(op, self.exists(p, p.name == op.name && p.componentName == op.componentName))\",message=\"name and componentName fields in networkPolicies are immutable\"\n99\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n100\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50\n101\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n102\t\t// +listType=map\n103\t\t// +listMapKey=name\n104\t\t// +listMapKey=componentName\n105\t\t// +optional\n106\t\tNetworkPolicies []NetworkPolicy `json:\"networkPolicies,omitempty\"`\n107\t}\n108\t\n109\t// DeploymentConfig defines the schema for\n110\t// overriding deployment of cert-manager operands,\n111\t// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\n112\ttype DeploymentConfig struct {\n113\t\n114\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n115\t\t// +optional\n116\t\tOverrideArgs []string `json:\"overrideArgs,omitempty\"`\n117\t\n118\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n119\t\t// +optional\n120\t\tOverrideEnv []corev1.EnvVar `json:\"overrideEnv,omitempty\"`\n121\t\n122\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n123\t\t// +optional\n124\t\tOverrideLabels map[string]string `json:\"overrideLabels,omitempty\"`\n125\t\n126\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n127\t\t// +optional\n128\t\tOverrideResources CertManagerResourceRequirements `json:\"overrideResources,omitempty\"`\n129\t\n130\t\t// OverrideReplicas defines the number of replicas for the operand deployment.\n131\t\t// If not specified, the default replicas from the deployment manifest will be used.\n132\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n133\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1\n134\t\t// +optional\n135\t\tOverrideReplicas *int32 `json:\"overrideReplicas,omitempty\"`\n136\t\n137\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n138\t\t// +optional\n139\t\tOverrideScheduling CertManagerScheduling `json:\"overrideScheduling,omitempty\"`\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// CertManagerResourceRequirements describes the compute resource requirements for the cert-manager operands,\n143\t// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\n144\ttype CertManagerResourceRequirements struct {\n145\t\t// Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.\n146\t\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n147\t\t// +optional\n148\t\tLimits corev1.ResourceList `json:\"limits,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=limits,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\"`\n149\t\t// Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.\n150\t\t// If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,\n151\t\t// otherwise to an implementation-defined value.\n152\t\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n153\t\t// +optional\n154\t\tRequests corev1.ResourceList `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=requests,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\"`\n155\t}\n156\t\n157\t// CertManagerScheduling describes the scheduling configurations for the cert-manager operands,\n158\t// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\n159\ttype CertManagerScheduling struct {\n160\t\t// NodeSelector is a selector which must be true for the pod to fit on a node.\n161\t\t// Selector which must match a node's labels for the pod to be scheduled on that node.\n162\t\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/\n163\t\t// +optional\n164\t\t// +mapType=atomic\n165\t\tNodeSelector map[string]string `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=nodeSelector\"`\n166\t\n167\t\t// Tolerations are attached to the pod to tolerate any taint that matches the\n168\t\t// taint's key, value and effect using the toleration's matching operator.\n169\t\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/\n170\t\t// +optional\n171\t\t// +listType=atomic\n172\t\tTolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,22,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t// CertManagerStatus defines the observed state of CertManager.\n176\ttype CertManagerStatus struct {\n177\t\tapiv1.OperatorStatus `json:\",inline\"`\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// +genclient\n181\t// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n182\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n183\t//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n184\t//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n185\t//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n186\t\n187\t// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n188\t// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\n189\ttype CertManager struct {\n190\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n191\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n192\t\n193\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n194\t\t// +required\n195\t\tSpec CertManagerSpec `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n196\t\t// +optional\n197\t\tStatus CertManagerStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\ttype UnsupportedConfigOverrides struct {\n201\t\tController UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController `json:\"controller,omitempty\"`\n202\t\tWebhook    UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook    `json:\"webhook,omitempty\"`\n203\t\tCAInjector UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector `json:\"cainjector,omitempty\"`\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\ttype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController struct {\n207\t\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\ttype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook struct {\n211\t\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n212\t}\n213\t\n214\ttype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector struct {\n215\t\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n219\t//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n220\t\n221\t// CertManagerList contains a list of CertManager.\n222\ttype CertManagerList struct {\n223\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n224\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n225\t\n226\t\tItems []CertManager `json:\"items\"`\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\n230\ttype ComponentName string\n231\t\n232\tconst (\n233\t\t// CAInjector represents the cert-manager CA injector component.\n234\t\tCAInjector ComponentName = \"CAInjector\"\n235\t\n236\t\t// CoreController represents the cert-manager core controller component.\n237\t\tCoreController ComponentName = \"CoreController\"\n238\t\n239\t\t// Webhook represents the cert-manager webhook component.\n240\t\tWebhook ComponentName = \"Webhook\"\n241\t)\n242\t\n243\t// NetworkPolicy represents a custom network policy configuration for operator-managed components.\n244\t// It includes a name for identification and the network policy rules to be enforced.\n245\ttype NetworkPolicy struct {\n246\t\t// Name is a unique identifier for this network policy configuration.\n247\t\t// This name will be used as part of the generated NetworkPolicy resource name.\n248\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n249\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=253\n250\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n251\t\t// +required\n252\t\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n253\t\n254\t\t// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\n255\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=CoreController\n256\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n257\t\t// +required\n258\t\tComponentName ComponentName `json:\"componentName\"`\n259\t\n260\t\t// egress is a list of egress rules to be applied to the selected pods. Outgoing traffic\n261\t\t// is allowed if there are no NetworkPolicies selecting the pod (and cluster policy\n262\t\t// otherwise allows the traffic), OR if the traffic matches at least one egress rule\n263\t\t// across all of the NetworkPolicy objects whose podSelector matches the pod. If\n264\t\t// this field is empty then this NetworkPolicy limits all outgoing traffic (and serves\n265\t\t// solely to ensure that the pods it selects are isolated by default).\n266\t\t// The operator will automatically handle ingress rules based on the current running ports.\n267\t\t// +optional\n268\t\t// +listType=atomic\n269\t\tEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule `json:\"egress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=egress\"`\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\tfunc init() {\n273\t\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&CertManager{}, &CertManagerList{})\n274\t}\n275\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aa1b72eb-5b09-4ce1-8453-da2c5f809fc4","uuid":"8312f9bb-7c3c-4018-b322-1448e5c21ad4","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:22:37.372Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go","content":"package v1alpha1\n\nimport (\n\tapiv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/operator/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tnetworkingv1 \"k8s.io/api/networking/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n)\n\n// CertManagerSpec defines the desired state of CertManager.\ntype CertManagerSpec struct {\n\tapiv1.OperatorSpec `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// ControllerConfig specifies further customization options for the controller's deployment spec.\n\t//\n\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n\t//\n\t// For OverrideArgs,\n\t// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].args. The container\n\t// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,\n\t// Eg:\n\t//   - \"--acme-http01-solver-nameservers=\"8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53\"\n\t//   - \"--dns01-recursive-nameservers=8.8.8.8:53,1.1.1.1:53\"\n\t//   - \"--dns01-recursive-nameservers-only\"\n\t//\n\t// For OverrideEnvs,\n\t// This field appends values to .spec.template.spec.containers[...].env. The container\n\t// that will be selected will be based on image present on the spec. Possible values are,\n\t// Eg:\n\t//   - \"HTTP_PROXY\": \"http://proxy:8080\"\n\t//\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tControllerConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"controllerConfig,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// WebhookConfig specifies further customization options for the webhook's deployment spec.\n\t//\n\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n\t//\n\t// For OverrideArgs,\n\t// --config string                                Path to a file containing a WebhookConfiguration object used to configure the webhook\n\t// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-name string        name of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates\n\t// --dynamic-serving-ca-secret-namespace string   namespace of the secret used to store the CA that signs serving certificates\n\t// --dynamic-serving-dns-names strings            DNS names that should be present on certificates generated by the dynamic serving CA\n\t// --v Level                                      number for the log level verbosity\n\t//\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tWebhookConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"webhookConfig,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// CAInjectorConfig specifies further customization options for the cainjector's deployment spec.\n\t//\n\t// Possible customizations include the following,\n\t//\n\t// For OverrideArgs,\n\t// --namespace string                          If set, this limits the scope of cainjector to a single namespace. If set, cainjector\n\t//                                             will not update resources with certificates outside of the configured namespace.\n\t// --v Level                                   number for the log level verbosity\n\t//\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tCAInjectorConfig *DeploymentConfig `json:\"cainjectorConfig,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// DefaultNetworkPolicy enables the default network policy for cert-manager components.\n\t// When set to \"true\", the operator will create default network policies to secure\n\t// communication between cert-manager controller, webhook, and cainjector components.\n\t// When set to \"false\" or empty, no default network policies are created.\n\t// Valid values are: \"true\", \"false\", or empty (default: false).\n\t//\n\t// This field is immutable once set to \"true\" for security reasons. Network policies\n\t// cannot be disabled once enabled to prevent accidental security degradation.\n\t// Users should carefully plan their network policy requirements before enabling this field.\n\t//\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"true\";\"false\";\"\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf != 'true' || self == 'true'\",message=\"defaultNetworkPolicy cannot be changed from 'true' to 'false' once set\"\n\t// +optional\n\tDefaultNetworkPolicy string `json:\"defaultNetworkPolicy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// NetworkPolicies specifies the egress network policy configuration to be applied to cert-manager\n\t// pods/operands when DefaultNetworkPolicy is \"true\". By default, enabling network policies\n\t// creates a deny-all policy that blocks all outgoing traffic from cert-manager components.\n\t// Ingress rules are automatically handled by the operator based on the current running ports.\n\t// Use this field to provide the necessary egress policy rules that allow required outbound traffic\n\t// for cert-manager to function properly (e.g., API server communication, external issuer access, etc.).\n\t//\n\t// Each NetworkPolicy in this slice will be created as a separate Kubernetes NetworkPolicy\n\t// resource. Multiple policies can be defined to organize egress rules logically (e.g., separate\n\t// policies for different types of outbound traffic or different security zones).\n\t//\n\t// This field is only effective when DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to \"true\".\n\t// If DefaultNetworkPolicy is \"true\" but this field is not provided, cert-manager\n\t// components will be isolated with deny-all egress policies.\n\t//\n\t// This field is immutable once DefaultNetworkPolicy is set to \"true\" for security reasons.\n\t//\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf.all(op, self.exists(p, p.name == op.name && p.componentName == op.componentName))\",message=\"name and componentName fields in networkPolicies are immutable\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +listType=map\n\t// +listMapKey=name\n\t// +listMapKey=componentName\n\t// +optional\n\tNetworkPolicies []NetworkPolicy `json:\"networkPolicies,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// DeploymentConfig defines the schema for\n// overriding deployment of cert-manager operands,\n// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\ntype DeploymentConfig struct {\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideArgs []string `json:\"overrideArgs,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideEnv []corev1.EnvVar `json:\"overrideEnv,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideLabels map[string]string `json:\"overrideLabels,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideResources CertManagerResourceRequirements `json:\"overrideResources,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// OverrideReplicas defines the number of replicas for the operand deployment.\n\t// If not specified, the default replicas from the deployment manifest will be used.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideReplicas *int32 `json:\"overrideReplicas,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tOverrideScheduling CertManagerScheduling `json:\"overrideScheduling,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// CertManagerResourceRequirements describes the compute resource requirements for the cert-manager operands,\n// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\ntype CertManagerResourceRequirements struct {\n\t// Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.\n\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n\t// +optional\n\tLimits corev1.ResourceList `json:\"limits,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=limits,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\"`\n\t// Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.\n\t// If Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,\n\t// otherwise to an implementation-defined value.\n\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n\t// +optional\n\tRequests corev1.ResourceList `json:\"requests,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=requests,casttype=ResourceList,castkey=ResourceName\"`\n}\n\n// CertManagerScheduling describes the scheduling configurations for the cert-manager operands,\n// namely the controller, webhook and cainjector.\ntype CertManagerScheduling struct {\n\t// NodeSelector is a selector which must be true for the pod to fit on a node.\n\t// Selector which must match a node's labels for the pod to be scheduled on that node.\n\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/\n\t// +optional\n\t// +mapType=atomic\n\tNodeSelector map[string]string `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,rep,name=nodeSelector\"`\n\n\t// Tolerations are attached to the pod to tolerate any taint that matches the\n\t// taint's key, value and effect using the toleration's matching operator.\n\t// More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/\n\t// +optional\n\t// +listType=atomic\n\tTolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,22,opt,name=tolerations\"`\n}\n\n// CertManagerStatus defines the observed state of CertManager.\ntype CertManagerStatus struct {\n\tapiv1.OperatorStatus `json:\",inline\"`\n}\n\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n\n// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\ntype CertManager struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n\t// +required\n\tSpec CertManagerSpec `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n\t// +optional\n\tStatus CertManagerStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype UnsupportedConfigOverrides struct {\n\tController UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController `json:\"controller,omitempty\"`\n\tWebhook    UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook    `json:\"webhook,omitempty\"`\n\tCAInjector UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector `json:\"cainjector,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerController struct {\n\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerWebhook struct {\n\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype UnsupportedConfigOverridesForCertManagerCAInjector struct {\n\tArgs []string `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n\n// CertManagerList contains a list of CertManager.\ntype CertManagerList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n\n\tItems []CertManager `json:\"items\"`\n}\n\n// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\ntype ComponentName string\n\nconst (\n\t// CAInjector represents the cert-manager CA injector component.\n\tCAInjector ComponentName = \"CAInjector\"\n\n\t// CoreController represents the cert-manager core controller component.\n\tCoreController ComponentName = \"CoreController\"\n\n\t// Webhook represents the cert-manager webhook component.\n\tWebhook ComponentName = \"Webhook\"\n)\n\n// NetworkPolicy represents a custom network policy configuration for operator-managed components.\n// It includes a name for identification and the network policy rules to be enforced.\ntype NetworkPolicy struct {\n\t// Name is a unique identifier for this network policy configuration.\n\t// This name will be used as part of the generated NetworkPolicy resource name.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=253\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n\t// +required\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\n\t// ComponentName represents the different cert-manager components that can have network policies applied.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=CoreController\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n\t// +required\n\tComponentName ComponentName `json:\"componentName\"`\n\n\t// egress is a list of egress rules to be applied to the selected pods. Outgoing traffic\n\t// is allowed if there are no NetworkPolicies selecting the pod (and cluster policy\n\t// otherwise allows the traffic), OR if the traffic matches at least one egress rule\n\t// across all of the NetworkPolicy objects whose podSelector matches the pod. If\n\t// this field is empty then this NetworkPolicy limits all outgoing traffic (and serves\n\t// solely to ensure that the pods it selects are isolated by default).\n\t// The operator will automatically handle ingress rules based on the current running ports.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +listType=atomic\n\tEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule `json:\"egress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=egress\"`\n}\n\nfunc init() {\n\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&CertManager{}, &CertManagerList{})\n}\n","numLines":275,"startLine":1,"totalLines":275}}}
