\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n5\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n6\t)\n7\t\n8\tfunc init() {\n9\t\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&TrustManager{}, &TrustManagerList{})\n10\t}\n11\t\n12\t// TrustManagerList contains a list of TrustManager resources.\n13\t//\n14\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n15\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n16\ttype TrustManagerList struct {\n17\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n18\t\n19\t\t// metadata is the standard list's metadata.\n20\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n21\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n22\t\tItems           []TrustManager `json:\"items\"`\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// TrustManager describes the configuration and information about the managed trust-manager deployment.\n26\t// The name must be `cluster` to make TrustManager a singleton, allowing only one instance per cluster.\n27\t// When a TrustManager CR is created, trust-manager operand is deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\n28\t//\n29\t// +genclient\n30\t// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n31\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n32\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n33\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n34\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=trustmanagers,scope=Cluster,categories={cert-manager-operator, trust-manager},shortName=trustmanager;tm\n35\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Ready\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].status\"\n36\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Message\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].message\"\n37\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"AGE\",type=\"date\",JSONPath=\".metadata.creationTimestamp\"\n38\t// +kubebuilder:metadata:labels={\"app.kubernetes.io/name=trustmanager\", \"app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager-operator\"}\n39\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"TrustManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n40\t// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"TrustManager\"\n41\ttype TrustManager struct {\n42\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n43\t\n44\t\t// metadata is the standard object's metadata.\n45\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n46\t\t// +required\n47\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n48\t\n49\t\t// spec is the specification of the desired behavior of the TrustManager.\n50\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n51\t\t// +required\n52\t\tSpec TrustManagerSpec `json:\"spec\"`\n53\t\n54\t\t// status is the most recently observed status of the TrustManager.\n55\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n56\t\t// +optional\n57\t\tStatus TrustManagerStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t// TrustManagerSpec defines the desired state of TrustManager.\n61\t// Note: trust-manager operand is always deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\n62\ttype TrustManagerSpec struct {\n63\t\t// trustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\n64\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n65\t\t// +required\n66\t\tTrustManagerConfig TrustManagerConfig `json:\"trustManagerConfig\"`\n67\t\n68\t\t// controllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for resource creation.\n69\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n70\t\t// +optional\n71\t\tControllerConfig TrustManagerControllerConfig `json:\"controllerConfig,omitempty\"`\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\t// TrustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\n75\ttype TrustManagerConfig struct {\n76\t\t// logLevel configures the verbosity of trust-manager logging.\n77\t\t// Follows [Kubernetes logging guidelines](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-instrumentation/logging.md#what-method-to-use).\n78\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=1\n79\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum:=1\n80\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum:=5\n81\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n82\t\t// +optional\n83\t\tLogLevel int32 `json:\"logLevel,omitempty\"`\n84\t\n85\t\t// logFormat specifies the output format for trust-manager logging.\n86\t\t// Supported formats are \"text\" and \"json\".\n87\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"text\";\"json\"\n88\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"text\"\n89\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n90\t\t// +optional\n91\t\tLogFormat string `json:\"logFormat,omitempty\"`\n92\t\n93\t\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources\n94\t\t// (ConfigMaps and Secrets containing CA certificates).\n95\t\t// Defaults to \"cert-manager\" if not specified.\n96\t\t// This field is immutable once set.\n97\t\t// This field can have a maximum of 63 characters.\n98\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"cert-manager\"\n99\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n100\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=63\n101\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf\",message=\"trustNamespace is immutable once set\"\n102\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n103\t\t// +optional\n104\t\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n105\t\n106\t\t// secretTargets configures whether trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n107\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n108\t\t// +optional\n109\t\tSecretTargets SecretTargetsConfig `json:\"secretTargets,omitempty\"`\n110\t\n111\t\t// filterExpiredCertificates controls whether trust-manager filters out\n112\t\t// expired certificates from trust bundles before distributing them.\n113\t\t// When set to \"Enabled\", expired certificates are removed from bundles.\n114\t\t// When set to \"Disabled\", expired certificates are included (default behavior).\n115\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n116\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n117\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n118\t\t// +optional\n119\t\tFilterExpiredCertificates FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificates,omitempty\"`\n120\t\n121\t\t// defaultCAPackage configures the default CA package for trust-manager.\n122\t\t// When enabled, the operator will use OpenShift's trusted CA bundle injection mechanism.\n123\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n124\t\t// +optional\n125\t\tDefaultCAPackage DefaultCAPackageConfig `json:\"defaultCAPackage,omitempty\"`\n126\t\n127\t\t// resources defines the compute resource requirements for the trust-manager pod.\n128\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n129\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n130\t\t// +optional\n131\t\tResources corev1.ResourceRequirements `json:\"resources,omitempty\"`\n132\t\n133\t\t// affinity defines scheduling constraints for the trust-manager pod.\n134\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/\n135\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n136\t\t// +optional\n137\t\tAffinity *corev1.Affinity `json:\"affinity,omitempty\"`\n138\t\n139\t\t// tolerations allows the trust-manager pod to be scheduled on tainted nodes.\n140\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/\n141\t\t// +listType=atomic\n142\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n143\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50\n144\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n145\t\t// +optional\n146\t\tTolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\"`\n147\t\n148\t\t// nodeSelector restricts which nodes the trust-manager pod can be scheduled on.\n149\t\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/\n150\t\t// +mapType=atomic\n151\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n152\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=50\n153\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n154\t\t// +optional\n155\t\tNodeSelector map[string]string `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\"`\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// SecretTargetsConfig configures whether and how trust-manager can write\n159\t// trust bundles to Secrets.\n160\t//\n161\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy != 'Custom' || (has(self.authorizedSecrets) && size(self.authorizedSecrets) > 0)\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must not be empty when policy is Custom\"\n162\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy == 'Custom' || !has(self.authorizedSecrets) || size(self.authorizedSecrets) == 0\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must be empty when policy is not Custom\"\n163\ttype SecretTargetsConfig struct {\n164\t\t// policy controls whether and how trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n165\t\t// Allowed values are \"Disabled\" or \"Custom\".\n166\t\t// \"Disabled\" means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets (default behavior).\n167\t\t// \"Custom\" grants trust-manager read access  to all secrets cluster-wide,\n168\t\t// and write access only to the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n169\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n170\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n171\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n172\t\t// +optional\n173\t\tPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n174\t\n175\t\t// authorizedSecrets is a list of specific secret names that trust-manager\n176\t\t// is authorized to create and update. This field is only valid when policy is \"Custom\".\n177\t\t// +listType=set\n178\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n179\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:items:MinLength:=1\n180\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n181\t\t// +optional\n182\t\tAuthorizedSecrets []string `json:\"authorizedSecrets,omitempty\"`\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// DefaultCAPackageConfig configures the default CA package feature for trust-manager.\n186\ttype DefaultCAPackageConfig struct {\n187\t\t// policy controls whether the default CA package feature is enabled.\n188\t\t// When set to \"Enabled\", the operator will inject OpenShift's trusted CA bundle\n189\t\t// into trust-manager, enabling the \"useDefaultCAs: true\" source in Bundle resources.\n190\t\t// When set to \"Disabled\", no default CA package is configured and Bundles cannot use useDefaultCAs (default behavior).\n191\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n192\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n193\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n194\t\t// +optional\n195\t\tPolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n196\t}\n197\t\n198\t// TrustManagerControllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for\n199\t// creating trust-manager resources.\n200\ttype TrustManagerControllerConfig struct {\n201\t\t// labels to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n202\t\t// +mapType=granular\n203\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n204\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n205\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n206\t\t// +optional\n207\t\tLabels map[string]string `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n208\t\n209\t\t// annotations to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n210\t\t// +mapType=granular\n211\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n212\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n213\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n214\t\t// +optional\n215\t\tAnnotations map[string]string `json:\"annotations,omitempty\"`\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy defines the policy for filtering expired certificates.\n219\ttype FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy string\n220\t\n221\tconst (\n222\t\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled filters out expired certificates from bundles.\n223\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Enabled\"\n224\t\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled includes expired certificates in bundles.\n225\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n226\t)\n227\t\n228\t// SecretTargetsPolicy defines the policy for writing trust bundles to Secrets.\n229\ttype SecretTargetsPolicy string\n230\t\n231\tconst (\n232\t\t// SecretTargetsPolicyDisabled means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets.\n233\t\tSecretTargetsPolicyDisabled SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n234\t\t// SecretTargetsPolicyCustom grants trust-manager read access to all cluster secrets\n235\t\t// and write access to only the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n236\t\tSecretTargetsPolicyCustom SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Custom\"\n237\t)\n238\t\n239\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicy defines the policy for the default CA package feature.\n240\ttype DefaultCAPackagePolicy string\n241\t\n242\tconst (\n243\t\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled enables the default CA package feature.\n244\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Enabled\"\n245\t\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled disables the default CA package feature.\n246\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Disabled\"\n247\t)\n248\t\n249\t// TrustManagerStatus defines the observed state of TrustManager.\n250\ttype TrustManagerStatus struct {\n251\t\t// conditions holds information about the current state of the trust-manager deployment.\n252\t\tConditionalStatus `json:\",inline,omitempty\"`\n253\t\n254\t\t// trustManagerImage is the container image (name:tag) used for trust-manager.\n255\t\tTrustManagerImage string `json:\"trustManagerImage,omitempty\"`\n256\t\n257\t\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources.\n258\t\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n259\t\n260\t\t// secretTargetsPolicy indicates the current secret targets policy.\n261\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n262\t\tSecretTargetsPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"secretTargetsPolicy,omitempty\"`\n263\t\n264\t\t// defaultCAPackagePolicy indicates the current default CA package policy.\n265\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n266\t\tDefaultCAPackagePolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"defaultCAPackagePolicy,omitempty\"`\n267\t\n268\t\t// filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy indicates the current policy for filtering expired certificates.\n269\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n270\t\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy,omitempty\"`\n271\t}\n272\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aa1b72eb-5b09-4ce1-8453-da2c5f809fc4","uuid":"ce0d9fe9-bf32-44a1-b612-e41b610049ad","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:22:51.106Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/api/operator/v1alpha1/trustmanager_types.go","content":"package v1alpha1\n\nimport (\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n)\n\nfunc init() {\n\tSchemeBuilder.Register(&TrustManager{}, &TrustManagerList{})\n}\n\n// TrustManagerList contains a list of TrustManager resources.\n//\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\ntype TrustManagerList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// metadata is the standard list's metadata.\n\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n\tItems           []TrustManager `json:\"items\"`\n}\n\n// TrustManager describes the configuration and information about the managed trust-manager deployment.\n// The name must be `cluster` to make TrustManager a singleton, allowing only one instance per cluster.\n// When a TrustManager CR is created, trust-manager operand is deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\n//\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n// +kubebuilder:resource:path=trustmanagers,scope=Cluster,categories={cert-manager-operator, trust-manager},shortName=trustmanager;tm\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Ready\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].status\"\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"Message\",type=\"string\",JSONPath=\".status.conditions[?(@.type=='Ready')].message\"\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=\"AGE\",type=\"date\",JSONPath=\".metadata.creationTimestamp\"\n// +kubebuilder:metadata:labels={\"app.kubernetes.io/name=trustmanager\", \"app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager-operator\"}\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"TrustManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"TrustManager\"\ntype TrustManager struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// metadata is the standard object's metadata.\n\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n\t// +required\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// spec is the specification of the desired behavior of the TrustManager.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n\t// +required\n\tSpec TrustManagerSpec `json:\"spec\"`\n\n\t// status is the most recently observed status of the TrustManager.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tStatus TrustManagerStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// TrustManagerSpec defines the desired state of TrustManager.\n// Note: trust-manager operand is always deployed in the cert-manager namespace.\ntype TrustManagerSpec struct {\n\t// trustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n\t// +required\n\tTrustManagerConfig TrustManagerConfig `json:\"trustManagerConfig\"`\n\n\t// controllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for resource creation.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tControllerConfig TrustManagerControllerConfig `json:\"controllerConfig,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// TrustManagerConfig configures the trust-manager operand's behavior.\ntype TrustManagerConfig struct {\n\t// logLevel configures the verbosity of trust-manager logging.\n\t// Follows [Kubernetes logging guidelines](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/sig-instrumentation/logging.md#what-method-to-use).\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum:=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum:=5\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tLogLevel int32 `json:\"logLevel,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// logFormat specifies the output format for trust-manager logging.\n\t// Supported formats are \"text\" and \"json\".\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=\"text\";\"json\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"text\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tLogFormat string `json:\"logFormat,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources\n\t// (ConfigMaps and Secrets containing CA certificates).\n\t// Defaults to \"cert-manager\" if not specified.\n\t// This field is immutable once set.\n\t// This field can have a maximum of 63 characters.\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"cert-manager\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=63\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf\",message=\"trustNamespace is immutable once set\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// secretTargets configures whether trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tSecretTargets SecretTargetsConfig `json:\"secretTargets,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// filterExpiredCertificates controls whether trust-manager filters out\n\t// expired certificates from trust bundles before distributing them.\n\t// When set to \"Enabled\", expired certificates are removed from bundles.\n\t// When set to \"Disabled\", expired certificates are included (default behavior).\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tFilterExpiredCertificates FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificates,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// defaultCAPackage configures the default CA package for trust-manager.\n\t// When enabled, the operator will use OpenShift's trusted CA bundle injection mechanism.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tDefaultCAPackage DefaultCAPackageConfig `json:\"defaultCAPackage,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// resources defines the compute resource requirements for the trust-manager pod.\n\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tResources corev1.ResourceRequirements `json:\"resources,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// affinity defines scheduling constraints for the trust-manager pod.\n\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tAffinity *corev1.Affinity `json:\"affinity,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// tolerations allows the trust-manager pod to be scheduled on tainted nodes.\n\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/taint-and-toleration/\n\t// +listType=atomic\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxItems:=50\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tTolerations []corev1.Toleration `json:\"tolerations,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// nodeSelector restricts which nodes the trust-manager pod can be scheduled on.\n\t// ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/\n\t// +mapType=atomic\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=50\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tNodeSelector map[string]string `json:\"nodeSelector,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// SecretTargetsConfig configures whether and how trust-manager can write\n// trust bundles to Secrets.\n//\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy != 'Custom' || (has(self.authorizedSecrets) && size(self.authorizedSecrets) > 0)\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must not be empty when policy is Custom\"\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.policy == 'Custom' || !has(self.authorizedSecrets) || size(self.authorizedSecrets) == 0\",message=\"authorizedSecrets must be empty when policy is not Custom\"\ntype SecretTargetsConfig struct {\n\t// policy controls whether and how trust-manager can write trust bundles to Secrets.\n\t// Allowed values are \"Disabled\" or \"Custom\".\n\t// \"Disabled\" means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets (default behavior).\n\t// \"Custom\" grants trust-manager read access  to all secrets cluster-wide,\n\t// and write access only to the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// authorizedSecrets is a list of specific secret names that trust-manager\n\t// is authorized to create and update. This field is only valid when policy is \"Custom\".\n\t// +listType=set\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:items:MinLength:=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tAuthorizedSecrets []string `json:\"authorizedSecrets,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// DefaultCAPackageConfig configures the default CA package feature for trust-manager.\ntype DefaultCAPackageConfig struct {\n\t// policy controls whether the default CA package feature is enabled.\n\t// When set to \"Enabled\", the operator will inject OpenShift's trusted CA bundle\n\t// into trust-manager, enabling the \"useDefaultCAs: true\" source in Bundle resources.\n\t// When set to \"Disabled\", no default CA package is configured and Bundles cannot use useDefaultCAs (default behavior).\n\t// +kubebuilder:default:=\"Disabled\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tPolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"policy,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// TrustManagerControllerConfig configures the operator's behavior for\n// creating trust-manager resources.\ntype TrustManagerControllerConfig struct {\n\t// labels to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n\t// +mapType=granular\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tLabels map[string]string `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// annotations to apply to all resources created for the trust-manager deployment.\n\t// +mapType=granular\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=25\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n\t// +optional\n\tAnnotations map[string]string `json:\"annotations,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy defines the policy for filtering expired certificates.\ntype FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy string\n\nconst (\n\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled filters out expired certificates from bundles.\n\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Enabled\"\n\t// FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled includes expired certificates in bundles.\n\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyDisabled FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n)\n\n// SecretTargetsPolicy defines the policy for writing trust bundles to Secrets.\ntype SecretTargetsPolicy string\n\nconst (\n\t// SecretTargetsPolicyDisabled means trust-manager cannot write trust bundles to Secrets.\n\tSecretTargetsPolicyDisabled SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Disabled\"\n\t// SecretTargetsPolicyCustom grants trust-manager read access to all cluster secrets\n\t// and write access to only the secrets listed in authorizedSecrets.\n\tSecretTargetsPolicyCustom SecretTargetsPolicy = \"Custom\"\n)\n\n// DefaultCAPackagePolicy defines the policy for the default CA package feature.\ntype DefaultCAPackagePolicy string\n\nconst (\n\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled enables the default CA package feature.\n\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Enabled\"\n\t// DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled disables the default CA package feature.\n\tDefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled DefaultCAPackagePolicy = \"Disabled\"\n)\n\n// TrustManagerStatus defines the observed state of TrustManager.\ntype TrustManagerStatus struct {\n\t// conditions holds information about the current state of the trust-manager deployment.\n\tConditionalStatus `json:\",inline,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// trustManagerImage is the container image (name:tag) used for trust-manager.\n\tTrustManagerImage string `json:\"trustManagerImage,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// trustNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources.\n\tTrustNamespace string `json:\"trustNamespace,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// secretTargetsPolicy indicates the current secret targets policy.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Disabled;Custom\n\tSecretTargetsPolicy SecretTargetsPolicy `json:\"secretTargetsPolicy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// defaultCAPackagePolicy indicates the current default CA package policy.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n\tDefaultCAPackagePolicy DefaultCAPackagePolicy `json:\"defaultCAPackagePolicy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy indicates the current policy for filtering expired certificates.\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum:=Enabled;Disabled\n\tFilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy `json:\"filterExpiredCertificatesPolicy,omitempty\"`\n}\n","numLines":272,"startLine":1,"totalLines":272}}}

Run tests.\n356:run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the operator from your host (for development).\n374:deploy: $(KUSTOMIZE) manifests ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.\n394:bundle: $(OPERATOR_SDK) $(KUSTOMIZE) manifests ## Generate bundle manifests and metadata, then validate generated files.\n395:\t$(OPERATOR_SDK) generate kustomize manifests -q\n397:\t$(KUSTOMIZE) build config/manifests | $(OPERATOR_SDK) generate bundle $(BUNDLE_GEN_FLAGS)\n484:update: generate update-manifests update-bindata ## Update all generated code and manifests.\n486:.PHONY: update-manifests\n487:update-manifests: $(HELM) $(JSONNET) $(YQ) ## Update cert-manager and istio-csr operand manifests.\n488:\thack/update-cert-manager-manifests.sh $(CERT_MANAGER_VERSION)\n489:\thack/update-istio-csr-manifests.sh $(ISTIO_CSR_VERSION)\n490:\thack/update-trust-manager-manifests.sh $(TRUST_MANAGER_VERSION)","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aa1b72eb-5b09-4ce1-8453-da2c5f809fc4","uuid":"e304ef69-436e-469a-be52-d23ba0c5856a","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:23:08.450Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"245:.PHONY: manifests\n246:manifests: $(CONTROLLER_GEN) ## Generate WebhookConfiguration, ClusterRole and CustomResourceDefinition objects.\n272:test: manifests generate vet test-apis test-unit ## Run tests.\n356:run: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run the operator from your host (for development).\n374:deploy: $(KUSTOMIZE) manifests ## Deploy controller to the K8s cluster specified in ~/.kube/config.\n394:bundle: $(OPERATOR_SDK) $(KUSTOMIZE) manifests ## Generate bundle manifests and metadata, then validate generated files.\n395:\t$(OPERATOR_SDK) generate kustomize manifests -q\n397:\t$(KUSTOMIZE) build config/manifests | $(OPERATOR_SDK) generate bundle $(BUNDLE_GEN_FLAGS)\n484:update: generate update-manifests update-bindata ## Update all generated code and manifests.\n486:.PHONY: update-manifests\n487:update-manifests: $(HELM) $(JSONNET) $(YQ) ## Update cert-manager and istio-csr operand manifests.\n488:\thack/update-cert-manager-manifests.sh $(CERT_MANAGER_VERSION)\n489:\thack/update-istio-csr-manifests.sh $(ISTIO_CSR_VERSION)\n490:\thack/update-trust-manager-manifests.sh $(TRUST_MANAGER_VERSION)","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01AMCh8RNcSev9MdyqC7hw4H","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here's the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## File to modify\n\n**`api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go`** — Add one line before the `type CertManager struct` declaration.\n\nThe change is to add a `+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation` marker comment, following the exact same pattern as TrustManager (line 39 of `trustmanager_types.go`).\n\n### Exact change\n\nIn `api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go`, between the existing markers and the `type CertManager struct` line, add the XValidation marker. The block currently reads (lines 180–189):\n\n```go\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n\n// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\ntype CertManager struct {\n```\n\nChange it to:\n\n```go\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n\n// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\ntype CertManager struct {\n```\n\nThe single new line is:\n```\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## Commands to run (in order)\n\nFrom the repository root (`/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment`):\n\n```bash\n# 1. Regenerate CRD manifests under config/crd/bases/\nmake manifests\n\n# 2. Regenerate the bundle (updates bundle/manifests/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml)\nmake bundle\n```\n\n`make manifests` runs `controller-gen` which reads the kubebuilder markers and regenerates `config/crd/bases/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml`. This will add an `x-kubernetes-validations` block at the top level of the CertManager CRD schema:\n\n```yaml\nx-kubernetes-validations:\n- message: CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\n  rule: self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\n```\n\n`make bundle` then propagates the updated CRD into `bundle/manifests/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n- **One file modified**: `api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go` — a single marker comment line added before the `CertManager` type declaration.\n- **Two commands**: `make manifests` then `make bundle` to regenerate the CRD and bundle manifests.\n- No new files, functions, or imports needed."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":623,"cache_read_input_tokens":43043,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":623,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"aa1b72eb-5b09-4ce1-8453-da2c5f809fc4","uuid":"15654df1-c6ec-4d60-9e88-727763b25887","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:23:24.796Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDP5YtYZ5qkdvNsEXquN"}

The block currently reads (lines 180\u2013189):\n\n```go\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n\n// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\ntype CertManager struct {\n```\n\nChange it to:\n\n```go\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n//+kubebuilder:subresource:status\n//+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Cluster\n\n// CertManager is the Schema for the certmanagers API\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n// +operator-sdk:csv:customresourcedefinitions:displayName=\"CertManager\"\ntype CertManager struct {\n```\n\nThe single new line is:\n```\n// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\",message=\"CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## Commands to run (in order)\n\nFrom the repository root (`/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment`):\n\n```bash\n# 1. Regenerate CRD manifests under config/crd/bases/\nmake manifests\n\n# 2. Regenerate the bundle (updates bundle/manifests/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml)\nmake bundle\n```\n\n`make manifests` runs `controller-gen` which reads the kubebuilder markers and regenerates `config/crd/bases/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml`. This will add an `x-kubernetes-validations` block at the top level of the CertManager CRD schema:\n\n```yaml\nx-kubernetes-validations:\n- message: CertManager is a singleton, .metadata.name must be 'cluster'\n  rule: self.metadata.name == 'cluster'\n```\n\n`make bundle` then propagates the updated CRD into `bundle/manifests/operator.openshift.io_certmanagers.yaml`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n- **One file modified**: `api/operator/v1alpha1/certmanager_types.go` \u2014 a single marker comment line added before the `CertManager` type declaration.\n- **Two commands**: `make manifests` then `make bundle` to regenerate the CRD and bundle manifests.\n- No new files, functions, or imports needed.", "stop_reason": "end_turn", "session_id": "aa1b72eb-5b09-4ce1-8453-da2c5f809fc4", "total_cost_usd": 0.434898, "usage": {"input_tokens": 3975, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 43666, "cache_read_input_tokens": 188371, "output_tokens": 1917, "server_tool_use": {"web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0}, "service_tier": "standard", "cache_creation": {"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 0, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 43666}, "inference_geo": "", "iterations": [], "speed": "standard"}, "modelUsage": {"claude-opus-4-6": {"inputTokens": 3975, "outputTokens": 1917, "cacheReadInputTokens": 188371, "cacheCreationInputTokens": 43666, "webSearchRequests": 0, "costUSD": 0.434898, "contextWindow": 200000, "maxOutputTokens": 64000}}, "permission_denials": [], "terminal_reason": "completed", "fast_mode_state": "off", "uuid": "44ce2de8-b770-4a84-b8c1-351bd9ac54de", "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:23:24.816Z"}
cm-002-validate-manifest (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness3

The implementation has several notable deviations from the reference and potential issues:

  1. Validation placement: The reference clearly states the validation should be called "BEFORE any mutation functions run" as a guard at the pipeline entry. The agent places it AFTER r.updateVolumes(), which defeats the purpose of catching bindata corruption before mutations. The agent explicitly acknowledges this deviation in their design notes, but it contradicts the core requirement of being a pre-mutation guard.
  2. Volume validation not in reference: The reference implementation only validates the container name, not volumes. The agent adds volume validation for caVolumeName ("root-ca"), but this volume doesn't exist in the bindata manifest — it's added dynamically by updateVolumeWithIssuerCA. This means if called before mutations (as intended), the validation would always fail on the volume check. The agent works around this by placing the check after mutations, but this contradicts the task's intent.
  3. common.NewIrrecoverableError usage: The agent calls common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, "invalid istio-csr deployment manifest") at the call site. This may or may not exist in the codebase — the reference uses fmt.Errorf("invalid IstioCSR deployment manifest from bindata: %w", err). If this function doesn't exist or has a different signature, the code won't compile.
  4. Go types and API usage are otherwise correct: The appsv1.Deployment, corev1.Container, corev1.Volume, and field access patterns are all correct. The test structure is proper table-driven Go testing. The constant promotion pattern is reasonable.
  5. Test cases: The agent provides 4 test cases (exceeding the 3 minimum), and they cover valid, wrong container, wrong volume, and empty spec. However, the test checks exact error string matching rather than just wantErr bool, which is a reasonable but different approach from the reference.

The key issues are the incorrect placement (post-mutation vs pre-mutation) and the potentially invented common.NewIrrecoverableError API, which together represent significant functional and compilation concerns.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/2 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence3

The agent's implementation follows the cert-manager-operator's "Deployment mutation pattern" convention by creating a validateDeploymentManifest function that checks for required container names and volumes, mirroring the TrustManager pattern. It uses named constants (istiocsrContainerName, caVolumeName) rather than raw string literals, and the function structure (loop-to-find, return descriptive error) mirrors the reference pattern. However, there are significant deviations from the reference implementation and conventions:

  1. Placement of the validation call: The reference specifies the validation must be called BEFORE any mutation functions run (guard at pipeline entry), but the agent places it AFTER r.updateVolumes() — i.e., after all mutations have already run. The agent explicitly acknowledges this design choice, reasoning that the root-ca volume is added dynamically. While this is a reasonable engineering decision, it directly contradicts the Score 5 criterion: "Called BEFORE any mutation functions run (guard at pipeline entry)." This is a major deviation from the specified pattern.
  2. Volume validation: The reference implementation only checks for the container name and does NOT check for volumes (since the istiocsr bindata has no pre-existing volumes). The agent adds a volume check for caVolumeName ("root-ca"), but this volume doesn't exist in the bindata — it's added by updateVolumeWithIssuerCA. This creates a circular dependency where the validation only works because it's called after mutations, further deviating from the "pre-mutation guard" pattern.
  3. Error wrapping: The reference uses fmt.Errorf("invalid IstioCSR deployment manifest from bindata: %w", err) at the call site, but the agent uses common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, "invalid istio-csr deployment manifest"). While using NewIrrecoverableError may be appropriate for the codebase, the error message doesn't match and the wrapping pattern differs.
  4. Test structure: The tests use raw t.Run table-driven tests with struct literals, which is consistent with the reference but the test assertions use exact string matching rather than the simpler (err != nil) != tt.wantErr boolean pattern from the reference. The tests do cover 4 cases (exceeding the minimum 3), which is good.

The implementation is recognizable and follows the general pattern but has the critical placement issue (post-mutation vs pre-mutation) and the unnecessary volume validation that creates a conceptual mismatch with the defensive guard pattern.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check3

The implementation is largely grounded but has some concerns:

  1. common.NewIrrecoverableError: The agent references common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, "invalid istio-csr deployment manifest") in the call site. This function may or may not exist in the common package. The reference implementation uses a simple fmt.Errorf("invalid IstioCSR deployment manifest from bindata: %w", err). If NewIrrecoverableError doesn't exist in the codebase, this would be a hallucination causing a compile error. However, since the agent appeared to explore the codebase and this is a plausible utility function in an operator codebase, it's uncertain.
  2. Volume validation divergence from reference: The reference implementation only validates the container name, not volumes. The agent adds volume validation for caVolumeName ("root-ca"), but this volume is added dynamically by updateVolumeWithIssuerCA and is NOT present in the bindata manifest. The agent acknowledges this and places the validation after r.updateVolumes() instead of before mutations as specified. This is a design divergence from the reference, not a hallucination per se.
  3. Promoting caVolumeName constant: The agent claims caVolumeName exists as a local const inside updateVolumeWithIssuerCA at specific line numbers. This appears to be based on the agent's exploration of the codebase and seems grounded.
  4. istiocsrContainerName constant: Referenced as already existing, which aligns with the reference implementation mentioning it.
  5. Placement concern: The reference says to call validation BEFORE any mutation functions run, but the agent places it AFTER r.updateVolumes(). This is a design choice difference, not a hallucination.

The main potential hallucination is common.NewIrrecoverableError - if this doesn't exist in the codebase, it would cause a compile error. The agent doesn't clearly mark this as a new creation; it uses it as if it already exists. The reference implementation uses plain fmt.Errorf. This is a moderate concern.

The test file references corev1 which needs to be imported, but the agent doesn't explicitly show imports - this is a minor omission rather than hallucination.

Overall, the implementation is mostly grounded with existing patterns but the common.NewIrrecoverableError reference is suspicious as a potential hallucination. The structural approach is sound and follows existing patterns.

task_success3

The agent implements a validateDeploymentManifest function that mirrors the TrustManager pattern structurally (loop for container name, loop for volume name, descriptive errors using named constants) and includes a table-driven test with 4 cases covering valid, missing container, missing volume, and empty specs — meeting the test coverage requirement. However, the critical placement criterion is violated: the reference requires the validation to be called BEFORE any mutation functions run (as a guard at the pipeline entry), but the agent places it AFTER r.updateVolumes() — essentially at the pipeline exit. This fundamentally changes the purpose of the guard from catching bindata corruption early to post-mutation validation, which contradicts the reference's explicit "Called BEFORE any mutation functions run" requirement. Additionally, the agent adds a volume check for caVolumeName ("root-ca") which doesn't exist in the bindata manifest and is only added dynamically, making this check only work because it's placed after mutations — further entangling the incorrect placement. The reference implementation only checks the container name (no volume check) since no volumes exist in the bindata.

pairwiseTIE

Both agents produced implementations for the same task: adding a validateDeploymentManifest function to the istiocsr package and writing table-driven tests. Let me compare them against the reference implementation.

Reference Implementation Key Elements:

  1. Add validateDeploymentManifest function in deployments.go checking for istiocsrContainerName container and rootCAVolumeName ("root-ca") volume
  2. Add rootCAVolumeName = "root-ca" constant to deployments.go (promoting from local const in updateVolumeWithIssuerCA)
  3. Call validation in getDeploymentObject right after decoding, BEFORE mutations
  4. Add root-ca emptyDir volume and volumeMount to bindata YAML
  5. Run make update-bindata to regenerate
  6. Table-driven test TestValidateDeploymentManifest with cases for valid, missing container, missing volume, empty containers, wrong volume name

Output A (Treatment):

  • ✅ Adds validateDeploymentManifest function checking container and volume
  • ✅ Promotes caVolumeName from local to package-level constant
  • ✅ Removes local caVolumeName from updateVolumeWithIssuerCA
  • ❌ Places validation call AFTER r.updateVolumes() — not before mutations as required by the task and reference. The reference clearly states validation should happen right after decoding, before mutations
  • ❌ Does NOT modify the bindata YAML to add the root-ca volume placeholder
  • ❌ Does not mention make update-bindata
  • ✅ Table-driven test with 4 cases (valid, missing container, missing volume, empty containers)
  • Issue: Since the validation runs after updateVolumes, it's checking the post-mutation state rather than the bindata manifest. The task explicitly says "before mutations run"

Output B (Baseline):

  • ✅ Adds validateDeploymentManifest function checking container and volume
  • ✅ Adds rootCAVolumeName = "root-ca" constant
  • ✅ Places validation call right after decoding, BEFORE mutations — matches the reference
  • ✅ Modifies bindata YAML to add root-ca emptyDir volume and volumeMount placeholder
  • ✅ Mentions make update-bindata to regenerate bindata.go
  • ✅ Table-driven test with 5 cases (valid, missing container, missing volume, empty containers, wrong volume name)
  • ✅ Uses common.NewIrrecoverableError wrapping matching the trustmanager pattern

Critical Difference: Output A places the validation AFTER r.updateVolumes(), which contradicts the task requirement ("before mutations run") and the reference implementation. The task explicitly asks to "verify expected container names and required volumes are present in the bindata manifest before mutations run." Output B correctly places validation immediately after decoding, before any mutations.

Bindata Modification: Output B correctly identifies that the bindata needs to be modified to include the root-ca volume as an emptyDir placeholder, which the reference implementation also requires. Output A does not modify bindata, which means the volume check would only work after updateVolumes adds the volume dynamically — this is a fundamental design flaw.

Test Coverage: Output B has 5 test cases vs Output A's 4, matching the reference more closely.

Output B is the stronger implementation overall — it correctly places validation before mutations (matching both the task requirements and reference), modifies bindata correctly, includes the make update-bindata step, and has more comprehensive test coverage.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment
task: |
  Only pkg/controller/trustmanager/deployments.go currently has a validateDeploymentManifest
  call before the mutation pipeline runs. Add an equivalent defensive validation function
  to pkg/controller/istiocsr/deployments.go that verifies expected container names and
  required volumes are present in the bindata manifest before mutations run.

  Write a table-driven unit test in pkg/controller/istiocsr/deployments_test.go that
  confirms the guard fires when passed a deliberately broken manifest (missing container
  or missing volume).
task_id: cm-002-validate-manifest
repo: cert-manager-operator
category: architecture
complexity: medium

    "content": "package trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"maps\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\t\"slices\"\n\n\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/assets\"\n)\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) error {\n\tdesired, err := r.getDeploymentObject(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tdeploymentName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling deployment resource\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\n\texisting := &appsv1.Deployment{}\n\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if deployment %q exists\", deploymentName)\n\t}\n\tif exists && !deploymentModified(desired, existing) {\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"deployment resource exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"deployment resource has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply deployment %q\", deploymentName)\n\t}\n\n\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"deployment resource %s applied\", deploymentName)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) getDeploymentObject(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) (*appsv1.Deployment, error) {\n\tdeployment := common.DecodeObjBytes[*appsv1.Deployment](codecs, appsv1.SchemeGroupVersion, assets.MustAsset(deploymentAssetName))\n\n\tif err := validateDeploymentManifest(deployment); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"invalid trust-manager deployment manifest\")\n\t}\n\n\tcommon.UpdateName(deployment, trustManagerDeploymentName)\n\tcommon.UpdateNamespace(deployment, operandNamespace)\n\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n\tupdateResourceAnnotations(deployment, resourceAnnotations)\n\tupdatePodTemplateLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n\tupdateDeploymentArgs(deployment, trustManager)\n\n\tupdateServiceAccountName(deployment)\n\tupdateTLSSecretVolume(deployment)\n\tupdateDefaultCAPackageVolume(\n\t\tdeployment,\n\t\ttrustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage,\n\t\tcaBundleHash,\n\t)\n\n\tif err := updateImage(deployment); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"failed to update trust-manager image\")\n\t}\n\n\tif err := updateResourceRequirements(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updateAffinityRules(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updatePodTolerations(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updateNodeSelector(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn deployment, nil\n}\n\n// validateDeploymentManifest checks that the bindata deployment contains the\n// expected container and TLS volume. Without these, the update helpers silently\n// no-op and the operator would apply a deployment with stale defaults.\nfunc validateDeploymentManifest(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n\thasContainer := false\n\tfor _, c := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif c.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\thasContainer = true\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif !hasContainer {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required container %q\", trustManagerContainerName)\n\t}\n\n\thasVolume := false\n\tfor _, v := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif v.Name == tlsVolumeName && v.Secret != nil {\n\t\t\thasVolume = true\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif !hasVolume {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required secret volume %q\", tlsVolumeName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updatePodTemplateLabels(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, resourceLabels map[string]string) {\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Labels = resourceLabels\n}\n\nfunc updateDeploymentArgs(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) {\n\tconfig := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig\n\ttrustNamespace := getTrustNamespace(trustManager)\n\n\targs := []string{\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-format=%s\", config.LogFormat),\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-level=%d\", config.LogLevel),\n\t\t\"--metrics-port=9402\",\n\t\t\"--readiness-probe-port=6060\",\n\t\t\"--readiness-probe-path=/readyz\",\n\t\t\"--leader-elect=true\",\n\t\t\"--leader-election-lease-duration=15s\",\n\t\t\"--leader-election-renew-deadline=10s\",\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--trust-namespace=%s\", trustNamespace),\n\t\t\"--webhook-host=0.0.0.0\",\n\t\t\"--webhook-port=6443\",\n\t\t\"--webhook-certificate-dir=/tls\",\n\t}\n\n\tif secretTargetsEnabled(config.SecretTargets) {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--secret-targets-enabled=true\")\n\t}\n\n\tif config.FilterExpiredCertificates == v1alpha1.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--filter-expired-certificates=true\")\n\t}\n\n\tif defaultCAPackageEnabled(config.DefaultCAPackage) {\n\t\targs = append(args, fmt.Sprintf(\"--default-package-location=%s\", defaultCAPackageLocation))\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Args = args\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// updateDefaultCAPackageVolume adds the default CA package ConfigMap volume\n// and mount to the deployment when the feature is enabled. It also sets the\n// hash annotation on the pod template to trigger rolling restarts when the\n// CA bundle content changes.\nfunc updateDefaultCAPackageVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, config v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackageConfig, caBundleHash string) {\n\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(config) {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations == nil {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations[defaultCAPackageHashAnnotation] = caBundleHash\n\n\t// Remove any existing volume with the same name to avoid duplicates.\n\tvar volumes []corev1.Volume\n\tfor _, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif vol.Name != defaultCAPackageVolumeName {\n\t\t\tvolumes = append(volumes, vol)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes = append(volumes, corev1.Volume{\n\t\tName: defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n\t\tVolumeSource: corev1.VolumeSource{\n\t\t\tConfigMap: &corev1.ConfigMapVolumeSource{\n\t\t\t\tLocalObjectReference: corev1.LocalObjectReference{\n\t\t\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\n\t// Remove any existing volume mount at the same path, then add ours.\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tvar mounts []corev1.VolumeMount\n\t\t\tfor _, vm := range container.VolumeMounts {\n\t\t\t\tif vm.MountPath != defaultCAPackageMountPath {\n\t\t\t\t\tmounts = append(mounts, vm)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].VolumeMounts = append(mounts, corev1.VolumeMount{\n\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n\t\t\t\tMountPath: defaultCAPackageMountPath,\n\t\t\t\tReadOnly:  true,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc updateImage(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n\timage := os.Getenv(trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n\tif image == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s environment variable with trust-manager image not set\", trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n\t}\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Image = image\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateResourceRequirements(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tresources := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Resources\n\tif len(resources.Limits) == 0 && len(resources.Requests) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateResourceRequirements(resources, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tfor i := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Resources = resources\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateAffinityRules(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateAffinityRules(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Affinity = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updatePodTolerations(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateTolerationsConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Tolerations = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateNodeSelector(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateNodeSelectorConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\t// Merge user-specified node selectors with any default selectors from the manifest.\n\t// User-specified selectors take precedence.\n\tfor k, v := range trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateServiceAccountName(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.ServiceAccountName = trustManagerServiceAccountName\n}\n\nconst tlsVolumeName = \"tls\"\n\nfunc updateTLSSecretVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n\tfor i, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif vol.Name == tlsVolumeName && vol.Secret != nil {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes[i].Secret.SecretName = trustManagerTLSSecretName\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// deploymentModified compares only the fields we manage via SSA.\n// For the readiness probe, only the fields explicitly set in our bindata are\n// compared to avoid false positives from API-server-defaulted probe scalars\n// (TimeoutSeconds, SuccessThreshold, FailureThreshold).\nfunc deploymentModified(desired, existing *appsv1.Deployment) bool {\n\tif managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !ptr.Equal(desired.Spec.Replicas, existing.Spec.Replicas) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Spec.Selector, existing.Spec.Selector) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Labels, existing.Spec.Template.Labels) ||\n\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Annotations, existing.Spec.Template.Annotations) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tdesiredPodSpec := desired.Spec.Template.Spec\n\texistingPodSpec := existing.Spec.Template.Spec\n\n\tif desiredPodSpec.ServiceAccountName != existingPodSpec.ServiceAccountName ||\n\t\t!maps.Equal(desiredPodSpec.NodeSelector, existingPodSpec.NodeSelector) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Volumes, existingPodSpec.Volumes) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Affinity, existingPodSpec.Affinity) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Tolerations, existingPodSpec.Tolerations) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif len(desiredPodSpec.Containers) != len(existingPodSpec.Containers) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range desiredPodSpec.Containers {\n\t\tif containerModified(&desiredPodSpec.Containers[i], &existingPodSpec.Containers[i]) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc containerModified(desired, existing *corev1.Container) bool {\n\tif desired.Name != existing.Name ||\n\t\tdesired.Image != existing.Image ||\n\t\tdesired.ImagePullPolicy != existing.ImagePullPolicy {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !slices.Equal(desired.Args, existing.Args) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Resources, existing.Resources) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.SecurityContext, existing.SecurityContext) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.VolumeMounts, existing.VolumeMounts) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !containerPortsMatch(desired.Ports, existing.Ports) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif readinessProbeModified(desired.ReadinessProbe, existing.ReadinessProbe) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc containerPortsMatch(desired, existing []corev1.ContainerPort) bool {\n\tif len(desired) != len(existing) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor _, dp := range desired {\n\t\tmatched := false\n\t\tfor _, ep := range existing {\n\t\t\tif dp.ContainerPort == ep.ContainerPort && dp.Name == ep.Name {\n\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matched {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// readinessProbeModified compares only the probe fields we explicitly set in\n// bindata (HTTPGet, InitialDelaySeconds, PeriodSeconds), avoiding\n// API-server-defaulted int32 fields like TimeoutSeconds and FailureThreshold.\nfunc readinessProbeModified(desired, existing *corev1.Probe) bool {\n\tif desired == nil && existing == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tif desired == nil || existing == nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif desired.InitialDelaySeconds != existing.InitialDelaySeconds ||\n\t\tdesired.PeriodSeconds != existing.PeriodSeconds {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif desired.HTTPGet != nil && existing.HTTPGet != nil {\n\t\tif desired.HTTPGet.Path != existing.HTTPGet.Path ||\n\t\t\tdesired.HTTPGet.Port != existing.HTTPGet.Port {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t} else if desired.HTTPGet != existing.HTTPGet {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:22:38.851Z"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_vrtx_0117hyAXCvPksMZD3uVttCUF",
