(\n\tscheme   = runtime.NewScheme()\n\tsetupLog = ctrl.Log.WithName(\"setup-manager\")\n)\n\n// ConfigMap is intentionally excluded from both istioCSRManagedResources and\n// trustManagerManagedResources. Multiple controllers need to watch ConfigMaps\n// that do not carry the managed-resource label:\n//\n//  1. TrustManager watches both its managed ConfigMaps (e.g., the default CA\n//     package ConfigMap, which carries the managed-resource label) and the\n//     cert-manager-operator-trusted-ca-bundle ConfigMap (added in the OLM bundle manifest).\n//     The latter does not carry the managed-resource label.\n//\n//  2. IstioCSR watches both its managed ConfigMaps (with the managed-resource\n//     label) and user-created ConfigMaps identified by the\n//     istiocsr.openshift.operator.io/watched-by label — a different label key\n//     entirely from the managed-resource label (app).\n//\n// The cache uses a single labels.Selector per GVK. The In operator can match\n// multiple values for the same key (e.g., app in (value1, value2)), but\n// requirements on different keys are always ANDed. There is no way to express\n// \"app in (...) OR watched-by exists\" in a single selector. A shared app label\n// value could solve case 1, but case 2 requires matching across different label\n// keys, which the Kubernetes label selector spec does not support.\n//\n// ConfigMaps therefore use the default unfiltered informer, and each controller\n// applies predicate-level filtering to select only the events it cares about.\n\n// istioCSRManagedResources defines the resources managed by the IstioCSR controller.\n// These resources will be watched with a label selector filter.\nvar istioCSRManagedResources = []client.Object{\n\t&certmanagerv1.Certificate{},\n\t&appsv1.Deployment{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRole{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{},\n\t&rbacv1.Role{},\n\t&rbacv1.RoleBinding{},\n\t&corev1.Service{},\n\t&corev1.ServiceAccount{},\n\t&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{},\n}\n\n// trustManagerManagedResources defines the resources managed by the TrustManager controller.\n// These resources will be watched with a label selector filter.\n//\n// cert-manager Issuer (and ClusterIssuer, which is never listed here) must not use a\n// managed-resource label selector: IstioCSR reconciles user-created Issuers referenced\n// from the spec, which are not labeled by the operator. Those types are left out of\n// ByObject so they use the manager cache’s default unfiltered informer per GVK.\nvar trustManagerManagedResources = []client.Object{\n\t&certmanagerv1.Certificate{},\n\t&appsv1.Deployment{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRole{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{},\n\t&rbacv1.Role{},\n\t&rbacv1.RoleBinding{},\n\t&corev1.Service{},\n\t&corev1.ServiceAccount{},\n\t&admissionregistrationv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration{},\n}\n\nfunc init() {\n\tutilruntime.Must(clientscheme.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(appsv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(corev1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(networkingv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(rbacv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(admissionregistrationv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(certmanagerv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(configv1.Install(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(v1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\t// +kubebuilder:scaffold:scheme\n}\n\n// Manager holds the manager resource for the controller-runtime based controllers.\ntype Manager struct {\n\tmanager manager.Manager\n}\n\n// ControllerConfig specifies which controllers to enable in the unified manager.\ntype ControllerConfig struct {\n\tEnableIstioCSR     bool\n\tEnableTrustManager bool\n}\n\n// NewControllerManager creates a unified manager for all enabled operand controllers.\n// It shares a single metrics server, cache, and client across all controllers.\nfunc NewControllerManager(config ControllerConfig) (*Manager, error) {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up unified operator manager\")\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"controller\", \"version\", version.Get())\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"enabled controllers\", \"istioCSR\", config.EnableIstioCSR, \"trustManager\", config.EnableTrustManager)\n\n\tcacheBuilder := newUnifiedCacheBuilder(config)\n\n\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{\n\t\tScheme:   scheme,\n\t\tNewCache: cacheBuilder,\n\t\tLogger:   ctrl.Log.WithName(\"operator-manager\"),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create manager: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Setup enabled controllers\n\tif config.EnableIstioCSR {\n\t\tif err := setupIstioCSRController(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := setupTrustManagerController(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Manager{\n\t\tmanager: mgr,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// setupIstioCSRController creates and registers the IstioCSR controller with the manager.\nfunc setupIstioCSRController(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", istiocsr.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := istiocsr.New(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// setupTrustManagerController creates and registers the TrustManager controller with the manager.\nfunc setupTrustManagerController(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", trustmanager.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := trustmanager.New(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// newUnifiedCacheBuilder creates a cache builder that combines cache configurations\n// for all enabled controllers into a single unified cache.\nfunc newUnifiedCacheBuilder(config ControllerConfig) cache.NewCacheFunc {\n\treturn func(restConfig *rest.Config, opts cache.Options) (cache.Cache, error) {\n\t\tobjectList, err := buildCacheObjectList(config)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\topts.ByObject = objectList\n\t\treturn cache.New(restConfig, opts)\n\t}\n}\n\n// buildCacheObjectList creates the cache configuration with label selectors\n// for managed resources based on enabled controllers.\n// All controllers use common.ManagedResourceLabelKey as the label key.\nfunc buildCacheObjectList(config ControllerConfig) (map[client.Object]cache.ByObject, error) {\n\tobjectList := make(map[client.Object]cache.ByObject)\n\n\tif config.EnableIstioCSR {\n\t\tif err := addControllerCacheConfig(objectList, istiocsr.RequestEnqueueLabelValue, istioCSRManagedResources); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to configure IstioCSR cache: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// IstioCSR CR - no label filter needed\n\t\tobjectList[&v1alpha1.IstioCSR{}] = cache.ByObject{}\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := addControllerCacheConfig(objectList, trustmanager.RequestEnqueueLabelValue, trustManagerManagedResources); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to configure TrustManager cache: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// TrustManager CR - no label filter needed\n\t\tobjectList[&v1alpha1.TrustManager{}] = cache.ByObject{}\n\t}\n\n\treturn objectList, nil\n}\n\n// addControllerCacheConfig adds cache configuration for a controller's managed resources.\n// All controllers use common.ManagedResourceLabelKey as the label key.\n// If a resource type already exists (from another controller), the label selector is updated\n// to use the 'In' operator to match resources from either controller.\nfunc addControllerCacheConfig(objectList map[client.Object]cache.ByObject, labelValue string, resources []client.Object) error {\n\tlabelKey := common.ManagedResourceLabelKey\n\n\tfor _, res := range resources {\n\t\tresType := fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", res)\n\n\t\tif existingKey, existing, found := findExistingCacheEntry(objectList, res); found {\n\t\t\t// Resource already configured by another controller\n\t\t\t// Merge label values using 'In' operator: app in (value1, value2)\n\t\t\texistingReqs, _ := existing.Label.Requirements()\n\t\t\tvar existingValues []string\n\t\t\tfor _, req := range existingReqs {\n\t\t\t\tif req.Key() == labelKey {\n\t\t\t\t\texistingValues = req.Values().List()\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Create new requirement with both values\n\t\t\tmergedValues := append(existingValues, labelValue)\n\t\t\tmergedReq, err := labels.NewRequirement(labelKey, selection.In, mergedValues)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create merged label requirement for key %q with values %v: %w\", labelKey, mergedValues, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tobjectList[existingKey] = cache.ByObject{Label: labels.NewSelector().Add(*mergedReq)}\n\t\t\tsetupLog.V(4).Info(\"merged label selector for shared resource\", \"type\", resType, \"values\", mergedValues)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// First controller to configure this resource\n\t\t\tlabelReq, err := labels.NewRequirement(labelKey, selection.Equals, []string{labelValue})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create label requirement for key %q with value %q: %w\", labelKey, labelValue, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tobjectList[res] = cache.ByObject{Label: labels.NewSelector().Add(*labelReq)}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// findExistingCacheEntry looks up an entry in the cache object map by Go type\n// rather than pointer identity, since map[client.Object] uses interface comparison\n// which compares pointers, not types.\nfunc findExistingCacheEntry(objectList map[client.Object]cache.ByObject, target client.Object) (client.Object, cache.ByObject, bool) {\n\ttargetType := reflect.TypeOf(target)\n\tfor key, val := range objectList {\n\t\tif reflect.TypeOf(key) == targetType {\n\t\t\treturn key, val, true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil, cache.ByObject{}, false\n}\n\n// Start starts the unified controller manager synchronously until ctx is cancelled.\nfunc (mgr *Manager) Start(ctx context.Context) error {\n\treturn mgr.manager.Start(ctx)\n}\n","numLines":279,"startLine":1,"totalLines":279}}}

err != nil {\n31\t\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile serviceaccount resource\")\n32\t\t\treturn err\n33\t\t}\n34\t\n35\t\tif err := r.createOrApplyRBACResources(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, trustNamespace); err != nil {\n51\t\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile certificate resource\")\n52\t\t\treturn err\n53\t\t}\n54\t\n55\t\tif err := r.createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash); trustManager.Status.TrustManagerImage != image {\n91\t\t\ttrustManager.Status.TrustManagerImage = image\n92\t\t\tchanged = true\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\tif ns := getTrustNamespace(trustManager); trustManager.Status.TrustNamespace != ns {\n96\t\t\ttrustManager.Status.TrustNamespace = ns\n97\t\t\tchanged = true\n98\t\t}\n99\t\n100\t\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.SecretTargets.Policy; trustManager.Status.SecretTargetsPolicy != policy {\n101\t\t\ttrustManager.Status.SecretTargetsPolicy = policy\n102\t\t\tchanged = true\n103\t\t}\n104\t\n105\t\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage.Policy; trustManager.Status.DefaultCAPackagePolicy != policy {\n106\t\t\ttrustManager.Status.DefaultCAPackagePolicy = policy\n107\t\t\tchanged = true\n108\t\t}\n109\t\n110\t\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.FilterExpiredCertificates; trustManager.Status.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy != policy {\n111\t\t\ttrustManager.Status.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = policy\n112\t\t\tchanged = true\n113\t\t}\n114\t\n115\t\tif !changed {\n116\t\t\treturn nil\n117\t\t}\n118\t\n119\t\treturn r.updateStatus(r.ctx, trustManager)\n120\t}\n121\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58","uuid":"5fb23ec4-fcd3-4321-a86b-3231e45425ca","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:24:11.695Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/pkg/controller/trustmanager/install_trustmanager.go","content":"package trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n)\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) reconcileTrustManagerDeployment(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif err := validateTrustManagerConfig(trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"%s configuration validation failed\", trustManager.GetName())\n\t}\n\n\tresourceLabels := getResourceLabels(trustManager)\n\tresourceAnnotations := getResourceAnnotations(trustManager)\n\n\ttrustNamespace := getTrustNamespace(trustManager)\n\tif err := r.validateTrustNamespace(trustNamespace); err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"trust namespace %q validation failed\", trustNamespace)\n\t}\n\n\tcaBundleHash, err := r.createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile default CA package ConfigMap\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyServiceAccounts(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile serviceaccount resource\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyRBACResources(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, trustNamespace); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile RBAC resources\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyServices(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile service resources\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyIssuer(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile issuer resource\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyCertificate(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile certificate resource\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile deployment resource\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.createOrApplyValidatingWebhookConfiguration(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations); err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(err, \"failed to reconcile validatingwebhookconfiguration resource\")\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.updateStatusObservedState(trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to update status observed state\")\n\t}\n\n\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"finished reconciliation of trustmanager\", \"name\", trustManager.GetName())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// validateTrustNamespace validates that the trust namespace exists.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) validateTrustNamespace(namespace string) error {\n\texists, err := r.namespaceExists(namespace)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to check if namespace %q exists: %w\", namespace, err)\n\t}\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"trust namespace %q does not exist, create the namespace before creating TrustManager CR\", namespace)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// updateStatusObservedState populates and persists the TrustManager status with the observed state.\n// Returns nil if no changes were needed, otherwise returns an error if the update fails.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) updateStatusObservedState(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tchanged := false\n\n\tif image := os.Getenv(trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName); trustManager.Status.TrustManagerImage != image {\n\t\ttrustManager.Status.TrustManagerImage = image\n\t\tchanged = true\n\t}\n\n\tif ns := getTrustNamespace(trustManager); trustManager.Status.TrustNamespace != ns {\n\t\ttrustManager.Status.TrustNamespace = ns\n\t\tchanged = true\n\t}\n\n\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.SecretTargets.Policy; trustManager.Status.SecretTargetsPolicy != policy {\n\t\ttrustManager.Status.SecretTargetsPolicy = policy\n\t\tchanged = true\n\t}\n\n\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage.Policy; trustManager.Status.DefaultCAPackagePolicy != policy {\n\t\ttrustManager.Status.DefaultCAPackagePolicy = policy\n\t\tchanged = true\n\t}\n\n\tif policy := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.FilterExpiredCertificates; trustManager.Status.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy != policy {\n\t\ttrustManager.Status.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicy = policy\n\t\tchanged = true\n\t}\n\n\tif !changed {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn r.updateStatus(r.ctx, trustManager)\n}\n","numLines":121,"startLine":1,"totalLines":121}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_0139xdmRtFeXT5DMtYzeVZuN","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage v1\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n5\t)\n6\t\n7\t// +genclient\n8\t// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n9\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n10\t\n11\t// Proxy holds cluster-wide information on how to configure default proxies for the cluster. The canonical name is `cluster`\n12\t//\n13\t// Compatibility level 1: Stable within a major release for a minimum of 12 months or 3 minor releases (whichever is longer).\n14\t// +openshift:compatibility-gen:level=1\n15\t// +openshift:api-approved.openshift.io=https://github.com/openshift/api/pull/470\n16\t// +openshift:file-pattern=cvoRunLevel=0000_03,operatorName=config-operator,operatorOrdering=01\n17\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n18\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=proxies,scope=Cluster\n19\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n20\t// +kubebuilder:metadata:annotations=release.openshift.io/bootstrap-required=true\n21\ttype Proxy struct {\n22\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n23\t\n24\t\t// metadata is the standard object's metadata.\n25\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n26\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n27\t\n28\t\t// spec holds user-settable values for the proxy configuration\n29\t\t// +required\n30\t\tSpec ProxySpec `json:\"spec\"`\n31\t\t// status holds observed values from the cluster. They may not be overridden.\n32\t\t// +optional\n33\t\tStatus ProxyStatus `json:\"status\"`\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t// ProxySpec contains cluster proxy creation configuration.\n37\ttype ProxySpec struct {\n38\t\t// httpProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTP requests.  Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n39\t\t// +optional\n40\t\tHTTPProxy string `json:\"httpProxy,omitempty\"`\n41\t\n42\t\t// httpsProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTPS requests.  Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n43\t\t// +optional\n44\t\tHTTPSProxy string `json:\"httpsProxy,omitempty\"`\n45\t\n46\t\t// noProxy is a comma-separated list of hostnames and/or CIDRs and/or IPs for which the proxy should not be used.\n47\t\t// Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n48\t\t// +optional\n49\t\tNoProxy string `json:\"noProxy,omitempty\"`\n50\t\n51\t\t// readinessEndpoints is a list of endpoints used to verify readiness of the proxy.\n52\t\t// +optional\n53\t\tReadinessEndpoints []string `json:\"readinessEndpoints,omitempty\"`\n54\t\n55\t\t// trustedCA is a reference to a ConfigMap containing a CA certificate bundle.\n56\t\t// The trustedCA field should only be consumed by a proxy validator. The\n57\t\t// validator is responsible for reading the certificate bundle from the required\n58\t\t// key \"ca-bundle.crt\", merging it with the system default trust bundle,\n59\t\t// and writing the merged trust bundle to a ConfigMap named \"trusted-ca-bundle\"\n60\t\t// in the \"openshift-config-managed\" namespace. Clients that expect to make\n61\t\t// proxy connections must use the trusted-ca-bundle for all HTTPS requests to\n62\t\t// the proxy, and may use the trusted-ca-bundle for non-proxy HTTPS requests as\n63\t\t// well.\n64\t\t//\n65\t\t// The namespace for the ConfigMap referenced by trustedCA is\n66\t\t// \"openshift-config\". Here is an example ConfigMap (in yaml):\n67\t\t//\n68\t\t// apiVersion: v1\n69\t\t// kind: ConfigMap\n70\t\t// metadata:\n71\t\t//  name: user-ca-bundle\n72\t\t//  namespace: openshift-config\n73\t\t//  data:\n74\t\t//    ca-bundle.crt: |\n75\t\t//      -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n76\t\t//      Custom CA certificate bundle.\n77\t\t//      -----END CERTIFICATE-----\n78\t\t//\n79\t\t// +optional\n80\t\tTrustedCA ConfigMapNameReference `json:\"trustedCA,omitempty\"`\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\t// ProxyStatus shows current known state of the cluster proxy.\n84\ttype ProxyStatus struct {\n85\t\t// httpProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTP requests.\n86\t\t// +optional\n87\t\tHTTPProxy string `json:\"httpProxy,omitempty\"`\n88\t\n89\t\t// httpsProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTPS requests.\n90\t\t// +optional\n91\t\tHTTPSProxy string `json:\"httpsProxy,omitempty\"`\n92\t\n93\t\t// noProxy is a comma-separated list of hostnames and/or CIDRs for which the proxy should not be used.\n94\t\t// +optional\n95\t\tNoProxy string `json:\"noProxy,omitempty\"`\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\t// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n99\t\n100\t// Compatibility level 1: Stable within a major release for a minimum of 12 months or 3 minor releases (whichever is longer).\n101\t// +openshift:compatibility-gen:level=1\n102\ttype ProxyList struct {\n103\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n104\t\n105\t\t// metadata is the standard list's metadata.\n106\t\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n107\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n108\t\n109\t\tItems []Proxy `json:\"items\"`\n110\t}\n111\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58","uuid":"368db9ac-5a98-49be-a8d1-5fb38fb33481","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:24:37.477Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/vendor/github.com/openshift/api/config/v1/types_proxy.go","content":"package v1\n\nimport (\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n)\n\n// +genclient\n// +genclient:nonNamespaced\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n\n// Proxy holds cluster-wide information on how to configure default proxies for the cluster. The canonical name is `cluster`\n//\n// Compatibility level 1: Stable within a major release for a minimum of 12 months or 3 minor releases (whichever is longer).\n// +openshift:compatibility-gen:level=1\n// +openshift:api-approved.openshift.io=https://github.com/openshift/api/pull/470\n// +openshift:file-pattern=cvoRunLevel=0000_03,operatorName=config-operator,operatorOrdering=01\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n// +kubebuilder:resource:path=proxies,scope=Cluster\n// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n// +kubebuilder:metadata:annotations=release.openshift.io/bootstrap-required=true\ntype Proxy struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// metadata is the standard object's metadata.\n\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// spec holds user-settable values for the proxy configuration\n\t// +required\n\tSpec ProxySpec `json:\"spec\"`\n\t// status holds observed values from the cluster. They may not be overridden.\n\t// +optional\n\tStatus ProxyStatus `json:\"status\"`\n}\n\n// ProxySpec contains cluster proxy creation configuration.\ntype ProxySpec struct {\n\t// httpProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTP requests.  Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n\t// +optional\n\tHTTPProxy string `json:\"httpProxy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// httpsProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTPS requests.  Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n\t// +optional\n\tHTTPSProxy string `json:\"httpsProxy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// noProxy is a comma-separated list of hostnames and/or CIDRs and/or IPs for which the proxy should not be used.\n\t// Empty means unset and will not result in an env var.\n\t// +optional\n\tNoProxy string `json:\"noProxy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// readinessEndpoints is a list of endpoints used to verify readiness of the proxy.\n\t// +optional\n\tReadinessEndpoints []string `json:\"readinessEndpoints,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// trustedCA is a reference to a ConfigMap containing a CA certificate bundle.\n\t// The trustedCA field should only be consumed by a proxy validator. The\n\t// validator is responsible for reading the certificate bundle from the required\n\t// key \"ca-bundle.crt\", merging it with the system default trust bundle,\n\t// and writing the merged trust bundle to a ConfigMap named \"trusted-ca-bundle\"\n\t// in the \"openshift-config-managed\" namespace. Clients that expect to make\n\t// proxy connections must use the trusted-ca-bundle for all HTTPS requests to\n\t// the proxy, and may use the trusted-ca-bundle for non-proxy HTTPS requests as\n\t// well.\n\t//\n\t// The namespace for the ConfigMap referenced by trustedCA is\n\t// \"openshift-config\". Here is an example ConfigMap (in yaml):\n\t//\n\t// apiVersion: v1\n\t// kind: ConfigMap\n\t// metadata:\n\t//  name: user-ca-bundle\n\t//  namespace: openshift-config\n\t//  data:\n\t//    ca-bundle.crt: |\n\t//      -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n\t//      Custom CA certificate bundle.\n\t//      -----END CERTIFICATE-----\n\t//\n\t// +optional\n\tTrustedCA ConfigMapNameReference `json:\"trustedCA,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ProxyStatus shows current known state of the cluster proxy.\ntype ProxyStatus struct {\n\t// httpProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTP requests.\n\t// +optional\n\tHTTPProxy string `json:\"httpProxy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// httpsProxy is the URL of the proxy for HTTPS requests.\n\t// +optional\n\tHTTPSProxy string `json:\"httpsProxy,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// noProxy is a comma-separated list of hostnames and/or CIDRs for which the proxy should not be used.\n\t// +optional\n\tNoProxy string `json:\"noProxy,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n\n// Compatibility level 1: Stable within a major release for a minimum of 12 months or 3 minor releases (whichever is longer).\n// +openshift:compatibility-gen:level=1\ntype ProxyList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\n\t// metadata is the standard list's metadata.\n\t// More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata\"`\n\n\tItems []Proxy `json:\"items\"`\n}\n","numLines":111,"startLine":1,"totalLines":111}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01BUiNuPZAhjnBiiP4UrvbEM","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage utils\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"testing\"\n6\t\n7\t\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n8\t\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n9\t\n10\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n11\t\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n12\t\tfakediscovery \"k8s.io/client-go/discovery/fake\"\n13\t\n14\t\toperatorv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n15\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/clientset/versioned/fake\"\n16\t)\n17\t\n18\tfunc createFakeClient(isResourcePresent bool) *fake.Clientset {\n19\t\tif !isResourcePresent {\n20\t\t\treturn fake.NewClientset()\n21\t\t}\n22\t\n23\t\tfakeClient := fake.NewClientset(&operatorv1alpha1.CertManager{\n24\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n25\t\t\t\tName: \"cluster\",\n26\t\t\t},\n27\t\t\tStatus: operatorv1alpha1.CertManagerStatus{},\n28\t\t})\n29\t\n30\t\t// The fake clientset does not populate APIResourceList by default; discovery\n31\t\t// in tests requires these resources.\n32\t\tfakeClient.Resources = []*metav1.APIResourceList{\n33\t\t\t{\n34\t\t\t\tGroupVersion: operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),\n35\t\t\t\tAPIResources: []metav1.APIResource{\n36\t\t\t\t\t{\n37\t\t\t\t\t\tName:         \"certmanagers\",\n38\t\t\t\t\t\tSingularName: \"certmanager\",\n39\t\t\t\t\t\tNamespaced:   false,\n40\t\t\t\t\t\tGroup:        operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.Group,\n41\t\t\t\t\t\tVersion:      operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.Version,\n42\t\t\t\t\t\tKind:         \"CertManager\",\n43\t\t\t\t\t\tVerbs:        []string{\"get\", \"list\", \"create\", \"update\", \"patch\", \"watch\", \"delete\"},\n44\t\t\t\t\t},\n45\t\t\t\t},\n46\t\t\t},\n47\t\t}\n48\t\n49\t\treturn fakeClient\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\ttype alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery struct {\n53\t\tfakediscovery.FakeDiscovery\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t// ServerResourcesForGroupVersion is the only func apiResourceDiscoverer's discovery client calls.\n57\tfunc (f *alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery) ServerResourcesForGroupVersion(groupVersion string) (*metav1.APIResourceList, error) {\n58\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"simulated discovery error\")\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\tfunc createErroneousFakeDiscoveryClient() discovery.DiscoveryInterface {\n62\t\treturn &alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery{}\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t// TestOptionalInformer covers NewResourceDiscoverer, Discover, and\n66\t// InitInformerIfAvailable end-to-end.\n67\tfunc TestOptionalInformer(t *testing.T) {\n68\t\ttype fakeInformerFactoryStub struct{}\n69\t\tdummyInformerInit := func() fakeInformerFactoryStub {\n70\t\t\treturn struct{}{}\n71\t\t}\n72\t\n73\t\tfixedGVRForTest := operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(\"certmanagers\")\n74\t\n75\t\tt.Run(\"positive cases with no expected errors\", func(t *testing.T) {\n76\t\t\ttests := []struct {\n77\t\t\t\tisCRDPresent   bool\n78\t\t\t\texpectInformer bool\n79\t\t\t}{\n80\t\t\t\t// positive cases with no error\n81\t\t\t\t// false => false, true => true\n82\t\t\t\t{isCRDPresent: false, expectInformer: false},\n83\t\t\t\t{isCRDPresent: true, expectInformer: true},\n84\t\t\t}\n85\t\n86\t\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n87\t\t\t\tfakeClient := createFakeClient(tt.isCRDPresent)\n88\t\n89\t\t\t\tprobe := NewResourceDiscoverer(fixedGVRForTest, fakeClient.Discovery())\n90\t\t\t\toptInformer, err := InitInformerIfAvailable(probe, dummyInformerInit)\n91\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n92\t\n93\t\t\t\tdiscovered, err := probe.Discover()\n94\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n95\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.isCRDPresent, discovered, \"discovery does not match fake API resource list\")\n96\t\n97\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.expectInformer, optInformer.Applicable(), \"undesired optional informer applicable(ity)\")\n98\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.expectInformer, optInformer.InformerFactory != nil, \"broken informer factory init func call\")\n99\t\t\t}\n100\t\t})\n101\t\n102\t\tt.Run(\"negative case with an expected error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n103\t\t\terrorProneDiscoveryClient := createErroneousFakeDiscoveryClient()\n104\t\t\t_, err := InitInformerIfAvailable(\n105\t\t\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(fixedGVRForTest, errorProneDiscoveryClient),\n106\t\t\t\tdummyInformerInit,\n107\t\t\t)\n108\t\n109\t\t\trequire.Error(t, err)\n110\t\t})\n111\t}\n112\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58","uuid":"b822754c-ab83-481e-bb7d-599750fe2bea","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:24:58.714Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery_test.go","content":"package utils\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n\tfakediscovery \"k8s.io/client-go/discovery/fake\"\n\n\toperatorv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/clientset/versioned/fake\"\n)\n\nfunc createFakeClient(isResourcePresent bool) *fake.Clientset {\n\tif !isResourcePresent {\n\t\treturn fake.NewClientset()\n\t}\n\n\tfakeClient := fake.NewClientset(&operatorv1alpha1.CertManager{\n\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n\t\t\tName: \"cluster\",\n\t\t},\n\t\tStatus: operatorv1alpha1.CertManagerStatus{},\n\t})\n\n\t// The fake clientset does not populate APIResourceList by default; discovery\n\t// in tests requires these resources.\n\tfakeClient.Resources = []*metav1.APIResourceList{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tGroupVersion: operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.String(),\n\t\t\tAPIResources: []metav1.APIResource{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:         \"certmanagers\",\n\t\t\t\t\tSingularName: \"certmanager\",\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespaced:   false,\n\t\t\t\t\tGroup:        operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.Group,\n\t\t\t\t\tVersion:      operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.Version,\n\t\t\t\t\tKind:         \"CertManager\",\n\t\t\t\t\tVerbs:        []string{\"get\", \"list\", \"create\", \"update\", \"patch\", \"watch\", \"delete\"},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\treturn fakeClient\n}\n\ntype alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery struct {\n\tfakediscovery.FakeDiscovery\n}\n\n// ServerResourcesForGroupVersion is the only func apiResourceDiscoverer's discovery client calls.\nfunc (f *alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery) ServerResourcesForGroupVersion(groupVersion string) (*metav1.APIResourceList, error) {\n\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"simulated discovery error\")\n}\n\nfunc createErroneousFakeDiscoveryClient() discovery.DiscoveryInterface {\n\treturn &alwaysErrorFakeDiscovery{}\n}\n\n// TestOptionalInformer covers NewResourceDiscoverer, Discover, and\n// InitInformerIfAvailable end-to-end.\nfunc TestOptionalInformer(t *testing.T) {\n\ttype fakeInformerFactoryStub struct{}\n\tdummyInformerInit := func() fakeInformerFactoryStub {\n\t\treturn struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tfixedGVRForTest := operatorv1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource(\"certmanagers\")\n\n\tt.Run(\"positive cases with no expected errors\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttests := []struct {\n\t\t\tisCRDPresent   bool\n\t\t\texpectInformer bool\n\t\t}{\n\t\t\t// positive cases with no error\n\t\t\t// false => false, true => true\n\t\t\t{isCRDPresent: false, expectInformer: false},\n\t\t\t{isCRDPresent: true, expectInformer: true},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\t\tfakeClient := createFakeClient(tt.isCRDPresent)\n\n\t\t\tprobe := NewResourceDiscoverer(fixedGVRForTest, fakeClient.Discovery())\n\t\t\toptInformer, err := InitInformerIfAvailable(probe, dummyInformerInit)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tdiscovered, err := probe.Discover()\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.isCRDPresent, discovered, \"discovery does not match fake API resource list\")\n\n\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.expectInformer, optInformer.Applicable(), \"undesired optional informer applicable(ity)\")\n\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, tt.expectInformer, optInformer.InformerFactory != nil, \"broken informer factory init func call\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"negative case with an expected error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\terrorProneDiscoveryClient := createErroneousFakeDiscoveryClient()\n\t\t_, err := InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(fixedGVRForTest, errorProneDiscoveryClient),\n\t\t\tdummyInformerInit,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\trequire.Error(t, err)\n\t})\n}\n","numLines":112,"startLine":1,"totalLines":112}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01WZrwJRrY3rt81yQwi1nPM4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"maps\"\n6\t\t\"os\"\n7\t\t\"reflect\"\n8\t\t\"slices\"\n9\t\n10\t\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n11\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n12\t\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n13\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n14\t\n15\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n16\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n17\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/assets\"\n18\t)\n19\t\n20\tfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) error {\n21\t\tdesired, err := r.getDeploymentObject(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash)\n22\t\tif err != nil {\n23\t\t\treturn err\n24\t\t}\n25\t\n26\t\tdeploymentName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n27\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling deployment resource\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n28\t\n29\t\texisting := &appsv1.Deployment{}\n30\t\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n31\t\tif err != nil {\n32\t\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if deployment %q exists\", deploymentName)\n33\t\t}\n34\t\tif exists && !deploymentModified(desired, existing) {\n35\t\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"deployment resource exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n36\t\t\treturn nil\n37\t\t}\n38\t\n39\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"deployment resource has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n40\t\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n41\t\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply deployment %q\", deploymentName)\n42\t\t}\n43\t\n44\t\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"deployment resource %s applied\", deploymentName)\n45\t\treturn nil\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\tfunc (r *Reconciler) getDeploymentObject(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) (*appsv1.Deployment, error) {\n49\t\tdeployment := common.DecodeObjBytes[*appsv1.Deployment](codecs, appsv1.SchemeGroupVersion, assets.MustAsset(deploymentAssetName))\n50\t\n51\t\tif err := validateDeploymentManifest(deployment); err != nil {\n52\t\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"invalid trust-manager deployment manifest\")\n53\t\t}\n54\t\n55\t\tcommon.UpdateName(deployment, trustManagerDeploymentName)\n56\t\tcommon.UpdateNamespace(deployment, operandNamespace)\n57\t\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n58\t\tupdateResourceAnnotations(deployment, resourceAnnotations)\n59\t\tupdatePodTemplateLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n60\t\tupdateDeploymentArgs(deployment, trustManager)\n61\t\n62\t\tupdateServiceAccountName(deployment)\n63\t\tupdateTLSSecretVolume(deployment)\n64\t\tupdateDefaultCAPackageVolume(\n65\t\t\tdeployment,\n66\t\t\ttrustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage,\n67\t\t\tcaBundleHash,\n68\t\t)\n69\t\n70\t\tif err := updateImage(deployment); err != nil {\n71\t\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"failed to update trust-manager image\")\n72\t\t}\n73\t\n74\t\tif err := updateResourceRequirements(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n75\t\t\treturn nil, err\n76\t\t}\n77\t\tif err := updateAffinityRules(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n78\t\t\treturn nil, err\n79\t\t}\n80\t\tif err := updatePodTolerations(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n81\t\t\treturn nil, err\n82\t\t}\n83\t\tif err := updateNodeSelector(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n84\t\t\treturn nil, err\n85\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\treturn deployment, nil\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\t// validateDeploymentManifest checks that the bindata deployment contains the\n91\t// expected container and TLS volume. Without these, the update helpers silently\n92\t// no-op and the operator would apply a deployment with stale defaults.\n93\tfunc validateDeploymentManifest(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n94\t\thasContainer := false\n95\t\tfor _, c := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n96\t\t\tif c.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n97\t\t\t\thasContainer = true\n98\t\t\t\tbreak\n99\t\t\t}\n100\t\t}\n101\t\tif !hasContainer {\n102\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required container %q\", trustManagerContainerName)\n103\t\t}\n104\t\n105\t\thasVolume := false\n106\t\tfor _, v := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n107\t\t\tif v.Name == tlsVolumeName && v.Secret != nil {\n108\t\t\t\thasVolume = true\n109\t\t\t\tbreak\n110\t\t\t}\n111\t\t}\n112\t\tif !hasVolume {\n113\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required secret volume %q\", tlsVolumeName)\n114\t\t}\n115\t\n116\t\treturn nil\n117\t}\n118\t\n119\tfunc updatePodTemplateLabels(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, resourceLabels map[string]string) {\n120\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Labels = resourceLabels\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\tfunc updateDeploymentArgs(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) {\n124\t\tconfig := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig\n125\t\ttrustNamespace := getTrustNamespace(trustManager)\n126\t\n127\t\targs := []string{\n128\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-format=%s\", config.LogFormat),\n129\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-level=%d\", config.LogLevel),\n130\t\t\t\"--metrics-port=9402\",\n131\t\t\t\"--readiness-probe-port=6060\",\n132\t\t\t\"--readiness-probe-path=/readyz\",\n133\t\t\t\"--leader-elect=true\",\n134\t\t\t\"--leader-election-lease-duration=15s\",\n135\t\t\t\"--leader-election-renew-deadline=10s\",\n136\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--trust-namespace=%s\", trustNamespace),\n137\t\t\t\"--webhook-host=0.0.0.0\",\n138\t\t\t\"--webhook-port=6443\",\n139\t\t\t\"--webhook-certificate-dir=/tls\",\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\tif secretTargetsEnabled(config.SecretTargets) {\n143\t\t\targs = append(args, \"--secret-targets-enabled=true\")\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\tif config.FilterExpiredCertificates == v1alpha1.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled {\n147\t\t\targs = append(args, \"--filter-expired-certificates=true\")\n148\t\t}\n149\t\n150\t\tif defaultCAPackageEnabled(config.DefaultCAPackage) {\n151\t\t\targs = append(args, fmt.Sprintf(\"--default-package-location=%s\", defaultCAPackageLocation))\n152\t\t}\n153\t\n154\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n155\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n156\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Args = args\n157\t\t\t}\n158\t\t}\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t// updateDefaultCAPackageVolume adds the default CA package ConfigMap volume\n162\t// and mount to the deployment when the feature is enabled. It also sets the\n163\t// hash annotation on the pod template to trigger rolling restarts when the\n164\t// CA bundle content changes.\n165\tfunc updateDefaultCAPackageVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, config v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackageConfig, caBundleHash string) {\n166\t\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(config) {\n167\t\t\treturn\n168\t\t}\n169\t\n170\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations == nil {\n171\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n172\t\t}\n173\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations[defaultCAPackageHashAnnotation] = caBundleHash\n174\t\n175\t\t// Remove any existing volume with the same name to avoid duplicates.\n176\t\tvar volumes []corev1.Volume\n177\t\tfor _, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n178\t\t\tif vol.Name != defaultCAPackageVolumeName {\n179\t\t\t\tvolumes = append(volumes, vol)\n180\t\t\t}\n181\t\t}\n182\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes = append(volumes, corev1.Volume{\n183\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n184\t\t\tVolumeSource: corev1.VolumeSource{\n185\t\t\t\tConfigMap: &corev1.ConfigMapVolumeSource{\n186\t\t\t\t\tLocalObjectReference: corev1.LocalObjectReference{\n187\t\t\t\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n188\t\t\t\t\t},\n189\t\t\t\t},\n190\t\t\t},\n191\t\t})\n192\t\n193\t\t// Remove any existing volume mount at the same path, then add ours.\n194\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n195\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n196\t\t\t\tvar mounts []corev1.VolumeMount\n197\t\t\t\tfor _, vm := range container.VolumeMounts {\n198\t\t\t\t\tif vm.MountPath != defaultCAPackageMountPath {\n199\t\t\t\t\t\tmounts = append(mounts, vm)\n200\t\t\t\t\t}\n201\t\t\t\t}\n202\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].VolumeMounts = append(mounts, corev1.VolumeMount{\n203\t\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n204\t\t\t\t\tMountPath: defaultCAPackageMountPath,\n205\t\t\t\t\tReadOnly:  true,\n206\t\t\t\t})\n207\t\t\t\tbreak\n208\t\t\t}\n209\t\t}\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\tfunc updateImage(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n213\t\timage := os.Getenv(trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n214\t\tif image == \"\" {\n215\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s environment variable with trust-manager image not set\", trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n216\t\t}\n217\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n218\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n219\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Image = image\n220\t\t\t}\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn nil\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\tfunc updateResourceRequirements(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n226\t\tresources := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Resources\n227\t\tif len(resources.Limits) == 0 && len(resources.Requests) == 0 {\n228\t\t\treturn nil\n229\t\t}\n230\t\tif err := common.ValidateResourceRequirements(resources, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n231\t\t\treturn err\n232\t\t}\n233\t\tfor i := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n234\t\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n235\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Resources = resources\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t}\n238\t\treturn nil\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\tfunc updateAffinityRules(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n242\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity == nil {\n243\t\t\treturn nil\n244\t\t}\n245\t\tif err := common.ValidateAffinityRules(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n246\t\t\treturn err\n247\t\t}\n248\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Affinity = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity\n249\t\treturn nil\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\tfunc updatePodTolerations(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n253\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations == nil {\n254\t\t\treturn nil\n255\t\t}\n256\t\tif err := common.ValidateTolerationsConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n257\t\t\treturn err\n258\t\t}\n259\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Tolerations = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations\n260\t\treturn nil\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\tfunc updateNodeSelector(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n264\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector == nil {\n265\t\t\treturn nil\n266\t\t}\n267\t\tif err := common.ValidateNodeSelectorConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n268\t\t\treturn err\n269\t\t}\n270\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n271\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector = make(map[string]string)\n272\t\t}\n273\t\t// Merge user-specified node selectors with any default selectors from the manifest.\n274\t\t// User-specified selectors take precedence.\n275\t\tfor k, v := range trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector {\n276\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector[k] = v\n277\t\t}\n278\t\treturn nil\n279\t}\n280\t\n281\tfunc updateServiceAccountName(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n282\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.ServiceAccountName = trustManagerServiceAccountName\n283\t}\n284\t\n285\tconst tlsVolumeName = \"tls\"\n286\t\n287\tfunc updateTLSSecretVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n288\t\tfor i, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n289\t\t\tif vol.Name == tlsVolumeName && vol.Secret != nil {\n290\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes[i].Secret.SecretName = trustManagerTLSSecretName\n291\t\t\t\treturn\n292\t\t\t}\n293\t\t}\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// deploymentModified compares only the fields we manage via SSA.\n297\t// For the readiness probe, only the fields explicitly set in our bindata are\n298\t// compared to avoid false positives from API-server-defaulted probe scalars\n299\t// (TimeoutSeconds, SuccessThreshold, FailureThreshold).\n300\tfunc deploymentModified(desired, existing *appsv1.Deployment) bool {\n301\t\tif managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) {\n302\t\t\treturn true\n303\t\t}\n304\t\n305\t\tif !ptr.Equal(desired.Spec.Replicas, existing.Spec.Replicas) ||\n306\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Spec.Selector, existing.Spec.Selector) {\n307\t\t\treturn true\n308\t\t}\n309\t\n310\t\tif !maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Labels, existing.Spec.Template.Labels) ||\n311\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Annotations, existing.Spec.Template.Annotations) {\n312\t\t\treturn true\n313\t\t}\n314\t\n315\t\tdesiredPodSpec := desired.Spec.Template.Spec\n316\t\texistingPodSpec := existing.Spec.Template.Spec\n317\t\n318\t\tif desiredPodSpec.ServiceAccountName != existingPodSpec.ServiceAccountName ||\n319\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desiredPodSpec.NodeSelector, existingPodSpec.NodeSelector) ||\n320\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Volumes, existingPodSpec.Volumes) ||\n321\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Affinity, existingPodSpec.Affinity) ||\n322\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Tolerations, existingPodSpec.Tolerations) {\n323\t\t\treturn true\n324\t\t}\n325\t\n326\t\tif len(desiredPodSpec.Containers) != len(existingPodSpec.Containers) {\n327\t\t\treturn true\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\tfor i := range desiredPodSpec.Containers {\n331\t\t\tif containerModified(&desiredPodSpec.Containers[i], &existingPodSpec.Containers[i]) {\n332\t\t\t\treturn true\n333\t\t\t}\n334\t\t}\n335\t\n336\t\treturn false\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\tfunc containerModified(desired, existing *corev1.Container) bool {\n340\t\tif desired.Name != existing.Name ||\n341\t\t\tdesired.Image != existing.Image ||\n342\t\t\tdesired.ImagePullPolicy != existing.ImagePullPolicy {\n343\t\t\treturn true\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\tif !slices.Equal(desired.Args, existing.Args) ||\n347\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Resources, existing.Resources) ||\n348\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.SecurityContext, existing.SecurityContext) ||\n349\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.VolumeMounts, existing.VolumeMounts) {\n350\t\t\treturn true\n351\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\tif !containerPortsMatch(desired.Ports, existing.Ports) {\n354\t\t\treturn true\n355\t\t}\n356\t\n357\t\tif readinessProbeModified(desired.ReadinessProbe, existing.ReadinessProbe) {\n358\t\t\treturn true\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\treturn false\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\tfunc containerPortsMatch(desired, existing []corev1.ContainerPort) bool {\n365\t\tif len(desired) != len(existing) {\n366\t\t\treturn false\n367\t\t}\n368\t\tfor _, dp := range desired {\n369\t\t\tmatched := false\n370\t\t\tfor _, ep := range existing {\n371\t\t\t\tif dp.ContainerPort == ep.ContainerPort && dp.Name == ep.Name {\n372\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n373\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n374\t\t\t\t}\n375\t\t\t}\n376\t\t\tif !matched {\n377\t\t\t\treturn false\n378\t\t\t}\n379\t\t}\n380\t\treturn true\n381\t}\n382\t\n383\t// readinessProbeModified compares only the probe fields we explicitly set in\n384\t// bindata (HTTPGet, InitialDelaySeconds, PeriodSeconds), avoiding\n385\t// API-server-defaulted int32 fields like TimeoutSeconds and FailureThreshold.\n386\tfunc readinessProbeModified(desired, existing *corev1.Probe) bool {\n387\t\tif desired == nil && existing == nil {\n388\t\t\treturn false\n389\t\t}\n390\t\tif desired == nil || existing == nil {\n391\t\t\treturn true\n392\t\t}\n393\t\tif desired.InitialDelaySeconds != existing.InitialDelaySeconds ||\n394\t\t\tdesired.PeriodSeconds != existing.PeriodSeconds {\n395\t\t\treturn true\n396\t\t}\n397\t\tif desired.HTTPGet != nil && existing.HTTPGet != nil {\n398\t\t\tif desired.HTTPGet.Path != existing.HTTPGet.Path ||\n399\t\t\t\tdesired.HTTPGet.Port != existing.HTTPGet.Port {\n400\t\t\t\treturn true\n401\t\t\t}\n402\t\t} else if desired.HTTPGet != existing.HTTPGet {\n403\t\t\treturn true\n404\t\t}\n405\t\treturn false\n406\t}\n407\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58","uuid":"1da7e39e-2bc8-4bff-870b-5c6b59f327ca","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:25:05.774Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/pkg/controller/trustmanager/deployments.go","content":"package trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"maps\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\t\"slices\"\n\n\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/assets\"\n)\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) error {\n\tdesired, err := r.getDeploymentObject(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tdeploymentName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling deployment resource\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\n\texisting := &appsv1.Deployment{}\n\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if deployment %q exists\", deploymentName)\n\t}\n\tif exists && !deploymentModified(desired, existing) {\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"deployment resource exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"deployment resource has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply deployment %q\", deploymentName)\n\t}\n\n\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"deployment resource %s applied\", deploymentName)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) getDeploymentObject(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) (*appsv1.Deployment, error) {\n\tdeployment := common.DecodeObjBytes[*appsv1.Deployment](codecs, appsv1.SchemeGroupVersion, assets.MustAsset(deploymentAssetName))\n\n\tif err := validateDeploymentManifest(deployment); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"invalid trust-manager deployment manifest\")\n\t}\n\n\tcommon.UpdateName(deployment, trustManagerDeploymentName)\n\tcommon.UpdateNamespace(deployment, operandNamespace)\n\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n\tupdateResourceAnnotations(deployment, resourceAnnotations)\n\tupdatePodTemplateLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n\tupdateDeploymentArgs(deployment, trustManager)\n\n\tupdateServiceAccountName(deployment)\n\tupdateTLSSecretVolume(deployment)\n\tupdateDefaultCAPackageVolume(\n\t\tdeployment,\n\t\ttrustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage,\n\t\tcaBundleHash,\n\t)\n\n\tif err := updateImage(deployment); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"failed to update trust-manager image\")\n\t}\n\n\tif err := updateResourceRequirements(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updateAffinityRules(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updatePodTolerations(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tif err := updateNodeSelector(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn deployment, nil\n}\n\n// validateDeploymentManifest checks that the bindata deployment contains the\n// expected container and TLS volume. Without these, the update helpers silently\n// no-op and the operator would apply a deployment with stale defaults.\nfunc validateDeploymentManifest(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n\thasContainer := false\n\tfor _, c := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif c.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\thasContainer = true\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif !hasContainer {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required container %q\", trustManagerContainerName)\n\t}\n\n\thasVolume := false\n\tfor _, v := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif v.Name == tlsVolumeName && v.Secret != nil {\n\t\t\thasVolume = true\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif !hasVolume {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required secret volume %q\", tlsVolumeName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updatePodTemplateLabels(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, resourceLabels map[string]string) {\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Labels = resourceLabels\n}\n\nfunc updateDeploymentArgs(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) {\n\tconfig := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig\n\ttrustNamespace := getTrustNamespace(trustManager)\n\n\targs := []string{\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-format=%s\", config.LogFormat),\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-level=%d\", config.LogLevel),\n\t\t\"--metrics-port=9402\",\n\t\t\"--readiness-probe-port=6060\",\n\t\t\"--readiness-probe-path=/readyz\",\n\t\t\"--leader-elect=true\",\n\t\t\"--leader-election-lease-duration=15s\",\n\t\t\"--leader-election-renew-deadline=10s\",\n\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--trust-namespace=%s\", trustNamespace),\n\t\t\"--webhook-host=0.0.0.0\",\n\t\t\"--webhook-port=6443\",\n\t\t\"--webhook-certificate-dir=/tls\",\n\t}\n\n\tif secretTargetsEnabled(config.SecretTargets) {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--secret-targets-enabled=true\")\n\t}\n\n\tif config.FilterExpiredCertificates == v1alpha1.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled {\n\t\targs = append(args, \"--filter-expired-certificates=true\")\n\t}\n\n\tif defaultCAPackageEnabled(config.DefaultCAPackage) {\n\t\targs = append(args, fmt.Sprintf(\"--default-package-location=%s\", defaultCAPackageLocation))\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Args = args\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// updateDefaultCAPackageVolume adds the default CA package ConfigMap volume\n// and mount to the deployment when the feature is enabled. It also sets the\n// hash annotation on the pod template to trigger rolling restarts when the\n// CA bundle content changes.\nfunc updateDefaultCAPackageVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, config v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackageConfig, caBundleHash string) {\n\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(config) {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations == nil {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations[defaultCAPackageHashAnnotation] = caBundleHash\n\n\t// Remove any existing volume with the same name to avoid duplicates.\n\tvar volumes []corev1.Volume\n\tfor _, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif vol.Name != defaultCAPackageVolumeName {\n\t\t\tvolumes = append(volumes, vol)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes = append(volumes, corev1.Volume{\n\t\tName: defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n\t\tVolumeSource: corev1.VolumeSource{\n\t\t\tConfigMap: &corev1.ConfigMapVolumeSource{\n\t\t\t\tLocalObjectReference: corev1.LocalObjectReference{\n\t\t\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\n\t// Remove any existing volume mount at the same path, then add ours.\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tvar mounts []corev1.VolumeMount\n\t\t\tfor _, vm := range container.VolumeMounts {\n\t\t\t\tif vm.MountPath != defaultCAPackageMountPath {\n\t\t\t\t\tmounts = append(mounts, vm)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].VolumeMounts = append(mounts, corev1.VolumeMount{\n\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n\t\t\t\tMountPath: defaultCAPackageMountPath,\n\t\t\t\tReadOnly:  true,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc updateImage(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n\timage := os.Getenv(trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n\tif image == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s environment variable with trust-manager image not set\", trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n\t}\n\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Image = image\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateResourceRequirements(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tresources := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Resources\n\tif len(resources.Limits) == 0 && len(resources.Requests) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateResourceRequirements(resources, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tfor i := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Resources = resources\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateAffinityRules(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateAffinityRules(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Affinity = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updatePodTolerations(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateTolerationsConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Tolerations = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateNodeSelector(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif err := common.ValidateNodeSelectorConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\t// Merge user-specified node selectors with any default selectors from the manifest.\n\t// User-specified selectors take precedence.\n\tfor k, v := range trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector {\n\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc updateServiceAccountName(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.ServiceAccountName = trustManagerServiceAccountName\n}\n\nconst tlsVolumeName = \"tls\"\n\nfunc updateTLSSecretVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n\tfor i, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n\t\tif vol.Name == tlsVolumeName && vol.Secret != nil {\n\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes[i].Secret.SecretName = trustManagerTLSSecretName\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// deploymentModified compares only the fields we manage via SSA.\n// For the readiness probe, only the fields explicitly set in our bindata are\n// compared to avoid false positives from API-server-defaulted probe scalars\n// (TimeoutSeconds, SuccessThreshold, FailureThreshold).\nfunc deploymentModified(desired, existing *appsv1.Deployment) bool {\n\tif managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !ptr.Equal(desired.Spec.Replicas, existing.Spec.Replicas) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Spec.Selector, existing.Spec.Selector) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Labels, existing.Spec.Template.Labels) ||\n\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Annotations, existing.Spec.Template.Annotations) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tdesiredPodSpec := desired.Spec.Template.Spec\n\texistingPodSpec := existing.Spec.Template.Spec\n\n\tif desiredPodSpec.ServiceAccountName != existingPodSpec.ServiceAccountName ||\n\t\t!maps.Equal(desiredPodSpec.NodeSelector, existingPodSpec.NodeSelector) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Volumes, existingPodSpec.Volumes) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Affinity, existingPodSpec.Affinity) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Tolerations, existingPodSpec.Tolerations) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif len(desiredPodSpec.Containers) != len(existingPodSpec.Containers) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := range desiredPodSpec.Containers {\n\t\tif containerModified(&desiredPodSpec.Containers[i], &existingPodSpec.Containers[i]) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc containerModified(desired, existing *corev1.Container) bool {\n\tif desired.Name != existing.Name ||\n\t\tdesired.Image != existing.Image ||\n\t\tdesired.ImagePullPolicy != existing.ImagePullPolicy {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !slices.Equal(desired.Args, existing.Args) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Resources, existing.Resources) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.SecurityContext, existing.SecurityContext) ||\n\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.VolumeMounts, existing.VolumeMounts) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif !containerPortsMatch(desired.Ports, existing.Ports) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\tif readinessProbeModified(desired.ReadinessProbe, existing.ReadinessProbe) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc containerPortsMatch(desired, existing []corev1.ContainerPort) bool {\n\tif len(desired) != len(existing) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor _, dp := range desired {\n\t\tmatched := false\n\t\tfor _, ep := range existing {\n\t\t\tif dp.ContainerPort == ep.ContainerPort && dp.Name == ep.Name {\n\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !matched {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// readinessProbeModified compares only the probe fields we explicitly set in\n// bindata (HTTPGet, InitialDelaySeconds, PeriodSeconds), avoiding\n// API-server-defaulted int32 fields like TimeoutSeconds and FailureThreshold.\nfunc readinessProbeModified(desired, existing *corev1.Probe) bool {\n\tif desired == nil && existing == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tif desired == nil || existing == nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif desired.InitialDelaySeconds != existing.InitialDelaySeconds ||\n\t\tdesired.PeriodSeconds != existing.PeriodSeconds {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif desired.HTTPGet != nil && existing.HTTPGet != nil {\n\t\tif desired.HTTPGet.Path != existing.HTTPGet.Path ||\n\t\t\tdesired.HTTPGet.Port != existing.HTTPGet.Port {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t} else if desired.HTTPGet != existing.HTTPGet {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n","numLines":407,"startLine":1,"totalLines":407}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01LSxTwZwQ7BeLx26z6f3K6g","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"os\"\n5\t\t\"time\"\n6\t\n7\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\"\n8\t)\n9\t\n10\tconst (\n11\t\t// trustManagerCommonName is the name commonly used for naming resources.\n12\t\ttrustManagerCommonName = \"cert-manager-trust-manager\"\n13\t\n14\t\t// ControllerName is the name of the controller used in logs and events.\n15\t\tControllerName = TestReconcile(t *testing.T) {\n23\t\ttests := []struct {\n24\t\t\tname    string\n25\t\t\tpreReq  func(*Reconciler, *fakes.FakeCtrlClient)\n26\t\t\twantErr string\n27\t\t}{\n28\t\t\t{\n29\t\t\t\tname: \"resource not found returns no error\",\n30\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n31\t\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context, key client.ObjectKey, obj client.Object) error {\n32\t\t\t\t\t\treturn apierrors.NewNotFound(v1alpha1.Resource(\"trustmanager\"), trustManagerObjectName)\n33\t\t\t\t\t})\n34\t\t\t\t},\n35\t\t\t},\n36\t\t\t{\n37\t\t\t\tname: \"failed to fetch Here is the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## Complete Implementation\n\n### File 1: `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go` — Add `NewProxyInformerIfAvailable`\n\nAdd the following function at the end of the file (after the `Applicable()` method):\n\n```go\n// pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go\n// ADD these lines at the end of the file, after the Applicable() method:\n\n// NewProxyInformerIfAvailable discovers whether config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy is\n// served by the cluster and, if so, initialises a SharedInformerFactory that can\n// list/watch Proxy objects. On non-OpenShift clusters the returned\n// OptionalInformer will have InformerFactory == nil and Applicable() == false.\nfunc NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory,\n) (*OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory], error) {\n\tproxyGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"proxies\")\n\treturn InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(proxyGVR, discoveryClient),\n\t\tinformerInitFunc,\n\t)\n}\n```\n\nAnd add the required imports. The full modified import block becomes:\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n)\n```\n\n**Full file `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage utils\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n)\n\ntype ResourceDiscoverer interface {\n\tDiscover() (bool, error)\n}\n\ntype apiResourceDiscoverer struct {\n\tgvr             schema.GroupVersionResource\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface\n}\n\ntype OptionalInformer[GroupInformer any] struct {\n\tInformerFactory *GroupInformer\n}\n\nfunc NewResourceDiscoverer(gvr schema.GroupVersionResource, discoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface) ResourceDiscoverer {\n\treturn &apiResourceDiscoverer{\n\t\tgvr:             gvr,\n\t\tdiscoveryClient: discoveryClient,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (a *apiResourceDiscoverer) Discover() (bool, error) {\n\tresources, err := a.discoveryClient.ServerResourcesForGroupVersion(a.gvr.GroupVersion().String())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover resources: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, res := range resources.APIResources {\n\t\tif res.Name == a.gvr.Resource {\n\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc InitInformerIfAvailable[groupInformer any](\n\td ResourceDiscoverer,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() groupInformer,\n) (*OptionalInformer[groupInformer], error) {\n\to := new(OptionalInformer[groupInformer])\n\n\tdiscovered, err := d.Discover()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif discovered {\n\t\tinformer := informerInitFunc()\n\t\to.InformerFactory = &informer\n\t}\n\n\treturn o, nil\n}\n\nfunc (o *OptionalInformer[GroupInformer]) Applicable() bool {\n\treturn o.InformerFactory != nil\n}\n\n// NewProxyInformerIfAvailable discovers whether config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy is\n// served by the cluster and, if so, initialises a SharedInformerFactory that can\n// list/watch Proxy objects. On non-OpenShift clusters the returned\n// OptionalInformer will have InformerFactory == nil and Applicable() == false.\nfunc NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory,\n) (*OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory], error) {\n\tproxyGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"proxies\")\n\treturn InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(proxyGVR, discoveryClient),\n\t\tinformerInitFunc,\n\t)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 2: `pkg/operator/starter.go` — Discover the Proxy informer and pass it to the controller manager\n\n**Changes:**\n1. After the `optInfraInformer` block (~line 97), add discovery of the Proxy informer.\n2. Pass it into `ControllerConfig`.\n3. Start the informer if applicable.\n\n**Modified `RunOperator` function (showing the diff region):**\n\nAfter line 99 (`}`), insert:\n\n```go\n\toptProxyInformer, err := utils.NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tconfigClient.Discovery(),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Proxy presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n```\n\nIn the informer start block (after line 135 where `optInfraInformer` is started), add:\n\n```go\n\tif optProxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optProxyInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n```\n\nUpdate `ControllerConfig` struct and the `NewControllerManager` call to carry the proxy informer. In the manager creation block (~line 150–153), update to:\n\n```go\n\t\tmanager, err := NewControllerManager(ControllerConfig{\n\t\t\tEnableIstioCSR:     istioCSREnabled,\n\t\t\tEnableTrustManager: trustManagerEnabled,\n\t\t\tProxyInformer:      optProxyInformer,\n\t\t})\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 3: `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go` — Thread ProxyInformer through to the TrustManager controller\n\n**Changes to `ControllerConfig`:**\n\n```go\ntype ControllerConfig struct {\n\tEnableIstioCSR     bool\n\tEnableTrustManager bool\n\tProxyInformer \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/utils\"\n```\n\n**Change `setupTrustManagerController`** to pass the proxy informer:\n\n```go\nfunc setupTrustManagerController(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", trustmanager.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := trustmanager.New(mgr, proxyInformer)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n**Update `NewControllerManager`** call site for trust manager:\n\n```go\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := setupTrustManagerController(mgr, config.ProxyInformer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n```\n\n**Full modified `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage operator\n\nimport \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\tclientscheme \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/manager\"\n\n\tcertmanagerv1 (\n\tscheme   = runtime.NewScheme()\n\tsetupLog = ctrl.Log.WithName(\"setup-manager\")\n)\n\nvar istioCSRManagedResources = []client.Object{\n\t&certmanagerv1.Certificate{},\n\t&appsv1.Deployment{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRole{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{},\n\t&rbacv1.Role{},\n\t&rbacv1.RoleBinding{},\n\t&corev1.Service{},\n\t&corev1.ServiceAccount{},\n\t&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{},\n}\n\nvar trustManagerManagedResources = Manager struct {\n\tmanager manager.Manager\n}\n\ntype ControllerConfig struct {\n\tEnableIstioCSR     bool\n\tEnableTrustManager bool\n\tProxyInformer      *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]\n}\n\nfunc NewControllerManager(config ControllerConfig) (*Manager, error) {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up unified operator manager\")\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"controller\", \"version\", version.Get())\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"enabled controllers\", \"istioCSR\", config.EnableIstioCSR, \"trustManager\", config.EnableTrustManager)\n\n\tcacheBuilder := newUnifiedCacheBuilder(config)\n\n\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{\n\t\tScheme:   scheme,\n\t\tNewCache: cacheBuilder,\n\t\tLogger:   ctrl.Log.WithName(\"operator-manager\"),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create manager: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableIstioCSR {\n\t\tif err := setupIstioCSRController(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := setupTrustManagerController(mgr, config.ProxyInformer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Manager{\n\t\tmanager: mgr,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc setupIstioCSRController(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", istiocsr.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := istiocsr.New(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc setupTrustManagerController(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", trustmanager.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := trustmanager.New(mgr, proxyInformer)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ... rest of file unchanged (newUnifiedCacheBuilder, buildCacheObjectList, etc.)\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 4: `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go` — Accept and use the ProxyInformer\n\n**Changes:**\n1.
