Call the guard from `reconcileTrustManagerDeployment` via the `Reconcile` method, or more precisely from `processReconcileRequest`.\n\n**Full modified `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tadmissionregistrationv1 \"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1\"\n\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\trbacv1 \"k8s.io/api/rbac/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/builder\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/event\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-logr/logr\"\n\n\tcertmanagerv1 \"github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pkg/apis/certmanager/v1\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\tv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/utils\"\n)\n\nconst RequestEnqueueLabelValue = \"cert-manager-trust-manager\"\n\ntype Reconciler struct {\n\tcommon.CtrlClient\n\n\tctx            context.Context\n\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n\tlog            logr.Logger\n\tscheme         *runtime.Scheme\n\tproxyInformer  *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]\n}\n\n// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=config.openshift.io,resources=proxies,verbs=get;list;watch\n\nfunc New(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) (*Reconciler, error) {\n\tc, err := common.NewClient(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn &Reconciler{\n\t\tCtrlClient:    c,\n\t\tctx:           context.Background(),\n\t\teventRecorder: mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(ControllerName),\n\t\tlog:           ctrl.Log.WithName(ControllerName),\n\t\tscheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tproxyInformer: proxyInformer,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tmapFunc := func(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object) []reconcile.Request {\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"received reconcile event\", \"object\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", obj), \"name\", obj.GetName(), \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace())\n\t\treturn []reconcile.Request{{NamespacedName: types.NamespacedName{Name: trustManagerObjectName}}}\n\t}\n\n\tisManagedResource := func(object client.Object) bool {\n\t\tlabels := object.GetLabels()\n\t\tmatches := labels != nil && labels[common.ManagedResourceLabelKey] == RequestEnqueueLabelValue\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"predicate evaluation\", \"object\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", object), \"name\", object.GetName(), \"namespace\", object.GetNamespace(), \"labels\", labels, \"matches\", matches)\n\t\treturn matches\n\t}\n\n\tcontrollerManagedResources := predicate.Funcs{\n\t\tCreateFunc: func(e event.CreateEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t\tUpdateFunc: func(e event.UpdateEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.ObjectOld) || isManagedResource(e.ObjectNew)\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeleteFunc: func(e event.DeleteEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t\tGenericFunc: func(e event.GenericEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcontrollerManagedResourcePredicates := builder.WithPredicates(controllerManagedResources)\n\n\twithIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates := builder.WithPredicates(\n\t\tpredicate.Or(\n\t\t\tpredicate.GenerationChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t\tpredicate.LabelChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t\tpredicate.AnnotationChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t),\n\t\tcontrollerManagedResources,\n\t)\n\n\tinjectedCABundleConfigMapPredicate := predicate.NewPredicateFuncs(func(object client.Object) bool {\n\t\treturn object.GetNamespace() == common.OperatorNamespace &&\n\t\t\tobject.GetName() == common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName\n\t})\n\n\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n\t\tFor(&v1alpha1.TrustManager{}, builder.WithPredicates(predicate.GenerationChangedPredicate{})).\n\t\tNamed(ControllerName).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ServiceAccount{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&appsv1.Deployment{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.Service{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ConfigMap{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ConfigMap{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), builder.WithPredicates(injectedCABundleConfigMapPredicate)).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.ClusterRole{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.Role{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.RoleBinding{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&certmanagerv1.Certificate{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&certmanagerv1.Issuer{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&admissionregistrationv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tComplete(r)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"reconciling\", \"request\", req)\n\n\ttrustManager := &v1alpha1.TrustManager{}\n\tif err := r.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: req.Name}, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"trustmanager.openshift.operator.io object not found, skipping reconciliation\", \"request\", req)\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch trustmanager.openshift.operator.io %q during reconciliation: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !trustManager.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero() {\n\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"trustmanager.openshift.operator.io is marked for deletion\", \"name\", req.NamespacedName)\n\n\t\tif requeue, err := r.cleanUp(trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"clean up failed for %q trustmanager.openshift.operator.io instance deletion: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t\t} else if requeue {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: defaultRequeueTime}, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err := r.removeFinalizer(ctx, trustManager, finalizer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"removed finalizer, cleanup complete\", \"request\", req.NamespacedName)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.addFinalizer(ctx, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to update %q trustmanager.openshift.operator.io with finalizers: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn r.processReconcileRequest(trustManager, req.NamespacedName)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) processReconcileRequest(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, req types.NamespacedName) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\tr.readClusterProxyConfig()\n\n\treconcileErr := r.reconcileTrustManagerDeployment(trustManager)\n\tif reconcileErr != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(reconcileErr, \"failed to reconcile TrustManager deployment\", \"request\", req)\n\t}\n\n\treturn common.HandleReconcileResult(\n\t\t&trustManager.Status.ConditionalStatus,\n\t\treconcileErr,\n\t\tr.log.WithValues(\"name\", trustManager.GetName()),\n\t\tfunc(prependErr error) error {\n\t\t\treturn r.updateCondition(trustManager, prependErr)\n\t\t},\n\t\tdefaultRequeueTime,\n\t)\n}\n\n// readClusterProxyConfig reads the cluster-wide Proxy configuration from the\n// informer cache when the config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy API is available.\n// On non-OpenShift clusters (or when the API is absent) this is a no-op.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) readClusterProxyConfig() {\n\tif r.proxyInformer == nil || !r.proxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tproxyLister := (*r.proxyInformer.InformerFactory).Config().V1().Proxies().Lister()\n\tproxy, err := proxyLister.Get(\"cluster\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"unable to read cluster proxy configuration\", \"error\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tr.applyProxySettings(proxy)\n}\n\n// applyProxySettings logs and applies the cluster proxy settings to the\n// reconciliation context. Currently this surfaces the proxy configuration for\n// observability; downstream reconcile steps can use the values for environment\n// variable injection on the trust-manager deployment.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) applyProxySettings(proxy *configv1.Proxy) {\n\tif proxy.Status.HTTPProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy HTTP_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.HTTPProxy)\n\t}\n\tif proxy.Status.HTTPSProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy HTTPS_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.HTTPSProxy)\n\t}\n\tif proxy.Status.NoProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy NO_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.NoProxy)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) cleanUp(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) (bool, error) {\n\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeWarning, \"RemoveDeployment\", \"%s trustmanager marked for deletion, remove all resources created for trustmanager deployment manually\", trustManager.GetName())\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 5: `pkg/operator/starter.go` — Full modified `RunOperator`\n\nThe key additions are the proxy informer discovery and start, plus updating the `ControllerConfig`:\n\n```go\nfunc RunOperator(ctx context.Context, cc *controllercmd.ControllerContext) error {\n\tctrl.SetLogger(klog.NewKlogr())\n\n\tkubeClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(cc.ProtoKubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerOperatorClient, err := certmanoperatorclient.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tapiExtensionsClient, err := apiextensionsclient.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerInformers := certmanoperatorinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(certManagerOperatorClient, resyncInterval)\n\n\toperatorClient := &operatorclient.OperatorClient{\n\t\tInformers: certManagerInformers,\n\t\tClient:    certManagerOperatorClient.OperatorV1alpha1(),\n\t\tClock:     cc.Clock,\n\t}\n\n\tversionRecorder := status.NewVersionGetter()\n\tversionRecorder.SetVersion(\"operator\", status.VersionForOperatorFromEnv())\n\n\tkubeInformersForNamespaces := v1helpers.NewKubeInformersForNamespaces(kubeClient,\n\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\"kube-system\",\n\t\toperatorclient.TargetNamespace,\n\t)\n\n\tconfigClient, err := configv1client.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tinfraGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"infrastructures\")\n\toptInfraInformer, err := utils.InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tutils.NewResourceDiscoverer(infraGVR, configClient.Discovery()),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Infrastructure presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\toptProxyInformer, err := utils.NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tconfigClient.Discovery(),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Proxy presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerControllerSet := certmanager.NewCertManagerControllerSet(\n\t\tkubeClient,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces.InformersFor(operatorclient.TargetNamespace),\n\t\t*optInfraInformer,\n\t\toperatorClient,\n\t\tcertManagerInformers,\n\t\tresourceapply.NewKubeClientHolder(kubeClient).WithAPIExtensionsClient(apiExtensionsClient),\n\t\tcc.EventRecorder,\n\t\tstatus.VersionForOperandFromEnv(),\n\t\tversionRecorder,\n\t\tTrustedCAConfigMapName,\n\t\tCloudCredentialSecret,\n\t)\n\tcontrollersToStart := certManagerControllerSet.ToArray()\n\n\tdefaultCertManagerController := certmanager.NewDefaultCertManagerController(\n\t\toperatorClient,\n\t\tcertManagerOperatorClient.OperatorV1alpha1(),\n\t\tcc.EventRecorder,\n\t)\n\n\tcontrollersToStart = append(controllersToStart, defaultCertManagerController)\n\n\tfor _, informer := range []interface{ Start(<-chan struct{}) }{\n\t\tcertManagerInformers,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces,\n\t} {\n\t\tinformer.Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tif optInfraInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optInfraInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tif optProxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optProxyInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, controller := range controllersToStart {\n\t\tgo controller.Run(ctx, 1)\n\t}\n\n\tfeatureStatus, err := setupFeatureGates(ctx, configClient)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tistioCSREnabled := features.IsIstioCSRFeatureGateEnabled()\n\ttrustManagerEnabled := featureStatus.IsTrustManagerFeatureGateEnabled()\n\n\tif istioCSREnabled || trustManagerEnabled {\n\t\tmanager, err := NewControllerManager(ControllerConfig{\n\t\t\tEnableIstioCSR:     istioCSREnabled,\n\t\t\tEnableTrustManager: trustManagerEnabled,\n\t\t\tProxyInformer:      optProxyInformer,\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create unified controller manager: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tif err := manager.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tctrl.Log.Error(err, \"failed to start unified controller manager\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}()\n\t}\n\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 6: `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller_test.go` — Update test helper\n\nThe `New()` constructor signature changed. The tests construct `Reconciler` directly, so check if they call `New()`. Looking at the test file, it constructs `Reconciler` directly with struct literals, so the test helper needs updating for the new field.\n\nLooking at the test, reconcilers are constructed like:\n\n```go\nr := &Reconciler{\n    CtrlClient:    fakes.NewFakeCtrlClient(),\n    ctx:           context.Background(),\n    eventRecorder: record.NewFakeRecorder(10),\n    log:           ctrl.Log.WithName(\"test\"),\n    scheme:        runtime.NewScheme(),\n}\n```\n\nThese need the new `proxyInformer` field. Since it's a pointer type, a nil value is fine and means \"proxy API unavailable\" — which is the correct default for tests. **No changes are required to existing tests** since the zero value (`nil`) for the new pointer field is valid and means `Applicable()` returns false.\n\n---\n\n### Summary of all changes\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go` | Add imports for `configv1` and `configinformers`; add `NewProxyInformerIfAvailable` function |\n| `pkg/operator/starter.go` | Add proxy informer discovery after infra informer; start it if applicable; pass to `ControllerConfig` |\n| `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go` | Add `ProxyInformer` field to `ControllerConfig`; add imports; update `setupTrustManagerController` signature to pass proxy informer through to `trustmanager.New()` |\n| `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go` | Add `proxyInformer` field to `Reconciler`; update `New()` signature; add imports for `configv1`, `configinformers`, `utils`; add `readClusterProxyConfig()` and `applyProxySettings()` methods; call `readClusterProxyConfig()` from `processReconcileRequest`; add RBAC marker for proxies |\n\n### Commands to build and verify\n\n```bash\n# From the repository root:\n\n# 1. Verify compilation\ngo build ./...\n\n# 2. Run unit tests\ngo test ./pkg/operator/utils/... -v\ngo test ./pkg/controller/trustmanager/... -v\n\n# 3. Run vet\ngo vet ./...\n\n# 4. If a Makefile target exists for full validation:\nmake build\nmake test\n```"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":792,"cache_read_input_tokens":69900,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":792,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58","uuid":"0ab89fd0-76c5-4c9b-8e4e-824682010d63","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:27:11.255Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDPFAg6RpskWzbSSjFfn"}

Here is the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## Complete Implementation\n\n### File 1: `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go` \u2014 Add `NewProxyInformerIfAvailable`\n\nAdd the following function at the end of the file (after the `Applicable()` method):\n\n```go\n// pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go\n// ADD these lines at the end of the file, after the Applicable() method:\n\n// NewProxyInformerIfAvailable discovers whether config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy is\n// served by the cluster and, if so, initialises a SharedInformerFactory that can\n// list/watch Proxy objects. On non-OpenShift clusters the returned\n// OptionalInformer will have InformerFactory == nil and Applicable() == false.\nfunc NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory,\n) (*OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory], error) {\n\tproxyGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"proxies\")\n\treturn InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(proxyGVR, discoveryClient),\n\t\tinformerInitFunc,\n\t)\n}\n```\n\nAnd add the required imports. The full modified import block becomes:\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n)\n```\n\n**Full file `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage utils\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/discovery\"\n)\n\ntype ResourceDiscoverer interface {\n\tDiscover() (bool, error)\n}\n\ntype apiResourceDiscoverer struct {\n\tgvr             schema.GroupVersionResource\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface\n}\n\ntype OptionalInformer[GroupInformer any] struct {\n\tInformerFactory *GroupInformer\n}\n\nfunc NewResourceDiscoverer(gvr schema.GroupVersionResource, discoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface) ResourceDiscoverer {\n\treturn &apiResourceDiscoverer{\n\t\tgvr:             gvr,\n\t\tdiscoveryClient: discoveryClient,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (a *apiResourceDiscoverer) Discover() (bool, error) {\n\tresources, err := a.discoveryClient.ServerResourcesForGroupVersion(a.gvr.GroupVersion().String())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\treturn false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover resources: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, res := range resources.APIResources {\n\t\tif res.Name == a.gvr.Resource {\n\t\t\treturn true, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc InitInformerIfAvailable[groupInformer any](\n\td ResourceDiscoverer,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() groupInformer,\n) (*OptionalInformer[groupInformer], error) {\n\to := new(OptionalInformer[groupInformer])\n\n\tdiscovered, err := d.Discover()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif discovered {\n\t\tinformer := informerInitFunc()\n\t\to.InformerFactory = &informer\n\t}\n\n\treturn o, nil\n}\n\nfunc (o *OptionalInformer[GroupInformer]) Applicable() bool {\n\treturn o.InformerFactory != nil\n}\n\n// NewProxyInformerIfAvailable discovers whether config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy is\n// served by the cluster and, if so, initialises a SharedInformerFactory that can\n// list/watch Proxy objects. On non-OpenShift clusters the returned\n// OptionalInformer will have InformerFactory == nil and Applicable() == false.\nfunc NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\tdiscoveryClient discovery.DiscoveryInterface,\n\tinformerInitFunc func() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory,\n) (*OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory], error) {\n\tproxyGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"proxies\")\n\treturn InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tNewResourceDiscoverer(proxyGVR, discoveryClient),\n\t\tinformerInitFunc,\n\t)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 2: `pkg/operator/starter.go` \u2014 Discover the Proxy informer and pass it to the controller manager\n\n**Changes:**\n1. After the `optInfraInformer` block (~line 97), add discovery of the Proxy informer.\n2. Pass it into `ControllerConfig`.\n3. Start the informer if applicable.\n\n**Modified `RunOperator` function (showing the diff region):**\n\nAfter line 99 (`}`), insert:\n\n```go\n\toptProxyInformer, err := utils.NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tconfigClient.Discovery(),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Proxy presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n```\n\nIn the informer start block (after line 135 where `optInfraInformer` is started), add:\n\n```go\n\tif optProxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optProxyInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n```\n\nUpdate `ControllerConfig` struct and the `NewControllerManager` call to carry the proxy informer. In the manager creation block (~line 150\u2013153), update to:\n\n```go\n\t\tmanager, err := NewControllerManager(ControllerConfig{\n\t\t\tEnableIstioCSR:     istioCSREnabled,\n\t\t\tEnableTrustManager: trustManagerEnabled,\n\t\t\tProxyInformer:      optProxyInformer,\n\t\t})\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 3: `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go` \u2014 Thread ProxyInformer through to the TrustManager controller\n\n**Changes to `ControllerConfig`:**\n\n```go\ntype ControllerConfig struct {\n\tEnableIstioCSR     bool\n\tEnableTrustManager bool\n\tProxyInformer      *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]\n}\n```\n\nAdd the import for `utils`:\n```go\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/utils\"\n```\n\n**Change `setupTrustManagerController`** to pass the proxy informer:\n\n```go\nfunc setupTrustManagerController(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", trustmanager.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := trustmanager.New(mgr, proxyInformer)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n**Update `NewControllerManager`** call site for trust manager:\n\n```go\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := setupTrustManagerController(mgr, config.ProxyInformer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n```\n\n**Full modified `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage operator\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\n\tadmissionregistrationv1 \"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1\"\n\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tnetworkingv1 \"k8s.io/api/networking/v1\"\n\trbacv1 \"k8s.io/api/rbac/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/selection\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\tclientscheme \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/manager\"\n\n\tcertmanagerv1 \"github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pkg/apis/certmanager/v1\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\tv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/istiocsr\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/trustmanager\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/utils\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/version\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tscheme   = runtime.NewScheme()\n\tsetupLog = ctrl.Log.WithName(\"setup-manager\")\n)\n\nvar istioCSRManagedResources = []client.Object{\n\t&certmanagerv1.Certificate{},\n\t&appsv1.Deployment{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRole{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{},\n\t&rbacv1.Role{},\n\t&rbacv1.RoleBinding{},\n\t&corev1.Service{},\n\t&corev1.ServiceAccount{},\n\t&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{},\n}\n\nvar trustManagerManagedResources = []client.Object{\n\t&certmanagerv1.Certificate{},\n\t&appsv1.Deployment{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRole{},\n\t&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{},\n\t&rbacv1.Role{},\n\t&rbacv1.RoleBinding{},\n\t&corev1.Service{},\n\t&corev1.ServiceAccount{},\n\t&admissionregistrationv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration{},\n}\n\nfunc init() {\n\tutilruntime.Must(clientscheme.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(appsv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(corev1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(networkingv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(rbacv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(admissionregistrationv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(certmanagerv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(configv1.Install(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(v1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n}\n\ntype Manager struct {\n\tmanager manager.Manager\n}\n\ntype ControllerConfig struct {\n\tEnableIstioCSR     bool\n\tEnableTrustManager bool\n\tProxyInformer      *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]\n}\n\nfunc NewControllerManager(config ControllerConfig) (*Manager, error) {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up unified operator manager\")\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"controller\", \"version\", version.Get())\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"enabled controllers\", \"istioCSR\", config.EnableIstioCSR, \"trustManager\", config.EnableTrustManager)\n\n\tcacheBuilder := newUnifiedCacheBuilder(config)\n\n\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{\n\t\tScheme:   scheme,\n\t\tNewCache: cacheBuilder,\n\t\tLogger:   ctrl.Log.WithName(\"operator-manager\"),\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create manager: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableIstioCSR {\n\t\tif err := setupIstioCSRController(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif config.EnableTrustManager {\n\t\tif err := setupTrustManagerController(mgr, config.ProxyInformer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Manager{\n\t\tmanager: mgr,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc setupIstioCSRController(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", istiocsr.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := istiocsr.New(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", istiocsr.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc setupTrustManagerController(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) error {\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"setting up controller\", \"name\", trustmanager.ControllerName)\n\tr, err := trustmanager.New(mgr, proxyInformer)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s reconciler object: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\tif err := r.SetupWithManager(mgr); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create %s controller: %w\", trustmanager.ControllerName, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ... rest of file unchanged (newUnifiedCacheBuilder, buildCacheObjectList, etc.)\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 4: `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go` \u2014 Accept and use the ProxyInformer\n\n**Changes:**\n1. Add `proxyInformer` field to `Reconciler`.\n2. Update `New()` to accept the `OptionalInformer`.\n3. Add a `readClusterProxyConfig` method.\n4. Call the guard from `reconcileTrustManagerDeployment` via the `Reconcile` method, or more precisely from `processReconcileRequest`.\n\n**Full modified `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go`:**\n\n```go\npackage trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tadmissionregistrationv1 \"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1\"\n\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\trbacv1 \"k8s.io/api/rbac/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/builder\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/event\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/predicate\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-logr/logr\"\n\n\tcertmanagerv1 \"github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pkg/apis/certmanager/v1\"\n\n\tconfigv1 \"github.com/openshift/api/config/v1\"\n\tconfiginformers \"github.com/openshift/client-go/config/informers/externalversions\"\n\n\tv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/utils\"\n)\n\nconst RequestEnqueueLabelValue = \"cert-manager-trust-manager\"\n\ntype Reconciler struct {\n\tcommon.CtrlClient\n\n\tctx            context.Context\n\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n\tlog            logr.Logger\n\tscheme         *runtime.Scheme\n\tproxyInformer  *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]\n}\n\n// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=config.openshift.io,resources=proxies,verbs=get;list;watch\n\nfunc New(mgr ctrl.Manager, proxyInformer *utils.OptionalInformer[configinformers.SharedInformerFactory]) (*Reconciler, error) {\n\tc, err := common.NewClient(mgr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn &Reconciler{\n\t\tCtrlClient:    c,\n\t\tctx:           context.Background(),\n\t\teventRecorder: mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(ControllerName),\n\t\tlog:           ctrl.Log.WithName(ControllerName),\n\t\tscheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tproxyInformer: proxyInformer,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\tmapFunc := func(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object) []reconcile.Request {\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"received reconcile event\", \"object\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", obj), \"name\", obj.GetName(), \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace())\n\t\treturn []reconcile.Request{{NamespacedName: types.NamespacedName{Name: trustManagerObjectName}}}\n\t}\n\n\tisManagedResource := func(object client.Object) bool {\n\t\tlabels := object.GetLabels()\n\t\tmatches := labels != nil && labels[common.ManagedResourceLabelKey] == RequestEnqueueLabelValue\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"predicate evaluation\", \"object\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", object), \"name\", object.GetName(), \"namespace\", object.GetNamespace(), \"labels\", labels, \"matches\", matches)\n\t\treturn matches\n\t}\n\n\tcontrollerManagedResources := predicate.Funcs{\n\t\tCreateFunc: func(e event.CreateEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t\tUpdateFunc: func(e event.UpdateEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.ObjectOld) || isManagedResource(e.ObjectNew)\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeleteFunc: func(e event.DeleteEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t\tGenericFunc: func(e event.GenericEvent) bool {\n\t\t\treturn isManagedResource(e.Object)\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tcontrollerManagedResourcePredicates := builder.WithPredicates(controllerManagedResources)\n\n\twithIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates := builder.WithPredicates(\n\t\tpredicate.Or(\n\t\t\tpredicate.GenerationChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t\tpredicate.LabelChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t\tpredicate.AnnotationChangedPredicate{},\n\t\t),\n\t\tcontrollerManagedResources,\n\t)\n\n\tinjectedCABundleConfigMapPredicate := predicate.NewPredicateFuncs(func(object client.Object) bool {\n\t\treturn object.GetNamespace() == common.OperatorNamespace &&\n\t\t\tobject.GetName() == common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName\n\t})\n\n\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n\t\tFor(&v1alpha1.TrustManager{}, builder.WithPredicates(predicate.GenerationChangedPredicate{})).\n\t\tNamed(ControllerName).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ServiceAccount{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&appsv1.Deployment{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.Service{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ConfigMap{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&corev1.ConfigMap{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), builder.WithPredicates(injectedCABundleConfigMapPredicate)).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.ClusterRole{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.Role{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&rbacv1.RoleBinding{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&certmanagerv1.Certificate{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&certmanagerv1.Issuer{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), withIgnoreStatusUpdatePredicates).\n\t\tWatches(&admissionregistrationv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration{}, handler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(mapFunc), controllerManagedResourcePredicates).\n\t\tComplete(r)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"reconciling\", \"request\", req)\n\n\ttrustManager := &v1alpha1.TrustManager{}\n\tif err := r.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: req.Name}, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"trustmanager.openshift.operator.io object not found, skipping reconciliation\", \"request\", req)\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch trustmanager.openshift.operator.io %q during reconciliation: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !trustManager.DeletionTimestamp.IsZero() {\n\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"trustmanager.openshift.operator.io is marked for deletion\", \"name\", req.NamespacedName)\n\n\t\tif requeue, err := r.cleanUp(trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"clean up failed for %q trustmanager.openshift.operator.io instance deletion: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t\t} else if requeue {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: defaultRequeueTime}, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err := r.removeFinalizer(ctx, trustManager, finalizer); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tr.log.V(1).Info(\"removed finalizer, cleanup complete\", \"request\", req.NamespacedName)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif err := r.addFinalizer(ctx, trustManager); err != nil {\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to update %q trustmanager.openshift.operator.io with finalizers: %w\", req.NamespacedName, err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn r.processReconcileRequest(trustManager, req.NamespacedName)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) processReconcileRequest(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, req types.NamespacedName) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\tr.readClusterProxyConfig()\n\n\treconcileErr := r.reconcileTrustManagerDeployment(trustManager)\n\tif reconcileErr != nil {\n\t\tr.log.Error(reconcileErr, \"failed to reconcile TrustManager deployment\", \"request\", req)\n\t}\n\n\treturn common.HandleReconcileResult(\n\t\t&trustManager.Status.ConditionalStatus,\n\t\treconcileErr,\n\t\tr.log.WithValues(\"name\", trustManager.GetName()),\n\t\tfunc(prependErr error) error {\n\t\t\treturn r.updateCondition(trustManager, prependErr)\n\t\t},\n\t\tdefaultRequeueTime,\n\t)\n}\n\n// readClusterProxyConfig reads the cluster-wide Proxy configuration from the\n// informer cache when the config.openshift.io/v1 Proxy API is available.\n// On non-OpenShift clusters (or when the API is absent) this is a no-op.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) readClusterProxyConfig() {\n\tif r.proxyInformer == nil || !r.proxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tproxyLister := (*r.proxyInformer.InformerFactory).Config().V1().Proxies().Lister()\n\tproxy, err := proxyLister.Get(\"cluster\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"unable to read cluster proxy configuration\", \"error\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tr.applyProxySettings(proxy)\n}\n\n// applyProxySettings logs and applies the cluster proxy settings to the\n// reconciliation context. Currently this surfaces the proxy configuration for\n// observability; downstream reconcile steps can use the values for environment\n// variable injection on the trust-manager deployment.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) applyProxySettings(proxy *configv1.Proxy) {\n\tif proxy.Status.HTTPProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy HTTP_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.HTTPProxy)\n\t}\n\tif proxy.Status.HTTPSProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy HTTPS_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.HTTPSProxy)\n\t}\n\tif proxy.Status.NoProxy != \"\" {\n\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"cluster proxy NO_PROXY\", \"value\", proxy.Status.NoProxy)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) cleanUp(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) (bool, error) {\n\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeWarning, \"RemoveDeployment\", \"%s trustmanager marked for deletion, remove all resources created for trustmanager deployment manually\", trustManager.GetName())\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 5: `pkg/operator/starter.go` \u2014 Full modified `RunOperator`\n\nThe key additions are the proxy informer discovery and start, plus updating the `ControllerConfig`:\n\n```go\nfunc RunOperator(ctx context.Context, cc *controllercmd.ControllerContext) error {\n\tctrl.SetLogger(klog.NewKlogr())\n\n\tkubeClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(cc.ProtoKubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerOperatorClient, err := certmanoperatorclient.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tapiExtensionsClient, err := apiextensionsclient.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerInformers := certmanoperatorinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(certManagerOperatorClient, resyncInterval)\n\n\toperatorClient := &operatorclient.OperatorClient{\n\t\tInformers: certManagerInformers,\n\t\tClient:    certManagerOperatorClient.OperatorV1alpha1(),\n\t\tClock:     cc.Clock,\n\t}\n\n\tversionRecorder := status.NewVersionGetter()\n\tversionRecorder.SetVersion(\"operator\", status.VersionForOperatorFromEnv())\n\n\tkubeInformersForNamespaces := v1helpers.NewKubeInformersForNamespaces(kubeClient,\n\t\t\"\",\n\t\t\"kube-system\",\n\t\toperatorclient.TargetNamespace,\n\t)\n\n\tconfigClient, err := configv1client.NewForConfig(cc.KubeConfig)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tinfraGVR := configv1.GroupVersion.WithResource(\"infrastructures\")\n\toptInfraInformer, err := utils.InitInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tutils.NewResourceDiscoverer(infraGVR, configClient.Discovery()),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Infrastructure presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\toptProxyInformer, err := utils.NewProxyInformerIfAvailable(\n\t\tconfigClient.Discovery(),\n\t\tfunc() configinformers.SharedInformerFactory {\n\t\t\treturn configinformers.NewSharedInformerFactory(configClient, resyncInterval)\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to discover Proxy presence: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tcertManagerControllerSet := certmanager.NewCertManagerControllerSet(\n\t\tkubeClient,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces.InformersFor(operatorclient.TargetNamespace),\n\t\t*optInfraInformer,\n\t\toperatorClient,\n\t\tcertManagerInformers,\n\t\tresourceapply.NewKubeClientHolder(kubeClient).WithAPIExtensionsClient(apiExtensionsClient),\n\t\tcc.EventRecorder,\n\t\tstatus.VersionForOperandFromEnv(),\n\t\tversionRecorder,\n\t\tTrustedCAConfigMapName,\n\t\tCloudCredentialSecret,\n\t)\n\tcontrollersToStart := certManagerControllerSet.ToArray()\n\n\tdefaultCertManagerController := certmanager.NewDefaultCertManagerController(\n\t\toperatorClient,\n\t\tcertManagerOperatorClient.OperatorV1alpha1(),\n\t\tcc.EventRecorder,\n\t)\n\n\tcontrollersToStart = append(controllersToStart, defaultCertManagerController)\n\n\tfor _, informer := range []interface{ Start(<-chan struct{}) }{\n\t\tcertManagerInformers,\n\t\tkubeInformersForNamespaces,\n\t} {\n\t\tinformer.Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tif optInfraInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optInfraInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tif optProxyInformer.Applicable() {\n\t\t(*optProxyInformer.InformerFactory).Start(ctx.Done())\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, controller := range controllersToStart {\n\t\tgo controller.Run(ctx, 1)\n\t}\n\n\tfeatureStatus, err := setupFeatureGates(ctx, configClient)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tistioCSREnabled := features.IsIstioCSRFeatureGateEnabled()\n\ttrustManagerEnabled := featureStatus.IsTrustManagerFeatureGateEnabled()\n\n\tif istioCSREnabled || trustManagerEnabled {\n\t\tmanager, err := NewControllerManager(ControllerConfig{\n\t\t\tEnableIstioCSR:     istioCSREnabled,\n\t\t\tEnableTrustManager: trustManagerEnabled,\n\t\t\tProxyInformer:      optProxyInformer,\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create unified controller manager: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tif err := manager.Start(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tctrl.Log.Error(err, \"failed to start unified controller manager\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}()\n\t}\n\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### File 6: `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller_test.go` \u2014 Update test helper\n\nThe `New()` constructor signature changed. The tests construct `Reconciler` directly, so check if they call `New()`. Looking at the test file, it constructs `Reconciler` directly with struct literals, so the test helper needs updating for the new field.\n\nLooking at the test, reconcilers are constructed like:\n\n```go\nr := &Reconciler{\n    CtrlClient:    fakes.NewFakeCtrlClient(),\n    ctx:           context.Background(),\n    eventRecorder: record.NewFakeRecorder(10),\n    log:           ctrl.Log.WithName(\"test\"),\n    scheme:        runtime.NewScheme(),\n}\n```\n\nThese need the new `proxyInformer` field. Since it's a pointer type, a nil value is fine and means \"proxy API unavailable\" \u2014 which is the correct default for tests. **No changes are required to existing tests** since the zero value (`nil`) for the new pointer field is valid and means `Applicable()` returns false.\n\n---\n\n### Summary of all changes\n\n| File | Change |\n|------|--------|\n| `pkg/operator/utils/apidiscovery.go` | Add imports for `configv1` and `configinformers`; add `NewProxyInformerIfAvailable` function |\n| `pkg/operator/starter.go` | Add proxy informer discovery after infra informer; start it if applicable; pass to `ControllerConfig` |\n| `pkg/operator/setup_manager.go` | Add `ProxyInformer` field to `ControllerConfig`; add imports; update `setupTrustManagerController` signature to pass proxy informer through to `trustmanager.New()` |\n| `pkg/controller/trustmanager/controller.go` | Add `proxyInformer` field to `Reconciler`; update `New()` signature; add imports for `configv1`, `configinformers`, `utils`; add `readClusterProxyConfig()` and `applyProxySettings()` methods; call `readClusterProxyConfig()` from `processReconcileRequest`; add RBAC marker for proxies |\n\n### Commands to build and verify\n\n```bash\n# From the repository root:\n\n# 1. Verify compilation\ngo build ./...\n\n# 2. Run unit tests\ngo test ./pkg/operator/utils/... -v\ngo test ./pkg/controller/trustmanager/... -v\n\n# 3. Run vet\ngo vet ./...\n\n# 4. If a Makefile target exists for full validation:\nmake build\nmake test\n```", "stop_reason": "end_turn", "session_id": "3f1d0ac0-313b-45d7-a4d8-a0551ece6c58", "total_cost_usd": 1.03472225, "usage": {"input_tokens": 312, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 47665, "cache_read_input_tokens": 840262, "output_tokens": 12605, "server_tool_use": {"web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0}, "service_tier": "standard", "cache_creation": {"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 0, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 47665}, "inference_geo": "", "iterations": [], "speed": "standard"}, "modelUsage": {"claude-opus-4-6": {"inputTokens": 312, "outputTokens": 12605, "cacheReadInputTokens": 840262, "cacheCreationInputTokens": 47665, "webSearchRequests": 0, "costUSD": 1.03472225, "contextWindow": 200000, "maxOutputTokens": 64000}}, "permission_denials": [], "terminal_reason": "completed", "fast_mode_state": "off", "uuid": "278ae7e6-d808-4042-bcea-25c529bf3372", "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:27:11.286Z"}
cm-004-immutable-field (3/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness3

The agent's implementation has several notable deviations from the reference:

  1. Non-pointer type: The agent uses TLSMinVersion string instead of TLSMinVersion string. The reference explicitly calls for a pointer type (string) for the optional field. This affects how omitempty works and how nil vs empty string is handled, and impacts the CEL validation semantics.
  2. CEL rule structure differs significantly: The reference requires two separate XValidation markers on the field itself using has() for presence checking. The agent instead splits them: one rule on the parent struct type (IstioCSRConfig) for presence-immutability and one on the field for value-immutability. While this could work in some contexts, the field-level value-immutability rule uses oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf which compares against empty string rather than using has(). For a non-pointer string with omitempty, the zero value is "" and has() checks whether the field is present in the JSON, so this approach is semantically different. The reference's has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) pattern on the field-level markers is the canonical approach.
  3. Presence-immutability rule is overly restrictive: The struct-level rule !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion) prevents setting the field if it wasn't set at creation time (unset→set is blocked), which is MORE restrictive than the reference's rule !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) || has(self.tlsMinVersion) which only prevents removing it once set (set→unset is blocked, but unset→set is allowed).
  4. Mutation pipeline pattern: The agent modifies the existing updateArgList function inline rather than creating a separate updateTLSMinVersion mutation function as specified in the reference. While this works functionally, it doesn't follow the typed function pattern requested.

The kubebuilder markers are syntactically valid, enum values are correct, and the deployment argument passing logic is correct. The code would likely compile (with the string type), but the CEL validation semantics are wrong - particularly the presence-immutability rule blocking initial setting of the field.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/2 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence2

The agent's implementation deviates from several key conventions:

  1. Non-pointer type: The reference specifies *string (pointer type) for the optional field, which is the standard Go/Kubernetes convention for optional fields. The agent used a plain string, which changes the semantics — a plain string cannot distinguish between "not set" and "empty string," which breaks the has() CEL semantics for optional pointer fields.
  2. CEL validation rules don't follow the existing two-rule pattern: The reference calls for two separate XValidation markers on the field using has() syntax (presence-immutability and value-immutability), matching the exact pattern used for IssuerRef and privateKeyAlgorithm in the same file. The agent instead used oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf on the field (comparing against empty string rather than using has()), which is semantically different and doesn't match the codebase's established pattern. The struct-level rule also differs — the agent uses !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion), which actually prevents setting the field on update even if it was never set (it requires both old and new to either both have it or both not have it), which is more restrictive than intended.
  3. Deployment mutation pattern: The reference specifies a separate named mutation function (updateTLSMinVersion) following the typed deploymentMutationFunc pattern that gets chained in a mutation pipeline. The agent instead added the logic inline within the existing updateArgList function. While this works functionally, it doesn't follow the "named Go functions chained in a pipeline" convention described for deployment mutations in this codebase.
  4. The presence-immutability rule has wrong semantics: The agent's struct-level rule !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion) means you can never add the field after creation if it wasn't initially set, which is overly restrictive compared to the reference pattern !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) || has(self.tlsMinVersion) which only prevents removal.

The agent did correctly identify the Enum values (VersionTLS12, VersionTLS13) and placed the validation markers, but the core conventions around pointer types, CEL patterns, and mutation pipeline structure were not followed.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check3

The implementation is mostly grounded but has several deviations from the reference approach that affect correctness:

Grounded aspects:

  1. The file paths (api/operator/v1alpha1/istiocsr_types.go, pkg/controller/istiocsr/deployments.go, api/operator/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go) appear to be real files in the repository.
  2. The agent correctly references existing patterns like privateKeyAlgorithm, privateKeySize, issuerRef for immutability validation.
  3. The updateArgList function and istiocsrConfigs variable appear to reference real existing code.
  4. The istiocsrContainerName constant is referenced correctly.
  5. The enum values VersionTLS12 and VersionTLS13 match the reference.
  6. Make targets (make generate, make manifests, make build, make test) appear reasonable.

Deviations from reference (not hallucinations but design issues):

  1. Non-pointer type: The agent uses TLSMinVersion string instead of *string as specified in the reference. This changes the semantics - with a plain string, the zero value is "" rather than nil, which affects how has() works in CEL and how the omitempty behavior works.
  2. CEL rule pattern differs: The agent uses oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf for value-immutability on the field itself, comparing against empty string instead of using has() with a pointer type. This is a different pattern than the reference's !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || self.tlsMinVersion == oldSelf.tlsMinVersion.
  3. Presence-immutability rule differs: The agent's struct-level rule !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion) is logically different from the reference's !has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) || has(self.tlsMinVersion). The agent's rule would prevent setting the field on update even if it wasn't set before (both sides must match), which is overly restrictive.
  4. Mutation approach differs: The agent adds to the existing updateArgList function rather than creating a separate updateTLSMinVersion mutation function as specified in the reference. This is a pattern deviation but not a hallucination.

Not hallucinations: The agent appears to be referencing real existing code structures. The updateArgList function with its pattern of appending args, IstioDataPlaneNamespaceSelector, and the container name matching pattern all appear to exist in the codebase based on the context provided.

The implementation doesn't contain clear hallucinations of non-existent code references, but it deviates significantly from the reference approach in terms of type choice (string vs *string) and CEL rule patterns. The presence-immutability rule is also logically incorrect compared to the reference.

task_success3

The agent correctly identified the right files to modify, added the field with correct Enum values (VersionTLS12, VersionTLS13), and properly implemented the deployment mutation logic in updateArgList. However, there are significant deviations from the reference: (1) The field uses a plain string type instead of the required *string pointer type, which changes the semantics of optional/omitempty behavior and affects how CEL has() works. (2) The CEL validation rules use string equality checks (oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf) instead of the correct has() pattern (!has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) || has(self.tlsMinVersion) and !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || self.tlsMinVersion == oldSelf.tlsMinVersion). With a non-pointer string, the has() pattern won't work correctly for distinguishing absent vs. empty, making the immutability enforcement semantically different. (3) The mutation function wasn't implemented as a separate deploymentMutationFunc chained in the pipeline as the reference requires, but instead was inlined into the existing updateArgList function. (4) The presence-immutability rule on the struct level (!has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion)) is actually more restrictive than intended — it prevents setting the field after initial creation even if it was never set, which differs from the reference's intent of only preventing removal once set.

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs are very similar and largely correct, but there's a subtle difference in the value-immutability CEL rule that distinguishes them.

Output A uses oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf for the field-level value-immutability rule. This matches the existing pattern used for privateKeyAlgorithm in the same codebase (oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf). This is the correct approach for an optional string field with omitempty — the zero value check (oldSelf == '') handles the case where the field transitions from its zero value.

Output B uses self == oldSelf for the field-level value-immutability rule. While Output B argues this is correct because "the field-level rule is only evaluated when the field exists in both old and new," the reference implementation uses oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf, which is the established pattern in the codebase. Using self == oldSelf without the zero-value guard is technically functional but doesn't follow the codebase convention.

Both outputs correctly:

  • Add the TLSMinVersion field to IstioCSRConfig
  • Add the presence-immutability CEL rule on the parent struct (!has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && !has(self.tlsMinVersion) || has(oldSelf.tlsMinVersion) && has(self.tlsMinVersion))
  • Add the enum constraint (VersionTLS12, VersionTLS13)
  • Update updateArgList in deployments.go with the --tls-min-version flag
  • Correctly note that deepcopy doesn't need manual changes (string is a value type)
  • Specify make generate and make manifests commands

Output A's value-immutability rule more closely matches the reference implementation's pattern (oldSelf == '' || self == oldSelf), making it slightly more correct. This is a minor difference, so both would score 4-5, but A is marginally better.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment
task: |
  Add a tlsMinVersion field to IstioCSRConfig in api/operator/v1alpha1/istiocsr_types.go.
  Apply two CEL validation rules:
  1. Presence-immutability: once set, the field cannot be removed; once unset, it cannot be added
  2. Value-immutability: self == oldSelf when the field is set

  Add an enum constraint restricting valid values to VersionTLS12 and VersionTLS13.

  Update the mutation pipeline in pkg/controller/istiocsr/deployments.go to pass the
  value as a --tls-min-version flag to the IstioCSR container.
task_id: cm-004-immutable-field
repo: cert-manager-operator
category: api-change
complexity: high

be requested for the istiod certificate.\n276\t\t// This field can have a maximum of 25 entries.\n277\t\t// +listType=set\n278\t\t// +kubebuilder:default:={\"default\"}\n279\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:items:MaxLength:=63\n283\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n284\t\t// +optional\n285\t\tRevisions []string `json:\"revisions,omitempty\"`\n286\t\n287\t\t// namespace of the Istio control plane.\n288\t\t// This field can have a maximum of 63 characters.\n289\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule=\"self == oldSelf\",message=\"namespace is immutable once set\"\n290\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength:=1\n291\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength:=63\n292\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Required\n293\t\t// +required\n294\t\tNamespace string `json:\"namespace\"`\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t// ControllerConfig configures the controller for setting up defaults to\n298\t// enable the istio-csr agent.\n299\ttype ControllerConfig struct {\n300\t\t// labels to apply to all resources created for the istio-csr agent deployment.\n301\t\t// This field can have a maximum of 20 entries.\n302\t\t// +mapType=granular\n303\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinProperties:=0\n304\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MaxProperties:=20\n305\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Optional\n306\t\t// +optional\n307\t\tLabels map[string]string `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// IstioCSRStatus is the most recently observed status of the IstioCSR.\n311\ttype IstioCSRStatus struct {\n312\t\t// conditions holds information about the current state of the istio-csr agent deployment.\n313\t\tConditionalStatus `json:\",inline,omitempty\"`\n314\t\n315\t\t// istioCSRImage is the name of the image and the tag used for deploying istio-csr.\n316\t\tIstioCSRImage string `json:\"istioCSRImage,omitempty\"`\n317\t\n318\t\t// istioCSRGRPCEndpoint is the service endpoint of istio-csr, made available for users to configure in the istiod config to enable Istio to use istio-csr for certificate requests.\n319\t\tIstioCSRGRPCEndpoint string `json:\"istioCSRGRPCEndpoint,omitempty\"`\n320\t\n321\t\t// serviceAccount created by the controller for the istio-csr agent.\n322\t\tServiceAccount string `json:\"serviceAccount,omitempty\"`\n323\t\n324\t\t// clusterRole created by the controller for the istio-csr agent.\n325\t\tClusterRole string `json:\"clusterRole,omitempty\"`\n326\t\n327\t\t// clusterRoleBinding created by the controller for the istio-csr agent.\n328\t\tClusterRoleBinding string `json:\"clusterRoleBinding,omitempty\"`\n329\t}\n330\t",
