cm-005-hash-triggered-restart (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness4

The implementation correctly addresses all five critical correctness points: (1) hashes PEM content using SHA-256 via crypto/sha256, not resourceVersion; (2) has an idempotency guard through deploymentSpecModified comparing Spec.Template.Annotations; (3) stamps the annotation on Deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations (pod template), not on Deployment.ObjectMeta; (4) uses crypto/sha256; (5) mirrors the TrustManager pattern structure.

However, there are a few minor issues: (1) The Reconciler struct access pattern uses r.Get(r.ctx, ...) and r.CtrlClient which may not match the actual struct fields — the reference code uses r.Client.Get(ctx, ...) with an explicit context parameter. The readTrustedCABundle method takes no ctx parameter and instead uses r.ctx, which is an unusual pattern that may or may not exist on the actual reconciler struct. (2) The test file references fakes.FakeCtrlClient and helpers like testReconciler(t), testDeployment(), errTestClient, and baseDeployment() which are assumed to exist but their exact signatures aren't verified. (3) The common.FromClientError usage appears to wrap errors but its actual signature in the codebase isn't confirmed — it may not accept format strings with varargs. (4) The contains helper function reimplements strings.Contains unnecessarily, though this is a style issue not a correctness one.

These are relatively minor issues that would likely require small fixes but the overall architecture and approach are sound and would work correctly once adjusted.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/2 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence4

The implementation follows the critical cert-manager-operator conventions well:

  1. Hash-triggered restarts: Correctly uses SHA-256 hash of PEM content (not resourceVersion) via crypto/sha256, stamped as a pod template annotation (Deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations), which is the exact pattern specified.
  2. Idempotency guard: The implementation provides an idempotency guard through the deploymentSpecModified comparison in utils.go — when the existing deployment already has the same hash annotation, no update is issued. This mirrors the TrustManager pattern structure.
  3. Deployment mutation pattern: The hash stamping is implemented as a named function (stampCABundleHash) that fits into the deployment construction pipeline in getDeploymentObject, consistent with the repo's pattern of chaining named mutation functions.
  4. Image source / RELATED_IMAGE convention: Not violated — no hardcoded image references introduced.
  5. Annotation placement: Correctly on Spec.Template.Annotations (pod template), not ObjectMeta.

The one minor deviation is that the idempotency guard is indirect (through deploymentSpecModified comparing the full template annotations) rather than a direct hash comparison before patching as shown in the reference implementation's reconcileCABundle pattern. The reference explicitly checks currentHash == hash and returns early before any update call, while this implementation relies on the existing drift-detection mechanism. This is functionally equivalent but doesn't mirror the TrustManager pattern as closely as possible — it's a structural difference rather than a correctness issue. The tests also use t.Run table-driven style rather than Ginkgo BDD, but this is consistent with the existing istiocsr test files (which also use standard Go testing), so it's appropriate for this sub-package's conventions.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check3

The implementation meets all the critical correctness points from the reference:

  1. Hash of PEM content (not resourceVersion): ✅ Uses sha256.Sum256([]byte(caBundle)) on the actual PEM data.
  2. Idempotency guard: ✅ The guard works through deploymentSpecModified comparing Spec.Template.Annotations via reflect.DeepEqual, which skips the update when the hash is unchanged.
  3. Annotation on Deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations: ✅ stampCABundleHash sets the annotation on deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations.
  4. SHA-256: ✅ Uses crypto/sha256.
  5. Mirrors TrustManager pattern: ✅ Follows the same read-hash-stamp pattern.

Regarding hallucinations, there are some concerns:

  • References to common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.TrustedCABundleKey, common.OperatorNamespace, and common.FromClientError — these are assumed to exist in the common package. The task references pkg/controller/trustmanager/configmaps.go as the pattern source, and it's plausible these constants exist, but without seeing the actual common package, there's some risk.
  • fakes.FakeCtrlClient — referenced as existing in pkg/controller/common/fakes, which is plausible given the test patterns shown.
  • r.CtrlClient field on the Reconciler — the agent accesses this in tests (r.CtrlClient = mock), which may or may not match the actual struct field name.
  • testReconciler(t), testDeployment(), testIssuer(), baseDeployment(), errTestClient — these are referenced as existing test helpers, which is plausible given the existing test files.
  • The contains and searchSubstring helper functions are newly created, which is fine.

The implementation is largely grounded in existing patterns and makes reasonable assumptions about what exists in the codebase. The references to specific line numbers, existing function signatures, and file paths appear consistent with the described codebase structure. While there's some uncertainty about specific identifiers in the common package and fakes package, these are reasonable inferences from the described pattern source. The overall approach is sound and well-structured.

task_success4

The agent's implementation correctly addresses all five critical correctness points from the reference: (1) hashes PEM content using SHA-256, not resourceVersion; (2) has an idempotency guard via deploymentSpecModified comparing Spec.Template.Annotations before issuing an update; (3) places the annotation on Deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations (pod template), not on Deployment.ObjectMeta; (4) uses crypto/sha256; and (5) mirrors the TrustManager pattern structure. The implementation is spread across the correct files with proper separation of concerns. Minor gaps include: the annotation name differs slightly from the reference (ca-bundle-hash vs cert-manager-bundle-hash), the idempotency guard is indirect (via deployment drift detection rather than an explicit early-return comparison as shown in the reference), and the test file updates for install_instiocsr_test.go are described at a high level without full concrete diffs for all test cases. These are minor issues that would require only small adjustments in code review.

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs provide comprehensive implementations that correctly mirror the trustmanager pattern for content-hash-triggered rolling restarts in the IstioCSR controller. Let me compare them against the reference implementation:

Core sub-tasks completed by both:

  1. New configmaps.go file: Both create readTrustedCABundle(), computeCABundleHash(), and a stamping function. Output A names it stampCABundleHash, Output B also uses stampCABundleHash and adds a convenience readTrustedCABundleHash() wrapper.
  2. Constants: Both add caBundleHashAnnotation (A uses "operator.openshift.io/ca-bundle-hash", B uses the same annotation name). Both place it in constants.go.
  3. Deployment changes: Both correctly modify createOrApplyDeployments and getDeploymentObject to accept and thread through the caBundleHash parameter, and call stampCABundleHash after updateVolumes.
  4. Install flow: Both modify install_istiocsr.go to read the CA bundle hash before calling deployment reconciliation. Output A calls readTrustedCABundle() + computeCABundleHash() inline; Output B calls readTrustedCABundleHash() which wraps both.
  5. Idempotency guard: Both add !reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Spec.Template.Annotations, fetched.Spec.Template.Annotations) to deploymentSpecModified in utils.go.
  6. Test updates: Both update deployments_test.go to pass the new empty hash parameter. Both provide new test files for hash computation, stamping, and CA bundle reading.

Differences:

  • Output A's readTrustedCABundle() treats a missing ConfigMap or missing key as a hard error (matching the trustmanager pattern exactly), while Output B gracefully returns empty string on NotFound or missing key (more lenient). The reference implementation follows the trustmanager pattern (hard error), which A matches more closely. However, the task says "mirror the pattern" but the istiocsr controller has different semantics where this may be optional, so B's graceful degradation is defensible.
  • Output A updates existing test files (install_instiocsr_test.go) to handle the new CA bundle ConfigMap Get call, which is necessary for tests to pass with the hard-error approach. Output B notes that existing tests don't need updating since the graceful degradation handles the unmatched mock.
  • Output A adds annotation comparison test cases to utils_test.go (TestDeploymentSpecModified), while Output B does not explicitly add those tests but provides comprehensive tests in configmaps_test.go.
  • Output B provides a readTrustedCABundleHash() convenience method that cleanly encapsulates the read + hash computation, which is a nice design touch.

Both implementations are substantially complete, would work as described, and would pass code review with minor comments. Neither has a clear advantage that pushes it significantly above the other. The key trade-off is: A is more faithful to the exact trustmanager pattern (hard error) while B is more operationally resilient (graceful degradation). Both are valid design choices for this codebase.

Score: Both would likely receive a 4 ("Mostly correct") - the main implementations are correct with minor gaps. Both correctly identify all required files, provide the hash computation, annotation stamping, idempotency guard, and test coverage.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment
task: |
  Implement a content-hash-triggered rolling restart for the CNO-injected CA ConfigMap
  in the IstioCSR controller (pkg/controller/istiocsr/).

  Mirror the pattern already present in pkg/controller/trustmanager/configmaps.go:
  read the ConfigMap contents, compute a SHA-256 hash of the PEM data (not the
  resourceVersion), and stamp the hash as an annotation on the IstioCSR Deployment's
  pod template spec.

  Include an idempotency guard that skips the Patch call when ConfigMap content is unchanged.
task_id: cm-005-hash-triggered-restart
repo: cert-manager-operator
category: architecture
complexity: high

    "content": "1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n5\t\t\"encoding/hex\"\n6\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n7\t\t\"fmt\"\n8\t\t\"maps\"\n9\t\n10\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n11\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n12\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n13\t\n14\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n15\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n16\t)\n17\t\n18\t// caPackage represents the JSON format expected by trust-manager\n19\ttype caPackage struct {\n20\t\tName    string `json:\"name\"`\n21\t\tBundle  string `json:\"bundle\"`\n22\t\tVersion string `json:\"version\"`\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap reads the CNO-injected CA bundle,\n26\t// formats it into trust-manager's expected JSON package format, and creates\n27\t// or updates the package ConfigMap in the operand namespace.\n28\t// Returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle content and any error.\n29\t// Returns (\"\", nil) when defaultCAPackage is disabled.\n30\tfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) (string, error) {\n31\t\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage) {\n32\t\t\treturn \"\", nil\n33\t\t}\n34\t\n35\t\tcaBundle, resourceVersion, err := r.readTrustedCABundle()\n36\t\tif err != nil {\n37\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n38\t\t}\n39\t\n40\t\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(caBundle, resourceVersion)\n41\t\tif err != nil {\n42\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n43\t\t}\n44\t\n45\t\tbundleHash := computeCABundleHash(caBundle)\n46\t\n47\t\tdesired := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations)\n48\t\n49\t\tcmName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n50\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling default CA package ConfigMap\", \"name\", cmName)\n51\t\n52\t\texisting := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n53\t\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n54\t\tif err != nil {\n55\t\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if ConfigMap %q exists\", cmName)\n56\t\t}\n57\t\tif exists && !configMapModified(desired, existing) {\n58\t\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n59\t\t\treturn bundleHash, nil\n60\t\t}\n61\t\n62\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n63\t\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n64\t\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply ConfigMap %q\", cmName)\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"default CA package ConfigMap %s applied\", cmName)\n68\t\treturn bundleHash, nil\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t// readTrustedCABundle reads the CNO-injected CA bundle from the operator namespace.\n72\t// Returns the PEM bundle, the ConfigMap's resource version, and any error.\n73\tfunc (r *Reconciler) readTrustedCABundle() (string, string, error) {\n74\t\tinjectionCM := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n75\t\tkey := client.ObjectKey{\n76\t\t\tNamespace: common.OperatorNamespace,\n77\t\t\tName:      common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n78\t\t}\n79\t\tif err := r.Get(r.ctx, key, injectionCM); err != nil {\n80\t\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n81\t\t\t\terr,\n82\t\t\t\t\"failed to read CA bundle ConfigMap %q in namespace %q\",\n83\t\t\t\tcommon.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.OperatorNamespace,\n84\t\t\t)\n85\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\tcaBundle, ok := injectionCM.Data[common.TrustedCABundleKey]\n88\t\tif !ok || caBundle == \"\" {\n89\t\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n90\t\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"CA bundle ConfigMap %q does not contain key %q\", common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.TrustedCABundleKey),\n91\t\t\t\t\"missing key %q in ConfigMap %q\", common.TrustedCABundleKey, common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n92\t\t\t)\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\treturn caBundle, injectionCM.ResourceVersion, nil\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\t// formatCAPackage encodes the CA bundle into trust-manager's expected JSON package format.\n99\tfunc formatCAPackage(caBundle, version string) ([]byte, error) {\n100\t\tpkg := caPackage{\n101\t\t\tName:    defaultCAPackageName,\n102\t\t\tBundle:  caBundle,\n103\t\t\tVersion: version,\n104\t\t}\n105\t\t// TODO: cross check the formatting of the JSON package\n106\t\tpkgJSON, err := json.Marshal(pkg)\n107\t\tif err != nil {\n108\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to marshal CA package to JSON: %w\", err)\n109\t\t}\n110\t\treturn pkgJSON, nil\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// computeCABundleHash returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle PEM content.\n114\t// The hash is used as the pod template annotation to trigger rolling restarts\n115\t// only when the actual certificate content changes.\n116\tfunc computeCABundleHash(caBundle string) string {\n117\t\thash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(caBundle))\n118\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t// buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap constructs the desired ConfigMap for the\n122\t// formatted CA package in the operand namespace.\n123\tfunc buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON []byte, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) *corev1.ConfigMap {\n124\t\tcm := &corev1.ConfigMap{\n125\t\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n126\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n127\t\t\t\tKind:       \"ConfigMap\",\n128\t\t\t},\n129\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n130\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n131\t\t\t\tNamespace: operandNamespace,\n132\t\t\t},\n133\t\t\tData: map[string]string{\n134\t\t\t\tdefaultCAPackageFilename: string(pkgJSON),\n135\t\t\t},\n136\t\t}\n137\t\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(cm, resourceLabels)\n138\t\tupdateResourceAnnotations(cm, resourceAnnotations)\n139\t\treturn cm\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// configMapModified checks whether the desired ConfigMap differs from the existing one.\n143\tfunc configMapModified(desired, existing *corev1.ConfigMap) bool {\n144\t\treturn managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) ||\n145\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Data, existing.Data)\n146\t}\n147\t",
    "is_error": false,

    "content": "1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"context\"\n5\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n6\t\t\"testing\"\n7\t\n8\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n9\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n10\t\n11\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n12\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n13\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common/fakes\"\n14\t)\n15\t\n16\tconst testCABundle = \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\nthisIsATestCABundle\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\"\n17\t\n18\tfunc TestFormatCAPackage(t *testing.T) {\n19\t\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(testCABundle, \"12345\")\n20\t\tif err != nil {\n21\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n22\t\t}\n23\t\n24\t\tvar pkg caPackage\n25\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(pkgJSON, &pkg); err != nil {\n26\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to unmarshal package JSON: %v\", err)\n27\t\t}\n28\t\tif pkg.Name != defaultCAPackageName {\n29\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageName, pkg.Name)\n30\t\t}\n31\t\tif pkg.Version != \"12345\" {\n32\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package version %q, got %q\", \"12345\", pkg.Version)\n33\t\t}\n34\t\tif pkg.Bundle != testCABundle {\n35\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected bundle to match input, got different content\")\n36\t\t}\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\tfunc TestComputeCABundleHash(t *testing.T) {\n40\t\ttests := []struct {\n41\t\t\tname     string\n42\t\t\tbundle1  string\n43\t\t\tbundle2  string\n44\t\t\twantSame bool\n45\t\t}{\n46\t\t\t{\n47\t\t\t\tname:     \"same bundle produces same hash\",\n48\t\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n49\t\t\t\tbundle2:  testCABundle,\n50\t\t\t\twantSame: true,\n51\t\t\t},\n52\t\t\t{\n53\t\t\t\tname:     \"different bundles produce different hashes\",\n54\t\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n55\t\t\t\tbundle2:  \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\ndifferent\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\",\n56\t\t\t\twantSame: false,\n57\t\t\t},\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n61\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n62\t\t\t\thash1 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle1)\n63\t\t\t\thash2 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle2)\n64\t\n65\t\t\t\tif hash1 == \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected non-empty hash\")\n67\t\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\t\tif (hash1 == hash2) != tt.wantSame {\n69\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"hash equality=%v, want %v (hash1=%s, hash2=%s)\", hash1 == hash2, tt.wantSame, hash1, hash2)\n70\t\t\t\t}\n71\t\t\t})\n72\t\t}\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\tfunc TestBuildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(t *testing.T) {\n76\t\tpkgJSON := []byte(`{\"name\":\"test\",\"bundle\":\"test\",\"version\":\"1\"}`)\n77\t\tlabels := map[string]string{\"app\": \"test\"}\n78\t\tannotations := map[string]string{\"note\": \"test\"}\n79\t\n80\t\tcm := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, labels, annotations)\n81\t\n82\t\tif cm.Name != defaultCAPackageConfigMapName {\n83\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageConfigMapName, cm.Name)\n84\t\t}\n85\t\tif cm.Namespace != operandNamespace {\n86\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected namespace %q, got %q\", operandNamespace, cm.Namespace)\n87\t\t}\n88\t\tif cm.Labels[\"app\"] != \"test\" {\n89\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected label app=test, got %v\", cm.Labels)\n90\t\t}\n91\t\tif cm.Annotations[\"note\"] != \"test\" {\n92\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected annotation note=test, got %v\", cm.Annotations)\n93\t\t}\n94\t\tif cm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] != string(pkgJSON) {\n95\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected data key %q to contain package JSON\", defaultCAPackageFilename)\n96\t\t}\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\tfunc TestConfigMapModified(t *testing.T) {\n100\t\tbase := func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n101\t\t\treturn &corev1.ConfigMap{\n102\t\t\t\tData: map[string]string{defaultCAPackageFilename: `{\"name\":\"test\"}`},\n103\t\t\t}\n104\t\t}\n105\t\n106\t\ttests := []struct {\n107\t\t\tname     string\n108\t\t\tdesired  *corev1.ConfigMap\n109\t\t\texisting *corev1.ConfigMap\n110\t\t\twant     bool\n111\t\t}{\n112\t\t\t{\n113\t\t\t\tname:     \"identical ConfigMaps are not modified\",\n114\t\t\t\tdesired:  base(),\n115\t\t\t\texisting: base(),\n116\t\t\t\twant:     false,\n117\t\t\t},\n118\t\t\t{\n119\t\t\t\tname:    \"different data is detected as modified\",\n120\t\t\t\tdesired: base(),\n121\t\t\t\texisting: func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n122\t\t\t\t\tcm := base()\n123\t\t\t\t\tcm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] = `{\"name\":\"changed\"}`\n124\t\t\t\t\treturn cm\n125\t\t\t\t}(),\n126\t\t\t\twant: true,\n127\t\t\t},\n128\t\t\t{\n129\t\t\t\tname:    \"extra key in existing is detected as modified\",\n130\t\t\t\tdesired: base(),\n131\t\t\t\texisting: func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n132\t\t\t\t\tcm := base()\n133\t\t\t\t\tcm.Data[\"extra\"] = \"value\"\n134\t\t\t\t\treturn cm\n135\t\t\t\t}(),\n136\t\t\t\twant: true,\n137\t\t\t},\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n141\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n142\t\t\t\tgot := configMapModified(tt.desired, tt.existing)\n143\t\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n144\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"configMapModified() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n145\t\t\t\t}\n146\t\t\t})\n147\t\t}\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\tfunc TestDefaultCAPackageConfigMapReconciliation(t *testing.T) {\n151\t\ttests := []struct {\n152\t\t\tname            string\n153\t\t\ttm              *trustManagerBuilder\n154\t\t\tpreReq          func(*Reconciler, *fakes.FakeCtrlClient)\n155\t\t\twantHash        bool\n156\t\t\twantErr         string\n157\t\t\twantExistsCount int\n158\t\t\twantPatchCount  int\n159\t\t}{\n160\t\t\t{\n161\t\t\t\tname: \"skips when policy is Disabled\",\n162\t\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled),\n163\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n164\t\t\t\t},\n165\t\t\t\twantExistsCount: 0,\n166\t\t\t\twantPatchCount:  0,\n167\t\t\t},\n168\t\t\t{\n169\t\t\t\tname: \"skips when policy is unset (defaults to Disabled)\",\n170\t\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager(),\n171\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r ConfigMap is not found\",\n178\t\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n179\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) to read CA bundle ConfigMap\",\n185\t\t\t},\n186\t\t\t{\n187\t\t\t\tname: \"returns error when CA bundle key is missing\",\n188\t\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n189\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n190\t\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context, key\",\n211\t\t\t},\n212\t\t\t{\n213\t\t\t\tname: \"creates ConfigMap and returns hash when bundle is available\",\n214\t\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n215\t\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n216\t\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context,     "content": "1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"os\"\n5\t\t\"time\"\n6\t\n7\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\"\n8\t)\n9\t\n10\tconst (\n11\t\t// trustManagerCommonName is the name commonly used for naming resources.\n12\t\ttrustManagerCommonName = \"cert-manager-trust-manager\"\n13\t\n14\t\t// ControllerName is the name of the controller used in logs and events.\n15\t\tControllerName = trustManagerCommonName + \"-controller\"\n16\t\n17\t\t// finalizer name for trustmanager.openshift.operator.io resource.\n18\t\tfinalizer = \"trustmanager.openshift.operator.io/\" + ControllerName\n19\t\n20\t\t// defaultRequeueTime is the default reconcile requeue time.\n21\t\tdefaultRequeueTime = time.Second * 30\n22\t\n23\t\t// trustManagerObjectName is the name of the trust-manager resource created by user.\n24\t\t// trust-manager CRD enforces name to be `cluster`.\n25\t\ttrustManagerObjectName = \"cluster\"\n26\t\n27\t\t// trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName is the environment variable key name\n28\t\t// containing the image name of the trust-manager as value.\n29\t\ttrustManagerImageNameEnvVarName = \"RELATED_IMAGE_CERT_MANAGER_TRUST_MANAGER\"\n30\t\n31\t\t// trustManagerImageVersionEnvVarName is the environment variable key name\n32\t\t// containing the image version of the trust-manager as value.\n33\t\ttrustManagerImageVersionEnvVarName = \"TRUSTMANAGER_OPERAND_IMAGE_VERSION\"\n34\t\n35\t\t// defaultTrustNamespace is the default namespace where trust-manager looks for trust sources.\n36\t\tdefaultTrustNamespace = \"cert-manager\"\n37\t\n38\t\t// operandNamespace is the namespace where trust-manager operand is deployed.\n39\t\toperandNamespace = \"cert-manager\"\n40\t\n41\t\t// fieldOwner is the field manager name used for Server-Side Apply operations.\n42\t\t// All resource reconcilers should use this to identify ownership of fields.\n43\t\tfieldOwner = \"trust-manager-controller\"\n44\t\n45\t\t// trustManagerContainerName is the name of the trust-manager container in the deployment.\n46\t\ttrustManagerContainerName = \"trust-manager\"\n47\t\n48\t\t// roleBindingSubjectKind is the kind used in RBAC binding subjects.\n49\t\troleBindingSubjectKind = \"ServiceAccount\"\n50\t)\n51\t\n52\t// DefaultCAPackage constants.\n53\tconst (\n54\t\t// defaultCAPackageConfigMapName is the ConfigMap in the operand namespace that\n55\t\t// contains the formatted JSON CA package for trust-manager.\n56\t\tdefaultCAPackageConfigMapName = \"trust-manager-default-ca-package\"\n57\t\n58\t\t// defaultCAPackageName is the package name used in the JSON CA package.\n59\t\tdefaultCAPackageName = \"cert-manager-package-openshift\"\n60\t\n61\t\t// defaultCAPackageFilename is the filename used for the JSON package inside the ConfigMap.\n62\t\tdefaultCAPackageFilename = defaultCAPackageName + \".json\"\n63\t\n64\t\t// defaultCAPackageVolumeName is the volume name used in the deployment.\n65\t\tdefaultCAPackageVolumeName = \"packages\"\n66\t\n67\t\t// defaultCAPackageMountPath is where the package volume is mounted in the container.\n68\t\tdefaultCAPackageMountPath = \"/packages\"\n69\t\n70\t\t// defaultCAPackageLocation is the full path to the JSON package file inside the container.\n71\t\tdefaultCAPackageLocation = defaultCAPackageMountPath + \"/\" + defaultCAPackageFilename\n72\t\n73\t\t// defaultCAPackageHashAnnotation is the pod template annotation that tracks the CA bundle hash.\n74\t\tdefaultCAPackageHashAnnotation = \"operator.openshift.io/default-ca-package-hash\"\n75\t)\n76\t\n77\t// Resource names used for creating resources and cross-referencing between them.\n78\t// These must be set explicitly on each resource's .metadata.name and on every\n79\t// field in other resources that references them.\n80\tconst (\n81\t\ttrustManagerCommonResourceName = \"trust-manager\"\n82\t\n83\t\ttrustManagerServiceAccountName = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n84\t\ttrustManagerDeploymentName     = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n85\t\n86\t\ttrustManagerServiceName        = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n87\t\ttrustManagerMetricsServiceName = trustManagerCommonResourceName + \"-metrics\"\n88\t\n89\t\ttrustManagerClusterRoleName        = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n90\t\ttrustManagerClusterRoleBindingName = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n91\t\n92\t\ttrustManagerRoleName        = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n93\t\ttrustManagerRoleBindingName = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n94\t\n95\t\ttrustManagerLeaderElectionRoleName        = trustManagerCommonResourceName + \":leaderelection\"\n96\t\ttrustManagerLeaderElectionRoleBindingName = trustManagerCommonResourceName + \":leaderelection\"\n97\t\n98\t\ttrustManagerIssuerName      = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n99\t\ttrustManagerCertificateName = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n100\t\ttrustManagerTLSSecretName   = trustManagerCommonResourceName + \"-tls\"\n101\t\n102\t\ttrustManagerWebhookConfigName = trustManagerCommonResourceName\n103\t)\n104\t\n105\tvar (\n106\t\ttrustManagerConfigFieldPath     = field.NewPath(\"spec\", \"trustManagerConfig\")\n107\t\tcontrollerConfigFieldPath       = field.NewPath(\"spec\", \"controllerConfig\")\n108\t\tcontrollerDefaultResourceLabels = map[string]string{\n109\t\t\t\"app\":                          trustManagerCommonName,\n110\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/name\":       trustManagerCommonName,\n111\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/instance\":   trustManagerCommonName,\n112\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/version\":    os.Getenv(trustManagerImageVersionEnvVarName),\n113\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/managed-by\": \"cert-manager-operator\",\n114\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/part-of\":    \"cert-manager-operator\",\n115\t\t}\n116\t)\n117\t\n118\t// asset names are the files present in the root bindata/ dir. Which are then loaded\n119\t// and made available by the pkg/operator/assets package.\n120\tconst (\n121\t\tserviceAccountAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/serviceaccount_trust-manager.yml\"\n122\t\n123\t\tdeploymentAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/deployment_trust-manager.yml\"\n124\t\n125\t\tserviceAssetName        = \"trust-manager/resources/service_trust-manager.yml\"\n126\t\tmetricsServiceAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/service_trust-manager-metrics.yml\"\n127\t\n128\t\tclusterRoleAssetName        = \"trust-manager/resources/clusterrole_trust-manager.yml\"\n129\t\tclusterRoleBindingAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/clusterrolebinding_trust-manager.yml\"\n130\t\n131\t\troleAssetName        = \"trust-manager/resources/role_trust-manager.yml\"\n132\t\troleBindingAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/rolebinding_trust-manager.yml\"\n133\t\n134\t\troleLeaderElectionAssetName        = \"trust-manager/resources/role_trust-manager:leaderelection.yml\"\n135\t\troleBindingLeaderElectionAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/rolebinding_trust-manager:leaderelection.yml\"\n136\t\n137\t\tissuerAssetName      = \"trust-manager/resources/issuer_trust-manager.yml\"\n138\t\tcertificateAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/certificate_trust-manager.yml\"\n139\t\n140\t\tvalidatingWebhookConfigAssetName = \"trust-manager/resources/validatingwebhookconfiguration_trust-manager.yml\"\n141\t)\n142\t",

  {
    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_vrtx_01Y6WA92Whzua6ATdurWWYpg",
    "tool_name": "Read",
    "content": "1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"maps\"\n6\t\t\"os\"\n7\t\t\"reflect\"\n8\t\t\"slices\"\n9\t\n10\t\tappsv1 \"k8s.io/api/apps/v1\"\n11\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n12\t\t\"k8s.io/utils/ptr\"\n13\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n14\t\n15\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n16\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n17\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/operator/assets\"\n18\t)\n19\t\n20\tfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDeployment(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) error {\n21\t\tdesired, err := r.getDeploymentObject(trustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations, caBundleHash)\n22\t\tif err != nil {\n23\t\t\treturn err\n24\t\t}\n25\t\n26\t\tdeploymentName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n27\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling deployment resource\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n28\t\n29\t\texisting := &appsv1.Deployment{}\n30\t\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n31\t\tif err != nil {\n32\t\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if deployment %q exists\", deploymentName)\n33\t\t}\n34\t\tif exists && !deploymentModified(desired, existing) {\n35\t\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"deployment resource exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n36\t\t\treturn nil\n37\t\t}\n38\t\n39\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"deployment resource has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", deploymentName)\n40\t\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n41\t\t\treturn common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply deployment %q\", deploymentName)\n42\t\t}\n43\t\n44\t\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"deployment resource %s applied\", deploymentName)\n45\t\treturn nil\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\tfunc (r *Reconciler) getDeploymentObject(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string, caBundleHash string) (*appsv1.Deployment, error) {\n49\t\tdeployment := common.DecodeObjBytes[*appsv1.Deployment](codecs, appsv1.SchemeGroupVersion, assets.MustAsset(deploymentAssetName))\n50\t\n51\t\tif err := validateDeploymentManifest(deployment); err != nil {\n52\t\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"invalid trust-manager deployment manifest\")\n53\t\t}\n54\t\n55\t\tcommon.UpdateName(deployment, trustManagerDeploymentName)\n56\t\tcommon.UpdateNamespace(deployment, operandNamespace)\n57\t\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n58\t\tupdateResourceAnnotations(deployment, resourceAnnotations)\n59\t\tupdatePodTemplateLabels(deployment, resourceLabels)\n60\t\tupdateDeploymentArgs(deployment, trustManager)\n61\t\n62\t\tupdateServiceAccountName(deployment)\n63\t\tupdateTLSSecretVolume(deployment)\n64\t\tupdateDefaultCAPackageVolume(\n65\t\t\tdeployment,\n66\t\t\ttrustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage,\n67\t\t\tcaBundleHash,\n68\t\t)\n69\t\n70\t\tif err := updateImage(deployment); err != nil {\n71\t\t\treturn nil, common.NewIrrecoverableError(err, \"failed to update trust-manager image\")\n72\t\t}\n73\t\n74\t\tif err := updateResourceRequirements(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n75\t\t\treturn nil, err\n76\t\t}\n77\t\tif err := updateAffinityRules(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n78\t\t\treturn nil, err\n79\t\t}\n80\t\tif err := updatePodTolerations(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n81\t\t\treturn nil, err\n82\t\t}\n83\t\tif err := updateNodeSelector(deployment, trustManager); err != nil {\n84\t\t\treturn nil, err\n85\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\treturn deployment, nil\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\t// validateDeploymentManifest checks that the bindata deployment contains the\n91\t// expected container and TLS volume. Without these, the update helpers silently\n92\t// no-op and the operator would apply a deployment with stale defaults.\n93\tfunc validateDeploymentManifest(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n94\t\thasContainer := false\n95\t\tfor _, c := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n96\t\t\tif c.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n97\t\t\t\thasContainer = true\n98\t\t\t\tbreak\n99\t\t\t}\n100\t\t}\n101\t\tif !hasContainer {\n102\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required container %q\", trustManagerContainerName)\n103\t\t}\n104\t\n105\t\thasVolume := false\n106\t\tfor _, v := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n107\t\t\tif v.Name == tlsVolumeName && v.Secret != nil {\n108\t\t\t\thasVolume = true\n109\t\t\t\tbreak\n110\t\t\t}\n111\t\t}\n112\t\tif !hasVolume {\n113\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"deployment manifest missing required secret volume %q\", tlsVolumeName)\n114\t\t}\n115\t\n116\t\treturn nil\n117\t}\n118\t\n119\tfunc updatePodTemplateLabels(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, resourceLabels map[string]string) {\n120\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Labels = resourceLabels\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\tfunc updateDeploymentArgs(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) {\n124\t\tconfig := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig\n125\t\ttrustNamespace := getTrustNamespace(trustManager)\n126\t\n127\t\targs := []string{\n128\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-format=%s\", config.LogFormat),\n129\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--log-level=%d\", config.LogLevel),\n130\t\t\t\"--metrics-port=9402\",\n131\t\t\t\"--readiness-probe-port=6060\",\n132\t\t\t\"--readiness-probe-path=/readyz\",\n133\t\t\t\"--leader-elect=true\",\n134\t\t\t\"--leader-election-lease-duration=15s\",\n135\t\t\t\"--leader-election-renew-deadline=10s\",\n136\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"--trust-namespace=%s\", trustNamespace),\n137\t\t\t\"--webhook-host=0.0.0.0\",\n138\t\t\t\"--webhook-port=6443\",\n139\t\t\t\"--webhook-certificate-dir=/tls\",\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\tif secretTargetsEnabled(config.SecretTargets) {\n143\t\t\targs = append(args, \"--secret-targets-enabled=true\")\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\tif config.FilterExpiredCertificates == v1alpha1.FilterExpiredCertificatesPolicyEnabled {\n147\t\t\targs = append(args, \"--filter-expired-certificates=true\")\n148\t\t}\n149\t\n150\t\tif defaultCAPackageEnabled(config.DefaultCAPackage) {\n151\t\t\targs = append(args, fmt.Sprintf(\"--default-package-location=%s\", defaultCAPackageLocation))\n152\t\t}\n153\t\n154\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n155\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n156\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Args = args\n157\t\t\t}\n158\t\t}\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t// updateDefaultCAPackageVolume adds the default CA package ConfigMap volume\n162\t// and mount to the deployment when the feature is enabled. It also sets the\n163\t// hash annotation on the pod template to trigger rolling restarts when the\n164\t// CA bundle content changes.\n165\tfunc updateDefaultCAPackageVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, config v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackageConfig, caBundleHash string) {\n166\t\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(config) {\n167\t\t\treturn\n168\t\t}\n169\t\n170\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Annotations == nil {\n171\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n172\t\t}\n173\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Annotations[defaultCAPackageHashAnnotation] = caBundleHash\n174\t\n175\t\t// Remove any existing volume with the same name to avoid duplicates.\n176\t\tvar volumes []corev1.Volume\n177\t\tfor _, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n178\t\t\tif vol.Name != defaultCAPackageVolumeName {\n179\t\t\t\tvolumes = append(volumes, vol)\n180\t\t\t}\n181\t\t}\n182\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes = append(volumes, corev1.Volume{\n183\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n184\t\t\tVolumeSource: corev1.VolumeSource{\n185\t\t\t\tConfigMap: &corev1.ConfigMapVolumeSource{\n186\t\t\t\t\tLocalObjectReference: corev1.LocalObjectReference{\n187\t\t\t\t\t\tName: defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n188\t\t\t\t\t},\n189\t\t\t\t},\n190\t\t\t},\n191\t\t})\n192\t\n193\t\t// Remove any existing volume mount at the same path, then add ours.\n194\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n195\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n196\t\t\t\tvar mounts []corev1.VolumeMount\n197\t\t\t\tfor _, vm := range container.VolumeMounts {\n198\t\t\t\t\tif vm.MountPath != defaultCAPackageMountPath {\n199\t\t\t\t\t\tmounts = append(mounts, vm)\n200\t\t\t\t\t}\n201\t\t\t\t}\n202\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].VolumeMounts = append(mounts, corev1.VolumeMount{\n203\t\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageVolumeName,\n204\t\t\t\t\tMountPath: defaultCAPackageMountPath,\n205\t\t\t\t\tReadOnly:  true,\n206\t\t\t\t})\n207\t\t\t\tbreak\n208\t\t\t}\n209\t\t}\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\tfunc updateImage(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) error {\n213\t\timage := os.Getenv(trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n214\t\tif image == \"\" {\n215\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s environment variable with trust-manager image not set\", trustManagerImageNameEnvVarName)\n216\t\t}\n217\t\tfor i, container := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n218\t\t\tif container.Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n219\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Image = image\n220\t\t\t}\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn nil\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\tfunc updateResourceRequirements(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n226\t\tresources := trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Resources\n227\t\tif len(resources.Limits) == 0 && len(resources.Requests) == 0 {\n228\t\t\treturn nil\n229\t\t}\n230\t\tif err := common.ValidateResourceRequirements(resources, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n231\t\t\treturn err\n232\t\t}\n233\t\tfor i := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers {\n234\t\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Name == trustManagerContainerName {\n235\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[i].Resources = resources\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t}\n238\t\treturn nil\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\tfunc updateAffinityRules(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n242\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity == nil {\n243\t\t\treturn nil\n244\t\t}\n245\t\tif err := common.ValidateAffinityRules(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n246\t\t\treturn err\n247\t\t}\n248\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Affinity = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Affinity\n249\t\treturn nil\n250\t}\n251\t\n252\tfunc updatePodTolerations(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n253\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations == nil {\n254\t\t\treturn nil\n255\t\t}\n256\t\tif err := common.ValidateTolerationsConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n257\t\t\treturn err\n258\t\t}\n259\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Tolerations = trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.Tolerations\n260\t\treturn nil\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\tfunc updateNodeSelector(deployment *appsv1.Deployment, trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager) error {\n264\t\tif trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector == nil {\n265\t\t\treturn nil\n266\t\t}\n267\t\tif err := common.ValidateNodeSelectorConfig(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector, trustManagerConfigFieldPath); err != nil {\n268\t\t\treturn err\n269\t\t}\n270\t\tif deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n271\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector = make(map[string]string)\n272\t\t}\n273\t\t// Merge user-specified node selectors with any default selectors from the manifest.\n274\t\t// User-specified selectors take precedence.\n275\t\tfor k, v := range trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.NodeSelector {\n276\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.NodeSelector[k] = v\n277\t\t}\n278\t\treturn nil\n279\t}\n280\t\n281\tfunc updateServiceAccountName(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n282\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.ServiceAccountName = trustManagerServiceAccountName\n283\t}\n284\t\n285\tconst tlsVolumeName = \"tls\"\n286\t\n287\tfunc updateTLSSecretVolume(deployment *appsv1.Deployment) {\n288\t\tfor i, vol := range deployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes {\n289\t\t\tif vol.Name == tlsVolumeName && vol.Secret != nil {\n290\t\t\t\tdeployment.Spec.Template.Spec.Volumes[i].Secret.SecretName = trustManagerTLSSecretName\n291\t\t\t\treturn\n292\t\t\t}\n293\t\t}\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// deploymentModified compares only the fields we manage via SSA.\n297\t// For the readiness probe, only the fields explicitly set in our bindata are\n298\t// compared to avoid false positives from API-server-defaulted probe scalars\n299\t// (TimeoutSeconds, SuccessThreshold, FailureThreshold).\n300\tfunc deploymentModified(desired, existing *appsv1.Deployment) bool {\n301\t\tif managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) {\n302\t\t\treturn true\n303\t\t}\n304\t\n305\t\tif !ptr.Equal(desired.Spec.Replicas, existing.Spec.Replicas) ||\n306\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Spec.Selector, existing.Spec.Selector) {\n307\t\t\treturn true\n308\t\t}\n309\t\n310\t\tif !maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Labels, existing.Spec.Template.Labels) ||\n311\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Spec.Template.Annotations, existing.Spec.Template.Annotations) {\n312\t\t\treturn true\n313\t\t}\n314\t\n315\t\tdesiredPodSpec := desired.Spec.Template.Spec\n316\t\texistingPodSpec := existing.Spec.Template.Spec\n317\t\n318\t\tif desiredPodSpec.ServiceAccountName != existingPodSpec.ServiceAccountName ||\n319\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desiredPodSpec.NodeSelector, existingPodSpec.NodeSelector) ||\n320\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Volumes, existingPodSpec.Volumes) ||\n321\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Affinity, existingPodSpec.Affinity) ||\n322\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desiredPodSpec.Tolerations, existingPodSpec.Tolerations) {\n323\t\t\treturn true\n324\t\t}\n325\t\n326\t\tif len(desiredPodSpec.Containers) != len(existingPodSpec.Containers) {\n327\t\t\treturn true\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\tfor i := range desiredPodSpec.Containers {\n331\t\t\tif containerModified(&desiredPodSpec.Containers[i], &existingPodSpec.Containers[i]) {\n332\t\t\t\treturn true\n333\t\t\t}\n334\t\t}\n335\t\n336\t\treturn false\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\tfunc containerModified(desired, existing *corev1.Container) bool {\n340\t\tif desired.Name != existing.Name ||\n341\t\t\tdesired.Image != existing.Image ||\n342\t\t\tdesired.ImagePullPolicy != existing.ImagePullPolicy {\n343\t\t\treturn true\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\tif !slices.Equal(desired.Args, existing.Args) ||\n347\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.Resources, existing.Resources) ||\n348\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.SecurityContext, existing.SecurityContext) ||\n349\t\t\t!reflect.DeepEqual(desired.VolumeMounts, existing.VolumeMounts) {\n350\t\t\treturn true\n351\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\tif !containerPortsMatch(desired.Ports, existing.Ports) {\n354\t\t\treturn true\n355\t\t}\n356\t\n357\t\tif readinessProbeModified(desired.ReadinessProbe, existing.ReadinessProbe) {\n358\t\t\treturn true\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\treturn false\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\tfunc containerPortsMatch(desired, existing []corev1.ContainerPort) bool {\n365\t\tif len(desired) != len(existing) {\n366\t\t\treturn false\n367\t\t}\n368\t\tfor _, dp := range desired {\n369\t\t\tmatched := false\n370\t\t\tfor _, ep := range existing {\n371\t\t\t\tif dp.ContainerPort == ep.ContainerPort && dp.Name == ep.Name {\n372\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n373\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n374\t\t\t\t}\n375\t\t\t}\n376\t\t\tif !matched {\n377\t\t\t\treturn false\n378\t\t\t}\n379\t\t}\n380\t\treturn true\n381\t}\n382\t\n383\t// readinessProbeModified compares only the probe fields we explicitly set in\n384\t// bindata (HTTPGet, InitialDelaySeconds, PeriodSeconds), avoiding\n385\t// API-server-defaulted int32 fields like TimeoutSeconds and FailureThreshold.\n386\tfunc readinessProbeModified(desired, existing *corev1.Probe) bool {\n387\t\tif desired == nil && existing == nil {\n388\t\t\treturn false\n389\t\t}\n390\t\tif desired == nil || existing == nil {\n391\t\t\treturn true\n392\t\t}\n393\t\tif desired.InitialDelaySeconds != existing.InitialDelaySeconds ||\n394\t\t\tdesired.PeriodSeconds != existing.PeriodSeconds {\n395\t\t\treturn true\n396\t\t}\n397\t\tif desired.HTTPGet != nil && existing.HTTPGet != nil {\n398\t\t\tif desired.HTTPGet.Path != existing.HTTPGet.Path ||\n399\t\t\t\tdesired.HTTPGet.Port != existing.HTTPGet.Port {\n400\t\t\t\treturn true\n401\t\t\t}\n402\t\t} else if desired.HTTPGet != existing.HTTPGet {\n403\t\t\treturn true\n404\t\t}\n405\t\treturn false\n406\t}\n407\t",

{"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "You are a Go engineer working on a Kubernetes operator.\nThe repository is located at /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment.\nChange your working directory to that repository before beginning.\n\nTask:\nImplement a content-hash-triggered rolling restart for the CNO-injected CA ConfigMap\nin the IstioCSR controller (pkg/controller/istiocsr/).\n\nMirror the pattern already present in pkg/controller/trustmanager/configmaps.go:\nread the ConfigMap contents, compute a SHA-256 hash of the PEM data (not the\nresourceVersion), and stamp the hash as an annotation on the IstioCSR Deployment's\npod template spec.\n\nInclude an idempotency guard that skips the Patch call when ConfigMap content is unchanged.\n\n\nProduce a complete implementation. Include:\n- All files that must be modified with the exact changes\n- Any make targets or commands to run and in what order\n- The complete code for any new files or functions"}, "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:28:18.617Z"}

(\n4\t\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n5\t\t\"encoding/hex\"\n6\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n7\t\t\"fmt\"\n8\t\t\"maps\"\n9\t\n10\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n11\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n12\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n13\t\n14\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n15\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n16\t)\n17\t\n18\t// caPackage represents the JSON format expected by trust-manager\n19\ttype caPackage struct {\n20\t\tName    string `json:\"name\"`\n21\t\tBundle  string `json:\"bundle\"`\n22\t\tVersion string `json:\"version\"`\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap reads the CNO-injected CA bundle,\n26\t// formats it into trust-manager's expected JSON package format, and creates\n27\t// or updates the package ConfigMap in the operand namespace.\n28\t// Returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle content and any error.\n29\t// Returns (\"\", nil) when defaultCAPackage is disabled.\n30\tfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) (string, error) {\n31\t\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage) {\n32\t\t\treturn \"\", nil\n33\t\t}\n34\t\n35\t\tcaBundle, resourceVersion, err := r.readTrustedCABundle()\n36\t\tif err != nil {\n37\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n38\t\t}\n39\t\n40\t\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(caBundle, resourceVersion)\n41\t\tif err != nil {\n42\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n43\t\t}\n44\t\n45\t\tbundleHash := computeCABundleHash(caBundle)\n46\t\n47\t\tdesired := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations)\n48\t\n49\t\tcmName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n50\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling default CA package ConfigMap\", \"name\", cmName)\n51\t\n52\t\texisting := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n53\t\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n54\t\tif err != nil {\n55\t\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if ConfigMap %q exists\", cmName)\n56\t\t}\n57\t\tif exists && !configMapModified(desired, existing) {\n58\t\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n59\t\t\treturn bundleHash, nil\n60\t\t}\n61\t\n62\t\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n63\t\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n64\t\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply ConfigMap %q\", cmName)\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"default CA package ConfigMap %s applied\", cmName)\n68\t\treturn bundleHash, nil\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t// readTrustedCABundle reads the CNO-injected CA bundle from the operator namespace.\n72\t// Returns the PEM bundle, the ConfigMap's resource version, and any error.\n73\tfunc (r *Reconciler) readTrustedCABundle() (string, string, error) {\n74\t\tinjectionCM := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n75\t\tkey := client.ObjectKey{\n76\t\t\tNamespace: common.OperatorNamespace,\n77\t\t\tName:      common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n78\t\t}\n79\t\tif err := r.Get(r.ctx, key, injectionCM); err != nil {\n80\t\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n81\t\t\t\terr,\n82\t\t\t\t\"failed to read CA bundle ConfigMap %q in namespace %q\",\n83\t\t\t\tcommon.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.OperatorNamespace,\n84\t\t\t)\n85\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\tcaBundle, ok := injectionCM.Data[common.TrustedCABundleKey]\n88\t\tif !ok || caBundle == \"\" {\n89\t\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n90\t\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"CA bundle ConfigMap %q does not contain key %q\", common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.TrustedCABundleKey),\n91\t\t\t\t\"missing key %q in ConfigMap %q\", common.TrustedCABundleKey, common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n92\t\t\t)\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\treturn caBundle, injectionCM.ResourceVersion, nil\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\t// formatCAPackage encodes the CA bundle into trust-manager's expected JSON package format.\n99\tfunc formatCAPackage(caBundle, version string) ([]byte, error) {\n100\t\tpkg := caPackage{\n101\t\t\tName:    defaultCAPackageName,\n102\t\t\tBundle:  caBundle,\n103\t\t\tVersion: version,\n104\t\t}\n105\t\t// TODO: cross check the formatting of the JSON package\n106\t\tpkgJSON, err := json.Marshal(pkg)\n107\t\tif err != nil {\n108\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to marshal CA package to JSON: %w\", err)\n109\t\t}\n110\t\treturn pkgJSON, nil\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// computeCABundleHash returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle PEM content.\n114\t// The hash is used as the pod template annotation to trigger rolling restarts\n115\t// only when the actual certificate content changes.\n116\tfunc computeCABundleHash(caBundle string) string {\n117\t\thash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(caBundle))\n118\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t// buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap constructs the desired ConfigMap for the\n122\t// formatted CA package in the operand namespace.\n123\tfunc buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON []byte, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) *corev1.ConfigMap {\n124\t\tcm := &corev1.ConfigMap{\n125\t\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n126\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n127\t\t\t\tKind:       \"ConfigMap\",\n128\t\t\t},\n129\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n130\t\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n131\t\t\t\tNamespace: operandNamespace,\n132\t\t\t},\n133\t\t\tData: map[string]string{\n134\t\t\t\tdefaultCAPackageFilename: string(pkgJSON),\n135\t\t\t},\n136\t\t}\n137\t\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(cm, resourceLabels)\n138\t\tupdateResourceAnnotations(cm, resourceAnnotations)\n139\t\treturn cm\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// configMapModified checks whether the desired ConfigMap differs from the existing one.\n143\tfunc configMapModified(desired, existing *corev1.ConfigMap) bool {\n144\t\treturn managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) ||\n145\t\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Data, existing.Data)\n146\t}\n147\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"40f75bc1-181a-4148-8afe-533f8e2603b5","uuid":"27fabf3c-015c-4215-8327-cbfec5880e10","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:28:37.066Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/cert-manager-operator-treatment/pkg/controller/trustmanager/configmaps.go","content":"package trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"maps\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n)\n\n// caPackage represents the JSON format expected by trust-manager\ntype caPackage struct {\n\tName    string `json:\"name\"`\n\tBundle  string `json:\"bundle\"`\n\tVersion string `json:\"version\"`\n}\n\n// createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap reads the CNO-injected CA bundle,\n// formats it into trust-manager's expected JSON package format, and creates\n// or updates the package ConfigMap in the operand namespace.\n// Returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle content and any error.\n// Returns (\"\", nil) when defaultCAPackage is disabled.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(trustManager *v1alpha1.TrustManager, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) (string, error) {\n\tif !defaultCAPackageEnabled(trustManager.Spec.TrustManagerConfig.DefaultCAPackage) {\n\t\treturn \"\", nil\n\t}\n\n\tcaBundle, resourceVersion, err := r.readTrustedCABundle()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(caBundle, resourceVersion)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tbundleHash := computeCABundleHash(caBundle)\n\n\tdesired := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations)\n\n\tcmName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", desired.GetNamespace(), desired.GetName())\n\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"reconciling default CA package ConfigMap\", \"name\", cmName)\n\n\texisting := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n\texists, err := r.Exists(r.ctx, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(desired), existing)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to check if ConfigMap %q exists\", cmName)\n\t}\n\tif exists && !configMapModified(desired, existing) {\n\t\tr.log.V(4).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap exists and is in desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n\t\treturn bundleHash, nil\n\t}\n\n\tr.log.V(2).Info(\"default CA package ConfigMap has been modified, updating to desired state\", \"name\", cmName)\n\tif err := r.Patch(r.ctx, desired, client.Apply, client.FieldOwner(fieldOwner), client.ForceOwnership); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", common.FromClientError(err, \"failed to apply ConfigMap %q\", cmName)\n\t}\n\n\tr.eventRecorder.Eventf(trustManager, corev1.EventTypeNormal, \"Reconciled\", \"default CA package ConfigMap %s applied\", cmName)\n\treturn bundleHash, nil\n}\n\n// readTrustedCABundle reads the CNO-injected CA bundle from the operator namespace.\n// Returns the PEM bundle, the ConfigMap's resource version, and any error.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) readTrustedCABundle() (string, string, error) {\n\tinjectionCM := &corev1.ConfigMap{}\n\tkey := client.ObjectKey{\n\t\tNamespace: common.OperatorNamespace,\n\t\tName:      common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n\t}\n\tif err := r.Get(r.ctx, key, injectionCM); err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n\t\t\terr,\n\t\t\t\"failed to read CA bundle ConfigMap %q in namespace %q\",\n\t\t\tcommon.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.OperatorNamespace,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\tcaBundle, ok := injectionCM.Data[common.TrustedCABundleKey]\n\tif !ok || caBundle == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", \"\", common.FromClientError(\n\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"CA bundle ConfigMap %q does not contain key %q\", common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName, common.TrustedCABundleKey),\n\t\t\t\"missing key %q in ConfigMap %q\", common.TrustedCABundleKey, common.TrustedCABundleConfigMapName,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\treturn caBundle, injectionCM.ResourceVersion, nil\n}\n\n// formatCAPackage encodes the CA bundle into trust-manager's expected JSON package format.\nfunc formatCAPackage(caBundle, version string) ([]byte, error) {\n\tpkg := caPackage{\n\t\tName:    defaultCAPackageName,\n\t\tBundle:  caBundle,\n\t\tVersion: version,\n\t}\n\t// TODO: cross check the formatting of the JSON package\n\tpkgJSON, err := json.Marshal(pkg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to marshal CA package to JSON: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn pkgJSON, nil\n}\n\n// computeCABundleHash returns the SHA-256 hash of the CA bundle PEM content.\n// The hash is used as the pod template annotation to trigger rolling restarts\n// only when the actual certificate content changes.\nfunc computeCABundleHash(caBundle string) string {\n\thash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(caBundle))\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n}\n\n// buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap constructs the desired ConfigMap for the\n// formatted CA package in the operand namespace.\nfunc buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON []byte, resourceLabels, resourceAnnotations map[string]string) *corev1.ConfigMap {\n\tcm := &corev1.ConfigMap{\n\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n\t\t\tKind:       \"ConfigMap\",\n\t\t},\n\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n\t\t\tName:      defaultCAPackageConfigMapName,\n\t\t\tNamespace: operandNamespace,\n\t\t},\n\t\tData: map[string]string{\n\t\t\tdefaultCAPackageFilename: string(pkgJSON),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tcommon.UpdateResourceLabels(cm, resourceLabels)\n\tupdateResourceAnnotations(cm, resourceAnnotations)\n\treturn cm\n}\n\n// configMapModified checks whether the desired ConfigMap differs from the existing one.\nfunc configMapModified(desired, existing *corev1.ConfigMap) bool {\n\treturn managedMetadataModified(desired, existing) ||\n\t\t!maps.Equal(desired.Data, existing.Data)\n}\n","numLines":147,"startLine":1,"totalLines":147}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_011LNUJrSYK5Zbo8ddztNbZa","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage trustmanager\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"context\"\n5\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n6\t\t\"testing\"\n7\t\n8\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n9\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n10\t\n11\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n12\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n13\t\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common/fakes\"\n14\t)\n15\t\n16\tconst testCABundle = \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\nthisIsATestCABundle\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\"\n17\t\n18\tfunc TestFormatCAPackage(t *testing.T) {\n19\t\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(testCABundle, \"12345\")\n20\t\tif err != nil {\n21\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n22\t\t}\n23\t\n24\t\tvar pkg caPackage\n25\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(pkgJSON, &pkg); err != nil {\n26\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to unmarshal package JSON: %v\", err)\n27\t\t}\n28\t\tif pkg.Name != defaultCAPackageName {\n29\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageName, pkg.Name)\n30\t\t}\n31\t\tif pkg.Version != \"12345\" {\n32\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package version %q, got %q\", \"12345\", pkg.Version)\n33\t\t}\n34\t\tif pkg.Bundle != testCABundle {\n35\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected bundle to match input, got different content\")\n36\t\t}\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\tfunc TestComputeCABundleHash(t *testing.T) {\n40\t\ttests := []struct {\n41\t\t\tname     string\n42\t\t\tbundle1  string\n43\t\t\tbundle2  string\n44\t\t\twantSame bool\n45\t\t}{\n46\t\t\t{\n47\t\t\t\tname:     \"same bundle produces same hash\",\n48\t\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n49\t\t\t\tbundle2:  testCABundle,\n50\t\t\t\twantSame: true,\n51\t\t\t},\n52\t\t\t{\n53\t\t\t\tname:     \"different bundles produce different hashes\",\n54\t\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n55\t\t\t\tbundle2:  \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\ndifferent\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\",\n56\t\t\t\twantSame: false,\n57\t\t\t},\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n61\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n62\t\t\t\thash1 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle1)\n63\t\t\t\thash2 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle2)\n64\t\n65\t\t\t\tif hash1 == \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected non-empty hash\")\n67\t\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\t\tif (hash1 == hash2) != tt.wantSame {\n69\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"hash equality=%v, want %v (hash1=%s, hash2=%s)\", hash1 == hash2, tt.wantSame, hash1, hash2)\n70\t\t\t\t}\n71\t\t\t})\n72\t\t}\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\tfunc TestBuildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(t *testing.T) {\n76\t\tpkgJSON := []byte(`{\"name\":\"test\",\"bundle\":\"test\",\"version\":\"1\"}`)\n77\t\tlabels := map[string]string{\"app\": \"test\"}\n78\t\tannotations := map[string]string{\"note\": \"test\"}\n79\t\n80\t\tcm := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, labels, annotations)\n81\t\n82\t\tif cm.Name != defaultCAPackageConfigMapName {\n83\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageConfigMapName, cm.Name)\n84\t\t}\n85\t\tif cm.Namespace != operandNamespace {\n86\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected namespace %q, got %q\", operandNamespace, cm.Namespace)\n87\t\t}\n88\t\tif cm.Labels[\"app\"] != \"test\" {\n89\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected label app=test, got %v\", cm.Labels)\n90\t\t}\n91\t\tif cm.Annotations[\"note\"] != \"test\" {\n92\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected annotation note=test, got %v\", cm.Annotations)\n93\t\t}\n94\t\tif cm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] != string(pkgJSON) {\n95\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected data key %q to contain package JSON\", defaultCAPackageFilename)\n96\t\t}\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\tfunc TestConfigMapModified(t *testing.T) {\n100\t\tbase := func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n101\t\t\treturn &corev1.ConfigMap{\n102\t\t\t\tData: map[string]string{defaultCAPackageFilename: `{\"name\":\"test\"}`},\n103\t\t\t}\n104\t\t}\n105\t\n106\t\ttests := []struct {\n107\t\t\tname     string\n108\t\t\tdesired base(),\n121\t\t\t\texisting: func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n122\t\t\t\t\tcm := base()\n123\t\t\t\t\tcm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] = `{\"name\":\"changed\"}`\n124\t\t\t\t\treturn cm\n125\t\t\t\t}(),\n126\t\t\t\twant: true,\n127\t\t\t},\n128\t\t\t{\n129\t\t\t\tname:    \"extra key in existing is detected as modified\",\n130\t\t\t\tdesired: base(),\n131\t\t\t\texisting: func() _, tt := range tests {\n141\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n142\t\t\t\tgot := configMapModified(tt.desired, tt.existing)\n143\t\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n144\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"configMapModified() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n145\t\t\t\t}\n146\t\t\t})\n147\t\t}\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\tfunc TestDefaultCAPackageConfigMapReconciliation(t *testing.T) r.createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(tm, testResourceLabels(), testResourceAnnotations())\n322\t\t\t\tassertError(t, err, tt.wantErr)\n323\t\n324\t\t\t\tif tt.wantHash && hash == \"\" {\n325\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-empty hash, got empty\")\n326\t\t\t\t}\n327\t\t\t\tif !tt.wantHash && hash != \"\" {\n328\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected empty hash, got %q\", hash)\n329\t\t\t\t}\n330\t\n331\t\t\t\tif got := mock.ExistsCallCount(); got != tt.wantExistsCount {\n332\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected %d Exists calls, got %d\", tt.wantExistsCount, trustmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/api/operator/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/cert-manager-operator/pkg/controller/common/fakes\"\n)\n\nconst testCABundle = \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\nthisIsATestCABundle\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\"\n\nfunc TestFormatCAPackage(t *testing.T) {\n\tpkgJSON, err := formatCAPackage(testCABundle, \"12345\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar pkg caPackage\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(pkgJSON, &pkg); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to unmarshal package JSON: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif pkg.Name != defaultCAPackageName {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageName, pkg.Name)\n\t}\n\tif pkg.Version != \"12345\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected package version %q, got %q\", \"12345\", pkg.Version)\n\t}\n\tif pkg.Bundle != testCABundle {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected bundle to match input, got different content\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestComputeCABundleHash(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tbundle1  string\n\t\tbundle2  string\n\t\twantSame bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"same bundle produces same hash\",\n\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n\t\t\tbundle2:  testCABundle,\n\t\t\twantSame: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"different bundles produce different hashes\",\n\t\t\tbundle1:  testCABundle,\n\t\t\tbundle2:  \"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\\ndifferent\\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\",\n\t\t\twantSame: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\thash1 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle1)\n\t\t\thash2 := computeCABundleHash(tt.bundle2)\n\n\t\t\tif hash1 == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected non-empty hash\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (hash1 == hash2) != tt.wantSame {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"hash equality=%v, want %v (hash1=%s, hash2=%s)\", hash1 == hash2, tt.wantSame, hash1, hash2)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestBuildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(t *testing.T) {\n\tpkgJSON := []byte(`{\"name\":\"test\",\"bundle\":\"test\",\"version\":\"1\"}`)\n\tlabels := map[string]string{\"app\": \"test\"}\n\tannotations := map[string]string{\"note\": \"test\"}\n\n\tcm := buildDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(pkgJSON, labels, annotations)\n\n\tif cm.Name != defaultCAPackageConfigMapName {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected name %q, got %q\", defaultCAPackageConfigMapName, cm.Name)\n\t}\n\tif cm.Namespace != operandNamespace {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected namespace %q, got %q\", operandNamespace, cm.Namespace)\n\t}\n\tif cm.Labels[\"app\"] != \"test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected label app=test, got %v\", cm.Labels)\n\t}\n\tif cm.Annotations[\"note\"] != \"test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected annotation note=test, got %v\", cm.Annotations)\n\t}\n\tif cm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] != string(pkgJSON) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected data key %q to contain package JSON\", defaultCAPackageFilename)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestConfigMapModified(t *testing.T) {\n\tbase := func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n\t\treturn &corev1.ConfigMap{\n\t\t\tData: map[string]string{defaultCAPackageFilename: `{\"name\":\"test\"}`},\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tdesired  *corev1.ConfigMap\n\t\texisting *corev1.ConfigMap\n\t\twant     bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"identical ConfigMaps are not modified\",\n\t\t\tdesired:  base(),\n\t\t\texisting: base(),\n\t\t\twant:     false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"different data is detected as modified\",\n\t\t\tdesired: base(),\n\t\t\texisting: func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n\t\t\t\tcm := base()\n\t\t\t\tcm.Data[defaultCAPackageFilename] = `{\"name\":\"changed\"}`\n\t\t\t\treturn cm\n\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\twant: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"extra key in existing is detected as modified\",\n\t\t\tdesired: base(),\n\t\t\texisting: func() *corev1.ConfigMap {\n\t\t\t\tcm := base()\n\t\t\t\tcm.Data[\"extra\"] = \"value\"\n\t\t\t\treturn cm\n\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\twant: true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tgot := configMapModified(tt.desired, tt.existing)\n\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"configMapModified() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestDefaultCAPackageConfigMapReconciliation(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\ttm              *trustManagerBuilder\n\t\tpreReq          func(*Reconciler, *fakes.FakeCtrlClient)\n\t\twantHash        bool\n\t\twantErr         string\n\t\twantExistsCount int\n\t\twantPatchCount  int\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"skips when policy is Disabled\",\n\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyDisabled),\n\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantExistsCount: 0,\n\t\t\twantPatchCount:  0,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"skips when policy is unset (defaults to Disabled)\",\n\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager(),\n\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantExistsCount: 0,\n\t\t\twantPatchCount:  0,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"returns error when injection ConfigMap is not found\",\n\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context, key client.ObjectKey, obj client.Object) error {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn errTestClient\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: \"failed to read CA bundle ConfigMap\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"returns error when CA bundle key is missing\",\n\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context, key client.ObjectKey, obj client.Object) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tcm := obj.(*corev1.ConfigMap)\n\t\t\t\t\tcm.Data = map[string]string{}\n\t\t\t\t\tcm.ResourceVersion = \"100\"\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: \"does not contain key\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"returns error when CA bundle is empty\",\n\t\t\ttm:   testTrustManager().WithDefaultCAPackage(v1alpha1.DefaultCAPackagePolicyEnabled),\n\t\t\tpreReq: func(r *Reconciler, m *fakes.FakeCtrlClient) {\n\t\t\t\tm.GetCalls(func(ctx context.Context, key client.ObjectKey, obj client.Object) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tcm := obj.(*corev1.ConfigMap)\n\t\t\t\t\tcm.Data = errTestClient\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr:         \"failed to check if ConfigMap\",\n\t\t\twantExistsCount: 1,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"propagates Patch error\",\n\t\t\ttm: tt.tm.Build()\n\t\t\thash, err := r.createOrApplyDefaultCAPackageConfigMap(tm, testResourceLabels(), testResourceAnnotations())\n\t\t\tassertError(t, err, tt.wantErr)\n\n\t\t\tif tt.wantHash && hash == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-empty hash, got empty\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.wantHash && hash != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected empty hash, got %q\", hash)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif got := mock.ExistsCallCount(); got != tt.wantExistsCount {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected %d Exists calls, got %d\", tt.wantExistsCount, got)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif got := mock.PatchCallCount(); got !=
