IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {\n99\t\treturn s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {\n103\t\treturn s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {\n107\t\treturn s.canDeployMutatingWebhook\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.\n111\t// The build Conditions are:\n112\t//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning\n113\t//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted\n114\t//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist\n115\t//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date\n116\t//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date\n117\t//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.\n118\t//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).\n119\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(\n120\t\tpodPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,\n121\t\tpodPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,\n122\t\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,\n123\t\tdeprovisioning bool) {\n124\t\ts.deprovisioning = deprovisioning\n125\t\t// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration\n126\t\ts.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable\n127\t\t// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date\n128\t\ts.podPlacementControllerNotReady = !podPlacementControllerAvailable || !podPlacementControllerUpToDate\n129\t\ts.podPlacementWebhookNotReady = !podPlacementWebhookAvailable || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate\n130\t\t// if all the components exist and have at least one replica ready\n131\t\ts.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable\n132\t\t// if some components are not available (no replicas)\n133\t\ts.degraded = !s.available && !s.deprovisioning // degraded will not track deprovisioning\n134\t\t// allow the deployment of the mutating webhook configuration if the pod placement controller and webhook are available\n135\t\t// (at least one replica)\n136\t\ts.canDeployMutatingWebhook = podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable && !s.deprovisioning\n137\t\ts.progressing = (!podPlacementControllerUpToDate || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate || !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable) && !s.deprovisioning\n138\t\ts.buildConditions()\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) buildConditions() {\n142\t\tif s.Conditions == nil {\n143\t\t\ts.Conditions = []metav1.Condition{}\n144\t\t}\n145\t\treason := \"\"\n146\t\tif s.podPlacementControllerNotReady {\n147\t\t\treason += PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType\n148\t\t}\n149\t\tif s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady {\n150\t\t\treason += PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType\n151\t\t}\n152\t\tif s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable {\n153\t\t\treason += MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n154\t\t}\n155\t\tif reason == \"\" {\n156\t\t\treason = AllComponentsReady\n157\t\t}\n158\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n159\t\t\tType:    AvailableType,\n160\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.available),\n161\t\t\tReason:  reason,\n162\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ReadyMsg, notFromBool(s.available), strings.TrimSpace(notFromBool(s.available))),\n163\t\t})\n164\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n165\t\t\tType:    ProgressingType,\n166\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.progressing),\n167\t\t\tReason:  reason,\n168\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ProgressingMsg, notFromBool(s.progressing)),\n169\t\t})\n170\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n171\t\t\tType:    DegradedType,\n172\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.degraded),\n173\t\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.degraded)), DegradedType),\n174\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DegradedMsg, notFromBool(s.degraded)),\n175\t\t})\n176\t\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix := \"\"\n177\t\tif s.deprovisioning {\n178\t\t\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix = PendingDeprovisioningMsg\n179\t\t}\n180\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n181\t\t\tType:    DeprovisioningType,\n182\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.deprovisioning),\n183\t\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.deprovisioning)), DeprovisioningType),\n184\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DeprovisioningMsg, notFromBool(s.deprovisioning), deprovisinoingMessagePostfix),\n185\t\t})\n186\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n187\t\t\tType:    PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType,\n188\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementControllerNotReady),\n189\t\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementController%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady))),\n190\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementControllerRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady)),\n191\t\t})\n192\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n193\t\t\tType:    PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType,\n194\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady),\n195\t\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementWebhook%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady))),\n196\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementWebhookRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady)),\n197\t\t})\n198\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n199\t\t\tType:    MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable,\n200\t\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable),\n201\t\t\tReason:  reason,\n202\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(MutatingWebhookConfigurationReadyMsg, notFromBool(!s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable)),\n203\t\t})\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n207\t// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n208\t// Creating the object enables the operand.\n209\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n210\t// +kubebuilder:storageversion\n211\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n212\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\n213\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Available,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].status,type=string\n214\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Progressing,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].status,type=string\n215\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Degraded,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Degraded\")].status,type=string\n216\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Since,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].lastTransitionTime,type=date\n217\t// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Status,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].reason,type=string\n218\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n219\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n220\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n221\t\n222\t\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n223\t\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\tfunc (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) PluginsEnabled(plugin common.Plugin) bool {\n227\t\tif c.Spec.Plugins != nil {\n228\t\t\treturn c.Spec.Plugins.PluginEnabled(plugin)\n229\t\t}\n230\t\treturn false\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n234\t\n235\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n236\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n237\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n238\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n239\t\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\tfunc conditionFromBool(b bool) metav1.ConditionStatus {\n243\t\tif b {\n244\t\t\treturn metav1.ConditionTrue\n245\t\t}\n246\t\treturn metav1.ConditionFalse\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\tfunc notFromBool(b bool) string {\n250\t\tif b {\n251\t\t\treturn \"\"\n252\t\t}\n253\t\treturn \"not \"\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\tfunc trimAndCapitalize(s string) string {\n257\t\ttrimmed := strings.TrimSpace(s)\n258\t\tif len(trimmed) == 0 {\n259\t\t\treturn trimmed\n260\t\t}\n261\t\treturn strings.ToUpper(string(trimmed[0])) + trimmed[1:]\n262\t}\n263\t",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:28:47.946Z"
  },

  {
    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_vrtx_01NAWF1L2N1W1PyZ2YPugNeH",
    "tool_name": "Read",
    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage podplacement\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"fmt\"\n22\t\t\"strconv\"\n23\t\t\"strings\"\n24\t\t\"time\"\n25\t\n26\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n27\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n28\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n29\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n30\t\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n31\t\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n32\t\n33\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n34\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n35\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n36\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics\"\n37\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image\"\n38\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/models\"\n39\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\tvar (\n43\t\t// imageInspectionCache is the facade singleton used to inspect images. It is defined here to facilitate testing.\n44\t\timageInspectionCache image.ICache = image.FacadeSingleton()\n45\t)\n46\t\n47\tconst MaxRetryCount = 5\n48\t\n49\ttype containerImage struct {\n50\t\timageName string\n51\t\tskipCache bool\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\ttype Pod struct {\n55\t\tmodels.Pod\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\tfunc newPod(pod *corev1.Pod, ctx context.Context, recorder record.EventRecorder) *Pod {\n59\t\treturn &Pod{\n60\t\t\tPod: *models.NewPod(pod, ctx, recorder),\n61\t\t}\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t// HasSchedulingGate checks if the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\n65\tfunc (pod *Pod) HasSchedulingGate() bool {\n66\t\treturn pod.HasGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\t// RemoveSchedulingGate removes the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName from the pod.\n70\tfunc (pod *Pod) RemoveSchedulingGate() {\n71\t\tpod.RemoveGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n72\t\t// The scheduling gate is removed. We also add a label to the pod to indicate that the scheduling gate was removed\n73\t\t// and this pod was processed by the operator. That's useful for testing and debugging, but also gives the user\n74\t\t// an indication that the pod was processed by the operator.\n75\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SchedulingGateLabel, utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueRemoved)\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t// ensureSchedulingGate ensures that the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\n79\tfunc (pod *Pod) ensureSchedulingGate() {\n80\t\tpod.AddGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\t// getPodImagePullSecrets returns the names of the image pull secrets used by the pod.\n84\tfunc (pod *Pod) getPodImagePullSecrets() []string {\n85\t\tif pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets == nil {\n86\t\t\t// If the imagePullSecrets array is nil, return emptylist\n87\t\t\treturn []string{}\n88\t\t}\n89\t\tvar secretRefs = make([]string, len(pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets))\n90\t\tfor i, secret := range pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets {\n91\t\t\tsecretRefs[i] = secret.Name\n92\t\t}\n93\t\treturn secretRefs\n94\t}\n95\t\n96\t// SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement wraps the logic to set the nodeAffinity for the pod.\n97\t// It verifies first that no nodeSelector field is set for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n98\t// Then, it computes the intersection of the architectures supported by the images used by the pod via pod.getArchitecturePredicate.\n99\t// Finally, it initializes the nodeAffinity for the pod and set it to the computed requirement via the pod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity method.\n100\tfunc (pod *Pod) SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (bool, error) {\n101\t\tif pod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n102\t\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n103\t\t\treturn false, nil\n104\t\t}\n105\t\trequirement, err := pod.getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList)\n106\t\tif err != nil {\n107\t\t\treturn false, err\n108\t\t}\n109\t\tpod.EnsureNoLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel)\n110\t\tif len(requirement.Values) == 0 {\n111\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, NoSupportedArchitecturesFound, NoSupportedArchitecturesFoundMsg)\n112\t\t}\n113\t\tpod.ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement)\n114\t\n115\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n116\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n117\t\t}\n118\t\n119\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n120\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n121\t\t}\n122\t\n123\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n124\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n128\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n129\t\t\tArchitecturePredicateSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(requirement.Values, \", \")))\n130\t\treturn true, nil\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// setRequiredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the given requirement based on the rules in\n134\t// the sig-scheduling's KEP-3838: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/3838-pod-mutable-scheduling-directives.\n135\tfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n136\t\t// the .requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms are ORed\n137\t\tif len(pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\n138\t\t\t// We create a new array of NodeSelectorTerm of length 1 so that we can always iterate it in the next.\n139\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorTerm, 1)\n140\t\t}\n141\t\tnodeSelectorTerms := pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms\n142\t\n143\t\t// The expressions within the nodeSelectorTerms are ANDed.\n144\t\t// Therefore, we iterate over the nodeSelectorTerms and add an expression to each of the terms to verify the\n145\t\t// kubernetes.io/arch label has compatible values.\n146\t\t// Note that the NodeSelectorTerms will always be long at least 1, because we (re-)created it with size 1 above if it was nil (or having 0 length).\n147\t\tvar skipMatchExpressionPatch bool\n148\t\tfor i := range nodeSelectorTerms {\n149\t\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = false\n150\t\t\tif nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions == nil {\n151\t\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, 0, 1)\n152\t\t\t}\n153\t\t\t// Check if the nodeSelectorTerm already has a matchExpression for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n154\t\t\t// if yes, we ignore to add it.\n155\t\t\tfor _, expression := range nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions {\n156\t\t\t\tif expression.Key == requirement.Key {\n157\t\t\t\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = true\n158\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n159\t\t\t\t}\n160\t\t\t}\n161\t\t\t// if skipMatchExpressionPatch is true, we skip to add the matchExpression so that conflictual matchExpressions provided by the user are not overwritten.\n162\t\t\tif !skipMatchExpressionPatch {\n163\t\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = append(nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions, requirement)\n164\t\t\t}\n165\t\t}\n166\t\t// if the nodeSelectorTerms were patched at least once, we set the nodeAffinity label to the set value, to keep\n167\t\t// track of the fact that the nodeAffinity was patched by the operator.\n168\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the preferences given in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n172\t// The configSource parameter identifies which configuration is setting the preferences (e.g., \"ClusterPodPlacementConfig\" or \"PodPlacementConfig/my-ppc\").\n173\tfunc (pod *Pod) SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(nodeAffinity *plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource string) {\n174\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n175\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n176\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n177\t\t}\n178\t\n179\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n180\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n181\t\t}\n182\t\n183\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n184\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{}\n185\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\tseenArchitectures := pod.getExistingPreferredArchitectures()\n188\t\tvar preferredSchedulingTerms []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm\n189\t\tvar skippedArchitectures []string\n190\t\tfor _, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm := range nodeAffinity.Platforms {\n191\t\t\tif !seenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] {\n192\t\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerm := corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{\n193\t\t\t\t\tWeight: nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight,\n194\t\t\t\t\tPreference: corev1.NodeSelectorTerm{\n195\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchExpressions: []corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n196\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n197\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n198\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n199\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tValues:   []string{nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture},\n200\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n201\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n202\t\t\t\t\t},\n203\t\t\t\t}\n204\t\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerms = append(preferredSchedulingTerms, preferredSchedulingTerm)\n205\t\t\t\tseenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] = true\n206\t\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was applied from this source\n207\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, true)\n208\t\t\t} else {\n209\t\t\t\tskippedArchitectures = append(skippedArchitectures, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture)\n210\t\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was skipped from this source\n211\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, false)\n212\t\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Preferred affinity for pod is already set\", \"Architecture\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, \"Weight\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, \"Pod.Name\", pod.Name, \"Pod.Namespace\", pod.Namespace, \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n213\t\t\t}\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\tif preferredSchedulingTerms != nil {\n217\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = append(\n218\t\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution, preferredSchedulingTerms...)\n219\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\tswitch {\n222\t\t// Case 1: All architectures from this config were successfully added (no duplicates)\n223\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures == nil:\n224\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredPredicateSetupMsg, configSource))\n225\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied all architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n226\t\n227\t\t// Case 2: Some architectures were added, but some were skipped due to duplicates\n228\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n229\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, skipped: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityWithDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n230\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied some architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n231\t\n232\t\t// Case 3: All architectures from this config were already set\n233\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms == nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n234\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePreferredAffinityDuplicates, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, architectures: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityAllDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n235\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"All architectures from configuration were already set\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n236\t\n237\t\t// Case 4: No architectures were provided in the config\n238\t\tdefault:\n239\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No architecture preferences provided in configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n240\t\t}\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// getExistingPreferredArchitectures finds all\n244\t// architectures that already have a preferred node affinity configured on the pod.\n245\tfunc (pod *Pod) getExistingPreferredArchitectures() map[string]bool {\n246\t\tseen := make(map[string]bool)\n247\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n248\t\t\treturn seen\n249\t\t}\n250\t\n251\t\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n252\t\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n253\t\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n254\t\t\t\t\tfor _, value := range expr.Values {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tseen[value] = true\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t}\n259\t\t}\n260\t\treturn seen\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// trackAffinitySource tracks which config sources attempted to set affinity for which architectures.\n264\t// This information is later used to build the annotation showing the full audit trail.\n265\tfunc (pod *Pod) trackAffinitySource(arch string, weight int32, source string, applied bool) {\n266\t\t// Get existing annotation or start fresh\n267\t\texistingAnnotation := \"\"\n268\t\tif pod.Annotations != nil {\n269\t\t\texistingAnnotation = pod.Annotations[utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation]\n270\t\t}\n271\t\n272\t\t// Format: arch:weight:source or arch:weight:source:skipped\n273\t\tvar newEntry string\n274\t\tif applied {\n275\t\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s\", arch, weight, source)\n276\t\t} else {\n277\t\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s:skipped\", arch, weight, source)\n278\t\t}\n279\t\n280\t\t// Check if this entry already exists to avoid duplicates on retries\n281\t\tif existingAnnotation != \"\" {\n282\t\t\tentries := strings.Split(existingAnnotation, \",\")\n283\t\t\tfor _, entry := range entries {\n284\t\t\t\tif entry == newEntry {\n285\t\t\t\t\t// Entry already exists, don't add it again\n286\t\t\t\t\treturn\n287\t\t\t\t}\n288\t\t\t}\n289\t\t}\n290\t\n291\t\t// Append to existing annotation with comma separator\n292\t\tif existingAnnotation == \"\" {\n293\t\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, newEntry)\n294\t\t} else {\n295\t\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, existingAnnotation+\",\"+newEntry)\n296\t\t}\n297\t}\n298\t\n299\t// setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture sets the node affinity for the pod to the fallback architecture.\n300\tfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(architecture string) {\n301\t\trequirement := corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n302\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n303\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n304\t\t\tValues:   []string{architecture},\n305\t\t}\n306\t\n307\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n308\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n312\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n313\t\t}\n314\t\n315\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n316\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n317\t\t}\n318\t\n319\t\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n320\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.FallbackArchitectureLabel, architecture)\n321\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareFallbackNodeAffinitySet,\n322\t\t\tArchitectureFallbackSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", architecture))\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\tfunc (pod *Pod) getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, error) {\n326\t\tarchitectures, err := pod.intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList)\n327\t\t// if an error occurs, we return an empty NodeSelectorRequirement and the error.\n328\t\tif err != nil {\n329\t\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{}, err\n330\t\t}\n331\t\n332\t\tif len(architectures) == 0 {\n333\t\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n334\t\t\t\tKey:      utils.NoSupportedArchLabel,\n335\t\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpExists,\n336\t\t\t}, nil\n337\t\t}\n338\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n339\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n340\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n341\t\t\tValues:   architectures,\n342\t\t}, nil\n343\t}\n344\t\n345\tfunc (pod *Pod) imagesNamesSet() sets.Set[containerImage] {\n346\t\timageNamesSet := sets.New[containerImage]()\n347\t\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.Containers, pod.Spec.InitContainers...) {\n348\t\t\timageNamesSet.Insert(containerImage{\n349\t\t\t\timageName: fmt.Sprintf(\"//%s\", container.Image),\n350\t\t\t\tskipCache: container.ImagePullPolicy == corev1.PullAlways,\n351\t\t\t})\n352\t\t}\n353\t\treturn imageNamesSet\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\t// inspect returns the list of supported architectures for the images used by the pod.\n357\t// if an error occurs, it returns the error and a nil slice of strings.\n358\tfunc (pod *Pod) intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (supportedArchitectures []string, err error) {\n359\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n360\t\timageNamesSet := pod.imagesNamesSet()\n361\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Images list for pod\", \"imageNamesSet\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%+v\", imageNamesSet))\n362\t\t// https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/v1.11.1/cmd/skopeo/inspect.go#L72\n363\t\t// Iterate over the images, get their architectures and intersect (as in set intersection) them each other\n364\t\tvar supportedArchitecturesSet sets.Set[string]\n365\t\tnowExternal := time.Now()\n366\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(nowExternal, metrics.TimeToInspectPodImages)\n367\t\tfor imageContainer := range imageNamesSet {\n368\t\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Checking image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName,\n369\t\t\t\t\"skipCache (imagePullPolicy==Always)\", imageContainer.skipCache)\n370\t\t\t// We are collecting the time to inspect the image here to avoid implementing a metric in each of the\n371\t\t\t// cache implementations.\n372\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n373\t\t\tcurrentImageSupportedArchitectures, err := imageInspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(pod.Ctx(),\n374\t\t\t\timageContainer.imageName, imageContainer.skipCache, pullSecretDataList)\n375\t\t\tutils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImage)\n376\t\t\tif err != nil {\n377\t\t\t\tlog.V(1).Error(err, \"Error inspecting the image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName)\n378\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n379\t\t\t}\n380\t\t\tif supportedArchitecturesSet == nil {\n381\t\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = currentImageSupportedArchitectures\n382\t\t\t} else {\n383\t\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = supportedArchitecturesSet.Intersection(currentImageSupportedArchitectures)\n384\t\t\t}\n385\t\t}\n386\t\treturn sets.List(supportedArchitecturesSet), nil\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\tfunc (pod *Pod) maxRetries() bool {\n390\t\tif pod.Labels == nil {\n391\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t}\n393\t\tv, err := strconv.ParseInt(pod.Labels[utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel], 10, 32)\n394\t\tif err != nil {\n395\t\t\treturn true\n396\t\t}\n397\t\treturn v >= MaxRetryCount\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// ensureArchitectureLabels adds labels for the given requirement to the pod. Labels are added to indicate\n401\t// the supported architectures and index pods by architecture or by whether they support more than one architecture.\n402\t// In this case, single-architecture is meant as a pod that supports only one architecture: all the images in the pod\n403\t// may be manifest-list, but the intersection of the architectures is a single value.\n404\tfunc (pod *Pod) ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n405\t\tif requirement.Values == nil {\n406\t\t\treturn\n407\t\t}\n408\t\tswitch len(requirement.Values) {\n409\t\tcase 0:\n410\t\t\t// if the requirement has no values, we set the NoSupportedArchLabel as a label for the node. That's a dummy\n411\t\t\t// and non-available-by-default label that we use to prevent the pod from being scheduled when it cannot run all\n412\t\t\t// the containers in at least one architecture.\n413\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NoSupportedArchLabel, \"\")\n414\t\tcase 1:\n415\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SingleArchLabel, \"\")\n416\t\tdefault:\n417\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.MultiArchLabel, \"\")\n418\t\t}\n419\t\tvalidArchitectures := utils.AllSupportedArchitecturesSet()\n420\t\tfor _, value := range requirement.Values {\n421\t\t\tif validArchitectures.Has(value) {\n422\t\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ArchLabelValue(value), \"\")\n423\t\t\t}\n424\t\t}\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\t// shouldIgnorePod returns true if the pod should be ignored by the operator.\n428\t// The operator should ignore the pods in the following cases:\n429\t// - the pod is in the same namespace as the operator\n430\t// - the pod is in a namespace with prefix kube-\n431\t// - the pod has a node name set\n432\t// - the pod has a node selector that matches the control plane nodes\n433\t// - the pod is owned by a DaemonSet\n434\t// - the pod has required architecture affinity configured AND:\n435\t//   - preferred affinity is already configured, OR\n436\t//   - both CPPC and all matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin disabled\n437\tfunc (pod *Pod) shouldIgnorePod(cppc *v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n438\t\treturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n439\t\t\tpod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" || pod.HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() || pod.IsFromDaemonSet() ||\n440\t\t\tpod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() &&\n441\t\t\t\t(pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() ||\n442\t\t\t\t\t(!cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) && !pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs)))\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has already a nodeSelector for the architecture label\n446\t// or if all the nodeSelectorTerms in the nodeAffinity field have a matchExpression for the architecture label.\n447\tfunc (pod *Pod) isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n448\t\t// if the pod has the nodeSelector field set for the kubernetes.io/arch label, we ignore it.\n449\t\t// in fact, the nodeSelector field is ANDed with the nodeAffinity field, and we want to give the user the main control, if they\n450\t\t// manually set a predicate for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n451\t\t// The same behavior is implemented below within each\n452\t\t// nodeSelectorTerm's MatchExpressions field.\n453\t\tfor key := range pod.Spec.NodeSelector {\n454\t\t\tif key == utils.ArchLabel {\n455\t\t\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n456\t\t\t\treturn true\n457\t\t\t}\n458\t\t}\n459\t\t// Check if Affinity, NodeAffinity, or RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution is nil\n460\t\t// If any of these are nil, assume there are no specific node selector terms to check, so return true.\n461\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n462\t\t\treturn false\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\t// Iterate over NodeSelectorTerms (terms are ORed)\n466\t\tfor _, nodeSelectorTerm := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms {\n467\t\t\t// Assume the architecture label is not present\n468\t\t\thasArchLabel := false\n469\t\n470\t\t\t// Check all match expressions within the current NodeSelectorTerm (expressions are ANDed)\n471\t\t\tfor _, matchExpression := range nodeSelectorTerm.MatchExpressions {\n472\t\t\t\t// If we find the architecture label, mark it as found\n473\t\t\t\tif matchExpression.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n474\t\t\t\t\thasArchLabel = true\n475\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n476\t\t\t\t}\n477\t\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\t\t// If one of the NodeSelectorTerms does not have the architecture label, return false\n480\t\t\tif !hasArchLabel {\n481\t\t\t\treturn false\n482\t\t\t}\n483\t\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t// If all NodeSelectorTerms contain the architecture label, return true\n487\t\treturn true\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\tfunc (pod *Pod) publishIgnorePod() {\n491\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n492\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"The pod has the nodeSelector or all the nodeAffinityTerms set for the kubernetes.io/arch label. Ignoring the pod...\")\n493\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)\n494\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePredicatesConflict, ArchitecturePredicatesConflictMsg)\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\tfunc (pod *Pod) handleError(err error, s string) {\n498\t\tif err == nil {\n499\t\t\treturn\n500\t\t}\n501\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n502\t\tmetrics.FailedInspectionCounter.Inc()\n503\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n504\t\trunes := []rune(errMsg)\n505\t\tif len(runes) > 256 {\n506\t\t\terrMsg = string(runes[:256])\n507\t\t}\n508\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, \"\")\n509\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, errMsg)\n510\t\tpod.EnsureAndIncrementLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel)\n511\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, ImageArchitectureInspectionErrorMsg+errMsg)\n512\t\tlog.Error(err, s)\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has a MatchExpression in the PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution\n516\t// that matches kubernetes.io/arch\n517\tfunc (pod *Pod) isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n518\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil ||\n519\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\n520\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n521\t\t\treturn false\n522\t\t}\n523\t\n524\t\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n525\t\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n526\t\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n527\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n528\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t}\n531\t\treturn false\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t// filterMatchingPPCs returns only PPCs whose label selector matches this pod.\n535\t// This is done once per reconcile/webhook call to avoid redundant selector evaluations.\n536\tfunc (pod *Pod) filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList *v1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList) []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig {\n537\t\tvar matching []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig\n538\t\n539\t\tfor _, ppc := range ppcList.Items {\n540\t\t\tselector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(ppc.Spec.LabelSelector)\n541\t\t\tif err != nil {\n542\t\t\t\t// Invalid selector means it doesn't match - skip\n543\t\t\t\tcontinue\n544\t\t\t}\n545\t\n546\t\t\t// Empty selector (Nothing()) or matching selector\n547\t\t\tif selector == labels.Nothing() || selector.Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\n548\t\t\t\tmatching = append(matching, ppc)\n549\t\t\t}\n550\t\t}\n551\t\n552\t\treturn matching\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\t// hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin checks if any of the matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin enabled.\n556\t// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\n557\tfunc (pod *Pod) hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n558\t\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n559\t\t\tif ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n560\t\t\t\treturn true\n561\t\t\t}\n562\t\t}\n563\t\treturn false\n564\t}\n565\t",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:28:48.387Z"
  },

    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_vrtx_01TdSvuWVxyWSa1BSsY9REXy",
    "tool_name": "Read",
    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage v1alpha1\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n21\t\n22\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n23\t)\n24\t\n25\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n26\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n27\t\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n28\t\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n29\t\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n30\t\t// +optional\n31\t\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n32\t\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n33\t\n34\t\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n35\t\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n36\t\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n37\t\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n38\t\t// +optional\n39\t\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n43\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n44\t\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n45\t\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n49\t// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n50\t// Creating the object enables the operand.\n51\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n52\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n53\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\n54\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n55\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n56\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n57\t\n58\t\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n59\t\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n63\t\n64\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n65\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n66\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n67\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n68\t\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n69\t}\n70\t",

    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage v1alpha1\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/conversion\"\n21\t\n22\t\tmultiarchv1beta1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n23\t)\n24\t\n25\tconst FallbackArchAnnotation = \"multiarch.openshift.io/fallback-architecture\"\n26\t\n27\t// ConvertTo converts this ClusterPodPlacementConfig to the Hub version v1beta1.\n28\tfunc (src *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) ConvertTo(dstRaw conversion.Hub) error {\n29\t\tdst := dstRaw.(*multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig)\n30\t\n31\t\t// ObjectMeta\n32\t\tdst.ObjectMeta = src.ObjectMeta\n33\t\n34\t\t// Spec\n35\t\tdst.Spec.LogVerbosity = src.Spec.LogVerbosity\n36\t\tdst.Spec.NamespaceSelector = src.Spec.NamespaceSelector\n37\t\t// Restore FallbackArchitecture from the annotation, if present.\n38\t\t// This ensures the value is preserved across API version conversions.\n39\t\tif arch, ok := src.Annotations[FallbackArchAnnotation]; ok {\n40\t\t\tdst.Spec.FallbackArchitecture = arch\n41\t\t}\n42\t\n43\t\t// Status\n44\t\tdst.Status.Conditions = src.Status.Conditions\n45\t\n46\t\t// +kubebuilder:docs-gen:collapse=rote conversion\n47\t\treturn nil\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t/*\n51\tConvertFrom is expected to modify its receiver to contain the converted object.\n52\tMost of the conversion is straightforward copying, except for converting our changed field.\n53\t*/\n54\t\n55\t// ConvertFrom converts from the Hub version (v1beta1) to this.\n56\tfunc (dst *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) ConvertFrom(srcRaw conversion.Hub) error {\n57\t\tsrc := srcRaw.(*multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig)\n58\t\n59\t\t// ObjectMeta\n60\t\tdst.ObjectMeta = src.ObjectMeta\n61\t\t// v1alpha1 does not have the FallbackArchitecture field.\n62\t\t// Preserve the value in an annotation to avoid data loss during\n63\t\t// v1beta1 -> v1alpha1 -> v1beta1 round-trip conversions.\n64\t\tif dst.Annotations == nil {\n65\t\t\tdst.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n66\t\t}\n67\t\tif src.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != \"\" {\n68\t\t\tdst.Annotations[FallbackArchAnnotation] = src.Spec.FallbackArchitecture\n69\t\t} else {\n70\t\t\tdelete(dst.Annotations, FallbackArchAnnotation)\n71\t\t}\n72\t\n73\t\t// Spec\n74\t\tdst.Spec.LogVerbosity = src.Spec.LogVerbosity\n75\t\tdst.Spec.NamespaceSelector = src.Spec.NamespaceSelector\n76\t\n77\t\t// Status\n78\t\tdst.Status.Conditions = src.Status.Conditions\n79\t\n80\t\tif dst.Annotations == nil {\n81\t\t\tdst.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n82\t\t}\n83\t\n84\t\treturn nil\n85\t}\n86\t",
    "is_error": false,

{"type": "user", "message": {"role": "user", "content": "You are a Go engineer working on a Kubernetes operator.\nThe repository is located at /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment.\nChange your working directory to that repository before beginning.\n\nTask:\nAdd a new optional int32 field MaxImageInspectionRetries to ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec\nin api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go. Apply kubebuilder markers:\nminimum value of 1, maximum value of 20, and a default of 5.\n\nThen update the image inspection retry logic in internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go\nto read this value from the CPPC informer singleton (via pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig/)\ninstead of any hardcoded constant.\n\nAlso update the v1alpha1 conversion so the new field round-trips correctly across API versions.\n\nFinally, state which make targets must be run after the API change and in what order.\n\n\nProduce a complete implementation. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01AshEy2mQrxQsuc2oFCtGRD","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage v1beta1\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"fmt\"\n21\t\t\"strings\"\n22\t\n23\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n24\t\n25\t\t\"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/v1helpers\"\n26\t\n27\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n28\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n29\t)\n30\t\n31\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n32\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n33\t\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n34\t\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n35\t\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n36\t\t// +optional\n37\t\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n38\t\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n39\t\n40\t\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n41\t\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n42\t\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n43\t\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n44\t\t// +optional\n45\t\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n46\t\n47\t\t// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.\n48\t\t// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.\n49\t\t// +optional\n50\t\tPlugins *plugins.Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n51\t\n52\t\t// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n53\t\t// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture\n54\t\t// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n55\t\t// +optional\n56\t\t// +kubebuilder:default=\"\"\n57\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;\"\"\n58\t\tFallbackArchitecture string `json:\"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty\"`\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n62\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n63\t\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n64\t\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n65\t\n66\t\t// The following fields are used to derive the conditions. They are not exposed to the user.\n67\t\t//nolint:revive // controller-gen requires json tags on all fields, even unexported ones\n68\t\tavailable                                bool `json:\"-\"`\n69\t\tprogressing                              bool `json:\"-\"`\n70\t\tdegraded                                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n71\t\tdeprovisioning                           bool `json:\"-\"`\n72\t\tpodPlacementControllerNotReady           bool `json:\"-\"`\n73\t\tpodPlacementWebhookNotReady              bool `json:\"-\"`\n74\t\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable bool `json:\"-\"`\n75\t\tcanDeployMutatingWebhook                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsReady() bool {\n79\t\treturn s.available\n80\t}\n81\t\n82\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsProgressing() bool {\n83\t\treturn s.progressing\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDegraded() bool {\n87\t\treturn s.degraded\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDeprovisioning() bool {\n91\t\treturn s.deprovisioning\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementControllerNotReady() bool {\n95\t\treturn s.podPlacementControllerNotReady\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {\n99\t\treturn s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {\n103\t\treturn s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {\n107\t\treturn s.canDeployMutatingWebhook\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.\n111\t// The build Conditions are:\n112\t//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning\n113\t//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted\n114\t//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist\n115\t//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date\n116\t//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date\n117\t//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.\n118\t//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).\n119\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(\n120\t\tpodPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,\n121\t\tpodPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,\n122\t\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,\n123\t\tdeprovisioning bool) {\n124\t\ts.deprovisioning = deprovisioning\n125\t\t// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration\n126\t\ts.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable\n127\t\t// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date\n128\t\ts.podPlacementControllerNotReady = !podPlacementControllerAvailable || !podPlacementControllerUpToDate\n129\t\ts.podPlacementWebhookNotReady = !podPlacementWebhookAvailable || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate\n130\t\t// if all the components exist and have at least one replica ready\n131\t\ts.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable\n132\t\t// if some \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/v1helpers\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n)\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n\t// +optional\n\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.\n\t// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.\n\t// +optional\n\tPlugins *plugins.Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture\n\t// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=\"\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;\"\"\n\tFallbackArchitecture string `json:\"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// The following fields are used to derive the conditions. They are not exposed to the user.\n\t//nolint:revive // controller-gen requires json tags on all fields, even unexported ones\n\tavailable                                bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tprogressing                              bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tdegraded                                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tdeprovisioning                           bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tpodPlacementControllerNotReady           bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tpodPlacementWebhookNotReady              bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tcanDeployMutatingWebhook                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsReady() bool {\n\treturn s.available\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsProgressing() bool {\n\treturn s.progressing\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDegraded() bool {\n\treturn s.degraded\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDeprovisioning() bool {\n\treturn s.deprovisioning\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementControllerNotReady() bool {\n\treturn s.podPlacementControllerNotReady\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {\n\treturn s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {\n\treturn s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {\n\treturn s.canDeployMutatingWebhook\n}\n\n// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.\n// The build Conditions are:\n//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning\n//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted\n//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist\n//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date\n//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date\n//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.\n//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(\n\tpodPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,\n\tpodPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,\n\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,\n\tdeprovisioning bool) {\n\ts.deprovisioning = deprovisioning\n\t// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration\n\ts.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable\n\t// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date\n\ts.podPlacementControllerNotReady = !podPlacementControllerAvailable || !podPlacementControllerUpToDate\n\ts.podPlacementWebhookNotReady = !podPlacementWebhookAvailable || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate\n\t// if all the components exist and have at least one replica ready\n\ts.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable\n\t// if some components are not available (no replicas)\n\ts.degraded = !s.available && !s.deprovisioning // degraded will not track deprovisioning\n\t// allow the deployment of the mutating webhook configuration if the pod placement controller and webhook are available\n\t// (at least one replica)\n\ts.canDeployMutatingWebhook = podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable && !s.deprovisioning\n\ts.progressing = (!podPlacementControllerUpToDate || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate || !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable) && !s.deprovisioning\n\ts.buildConditions()\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) buildConditions() {\n\tif s.Conditions == nil {\n\t\ts.Conditions = []metav1.Condition{}\n\t}\n\treason := \"\"\n\tif s.podPlacementControllerNotReady {\n\t\treason += PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType\n\t}\n\tif s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady {\n\t\treason += PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType\n\t}\n\tif s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable {\n\t\treason += MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n\t}\n\tif reason == \"\" {\n\t\treason = AllComponentsReady\n\t}\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    AvailableType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.available),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ReadyMsg, notFromBool(s.available), strings.TrimSpace(notFromBool(s.available))),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    ProgressingType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.progressing),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ProgressingMsg, notFromBool(s.progressing)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    DegradedType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.degraded),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.degraded)), DegradedType),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DegradedMsg, notFromBool(s.degraded)),\n\t})\n\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix := \"\"\n\tif s.deprovisioning {\n\t\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix = PendingDeprovisioningMsg\n\t}\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    DeprovisioningType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.deprovisioning),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.deprovisioning)), DeprovisioningType),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DeprovisioningMsg, notFromBool(s.deprovisioning), deprovisinoingMessagePostfix),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementControllerNotReady),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementController%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady))),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementControllerRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementWebhook%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady))),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementWebhookRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(MutatingWebhookConfigurationReadyMsg, notFromBool(!s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable)),\n\t})\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n// Creating the object enables the operand.\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n// +kubebuilder:storageversion\n// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Available,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Progressing,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Degraded,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Degraded\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Since,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].lastTransitionTime,type=date\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Status,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].reason,type=string\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) PluginsEnabled(plugin common.Plugin) bool {\n\tif c.Spec.Plugins != nil {\n\t\treturn c.Spec.Plugins.PluginEnabled(plugin)\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n}\n\nfunc conditionFromBool(b bool) metav1.ConditionStatus {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn metav1.ConditionTrue\n\t}\n\treturn metav1.ConditionFalse\n}\n\nfunc notFromBool(b bool) string {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"not \"\n}\n\nfunc trimAndCapitalize(s string) string {\n\ttrimmed := strings.TrimSpace(s)\n\tif len(trimmed) == 0 {\n\t\treturn trimmed\n\t}\n\treturn strings.ToUpper(string(trimmed[0])) + trimmed[1:]\n}\n","numLines":263,"startLine":1,"totalLines":263}}}
