{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01NAWF1L2N1W1PyZ2YPugNeH","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage podplacement\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"fmt\"\n22\t\t\"strconv\"\n23\t\t\"strings\"\n24\t\t\"time\"\n25\t\n26\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n27\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n28\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n29\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n30\t\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n31\t\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n32\t\n33\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n34\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n35\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n36\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics\"\n37\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image\"\n38\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/models\"\n39\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\tvar (\n43\t\t// imageInspectionCache is the facade singleton used to inspect images. It is defined here to facilitate testing.\n44\t\timageInspectionCache image.ICache = image.FacadeSingleton()\n45\t)\n46\t\n47\tconst MaxRetryCount = 5\n48\t\n49\ttype containerImage struct {\n50\t\timageName string\n51\t\tskipCache bool\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\ttype Pod struct {\n55\t\tmodels.Pod\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\tfunc newPod(pod *corev1.Pod, ctx context.Context, recorder record.EventRecorder) *Pod {\n59\t\treturn &Pod{\n60\t\t\tPod: *models.NewPod(pod, ctx, recorder),\n61\t\t}\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t// HasSchedulingGate checks if the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\n65\tfunc (pod *Pod) HasSchedulingGate() bool {\n66\t\treturn pod.HasGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\t// RemoveSchedulingGate removes the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName from the pod.\n70\tfunc (pod *Pod) RemoveSchedulingGate() {\n71\t\tpod.RemoveGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n72\t\t// The scheduling gate is removed. We also add a label to the pod to indicate that the scheduling gate was removed\n73\t\t// and this pod was processed by the operator. That's useful for testing and debugging, but also gives the user\n74\t\t// an indication that the pod was processed by the operator.\n75\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SchedulingGateLabel, utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueRemoved)\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t// ensureSchedulingGate ensures that the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\n79\tfunc (pod *Pod) ensureSchedulingGate() {\n80\t\tpod.AddGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\t// getPodImagePullSecrets returns the names of the image pull secrets used by the pod.\n84\tfunc (pod *Pod) getPodImagePullSecrets() []string {\n85\t\tif pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets == nil {\n86\t\t\t// If the imagePullSecrets array is nil, return emptylist\n87\t\t\treturn []string{}\n88\t\t}\n89\t\tvar secretRefs = make([]string, len(pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets))\n90\t\tfor i, secret := range pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets {\n91\t\t\tsecretRefs[i] = secret.Name\n92\t\t}\n93\t\treturn secretRefs\n94\t}\n95\t\n96\t// SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement wraps the logic to set the nodeAffinity for the pod.\n97\t// It verifies first that no nodeSelector field is set for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n98\t// Then, it computes the intersection of the architectures supported by the images used by the pod via pod.getArchitecturePredicate.\n99\t// Finally, it initializes the nodeAffinity for the pod and set it to the computed requirement via the pod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity method.\n100\tfunc (pod *Pod) SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (bool, error) {\n101\t\tif pod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n102\t\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n103\t\t\treturn false, nil\n104\t\t}\n105\t\trequirement, err := pod.getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList)\n106\t\tif err != nil {\n107\t\t\treturn false, err\n108\t\t}\n109\t\tpod.EnsureNoLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel)\n110\t\tif len(requirement.Values) == 0 {\n111\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, NoSupportedArchitecturesFound, NoSupportedArchitecturesFoundMsg)\n112\t\t}\n113\t\tpod.ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement)\n114\t\n115\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n116\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n117\t\t}\n118\t\n119\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n120\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n121\t\t}\n122\t\n123\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n124\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n128\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n129\t\t\tArchitecturePredicateSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(requirement.Values, \", \")))\n130\t\treturn true, nil\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// setRequiredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the given requirement based on the rules in\n134\t// the sig-scheduling's KEP-3838: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/3838-pod-mutable-scheduling-directives.\n135\tfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n136\t\t// the .requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms are ORed\n137\t\tif len(pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\n138\t\t\t// We create a new array of NodeSelectorTerm of length 1 so that we can always iterate it in the next.\n139\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorTerm, 1)\n140\t\t}\n141\t\tnodeSelectorTerms := pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms\n142\t\n143\t\t// The expressions within the nodeSelectorTerms are ANDed.\n144\t\t// Therefore, we iterate over the nodeSelectorTerms and add an expression to each of the terms to verify the\n145\t\t// kubernetes.io/arch label has compatible values.\n146\t\t// Note that the NodeSelectorTerms will always be long at least 1, because we (re-)created it with size 1 above if it was nil (or having 0 length).\n147\t\tvar skipMatchExpressionPatch bool\n148\t\tfor i := range nodeSelectorTerms {\n149\t\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = false\n150\t\t\tif nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions == nil {\n151\t\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, 0, 1)\n152\t\t\t}\n153\t\t\t// Check if the nodeSelectorTerm already has a matchExpression for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n154\t\t\t// if yes, we ignore to add it.\n155\t\t\tfor _, expression := range nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions {\n156\t\t\t\tif expression.Key == requirement.Key {\n157\t\t\t\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = true\n158\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n159\t\t\t\t}\n160\t\t\t}\n161\t\t\t// if skipMatchExpressionPatch is true, we skip to add the matchExpression so that conflictual matchExpressions provided by the user are not overwritten.\n162\t\t\tif !skipMatchExpressionPatch {\n163\t\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = append(nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions, requirement)\n164\t\t\t}\n165\t\t}\n166\t\t// if the nodeSelectorTerms were patched at least once, we set the nodeAffinity label to the set value, to keep\n167\t\t// track of the fact that the nodeAffinity was patched by the operator.\n168\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the preferences given in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n172\t// The configSource parameter identifies which configuration is setting the preferences (e.g., \"ClusterPodPlacementConfig\" or \"PodPlacementConfig/my-ppc\").\n173\tfunc (pod *Pod) SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(nodeAffinity *plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource string) {\n174\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n175\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n176\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n177\t\t}\n178\t\n179\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n180\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n181\t\t}\n182\t\n183\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n184\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{}\n185\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\tseenArchitectures := pod.getExistingPreferredArchitectures()\n188\t\tvar preferredSchedulingTerms []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm\n189\t\tvar skippedArchitectures []string\n190\t\tfor _, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm := range nodeAffinity.Platforms {\n191\t\t\tif !seenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] {\n192\t\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerm := corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{\n193\t\t\t\t\tWeight: nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight,\n194\t\t\t\t\tPreference: corev1.NodeSelectorTerm{\n195\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchExpressions: []corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n196\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n197\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n198\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n199\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tValues:   []string{nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture},\n200\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n201\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n202\t\t\t\t\t},\n203\t\t\t\t}\n204\t\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerms = append(preferredSchedulingTerms, preferredSchedulingTerm)\n205\t\t\t\tseenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] = true\n206\t\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was applied from this source\n207\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, true)\n208\t\t\t} else {\n209\t\t\t\tskippedArchitectures = append(skippedArchitectures, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture)\n210\t\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was skipped from this source\n211\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, false)\n212\t\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Preferred affinity for pod is already set\", \"Architecture\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, \"Weight\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, \"Pod.Name\", pod.Name, \"Pod.Namespace\", pod.Namespace, \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n213\t\t\t}\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\tif preferredSchedulingTerms != nil {\n217\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = append(\n218\t\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution, preferredSchedulingTerms...)\n219\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\tswitch {\n222\t\t// Case 1: All architectures from this config were successfully added (no duplicates)\n223\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures == nil:\n224\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredPredicateSetupMsg, configSource))\n225\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied all architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n226\t\n227\t\t// Case 2: Some architectures were added, but some were skipped due to duplicates\n228\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n229\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, skipped: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityWithDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n230\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied some architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n231\t\n232\t\t// Case 3: All architectures from this config were already set\n233\t\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms == nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n234\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePreferredAffinityDuplicates, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, architectures: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityAllDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n235\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"All architectures from configuration were already set\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n236\t\n237\t\t// Case 4: No architectures were provided in the config\n238\t\tdefault:\n239\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No architecture preferences provided in configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n240\t\t}\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// getExistingPreferredArchitectures finds all\n244\t// architectures that already have a preferred node affinity configured on the pod.\n245\tfunc (pod *Pod) getExistingPreferredArchitectures() map[string]bool {\n246\t\tseen := make(map[string]bool)\n247\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n248\t\t\treturn seen\n249\t\t}\n250\t\n251\t\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n252\t\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n253\t\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n254\t\t\t\t\tfor _, value := range expr.Values {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tseen[value] = true\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t}\n259\t\t}\n260\t\treturn seen\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// trackAffinitySource tracks which config sources attempted to set affinity for which architectures.\n264\t// This information is later used to build the annotation showing the full audit trail.\n265\tfunc (pod *Pod) trackAffinitySource(arch string, weight int32, source string, applied bool) {\n266\t\t// Get existing annotation or start fresh\n267\t\texistingAnnotation := \"\"\n268\t\tif pod.Annotations != nil {\n269\t\t\texistingAnnotation = pod.Annotations[utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation]\n270\t\t}\n271\t\n272\t\t// Format: arch:weight:source or arch:weight:source:skipped\n273\t\tvar newEntry string\n274\t\tif applied {\n275\t\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s\", arch, weight, source)\n276\t\t} else {\n277\t\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s:skipped\", arch, weight, source)\n278\t\t}\n279\t\n280\t\t// Check if this entry already exists to avoid duplicates on retries\n281\t\tif existingAnnotation != \"\" {\n282\t\t\tentries := strings.Split(existingAnnotation, \",\")\n283\t\t\tfor _, entry := range entries {\n284\t\t\t\tif entry == newEntry {\n285\t\t\t\t\t// Entry already exists, don't add it again\n286\t\t\t\t\treturn\n287\t\t\t\t}\n288\t\t\t}\n289\t\t}\n290\t\n291\t\t// Append to existing annotation with comma separator\n292\t\tif existingAnnotation == \"\" {\n293\t\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, newEntry)\n294\t\t} else {\n295\t\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, existingAnnotation+\",\"+newEntry)\n296\t\t}\n297\t}\n298\t\n299\t// setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture sets the node affinity for the pod to the fallback architecture.\n300\tfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(architecture string) {\n301\t\trequirement := corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n302\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n303\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n304\t\t\tValues:   []string{architecture},\n305\t\t}\n306\t\n307\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n308\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n312\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n313\t\t}\n314\t\n315\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n316\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n317\t\t}\n318\t\n319\t\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n320\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.FallbackArchitectureLabel, architecture)\n321\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareFallbackNodeAffinitySet,\n322\t\t\tArchitectureFallbackSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", architecture))\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\tfunc (pod *Pod) getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, error) {\n326\t\tarchitectures, err := pod.intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList)\n327\t\t// if an error occurs, we return an empty NodeSelectorRequirement and the error.\n328\t\tif err != nil {\n329\t\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{}, err\n330\t\t}\n331\t\n332\t\tif len(architectures) == 0 {\n333\t\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n334\t\t\t\tKey:      utils.NoSupportedArchLabel,\n335\t\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpExists,\n336\t\t\t}, nil\n337\t\t}\n338\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n339\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n340\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n341\t\t\tValues:   architectures,\n342\t\t}, nil\n343\t}\n344\t\n345\tfunc (pod *Pod) imagesNamesSet() sets.Set[containerImage] {\n346\t\timageNamesSet := sets.New[containerImage]()\n347\t\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.Containers, pod.Spec.InitContainers...) {\n348\t\t\timageNamesSet.Insert(containerImage{\n349\t\t\t\timageName: fmt.Sprintf(\"//%s\", container.Image),\n350\t\t\t\tskipCache: container.ImagePullPolicy == corev1.PullAlways,\n351\t\t\t})\n352\t\t}\n353\t\treturn imageNamesSet\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\t// inspect returns the list of supported architectures for the images used by the pod.\n357\t// if an error occurs, it returns the error and a nil slice of strings.\n358\tfunc (pod *Pod) intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (supportedArchitectures []string, err error) {\n359\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n360\t\timageNamesSet := pod.imagesNamesSet()\n361\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Images list for pod\", \"imageNamesSet\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%+v\", imageNamesSet))\n362\t\t// https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/v1.11.1/cmd/skopeo/inspect.go#L72\n363\t\t// Iterate over the images, get their architectures and intersect (as in set intersection) them each other\n364\t\tvar supportedArchitecturesSet sets.Set[string]\n365\t\tnowExternal := time.Now()\n366\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(nowExternal, metrics.TimeToInspectPodImages)\n367\t\tfor imageContainer := range imageNamesSet {\n368\t\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Checking image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName,\n369\t\t\t\t\"skipCache (imagePullPolicy==Always)\", imageContainer.skipCache)\n370\t\t\t// We are collecting the time to inspect the image here to avoid implementing a metric in each of the\n371\t\t\t// cache implementations.\n372\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n373\t\t\tcurrentImageSupportedArchitectures, err := imageInspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(pod.Ctx(),\n374\t\t\t\timageContainer.imageName, imageContainer.skipCache, pullSecretDataList)\n375\t\t\tutils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImage)\n376\t\t\tif err != nil {\n377\t\t\t\tlog.V(1).Error(err, \"Error inspecting the image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName)\n378\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n379\t\t\t}\n380\t\t\tif supportedArchitecturesSet == nil {\n381\t\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = currentImageSupportedArchitectures\n382\t\t\t} else {\n383\t\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = supportedArchitecturesSet.Intersection(currentImageSupportedArchitectures)\n384\t\t\t}\n385\t\t}\n386\t\treturn sets.List(supportedArchitecturesSet), nil\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\tfunc (pod *Pod) maxRetries() bool {\n390\t\tif pod.Labels == nil {\n391\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t}\n393\t\tv, err := strconv.ParseInt(pod.Labels[utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel], 10, 32)\n394\t\tif err != nil {\n395\t\t\treturn true\n396\t\t}\n397\t\treturn v >= MaxRetryCount\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// ensureArchitectureLabels adds labels for the given requirement to the pod. Labels are added to indicate\n401\t// the supported architectures and index pods by architecture or by whether they support more than one architecture.\n402\t// In this case, single-architecture is meant as a pod that supports only one architecture: all the images in the pod\n403\t// may be manifest-list, but the intersection of the architectures is a single value.\n404\tfunc (pod *Pod) ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n405\t\tif requirement.Values == nil {\n406\t\t\treturn\n407\t\t}\n408\t\tswitch len(requirement.Values) {\n409\t\tcase 0:\n410\t\t\t// if the requirement has no values, we set the NoSupportedArchLabel as a label for the node. That's a dummy\n411\t\t\t// and non-available-by-default label that we use to prevent the pod from being scheduled when it cannot run all\n412\t\t\t// the containers in at least one architecture.\n413\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NoSupportedArchLabel, \"\")\n414\t\tcase 1:\n415\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SingleArchLabel, \"\")\n416\t\tdefault:\n417\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.MultiArchLabel, \"\")\n418\t\t}\n419\t\tvalidArchitectures := utils.AllSupportedArchitecturesSet()\n420\t\tfor _, value := range requirement.Values {\n421\t\t\tif validArchitectures.Has(value) {\n422\t\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ArchLabelValue(value), \"\")\n423\t\t\t}\n424\t\t}\n425\t}\n426\t\n427\t// shouldIgnorePod returns true if the pod should be ignored by the operator.\n428\t// The operator should ignore the pods in the following cases:\n429\t// - the pod is in the same namespace as the operator\n430\t// - the pod is in a namespace with prefix kube-\n431\t// - the pod has a node name set\n432\t// - the pod has a node selector that matches the control plane nodes\n433\t// - the pod is owned by a DaemonSet\n434\t// - the pod has required architecture affinity configured AND:\n435\t//   - preferred affinity is already configured, OR\n436\t//   - both CPPC and all matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin disabled\n437\tfunc (pod *Pod) shouldIgnorePod(cppc *v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n438\t\treturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n439\t\t\tpod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" || pod.HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() || pod.IsFromDaemonSet() ||\n440\t\t\tpod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() &&\n441\t\t\t\t(pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() ||\n442\t\t\t\t\t(!cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) && !pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs)))\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has already a nodeSelector for the architecture label\n446\t// or if all the nodeSelectorTerms in the nodeAffinity field have a matchExpression for the architecture label.\n447\tfunc (pod *Pod) isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n448\t\t// if the pod has the nodeSelector field set for the kubernetes.io/arch label, we ignore it.\n449\t\t// in fact, the nodeSelector field is ANDed with the nodeAffinity field, and we want to give the user the main control, if they\n450\t\t// manually set a predicate for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n451\t\t// The same behavior is implemented below within each\n452\t\t// nodeSelectorTerm's MatchExpressions field.\n453\t\tfor key := range pod.Spec.NodeSelector {\n454\t\t\tif key == utils.ArchLabel {\n455\t\t\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n456\t\t\t\treturn true\n457\t\t\t}\n458\t\t}\n459\t\t// Check if Affinity, NodeAffinity, or RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution is nil\n460\t\t// If any of these are nil, assume there are no specific node selector terms to check, so return true.\n461\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n462\t\t\treturn false\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\t// Iterate over NodeSelectorTerms (terms are ORed)\n466\t\tfor _, nodeSelectorTerm := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms {\n467\t\t\t// Assume the architecture label is not present\n468\t\t\thasArchLabel := false\n469\t\n470\t\t\t// Check all match expressions within the current NodeSelectorTerm (expressions are ANDed)\n471\t\t\tfor _, matchExpression := range nodeSelectorTerm.MatchExpressions {\n472\t\t\t\t// If we find the architecture label, mark it as found\n473\t\t\t\tif matchExpression.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n474\t\t\t\t\thasArchLabel = true\n475\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n476\t\t\t\t}\n477\t\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\t\t// If one of the NodeSelectorTerms does not have the architecture label, return false\n480\t\t\tif !hasArchLabel {\n481\t\t\t\treturn false\n482\t\t\t}\n483\t\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t// If all NodeSelectorTerms contain the architecture label, return true\n487\t\treturn true\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\tfunc (pod *Pod) publishIgnorePod() {\n491\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n492\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"The pod has the nodeSelector or all the nodeAffinityTerms set for the kubernetes.io/arch label. Ignoring the pod...\")\n493\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)\n494\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePredicatesConflict, ArchitecturePredicatesConflictMsg)\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\tfunc (pod *Pod) handleError(err error, s string) {\n498\t\tif err == nil {\n499\t\t\treturn\n500\t\t}\n501\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n502\t\tmetrics.FailedInspectionCounter.Inc()\n503\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n504\t\trunes := []rune(errMsg)\n505\t\tif len(runes) > 256 {\n506\t\t\terrMsg = string(runes[:256])\n507\t\t}\n508\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, \"\")\n509\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, errMsg)\n510\t\tpod.EnsureAndIncrementLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel)\n511\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, ImageArchitectureInspectionErrorMsg+errMsg)\n512\t\tlog.Error(err, s)\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has a MatchExpression in the PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution\n516\t// that matches kubernetes.io/arch\n517\tfunc (pod *Pod) isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n518\t\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil ||\n519\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\n520\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n521\t\t\treturn false\n522\t\t}\n523\t\n524\t\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n525\t\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n526\t\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n527\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n528\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t}\n531\t\treturn false\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t// filterMatchingPPCs returns only PPCs whose label selector matches this pod.\n535\t// This is done once per reconcile/webhook call to avoid redundant selector evaluations.\n536\tfunc (pod *Pod) filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList *v1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList) []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig {\n537\t\tvar matching []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig\n538\t\n539\t\tfor _, ppc := range ppcList.Items {\n540\t\t\tselector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(ppc.Spec.LabelSelector)\n541\t\t\tif err != nil {\n542\t\t\t\t// Invalid selector means it doesn't match - skip\n543\t\t\t\tcontinue\n544\t\t\t}\n545\t\n546\t\t\t// Empty selector (Nothing()) or matching selector\n547\t\t\tif selector == labels.Nothing() || selector.Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\n548\t\t\t\tmatching = append(matching, ppc)\n549\t\t\t}\n550\t\t}\n551\t\n552\t\treturn matching\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\t// hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin checks if any of the matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin enabled.\n556\t// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\n557\tfunc (pod *Pod) hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n558\t\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n559\t\t\tif ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n560\t\t\t\treturn true\n561\t\t\t}\n562\t\t}\n563\t\treturn false\n564\t}\n565\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3b5abb4c-2e8f-48c8-8e19-75a52b6ede56","uuid":"a85ea104-0d53-4b5e-be58-579eb26770fc","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:28:48.387Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage podplacement\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/models\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\t// imageInspectionCache is the facade singleton used to inspect images. It is defined here to facilitate testing.\n\timageInspectionCache image.ICache = image.FacadeSingleton()\n)\n\nconst MaxRetryCount = 5\n\ntype containerImage struct {\n\timageName string\n\tskipCache bool\n}\n\ntype Pod struct {\n\tmodels.Pod\n}\n\nfunc newPod(pod *corev1.Pod, ctx context.Context, recorder record.EventRecorder) *Pod {\n\treturn &Pod{\n\t\tPod: *models.NewPod(pod, ctx, recorder),\n\t}\n}\n\n// HasSchedulingGate checks if the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\nfunc (pod *Pod) HasSchedulingGate() bool {\n\treturn pod.HasGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n}\n\n// RemoveSchedulingGate removes the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName from the pod.\nfunc (pod *Pod) RemoveSchedulingGate() {\n\tpod.RemoveGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n\t// The scheduling gate is removed. We also add a label to the pod to indicate that the scheduling gate was removed\n\t// and this pod was processed by the operator. That's useful for testing and debugging, but also gives the user\n\t// an indication that the pod was processed by the operator.\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SchedulingGateLabel, utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueRemoved)\n}\n\n// ensureSchedulingGate ensures that the pod has the scheduling gate utils.SchedulingGateName.\nfunc (pod *Pod) ensureSchedulingGate() {\n\tpod.AddGate(utils.SchedulingGateName)\n}\n\n// getPodImagePullSecrets returns the names of the image pull secrets used by the pod.\nfunc (pod *Pod) getPodImagePullSecrets() []string {\n\tif pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets == nil {\n\t\t// If the imagePullSecrets array is nil, return emptylist\n\t\treturn []string{}\n\t}\n\tvar secretRefs = make([]string, len(pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets))\n\tfor i, secret := range pod.Spec.ImagePullSecrets {\n\t\tsecretRefs[i] = secret.Name\n\t}\n\treturn secretRefs\n}\n\n// SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement wraps the logic to set the nodeAffinity for the pod.\n// It verifies first that no nodeSelector field is set for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n// Then, it computes the intersection of the architectures supported by the images used by the pod via pod.getArchitecturePredicate.\n// Finally, it initializes the nodeAffinity for the pod and set it to the computed requirement via the pod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity method.\nfunc (pod *Pod) SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (bool, error) {\n\tif pod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\trequirement, err := pod.getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\tpod.EnsureNoLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel)\n\tif len(requirement.Values) == 0 {\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, NoSupportedArchitecturesFound, NoSupportedArchitecturesFoundMsg)\n\t}\n\tpod.ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement)\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n\t}\n\n\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n\t\tArchitecturePredicateSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(requirement.Values, \", \")))\n\treturn true, nil\n}\n\n// setRequiredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the given requirement based on the rules in\n// the sig-scheduling's KEP-3838: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-scheduling/3838-pod-mutable-scheduling-directives.\nfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n\t// the .requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms are ORed\n\tif len(pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms) == 0 {\n\t\t// We create a new array of NodeSelectorTerm of length 1 so that we can always iterate it in the next.\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorTerm, 1)\n\t}\n\tnodeSelectorTerms := pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms\n\n\t// The expressions within the nodeSelectorTerms are ANDed.\n\t// Therefore, we iterate over the nodeSelectorTerms and add an expression to each of the terms to verify the\n\t// kubernetes.io/arch label has compatible values.\n\t// Note that the NodeSelectorTerms will always be long at least 1, because we (re-)created it with size 1 above if it was nil (or having 0 length).\n\tvar skipMatchExpressionPatch bool\n\tfor i := range nodeSelectorTerms {\n\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = false\n\t\tif nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions == nil {\n\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = make([]corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, 0, 1)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Check if the nodeSelectorTerm already has a matchExpression for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n\t\t// if yes, we ignore to add it.\n\t\tfor _, expression := range nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions {\n\t\t\tif expression.Key == requirement.Key {\n\t\t\t\tskipMatchExpressionPatch = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// if skipMatchExpressionPatch is true, we skip to add the matchExpression so that conflictual matchExpressions provided by the user are not overwritten.\n\t\tif !skipMatchExpressionPatch {\n\t\t\tnodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions = append(nodeSelectorTerms[i].MatchExpressions, requirement)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// if the nodeSelectorTerms were patched at least once, we set the nodeAffinity label to the set value, to keep\n\t// track of the fact that the nodeAffinity was patched by the operator.\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n}\n\n// SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity sets the node affinity for the pod to the preferences given in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n// The configSource parameter identifies which configuration is setting the preferences (e.g., \"ClusterPodPlacementConfig\" or \"PodPlacementConfig/my-ppc\").\nfunc (pod *Pod) SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(nodeAffinity *plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource string) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{}\n\t}\n\n\tseenArchitectures := pod.getExistingPreferredArchitectures()\n\tvar preferredSchedulingTerms []corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm\n\tvar skippedArchitectures []string\n\tfor _, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm := range nodeAffinity.Platforms {\n\t\tif !seenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] {\n\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerm := corev1.PreferredSchedulingTerm{\n\t\t\t\tWeight: nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight,\n\t\t\t\tPreference: corev1.NodeSelectorTerm{\n\t\t\t\t\tMatchExpressions: []corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tValues:   []string{nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpreferredSchedulingTerms = append(preferredSchedulingTerms, preferredSchedulingTerm)\n\t\t\tseenArchitectures[nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture] = true\n\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was applied from this source\n\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, true)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tskippedArchitectures = append(skippedArchitectures, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture)\n\t\t\t// Track that this architecture was skipped from this source\n\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, configSource, false)\n\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Preferred affinity for pod is already set\", \"Architecture\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Architecture, \"Weight\", nodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm.Weight, \"Pod.Name\", pod.Name, \"Pod.Namespace\", pod.Namespace, \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif preferredSchedulingTerms != nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = append(\n\t\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution, preferredSchedulingTerms...)\n\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet)\n\t}\n\tswitch {\n\t// Case 1: All architectures from this config were successfully added (no duplicates)\n\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures == nil:\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredPredicateSetupMsg, configSource))\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied all architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n\n\t// Case 2: Some architectures were added, but some were skipped due to duplicates\n\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms != nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, skipped: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityWithDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Applied some architecture preferences from configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n\n\t// Case 3: All architectures from this config were already set\n\tcase preferredSchedulingTerms == nil && skippedArchitectures != nil:\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePreferredAffinityDuplicates, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s source: %s, architectures: %s\", ArchitecturePreferredAffinityAllDuplicatesMsg, configSource, strings.Join(skippedArchitectures, \", \")))\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"All architectures from configuration were already set\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource, \"SkippedArchitectures\", skippedArchitectures)\n\n\t// Case 4: No architectures were provided in the config\n\tdefault:\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No architecture preferences provided in configuration\", \"ConfigSource\", configSource)\n\t}\n}\n\n// getExistingPreferredArchitectures finds all\n// architectures that already have a preferred node affinity configured on the pod.\nfunc (pod *Pod) getExistingPreferredArchitectures() map[string]bool {\n\tseen := make(map[string]bool)\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n\t\treturn seen\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, value := range expr.Values {\n\t\t\t\t\tseen[value] = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn seen\n}\n\n// trackAffinitySource tracks which config sources attempted to set affinity for which architectures.\n// This information is later used to build the annotation showing the full audit trail.\nfunc (pod *Pod) trackAffinitySource(arch string, weight int32, source string, applied bool) {\n\t// Get existing annotation or start fresh\n\texistingAnnotation := \"\"\n\tif pod.Annotations != nil {\n\t\texistingAnnotation = pod.Annotations[utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation]\n\t}\n\n\t// Format: arch:weight:source or arch:weight:source:skipped\n\tvar newEntry string\n\tif applied {\n\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s\", arch, weight, source)\n\t} else {\n\t\tnewEntry = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d:%s:skipped\", arch, weight, source)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if this entry already exists to avoid duplicates on retries\n\tif existingAnnotation != \"\" {\n\t\tentries := strings.Split(existingAnnotation, \",\")\n\t\tfor _, entry := range entries {\n\t\t\tif entry == newEntry {\n\t\t\t\t// Entry already exists, don't add it again\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Append to existing annotation with comma separator\n\tif existingAnnotation == \"\" {\n\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, newEntry)\n\t} else {\n\t\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.PreferredNodeAffinitySourcesAnnotation, existingAnnotation+\",\"+newEntry)\n\t}\n}\n\n// setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture sets the node affinity for the pod to the fallback architecture.\nfunc (pod *Pod) setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(architecture string) {\n\trequirement := corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n\t\tValues:   []string{architecture},\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity = &corev1.Affinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity = &corev1.NodeAffinity{}\n\t}\n\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution = &corev1.NodeSelector{}\n\t}\n\n\tpod.setRequiredArchNodeAffinity(requirement)\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.FallbackArchitectureLabel, architecture)\n\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareFallbackNodeAffinitySet,\n\t\tArchitectureFallbackSetupMsg+fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", architecture))\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) getArchitecturePredicate(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement, error) {\n\tarchitectures, err := pod.intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList)\n\t// if an error occurs, we return an empty NodeSelectorRequirement and the error.\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{}, err\n\t}\n\n\tif len(architectures) == 0 {\n\t\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n\t\t\tKey:      utils.NoSupportedArchLabel,\n\t\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpExists,\n\t\t}, nil\n\t}\n\treturn corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement{\n\t\tKey:      utils.ArchLabel,\n\t\tOperator: corev1.NodeSelectorOpIn,\n\t\tValues:   architectures,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) imagesNamesSet() sets.Set[containerImage] {\n\timageNamesSet := sets.New[containerImage]()\n\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.Containers, pod.Spec.InitContainers...) {\n\t\timageNamesSet.Insert(containerImage{\n\t\t\timageName: fmt.Sprintf(\"//%s\", container.Image),\n\t\t\tskipCache: container.ImagePullPolicy == corev1.PullAlways,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn imageNamesSet\n}\n\n// inspect returns the list of supported architectures for the images used by the pod.\n// if an error occurs, it returns the error and a nil slice of strings.\nfunc (pod *Pod) intersectImagesArchitecture(pullSecretDataList [][]byte) (supportedArchitectures []string, err error) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n\timageNamesSet := pod.imagesNamesSet()\n\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Images list for pod\", \"imageNamesSet\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%+v\", imageNamesSet))\n\t// https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/v1.11.1/cmd/skopeo/inspect.go#L72\n\t// Iterate over the images, get their architectures and intersect (as in set intersection) them each other\n\tvar supportedArchitecturesSet sets.Set[string]\n\tnowExternal := time.Now()\n\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(nowExternal, metrics.TimeToInspectPodImages)\n\tfor imageContainer := range imageNamesSet {\n\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Checking image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName,\n\t\t\t\"skipCache (imagePullPolicy==Always)\", imageContainer.skipCache)\n\t\t// We are collecting the time to inspect the image here to avoid implementing a metric in each of the\n\t\t// cache implementations.\n\t\tnow := time.Now()\n\t\tcurrentImageSupportedArchitectures, err := imageInspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(pod.Ctx(),\n\t\t\timageContainer.imageName, imageContainer.skipCache, pullSecretDataList)\n\t\tutils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImage)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.V(1).Error(err, \"Error inspecting the image\", \"imageName\", imageContainer.imageName)\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif supportedArchitecturesSet == nil {\n\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = currentImageSupportedArchitectures\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsupportedArchitecturesSet = supportedArchitecturesSet.Intersection(currentImageSupportedArchitectures)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn sets.List(supportedArchitecturesSet), nil\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) maxRetries() bool {\n\tif pod.Labels == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tv, err := strconv.ParseInt(pod.Labels[utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel], 10, 32)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn v >= MaxRetryCount\n}\n\n// ensureArchitectureLabels adds labels for the given requirement to the pod. Labels are added to indicate\n// the supported architectures and index pods by architecture or by whether they support more than one architecture.\n// In this case, single-architecture is meant as a pod that supports only one architecture: all the images in the pod\n// may be manifest-list, but the intersection of the architectures is a single value.\nfunc (pod *Pod) ensureArchitectureLabels(requirement corev1.NodeSelectorRequirement) {\n\tif requirement.Values == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tswitch len(requirement.Values) {\n\tcase 0:\n\t\t// if the requirement has no values, we set the NoSupportedArchLabel as a label for the node. That's a dummy\n\t\t// and non-available-by-default label that we use to prevent the pod from being scheduled when it cannot run all\n\t\t// the containers in at least one architecture.\n\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NoSupportedArchLabel, \"\")\n\tcase 1:\n\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.SingleArchLabel, \"\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.MultiArchLabel, \"\")\n\t}\n\tvalidArchitectures := utils.AllSupportedArchitecturesSet()\n\tfor _, value := range requirement.Values {\n\t\tif validArchitectures.Has(value) {\n\t\t\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ArchLabelValue(value), \"\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// shouldIgnorePod returns true if the pod should be ignored by the operator.\n// The operator should ignore the pods in the following cases:\n// - the pod is in the same namespace as the operator\n// - the pod is in a namespace with prefix kube-\n// - the pod has a node name set\n// - the pod has a node selector that matches the control plane nodes\n// - the pod is owned by a DaemonSet\n// - the pod has required architecture affinity configured AND:\n//   - preferred affinity is already configured, OR\n//   - both CPPC and all matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin disabled\nfunc (pod *Pod) shouldIgnorePod(cppc *v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n\treturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n\t\tpod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" || pod.HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() || pod.IsFromDaemonSet() ||\n\t\tpod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() &&\n\t\t\t(pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() ||\n\t\t\t\t(!cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) && !pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs)))\n}\n\n// isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has already a nodeSelector for the architecture label\n// or if all the nodeSelectorTerms in the nodeAffinity field have a matchExpression for the architecture label.\nfunc (pod *Pod) isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n\t// if the pod has the nodeSelector field set for the kubernetes.io/arch label, we ignore it.\n\t// in fact, the nodeSelector field is ANDed with the nodeAffinity field, and we want to give the user the main control, if they\n\t// manually set a predicate for the kubernetes.io/arch label.\n\t// The same behavior is implemented below within each\n\t// nodeSelectorTerm's MatchExpressions field.\n\tfor key := range pod.Spec.NodeSelector {\n\t\tif key == utils.ArchLabel {\n\t\t\tpod.publishIgnorePod()\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Check if Affinity, NodeAffinity, or RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution is nil\n\t// If any of these are nil, assume there are no specific node selector terms to check, so return true.\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil || pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Iterate over NodeSelectorTerms (terms are ORed)\n\tfor _, nodeSelectorTerm := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.RequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution.NodeSelectorTerms {\n\t\t// Assume the architecture label is not present\n\t\thasArchLabel := false\n\n\t\t// Check all match expressions within the current NodeSelectorTerm (expressions are ANDed)\n\t\tfor _, matchExpression := range nodeSelectorTerm.MatchExpressions {\n\t\t\t// If we find the architecture label, mark it as found\n\t\t\tif matchExpression.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n\t\t\t\thasArchLabel = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If one of the NodeSelectorTerms does not have the architecture label, return false\n\t\tif !hasArchLabel {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\n\t}\n\n\t// If all NodeSelectorTerms contain the architecture label, return true\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) publishIgnorePod() {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n\tlog.V(1).Info(\"The pod has the nodeSelector or all the nodeAffinityTerms set for the kubernetes.io/arch label. Ignoring the pod...\")\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.LabelValueNotSet)\n\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitecturePredicatesConflict, ArchitecturePredicatesConflictMsg)\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) handleError(err error, s string) {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(pod.Ctx())\n\tmetrics.FailedInspectionCounter.Inc()\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\trunes := []rune(errMsg)\n\tif len(runes) > 256 {\n\t\terrMsg = string(runes[:256])\n\t}\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, \"\")\n\tpod.EnsureAnnotation(utils.ImageInspectionErrorLabel, errMsg)\n\tpod.EnsureAndIncrementLabel(utils.ImageInspectionErrorCountLabel)\n\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, ImageArchitectureInspectionErrorMsg+errMsg)\n\tlog.Error(err, s)\n}\n\n// isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture returns true if the pod has a MatchExpression in the PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution\n// that matches kubernetes.io/arch\nfunc (pod *Pod) isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() bool {\n\tif pod.Spec.Affinity == nil ||\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity == nil ||\n\t\tpod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, term := range pod.Spec.Affinity.NodeAffinity.PreferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution {\n\t\tfor _, expr := range term.Preference.MatchExpressions {\n\t\t\tif expr.Key == utils.ArchLabel {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// filterMatchingPPCs returns only PPCs whose label selector matches this pod.\n// This is done once per reconcile/webhook call to avoid redundant selector evaluations.\nfunc (pod *Pod) filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList *v1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList) []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig {\n\tvar matching []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig\n\n\tfor _, ppc := range ppcList.Items {\n\t\tselector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(ppc.Spec.LabelSelector)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// Invalid selector means it doesn't match - skip\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Empty selector (Nothing()) or matching selector\n\t\tif selector == labels.Nothing() || selector.Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\n\t\t\tmatching = append(matching, ppc)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn matching\n}\n\n// hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin checks if any of the matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin enabled.\n// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\nfunc (pod *Pod) hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n\t\tif ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n","numLines":565,"startLine":1,"totalLines":565}}}

If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n36\t\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n37\t\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n38\t\t// +optional\n39\t\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n43\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n44\t\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n45\t\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n49\t// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n50\t// Creating the object enables the operand.\n51\t// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n52\t// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n53\t// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\n54\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n55\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n56\t\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n57\t\n58\t\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n59\t\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n63\t\n64\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n65\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n66\t\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n67\t\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n68\t\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n69\t}\n70\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3b5abb4c-2e8f-48c8-8e19-75a52b6ede56","uuid":"6895e386-ab66-498c-8697-1888dfe90a5b","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:28:57.740Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/api/v1alpha1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage v1alpha1\n\nimport (\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n)\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n\t// +optional\n\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n// Creating the object enables the operand.\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n}\n","numLines":70,"startLine":1,"totalLines":70}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01BKCP9An3d9GFKAgD7hCkEG","type":"tool_result","content":"140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\t// Skip preferred affinity processing if the user has already configured architecture-related preferred affinity\n143\t\t// or if the reconcile loop has already applied the PPCs/CPPC (e.g., due to a retry or re-reconciliation)\n144\t\tif !pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n145\t\t\tr.applyMatchingPPCs(ctx, matchingPPCs, pod)\n146\t\n147\t\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n148\t\t\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind)\n149\t\t\t}\n150\t\t} else {\n151\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod already has architecture-related preferred affinity. This could be user-defined or from a previous reconcile loop. Skipping PPC/CPPC preferred affinity processing.\")\n152\t\t\t// Track that configs were skipped due to user-defined preferences\n153\t\t\tr.trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx, pod, cppc, matchingPPCs)\n154\t\t}\n155\t\n156\t\t// Prepare the requirement for the node affinity.\n157\t\tpsdl, err := r.pullSecretDataList(ctx, pod)\n158\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to retrieve the image pull secret data for the pod.\")\n159\t\t// If no error occurred when retrieving the image pull secret data, set the node affinity.\n160\t\tif err == nil {\n161\t\t\t_, err = pod.SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(psdl)\n162\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to set the node affinity for the pod.\")\n163\t\t}\n164\t\tif pod.maxRetries() && err != nil {\n165\t\t\t// the number of retries is incremented in the handleError function when the error is not nil.\n166\t\t\t// If we enter this branch, the retries counter has been incremented and reached the max retries.\n167\t\t\t// The counter starts at 1 when the first error occurs. Therefore, when the reconciler tries maxRetries times,\n168\t\t\t// the counter is equal to the maxRetries value and the pod should not be processed again.\n169\t\t\t// Publish this event and remove the scheduling gate.\n170\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Max retries Reached. The pod will not have the nodeAffinity set.\")\n171\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", ImageInspectionErrorMaxRetriesMsg, err.Error()))\n172\t\n173\t\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != \"\" {\n174\t\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Setting the nodeAffinity to the fallback architecture\", \"fallbackArchitecture\", cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n175\t\t\t\tpod.setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n176\t\t\t}\n177\t\t}\n178\t\t// If the pod has been processed successfully or the max retries have been reached, remove the scheduling gate.\n179\t\tif err == nil || pod.maxRetries() {\n180\t\t\t// If no preferred node affinity was set by any config, log and publish an event\n181\t\t\tif pod.Labels[utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel] == utils.LabelValueNotSet {\n182\t\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n183\t\t\t\t\tArchitecturePreferredPredicateSkippedMsg)\n184\t\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No preferred node affinity was set\")\n185\t\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n188\t\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n189\t\t}\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// applyMatchingPPCs applies the pre-filtered matching PodPlacementConfigs to the pod.\n193\t// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\n194\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) applyMatchingPPCs(ctx context.Context, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig, pod *Pod) {\n195\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithName(\"PodPlacementConfig\")\n196\t\n197\t\t// Sort the configurations by descending priority\n198\t\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n199\t\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3b5abb4c-2e8f-48c8-8e19-75a52b6ede56","uuid":"381a0ffc-811b-4907-b5ef-19d1b5c1f18c","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:29:13.585Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_reconciler.go","content":"\t}\n\n\t// Skip preferred affinity processing if the user has already configured architecture-related preferred affinity\n\t// or if the reconcile loop has already applied the PPCs/CPPC (e.g., due to a retry or re-reconciliation)\n\tif !pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n\t\tr.applyMatchingPPCs(ctx, matchingPPCs, pod)\n\n\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod already has architecture-related preferred affinity. This could be user-defined or from a previous reconcile loop. Skipping PPC/CPPC preferred affinity processing.\")\n\t\t// Track that configs were skipped due to user-defined preferences\n\t\tr.trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx, pod, cppc, matchingPPCs)\n\t}\n\n\t// Prepare the requirement for the node affinity.\n\tpsdl, err := r.pullSecretDataList(ctx, pod)\n\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to retrieve the image pull secret data for the pod.\")\n\t// If no error occurred when retrieving the image pull secret data, set the node affinity.\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t_, err = pod.SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(psdl)\n\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to set the node affinity for the pod.\")\n\t}\n\tif pod.maxRetries() && err != nil {\n\t\t// the number of retries is incremented in the handleError function when the error is not nil.\n\t\t// If we enter this branch, the retries counter has been incremented and reached the max retries.\n\t\t// The counter starts at 1 when the first error occurs. Therefore, when the reconciler tries maxRetries times,\n\t\t// the counter is equal to the maxRetries value and the pod should not be processed again.\n\t\t// Publish this event and remove the scheduling gate.\n\t\tlog.Info(\"Max retries Reached. The pod will not have the nodeAffinity set.\")\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", ImageInspectionErrorMaxRetriesMsg, err.Error()))\n\n\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != \"\" {\n\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Setting the nodeAffinity to the fallback architecture\", \"fallbackArchitecture\", cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n\t\t\tpod.setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// If the pod has been processed successfully or the max retries have been reached, remove the scheduling gate.\n\tif err == nil || pod.maxRetries() {\n\t\t// If no preferred node affinity was set by any config, log and publish an event\n\t\tif pod.Labels[utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel] == utils.LabelValueNotSet {\n\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n\t\t\t\tArchitecturePreferredPredicateSkippedMsg)\n\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No preferred node affinity was set\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n\t}\n}\n\n// applyMatchingPPCs applies the pre-filtered matching PodPlacementConfigs to the pod.\n// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) applyMatchingPPCs(ctx context.Context, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig, pod *Pod) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithName(\"PodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\t// Sort the configurations by descending priority\n\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority","numLines":60,"startLine":140,"totalLines":329}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01H5c3DnFpULbNDL2h9K9k9p","type":"tool_result","content":"95\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n96\t\t}\n97\t\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n98\t\t\t// Only publish the event if the scheduling gate has been removed and the pod has been updated successfully.\n99\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalSuccess, SchedulingGateRemovalSuccessMsg)\n100\t\t\tmetrics.GatedPodsGauge.Dec()\n101\t\t}\n102\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) processPod(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) {\n106\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n107\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing pod\")\n108\t\n109\t\tcppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()\n110\t\t// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace\n111\t\tppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}\n112\t\tif err := r.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n113\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace\")\n114\t\t\treturn\n115\t\t}\n116\t\t// The informer cache can lag behind the API server. If the cache shows no PPCs,\n117\t\t// verify with a direct API read to avoid a race where a just-created PPC is missed\n118\t\t// and the pod is permanently ungated without its preferred affinity.\n119\t\tif len(ppcList.Items) == 0 {\n120\t\t\tif err := r.APIReader.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n121\t\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list PodPlacementConfigs from API server\")\n122\t\t\t\treturn\n123\t\t\t}\n124\t\t}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3b5abb4c-2e8f-48c8-8e19-75a52b6ede56","uuid":"171ac35b-ee34-4b3b-8f85-e97dafab2063","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:29:24.059Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_reconciler.go","content":"\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t}\n\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n\t\t// Only publish the event if the scheduling gate has been removed and the pod has been updated successfully.\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalSuccess, SchedulingGateRemovalSuccessMsg)\n\t\tmetrics.GatedPodsGauge.Dec()\n\t}\n\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) processPod(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing pod\")\n\n\tcppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()\n\t// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace\n\tppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}\n\tif err := r.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// The informer cache can lag behind the API server. If the cache shows no PPCs,\n\t// verify with a direct API read to avoid a race where a just-created PPC is missed\n\t// and the pod is permanently ungated without its preferred affinity.\n\tif len(ppcList.Items) == 0 {\n\t\tif err := r.APIReader.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list PodPlacementConfigs from API server\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}","numLines":30,"startLine":95,"totalLines":329}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01WUqRKpudyTNpqn5ukXUHj3","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have all the information needed. Here is the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## File 1: `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_types.go`\n\nAdd the `MaxImageInspectionRetries` field to `ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec`. Insert after the `FallbackArchitecture` field (after line 58):\n\n```go\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n\t// +optional\n\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.\n\t// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.\n\t// +optional\n\tPlugins *plugins.Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture\n\t// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=\"\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;\"\"\n\tFallbackArchitecture string `json:\"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// MaxImageInspectionRetries is the maximum number of times the controller will retry\n\t// image inspection on failure before giving up and removing the scheduling gate.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=5\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum=20\n\tMaxImageInspectionRetries *int32 `json:\"maxImageInspectionRetries,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## File 2: `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go`\n\nTwo changes here:\n\n### Change A: Remove the hardcoded constant (line run these in this specific order:\n\n1. **`make generate`** — Regenerates `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` for both `v1beta1` and `v1alpha1` packages. The new `*int32` pointer field needs a `DeepCopyInto` implementation. This also runs `fmt`.\n\n2. **`make manifests`** — Regenerates CRD YAML files under `config/crd/bases/` with the new field, its kubebuilder validation markers (`minimum`, `maximum`, `default`), and the updated OpenAPI schema.\n\n3. **`make fmt`** — Already run by `generate`, but good to confirm formatting is clean.\n\n4. **`make vet`** — Runs `go vet` to catch any type errors or issues from the changes.\n\n5. **`make test`** (or `make unit`) — Runs the full test suite including envtest. Note that `make test` already depends on `manifests generate envtest fmt vet`, so in practice you can just run:\n\n```bash\nmake generate\nmake manifests\nmake test\n```\n\n`make test` will re-run `manifests` and `generate` (they're dependencies), then `fmt`, `vet`, and the unit tests.
