podplacement\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/tls\"\n\t\"crypto/x509\"\n\t\"encoding/base64\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/BurntSushi/toml\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image\"\n\n\t. \"github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2\"\n\t. \"github.com/onsi/gomega\"\n\n\t\"go.uber.org/zap/zapcore\"\n\n\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/admissionregistration/v1\"\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/json\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd/api\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/envtest\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/healthz\"\n\tlogf \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log/zap\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook\"\n\n\t\"github.com/panjf2000/ants/v2\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1alpha1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/e2e\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/builder\"\n\ttestingutils \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/framework\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/image/fake/registry\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n\t//+kubebuilder:scaffold:imports\n)\n\n// These tests use Ginkgo (BDD-style Go testing framework). Refer to\n// http://onsi.github.io/ginkgo/ to learn more about Ginkgo.\n\nvar (\n\tcfg             *rest.Config\n\tk8sClient       client.Client\n\tregistryAddress string\n\tregistryCert    []byte\n\tstopMgr         context.CancelFunc\n\tmgrStopped      chan struct{}\n\ttestEnv         *envtest.Environment\n\n\tdir      string\n\tsuiteLog = ctrl.Log.WithName(\"setup\")\n)\n\ntype sharedData struct {\n\tKubeconfig       api.Config `json:\"kubeconfig\"`\n\tRegistryAddress  string     `json:\"registryAddress\"`\n\tRegistryCert     []byte     `json:\"registryCert\"`\n\tRegistryCertPath string     `json:\"registryCertpath\"`\n}\n\nfunc init() {\n\te2e.CommonInit()\n}\n\nfunc TestAPIs(t *testing.T) {\n\tRegisterFailHandler(Fail)\n\tRunSpecs(t, \"Controllers Integration Suite\", Label(\"integration\"))\n}\n\nvar _ = BeforeAll\n\nvar _ = SynchronizedBeforeSuite(func() []byte {\n\tsuiteLog = zap.New(zap.WriteTo(GinkgoWriter), zap.UseDevMode(true), zap.Level(zapcore.Level(-5)))\n\n\tlogf.SetLogger(suiteLog)\n\tSetDefaultEventuallyPollingInterval(5 * time.Millisecond)\n\tSetDefaultEventuallyTimeout(5 * time.Second)\n\n\tBy(\"Set up a shared test environment and local registry for testing on process 1 node\")\n\twg := &sync.WaitGroup{}\n\ttestingutils.DecorateWithWaitGroup(wg, startTestEnv)\n\ttestingutils.DecorateWithWaitGroup(wg, startRegistry)\n\twg.Wait()\n\ttestingutils.DecorateWithWaitGroup(wg, seedRegistry)\n\ttestingutils.DecorateWithWaitGroup(wg, seedK8S)\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tvar err error\n\tdir, err = os.MkdirTemp(\"\", \"multiarch-tuning-operator\")\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tExpect(dir).NotTo(BeEmpty())\n\terr = os.Setenv(\"DOCKER_CERTS_DIR\", filepath.Join(dir, \"docker/certs.d\"))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\terr = os.Setenv(\"REGISTRIES_CERTS_DIR\", filepath.Join(dir, \"containers/registries.d\"))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\terr = os.Setenv(\"REGISTRIES_CONF_PATH\", filepath.Join(dir, \"containers/registries.conf\"))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\terr = os.Setenv(\"POLICY_CONF_PATH\", filepath.Join(dir, \"containers/policy.json\"))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\n\t// TODO: should we continue running the manager in the BeforeSuite node?\n\trunManager()\n\n\tBy(\"Creating the ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\terr = k8sClient.Create(ctx, builder.NewClusterPodPlacementConfig().\n\t\tWithName(common.SingletonResourceObjectName).\n\t\tWithPlugins().\n\t\tWithNodeAffinityScoring(true).\n\t\tWithNodeAffinityScoringTerm(utils.ArchitectureArm64, 50).\n\t\tBuild())\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to create ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\tBy(\"Checking initialization of the cache with the ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tppc := &v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}\n\terr = k8sClient.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Name: common.SingletonResourceObjectName}, ppc)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to get ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\t// Wait for the informer to update by polling\n\tBy(\"Waiting for the cache to reflect the ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tEventually(clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig).\n\t\tShould(Equal(ppc), \"cache did not update with ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\tBy(\"Updating the ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tppc.Spec.LogVerbosity = common.LogVerbosityLevelTraceAll\n\terr = k8sClient.Update(ctx, ppc)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to update ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\tBy(\"Get the updated ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\terr = k8sClient.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Name: common.SingletonResourceObjectName}, ppc)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to get ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\tBy(\"Waiting for the cache to reflect the updated ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tEventually(clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig).\n\t\tShould(Equal(ppc), \"cache did not update with new ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\tBy(\"Prepare shared data and Pass to all processes\")\n\t// Get cluster info and share with all processes\n\tkc := testingutils.FromEnvTestConfig(cfg)\n\t// The registry.perRegistryCertDirPath is used by registry.PushMockImage,\n\t// need to pass to all processes\n\tregistryCertPath := registry.GetCertPath()\n\tdata := sharedData{\n\t\tKubeconfig:       kc,\n\t\tRegistryAddress:  registryAddress,\n\t\tRegistryCert:     registryCert,\n\t\tRegistryCertPath: registryCertPath,\n\t}\n\tjsonData, err := json.Marshal(data)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to marshal sharedData\")\n\treturn jsonData\n}, func(data []byte) {\n\tsuiteLog = zap.New(zap.WriteTo(GinkgoWriter), zap.UseDevMode(true), zap.Level(zapcore.Level(-5)))\n\n\tlogf.SetLogger(suiteLog)\n\tSetDefaultEventuallyPollingInterval(5 * time.Millisecond)\n\tSetDefaultEventuallyTimeout(5 * time.Second)\n\tvar err error\n\tBy(\"Get shared data to all processes\")\n\tvar sharedData sharedData\n\terr = json.Unmarshal(data, &sharedData)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to unmarshal sharedData\")\n\t// Sync registry.perRegistryCertDirPath for registry.PushMockImage\n\tregistryCertPath := sharedData.RegistryCertPath\n\tregistry.SetCertPath(registryCertPath)\n\t// Sync registryAddress and registryCert\n\tregistryAddress = sharedData.RegistryAddress\n\tregistryCert = sharedData.RegistryCert\n\t// Sync test cluster environment\n\tkc := sharedData.Kubeconfig\n\tocg := clientcmd.NewDefaultClientConfig(kc, &clientcmd.ConfigOverrides{})\n\tcfg, err = ocg.ClientConfig()\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"Error loading kubeconfig\")\n\tk8sClient, err = client.New(cfg, client.Options{Scheme: scheme.Scheme})\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tExpect(k8sClient).NotTo(BeNil())\n})\n\nvar _ = SynchronizedAfterSuite(func() {}, func() {\n\tBy(\"Deleting the ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\terr := k8sClient.Delete(ctx, builder.NewClusterPodPlacementConfig().WithName(common.SingletonResourceObjectName).Build())\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to delete ClusterPodPlacementConfig\", err)\n\tEventually(testingutils.ValidateDeletion(k8sClient, ctx)).Should(Succeed(), \"the ClusterPodPlacementConfig should be deleted\")\n\tBy(\"Checking the cache is empty\")\n\tExpect(clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()).To(BeNil())\n\n\tBy(\"tearing down the test environment\")\n\tif stopMgr != nil {\n\t\tstopMgr()\n\t}\n\tif mgrStopped != nil {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-mgrStopped:\n\t\t\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Manager stopped successfully\")\n\t\tcase <-time.After(10 * time.Second):\n\t\t\tsuiteLog.Error(nil, \"Timeout waiting for manager to stop\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// TODO: we miss a way to gracefully shutdown the registry server in the AfterSuite fixture.\n\terr = testEnv.Stop()\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n})\n\nfunc startTestEnv() {\n\tBy(\"bootstrapping test environment\")\n\ttestEnv = &envtest.Environment{\n\t\tCRDDirectoryPaths:     []string{filepath.Join(\"..\", \"..\", \"..\", \"config\", \"crd\", \"bases\")},\n\t\tErrorIfCRDPathMissing: true,\n\t\tWebhookInstallOptions: envtest.WebhookInstallOptions{\n\t\t\tMutatingWebhooks: []*v1.MutatingWebhookConfiguration{getMutatingWebHook()},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tvar err error\n\n\t// cfg is defined in this file globally.\n\tcfg, err = testEnv.Start()\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tExpect(cfg).NotTo(BeNil())\n\n\tk8sClient, err = client.New(cfg, client.Options{Scheme: scheme.Scheme})\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tExpect(k8sClient).NotTo(BeNil())\n\t//+kubebuilder:scaffold:scheme\n}\n\nfunc startRegistry() {\n\tBy(\"bootstrapping test registry environment\")\n\tvar (\n\t\terr          error\n\t\tregistryChan = make(chan error)\n\t)\n\tregistryAddress, registryCert, err = registry.RunRegistry(ctx, registryChan)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to start registry server\")\n\ttestingutils.StartChannelMonitor(registryChan, \"registry server failed\")\n\n\t// TODO: we miss a way to gracefully shutdown the registry server in the AfterSuite fixture.\n\tBy(\"Waiting for the registry server to be ready...\")\n\tcaCertPool := x509.NewCertPool()\n\tcaCertPool.AppendCertsFromPEM(registryCert)\n\thttpClient := &http.Client{\n\t\tTransport: &http.Transport{\n\t\t\tTLSClientConfig: &tls.Config{\n\t\t\t\tRootCAs: caCertPool,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\t// See https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/28c8bc6c0e4b5dfc380e0fa3058d4877fabdfa4a/registry/registry.go#L143\n\t\tresp, err := httpClient.Get(fmt.Sprintf(\"https://%s/\", registryAddress))\n\t\tg.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t\tg.Expect(resp.StatusCode).To(Equal(http.StatusOK))\n\t}).MustPassRepeatedly(3).Should(\n\t\tSucceed(), \"registry server is not ready yet\")\n\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Registry server is ready\", \"registryAddress\", registryAddress)\n}\n\nfunc seedRegistry() {\n\tBy(\"Seeding the registry with images...\")\n\terr := registry.SeedMockRegistry(ctx)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t// Just print the seed images for debugging purposes.\n\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Seed completed. The registry contains the following images:\")\n\tfor _, image := range registry.GetMockImages() {\n\t\tsuiteLog.Info(image.GetURL())\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc seedK8S() {\n\tvar err error\n\tBy(\"Create internal namespaces\")\n\ttestingutils.EnsureNamespaces(ctx, k8sClient, \"openshift-config\", \"test-namespace\")\n\tBy(\"Create the global pull secret\")\n\tsecret := &corev1.Secret{\n\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n\t\t\tName:      \"pull-secret\",\n\t\t\tNamespace: \"openshift-config\",\n\t\t},\n\t\tType: corev1.SecretTypeDockerConfigJson,\n\t\tData: map[string][]byte{\n\t\t\t\".dockerconfigjson\": []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{\"auths\":{\"%s\":{\"auth\":\"%s\"}}}`,\n\t\t\t\tregistryAddress, base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString([]byte(fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"%s:%s\", registry.GlobalPullSecretUser, registry.GlobalPullSecretPassword))))),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\terr = k8sClient.Create(ctx, secret)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n}\n\nfunc runManager() {\n\tBy(\"Creating the manager\")\n\twebhookServer := webhook.NewServer(webhook.Options{\n\t\tPort:    testEnv.WebhookInstallOptions.LocalServingPort,\n\t\tHost:    testEnv.WebhookInstallOptions.LocalServingHost,\n\t\tCertDir: testEnv.WebhookInstallOptions.LocalServingCertDir,\n\t})\n\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(cfg, ctrl.Options{\n\t\tScheme:                 scheme.Scheme,\n\t\tHealthProbeBindAddress: \":4980\",\n\t\tLogger:                 suiteLog,\n\t\tWebhookServer:          webhookServer,\n\t})\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Manager created\")\n\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(cfg)\n\n\tBy(\"Setting up PodPlacement controller\")\n\tExpect((&PodReconciler{\n\t\tClient:    mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tAPIReader: mgr.GetAPIReader(),\n\t\tScheme:    mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tClientSet: clientset,\n\t\tRecorder:  mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr)).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tpool, err := ants.NewMultiPool(10, 10, ants.LeastTasks, ants.WithPreAlloc(true),\n\t\tants.WithNonblocking(true))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tmgr.GetWebhookServer().Register(\"/add-pod-scheduling-gate\", &webhook.Admission{\n\t\tHandler: NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(\n\t\t\tmgr.GetClient(), clientset, mgr.GetScheme(), mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), pool), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n\t})\n\n\tpolicyConfig := []byte(`{\"default\":[{\"type\":\"insecureAcceptAnything\"}],\"transports\":{\"atomic\":{},\"docker\":{},\"docker-daemon\":{\"\":[{\"type\":\"insecureAcceptAnything\"}]}}}`)\n\tregistryConfig, err := toml.Marshal(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"unqualified-search-registries\": []string{\"registry.access.redhat.com\", \"docker.io\"},\n\t\t\"short-name-mode\":               \"\",\n\t\t\"registry\":                      []string{},\n\t})\n\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tBy(\"Create containers/policy.json\")\n\tcreateFile(image.PolicyConfPath(), policyConfig)\n\tBy(\"Create containers/containers.conf\")\n\tcreateFile(image.RegistriesConfPath(), registryConfig)\n\tBy(\"Write fake image registry certificate to file\")\n\tcreateFile(filepath.Join(image.DockerCertsDir(),\n\t\tstrings.Replace(registryAddress, \"..\", \":\", 1), \"ca.crt\"), registryCert)\n\n\tBy(\"Setting up Global Pull Secret Syncer\")\n\terr = mgr.Add(NewGlobalPullSecretSyncer(clientset, \"openshift-config\",\n\t\t\"pull-secret\"))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\n\terr = mgr.AddReadyzCheck(\"readyz\", healthz.Ping)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\n\terr = v1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme.Scheme)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\terr = v1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme.Scheme)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\n\tBy(\"Setting up Cluster Podplacement Config informer\")\n\terr = mgr.Add(clusterpodplacementconfig.NewCPPCSyncer(mgr))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tBy(\"Checking the cache is empty\")\n\tExpect(clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()).To(BeNil())\n\n\tBy(\"Starting the manager\")\n\tmgrStopped = make(chan struct{})\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer close(mgrStopped)\n\t\tvar mgrCtx context.Context\n\t\tmgrCtx, stopMgr = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\t\terr = mgr.Start(mgrCtx)\n\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t}()\n\n\tBy(\"Waiting for the manager to be ready...\")\n\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\tresp, err := http.Get(\"http://127.0.0.1:4980/readyz\")\n\t\tg.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t\tg.Expect(resp.StatusCode).To(Equal(http.StatusOK))\n\t}).MustPassRepeatedly(3).Should(\n\t\tSucceed(), \"manager is not ready yet\")\n\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Manager is ready\")\n\n\tBy(\"Waiting for the manager cache to sync\")\n\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\tg.Expect(mgr.GetCache().WaitForCacheSync(ctx)).To(BeTrue())\n\t}).Should(Succeed(), \"cache did not sync\")\n\tsuiteLog.Info(\"Manager cache synced\")\n}\n\nfunc getMutatingWebHook() *v1.MutatingWebhookConfiguration {\n\treturn &v1.MutatingWebhookConfiguration{\n\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n\t\t\tName: \"mutating-webhook-configuration\",\n\t\t},\n\t\tWebhooks: []v1.MutatingWebhook{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tName:                    \"pod-placement-scheduling-gate.multiarch.openshift.io\",\n\t\t\t\tAdmissionReviewVersions: []string{\"v1\"},\n\t\t\t\tClientConfig: v1.WebhookClientConfig{\n\t\t\t\t\tService: &v1.ServiceReference{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tName: \"webhook-service\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPath: utils.NewPtr(\"/add-pod-scheduling-gate\"),\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tFailurePolicy: utils.NewPtr(v1.Ignore),\n\t\t\t\tRules: []v1.RuleWithOperations{\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tOperations: []v1.OperationType{\"CREATE\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\tRule: v1.Rule{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAPIGroups:   []string{\"\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tAPIVersions: []string{\"v1\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tResources:   []string{\"pods\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tSideEffects: utils.NewPtr(v1.SideEffectClassNone),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc createFile(path string, data []byte) {\n\terr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0775)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tf, err := os.Create(filepath.Clean(path))\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\terr = f.Close()\n\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t}()\n\t_, err = f.Write(data)\n\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n}\n","numLines":463,"startLine":1,"totalLines":463}}}

ok {\n24\t\t\tnamespace = ns\n25\t\t} else {\n26\t\t\tnamespace = \"default\"\n27\t\t}\n28\t\tif img, ok := os.LookupEnv(\"IMAGE\"); ok {\n29\t\t\timage = img\n30\t\t} else {\n31\t\t\timage = \"quay.io/example/example-operator:latest\"\n32\t\t}\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t// Namespace returns the namespace where the operator's pods are running.\n36\tfunc Namespace() string {\n37\t\treturn namespace\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t// Image returns the image used to run the operator.\n41\tfunc Image() string {\n42\t\treturn image\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfunc IsResourceAvailable(ctx context.Context, client *dynamic.DynamicClient, resource schema.GroupVersionResource) bool {\n46\t\trwMutex.RLock()\n47\t\tif v, ok := availableResourcesMap[resource]; ok {\n48\t\t\trwMutex.RUnlock()\n49\t\t\treturn v\n50\t\t}\n51\t\trwMutex.RUnlock()\n52\t\t_, err := client.Resource(resource).Namespace(Namespace()).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})\n53\t\trwMutex.Lock()\n54\t\tdefer rwMutex.Unlock()\n55\t\tavailableResourcesMap[resource] = err == nil\n56\t\treturn availableResourcesMap[resource]\n57\t}\n58\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"da4b0e82-9c1c-4466-872b-2ce9e4f3f65d","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:31:49.342Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/utils/runtime.go","content":"package utils\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic\"\n\n\t\"go.uber.org/zap\"\n)\n\nvar namespace string\nvar image string\nvar AtomicLevel zap.AtomicLevel = zap.NewAtomicLevelAt(-5)\nvar availableResourcesMap = map[schema.GroupVersionResource]bool{}\nvar rwMutex = sync.RWMutex{}\n\nfunc init() {\n\t// Get the namespace from the env variable\n\tif ns, ok := os.LookupEnv(\"NAMESPACE\"); ok {\n\t\tnamespace = ns\n\t} else {\n\t\tnamespace = \"default\"\n\t}\n\tif img, ok := os.LookupEnv(\"IMAGE\"); ok {\n\t\timage = img\n\t} else {\n\t\timage = \"quay.io/example/example-operator:latest\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// Namespace returns the namespace where the operator's pods are running.\nfunc Namespace() string {\n\treturn namespace\n}\n\n// Image returns the image used to run the operator.\nfunc Image() string {\n\treturn image\n}\n\nfunc IsResourceAvailable(ctx context.Context, client *dynamic.DynamicClient, resource schema.GroupVersionResource) bool {\n\trwMutex.RLock()\n\tif v, ok := availableResourcesMap[resource]; ok {\n\t\trwMutex.RUnlock()\n\t\treturn v\n\t}\n\trwMutex.RUnlock()\n\t_, err := client.Resource(resource).Namespace(Namespace()).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})\n\trwMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer rwMutex.Unlock()\n\tavailableResourcesMap[resource] = err == nil\n\treturn availableResourcesMap[resource]\n}\n","numLines":58,"startLine":1,"totalLines":58}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01CJxF8H9iT3RdhhnMHQngD2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage podplacement\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strconv\"\n6\t\t\"time\"\n7\t\n8\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n9\t\n10\t\t. \"github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2\"\n11\t\t. \"github.com/onsi/gomega\"\n12\t\n13\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n14\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n15\t\tcrclient \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n16\t\n17\t\timgspecv1 \"github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/specs-go/v1\"\n18\t\n19\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n20\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n21\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/e2e\"\n22\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig\"\n23\t\t. \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/builder\"\n24\t\t. \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/framework\"\n25\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/image/fake/registry\"\n26\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n27\t)\n28\t\n29\tvar _ = Describe(\"Internal/Controller/Podplacement/PodReconciler\", func() {\n30\t\tWhen(\"Handling Single-container Pods\", func() {\n31\t\t\tContext(\"with different image types\", func() {\n32\t\t\t\tDescribeTable(\"handles correctly\", func(imageType string, supportedArchitectures ...string) {\n33\t\t\t\t\tpod := NewPod().\n34\t\t\t\t\t\tWithContainersImages(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s/%s:latest\", registryAddress,\n35\t\t\t\t\t\t\tregistry.PublicRepo, registry.ComputeNameByMediaType(imageType))).\n36\t\t\t\t\t\tWithGenerateName(\"test-pod-\").\n37\t\t\t\t\t\tWithNamespace(\"test-namespace\").\n38\t\t\t\t\t\tBuild()\n39\t\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Create(ctx, pod)\n40\t\t\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to create pod\", err)\n41\t\t\t\t\t// Test the removal of the scheduling gate. However, since the pod is mutated and the reconciler\n42\t\t\t\t\t// removes the scheduling gate concurrently, we cannot ensure that the scheduling gate is added\n43\t\t\t\t\t// and that the following Eventually works on a pod with the scheduling gate.\n44\t\t\t\t\t// Waiting for the pod to be mutated is not enough, as the pod could be mutated and the reconciler\n45\t\t\t\t\t// could have removed the scheduling gate before our check.\n46\t\t\t\t\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n47\t\t\t\t\t\t// Get pod from the API server\n48\t\t\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Get(ctx, crclient.ObjectKeyFromObject(pod), pod)\n49\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to get pod\", err)\n50\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(pod.Spec.SchedulingGates).NotTo(ContainElement(corev1.PodSchedulingGate{\n51\t\t\t\t\t\t\tName: utils.SchedulingGateName,\n52\t\t\t\t\t\t}), \"scheduling gate not _ = Describe(\"Internal/Controller/Podplacement/PodReconciler\", func() {\n\tWhen(\"Handling Single-container Pods\", func() {\n\t\tContext(\"with different image types\", func() {\n\t\t\tDescribeTable(\"handles correctly\", func(imageType string, supportedArchitectures ...string) {\n\t\t\t\tpod := NewPod().\n\t\t\t\t\tWithContainersImages(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s/%s:latest\", registryAddress,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tregistry.PublicRepo, registry.ComputeNameByMediaType(imageType))).\n\t\t\t\t\tWithGenerateName(\"test-pod-\").\n\t\t\t\t\tWithNamespace(\"test-namespace\").\n\t\t\t\t\tBuild()\n\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Create(ctx, pod)\n\t\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to create pod\", err)\n\t\t\t\t// Test the removal of the scheduling gate. However, since the pod is mutated and the reconciler\n\t\t\t\t// removes the scheduling gate concurrently, we cannot ensure that the scheduling gate is added\n\t\t\t\t// and that the following Eventually works on a pod with the scheduling gate.\n\t\t\t\t// Waiting for the pod to be mutated is not enough, as the pod could be mutated and the reconciler\n\t\t\t\t// could have removed the scheduling gate before our check.\n\t\t\t\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Get pod from the API server\n\t\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Get(ctx, crclient.ObjectKeyFromObject(pod), utils.SchedulingGateName,\n\t\t\t\t\t}), \"scheduling gate not removed\")\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(pod.Labels).To(HaveKeyWithValue(utils.SchedulingGateLabel, utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueRemoved),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"scheduling gate annotation not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(pod.Labels).To(HaveKeyWithValue(utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"preferred node affinity label not found\")\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(pod.Labels).To(HaveKeyWithValue(utils.NodeAffinityLabel, utils.NodeAffinityLabelValueSet),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"node affinity label not found\")\n\t\t\t\t}).Should(Succeed(), \"failed to remove scheduling gate from pod\")\n\t\t\t\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(*pod).To(HaveEquivalentNodeAffinity(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t&corev1.NodeAffinity{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRequiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution: affinity\")\n\t\t\t\t}).Should(Succeed(), \"failed to set node affinity to pod\")\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tEntry(\"OCI Index Images\", imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageIndex, utils.ArchitectureAmd64, utils.ArchitectureArm64),\n\t\t\t\tEntry(\"Docker images\", imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageManifest, utils.ArchitecturePpc64le),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t})\n\t\tContext(\"with an image that cannot be inspected\", func() {\n\t\t\tIt(\"remove the scheduling gate after the max retries count\", func() {\n\t\t\t\tpod := {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_0186pspRYLCEzBDUYRHoREuR","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now let me check how the `shouldIgnorePod` logic handles `openshift-` namespaces. Looking at line 438, the conditions check `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\")`. But the `openshift-kube-apiserver` namespace starts with `openshift-`, not `kube-`. However, the webhook test is an integration test that creates pods via the API server — the webhook is what adds the scheduling gate. However, the task says the test should verify the pod is NOT assigned the scheduling gate. In real production, the `ClusterPodPlacementConfig`'s `NamespaceSelector` is applied to the `MutatingWebhookConfiguration`, so `openshift-kube-apiserver` would typically be excluded at the Kubernetes admission level.\n\nBut in our test, the `getMutatingWebHook()` doesn't set a `NamespaceSelector`, so the webhook fires for all namespaces. Looking at how the existing ignored pod test works — the pod with `nodeName` is created successfully without error because `shouldIgnorePod` returns true, and the webhook doesn't add the scheduling gate (it returns a patched response without calling `ensureSchedulingGate`).\n\nFor `openshift-kube-apiserver`, since it's not caught by `shouldIgnorePod`, the pod WOULD get gated unless something else prevents it. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01B1qjqRvrrM4WeQS3ybtcGp","type":"tool_result","content":"130\t\t}\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// HasControlPlaneNodeSelector returns true if the pod has a node selector that matches the control plane nodes.\n134\tfunc (pod *Pod) HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() bool {\n135\t\tif pod.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n136\t\t\treturn false\n137\t\t}\n138\t\trequiredSelectors := []string{utils.MasterNodeSelectorLabel, utils.ControlPlaneNodeSelectorLabel}\n139\t\tfor _, value := range requiredSelectors {\n140\t\t\tif _, ok := pod.Spec.NodeSelector[value]; ok {\n141\t\t\t\treturn true\n142\t\t\t}\n143\t\t}\n144\t\treturn false\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t// IsFromDaemonSet returns true if the pod is from a daemonSet.\n148\tfunc (pod *Pod) IsFromDaemonSet() bool {\n149\t\t// Check all ownerRef\n150\t\tfor _, ownerRef := range pod.OwnerReferences {\n151\t\t\tif ownerRef.Kind == \"DaemonSet\" && ownerRef.Controller != nil && *ownerRef.Controller {\n152\t\t\t\treturn true\n153\t\t\t}\n154\t\t}\n155\t\treturn false\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\tfunc (pod *Pod) ContainerNameFor(containerID string) (string, error) {\n159\t\t// The containerID is in the format: \"runtime://<64-hex-chars>\"\n160\t\tmatched, err := regexp.MatchString(`^.+://[a-f0-9]{64}$`, containerID)\n161\t\tif err != nil || !matched {\n162\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid container ID format: %s\", containerID)\n163\t\t}\n164\t\tfor _, container := range pod.PodObject().Status.ContainerStatuses {\n165\t\t\tif container.ContainerID == containerID {\n166\t\t\t\treturn container.Name, nil\n167\t\t\t}\n168\t\t}\n169\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"container with ID %s not found in pod %s\", containerID, pod.Name)"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"c89edca5-23cc-42a2-a261-8a92b72d6757","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:33:14.384Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/models/pod.go","content":"\t}\n}\n\n// HasControlPlaneNodeSelector returns true if the pod has a node selector that matches the control plane nodes.\nfunc (pod *Pod) HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() bool {\n\tif pod.Spec.NodeSelector == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\trequiredSelectors := []string{utils.MasterNodeSelectorLabel, utils.ControlPlaneNodeSelectorLabel}\n\tfor _, value := range requiredSelectors {\n\t\tif _, ok := pod.Spec.NodeSelector[value]; ok {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsFromDaemonSet returns true if the pod is from a daemonSet.\nfunc (pod *Pod) IsFromDaemonSet() bool {\n\t// Check all ownerRef\n\tfor _, ownerRef := range pod.OwnerReferences {\n\t\tif ownerRef.Kind == \"DaemonSet\" && ownerRef.Controller != nil && *ownerRef.Controller {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc (pod *Pod) ContainerNameFor(containerID string) (string, error) {\n\t// The containerID is in the format: \"runtime://<64-hex-chars>\"\n\tmatched, err := regexp.MatchString(`^.+://[a-f0-9]{64}$`, containerID)\n\tif err != nil || !matched {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid container ID format: %s\", containerID)\n\t}\n\tfor _, container := range pod.PodObject().Status.ContainerStatuses {\n\t\tif container.ContainerID == containerID {\n\t\t\treturn container.Name, nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"container with ID %s not found in pod %s\", containerID, pod.Name)","numLines":40,"startLine":130,"totalLines":171}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01RAXLLjGRF21HUu5DFVtW4n","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a clear picture. The `shouldIgnorePod` logic does NOT exclude `openshift-kube-apiserver` by namespace name — it checks `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\")` which won't match. So a pod in `openshift-kube-apiserver` will get the scheduling gate UNLESS the `ClusterPodPlacementConfig` has a `NamespaceSelector` that excludes it at the Kubernetes webhook configuration level.\n\nBut in the test environment, the `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` is hardcoded (in `getMutatingWebHook()`) without a `NamespaceSelector`, so pods in any namespace go through the webhook. The `shouldIgnorePod` function is the in-code filter.\n\nLooking at this more carefully — the task says the pod should NOT be assigned the scheduling gate in `openshift-kube-apiserver`. But based on the current code, it WOULD be gated (since `openshift-kube-apiserver` doesn't match any exclusion in `shouldIgnorePod`). \n\nWait, let me re-read — the task says to verify that a pod created in `openshift-kube-apiserver` is NOT assigned the scheduling gate \"even when ClusterPodPlacementConfig has a namespaceSelector that matches all namespaces.\" This implies there should be some mechanism that prevents gating in `openshift-kube-apiserver`. Maybe there's additional handling I haven't found yet, or perhaps this is testing the `kube-` prefix behavior and the assumption is wrong.\n\nActually, let me reconsider: `openshift-kube-apiserver` does NOT start with `kube-`. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01Teyi6tsAsQAFMmB3VVdWVz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I see it clearly. In production, the `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` uses `clusterPodPlacementConfig.Spec.NamespaceSelector` as the webhook's `NamespaceSelector`. When the CPPC has a `NamespaceSelector` matching all namespaces, the webhook fires for all namespaces. But the `shouldIgnorePod` function at line 438 checks `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\")`.\n\n`openshift-kube-apiserver` does NOT start with `kube-`, so it would NOT be filtered by `shouldIgnorePod`. This means a pod in `openshift-kube-apiserver` would actually GET the scheduling gate, not be excluded.\n\nUnless... the task is asking me to write a test that verifies a behavior that the code implements via some other mechanism. Let me look more carefully at the `shouldIgnorePod` or maybe there's an `openshift-` prefix check too:"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1317,"cache_read_input_tokens":65423,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1317,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"c104fb8c-ab9e-4f0c-9e55-81615d18c311","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:33:49.078Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDPtAMXdmP2i4snKgbRm"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01Teyi6tsAsQAFMmB3VVdWVz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_019DhpthAEUPQPNKmhYSzXdd","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go","offset":435,"limit":10}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1317,"cache_read_input_tokens":65423,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1317,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"375c1083-b211-42c2-bebf-6c3aef2a839c","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:33:50.695Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDPtAMXdmP2i4snKgbRm"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_019DhpthAEUPQPNKmhYSzXdd","type":"tool_result","content":"435\t//   - preferred affinity is already configured, OR\n436\t//   - both CPPC and all matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin disabled\n437\tfunc (pod *Pod) shouldIgnorePod(cppc *v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n438\t\treturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n439\t\t\tpod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" || pod.HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() || pod.IsFromDaemonSet() ||\n440\t\t\tpod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() &&\n441\t\t\t\t(pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() ||\n442\t\t\t\t\t(!cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) && !pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs)))\n443\t}\n444\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"cbf07ba3-80f0-4ac9-bb41-5f2298cc35ce","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:33:50.703Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go","content":"//   - preferred affinity is already configured, OR\n//   - both CPPC and all matching PPCs have the NodeAffinityScoring plugin disabled\nfunc (pod *Pod) shouldIgnorePod(cppc *v1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []v1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) bool {\n\treturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n\t\tpod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" || pod.HasControlPlaneNodeSelector() || pod.IsFromDaemonSet() ||\n\t\tpod.isNodeSelectorConfiguredForArchitecture() &&\n\t\t\t(pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() ||\n\t\t\t\t(!cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) && !pod.hasMatchingPPCWithPlugin(matchingPPCs)))\n}\n","numLines":10,"startLine":435,"totalLines":565}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01U12uEQotty82UPzPKCcyQf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_014ZVc7Z7nQESN3gqUFWqxTG","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"grep -rn \"openshift-\" pkg/utils/ --include=\"*.go\" | head -20","description":"Check for openshift- prefix handling in utils"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":650,"cache_read_input_tokens":66740,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":650,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":26},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"90a42e3c-0b0f-4706-b13e-b4cb8ff5dd16","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:33:55.612Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDPtxAL9nqAB4sMukccj"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01Tc7c7FnuPLfxk2CbkL5q4J","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"OK so there's no `openshift-` exclusion anywhere. The task asks me to write a test verifying that pods in `openshift-kube-apiserver` are NOT gated. The thing is, in the test environment, pods in that namespace **would** get gated because:\n1. The webhook has no `NamespaceSelector` in the test\n2. `shouldIgnorePod` doesn't filter `openshift-kube-apiserver`\n\nBut wait — the task says to verify the pod is NOT assigned the scheduling gate. Maybe the intent is for `openshift-kube-apiserver` namespace pods to get the scheduling gate, and this test is meant to verify specific behavior. Let me re-read the task more carefully.\n\nThe task says: \"verifying that a pod created in the namespace openshift-kube-apiserver is NOT assigned the scheduling gate, even when ClusterPodPlacementConfig has a namespaceSelector that matches all namespaces.\"\n\nThis could mean the test expects the `shouldIgnorePod` to already exclude `openshift-kube-apiserver` (perhaps via the `kube-` prefix — but it starts with `openshift-`, not `kube-`). OR perhaps the test is expected to set up the CPPC with a `NamespaceSelector` that explicitly matches all namespaces but the test should demonstrate that the in-code check still excludes it. But the code doesn't exclude it...\n\nLet me look at this from another angle — maybe the test is supposed to verify existing behavior where the pod gets the scheduling gate but then has it removed. But the task explicitly says \"NOT assigned the scheduling gate\".\n\nGiven that the task asks me to write the test, and the test follows the existing pattern of creating a pod and checking if it's ignored (like the `nodeName` test), I think the most reasonable interpretation is: the test should match the existing pattern for ignored pods, similar to how the `nodeName` test works. The key difference is that a pod in `openshift-kube-apiserver` may not actually be excluded by current code. But the task is asking me to write the test as specified — the test verifies the expected behavior.\n\nLet me check whether creating a namespace `openshift-kube-apiserver` might cause the webhook to skip it. Looking at the `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` in the test — it has no `NamespaceSelector`, so all namespaces match. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01L5Zkwa5qUEFSrDZH1KBTFJ","type":"tool_result","content":"55\t\t\tdefer GinkgoRecover()\n56\t\t\terr := <-ch\n57\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), descr, err)\n58\t\t}()\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\tfunc EnsureNamespaces(ctx context.Context, client client.Client, namespaces ...string) {\n62\t\tvar err error\n63\t\tfor _, ns := range namespaces {\n64\t\t\tnamespace := &corev1.Namespace{\n65\t\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n66\t\t\t\t\tName: ns,\n67\t\t\t\t},\n68\t\t\t}\n69\t\t\terr = client.Create(ctx, namespace)\n70\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n71\t\t}\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\tfunc FromEnvTestConfig(cfg *rest.Config) api.Config {\n75\t\tclusterName := \"envtest\"\n76\t\tcontextName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s@%s\", cfg.Username, clusterName)\n77\t\tc := api.Config{\n78\t\t\tClusters: map[string]*api.Cluster{\n79\t\t\t\tclusterName: {\n80\t\t\t\t\tServer:                   cfg.Host,\n81\t\t\t\t\tCertificateAuthorityData: cfg.CAData,\n82\t\t\t\t},\n83\t\t\t},\n84\t\t\tContexts: map[string]*api.Context{"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"8c661873-4a40-4898-b458-9e867d2f265f","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:34:24.034Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/testing/framework/utils.go","content":"\t\tdefer GinkgoRecover()\n\t\terr := <-ch\n\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), descr, err)\n\t}()\n}\n\nfunc EnsureNamespaces(ctx context.Context, client client.Client, namespaces ...string) {\n\tvar err error\n\tfor _, ns := range namespaces {\n\t\tnamespace := &corev1.Namespace{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n\t\t\t\tName: ns,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\terr = client.Create(ctx, namespace)\n\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc FromEnvTestConfig(cfg *rest.Config) api.Config {\n\tclusterName := \"envtest\"\n\tcontextName := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s@%s\", cfg.Username, clusterName)\n\tc := api.Config{\n\t\tClusters: map[string]*api.Cluster{\n\t\t\tclusterName: {\n\t\t\t\tServer:                   cfg.Host,\n\t\t\t\tCertificateAuthorityData: cfg.CAData,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tContexts: map[string]*api.Context{","numLines":30,"startLine":55,"totalLines":206}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_017zuYcwKVeddHj9LTQwW1Kt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a full understanding. Let me write the test. The existing test pattern for the webhook test file (the `nodeName` test) simply creates a pod and checks that the pod was created without error. Looking at this test, it verifies that `shouldIgnorePod` causes the webhook to not gate the pod (the pod is accepted without scheduling gate). The assertion is implicit — if the pod with `nodeName` is accepted without error and without the scheduling gate, the webhook properly ignored it.\n\nFor the `openshift-kube-apiserver` namespace test, I need to:\n1. Create the namespace `openshift-kube-apiserver` (it needs to exist for the pod creation to succeed)\n2. Create a pod in that namespace\n3. Verify the pod does NOT have the scheduling gate\n\nHowever, I realize there might be a catch: the `shouldIgnorePod` code doesn't actually exclude `openshift-kube-apiserver`. But since the task is asking me to write the test, and the existing test for `nodeName` is minimal (just verifies the pod creates without error), let me follow the same pattern. The test verifies behavior — if the behavior isn't implemented yet, the test would fail, which is fine (test-driven development).\n\nLet me also look at how the existing test verifies that a pod is NOT gated — does the pod reconciler test's assertion pattern apply here?\n\nLooking at the `nodeName` test more carefully — it creates a pod with `nodeName` set. The webhook's `shouldIgnorePod` returns true, so it just patches the pod with labels but without the scheduling gate. The test just checks that the pod creation succeeds without error. It doesn't explicitly assert that the scheduling gate is absent.\n\nFor a more thorough test, I should verify the scheduling gate is not present. Let me write the test following the patterns in the reconciler test for asserting gate absence.\n\nHere's my complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## File to modify: `internal/controller/podplacement/scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook_test.go`\n\nThe full file should be:\n\n```go\npackage podplacement\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t. \"github.com/onsi/ginkgo/v2\"\n\t. \"github.com/onsi/gomega\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\timgspecv1 \"github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/specs-go/v1\"\n\tcrclient \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/builder\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/testing/image/fake/registry\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n)\n\nvar _ = Describe(\"Internal/Controller/PodPlacement/scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook\", func() {\n\tWhen(\"The scheduling gat mutating webhook\", func() {\n\t\tContext(\"is handling the mutation of pods\", func() {\n\t\t\tIt(\"should ignore pods with a non-empty nodeName\", func() {\n\t\t\t\tpod := builder.NewPod().\n\t\t\t\t\tWithContainersImages(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s/%s:latest\", registryAddress,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tregistry.PublicRepo, registry.ComputeNameByMediaType(imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageIndex))).\n\t\t\t\t\tWithGenerateName(\"test-pod-\").\n\t\t\t\t\tWithNamespace(\"test-namespace\").\n\t\t\t\t\tWithNodeName(\"test-node-name\").\n\t\t\t\t\tBuild()\n\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Create(ctx, pod)\n\t\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to create the pod\", err)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t})\n\t\tContext(\"is handling a pod in the openshift-kube-apiserver namespace\", func() {\n\t\t\tIt(\"should not assign the scheduling gate even when the CPPC namespaceSelector matches all namespaces\", func() {\n\t\t\t\tpod := builder.NewPod().\n\t\t\t\t\tWithContainersImages(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s/%s:latest\", registryAddress,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tregistry.PublicRepo, registry.ComputeNameByMediaType(imgspecv1.MediaTypeImageIndex))).\n\t\t\t\t\tWithGenerateName(\"test-pod-\").\n\t\t\t\t\tWithNamespace(\"openshift-kube-apiserver\").\n\t\t\t\t\tBuild()\n\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Create(ctx, pod)\n\t\t\t\tExpect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to create the pod\", err)\n\t\t\t\tEventually(func(g Gomega) {\n\t\t\t\t\terr := k8sClient.Get(ctx, crclient.ObjectKeyFromObject(pod), pod)\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), \"failed to get the pod\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Expect(pod.Spec.SchedulingGates).NotTo(ContainElement(corev1.PodSchedulingGate{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tName: utils.SchedulingGateName,\n\t\t\t\t\t}), \"the scheduling gate should not be assigned to pods in the openshift-kube-apiserver namespace\")\n\t\t\t\t}).Should(Succeed())\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t})\n\t})\n})\n```\n\n## File to modify: `internal/controller/podplacement/suite_test.go`\n\nIn the `seedK8S()` function, add `\"openshift-kube-apiserver\"` to the `EnsureNamespaces` call so the namespace exists before the test creates pods in it.\n\n**Change at line 306:**\n\n```go\n// Before:\ntestingutils.EnsureNamespaces(ctx, k8sClient, \"openshift-config\", \"test-namespace\")\n\n// After:\ntestingutils.EnsureNamespaces(ctx, k8sClient, \"openshift-config\", \"test-namespace\", \"openshift-kube-apiserver\")\n```\n\n## Important note on code behavior\n\nThe `shouldIgnorePod` function in `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go:437` currently only excludes namespaces matching `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\")`. The namespace `openshift-kube-apiserver` starts with `openshift-`, not `kube-`, so it is **not** excluded by the current code.\n\nFor this test to pass, you would also need to update `shouldIgnorePod` at line 438 to exclude `openshift-` prefixed namespaces. The change would be:\n\n**File: `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go`, line 438:**\n\n```go\n// Before:\nreturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n\n// After:\nreturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"openshift-\") ||\n```\n\n## Commands to run\n\n```bash\n# Run the specific integration test\nmake test-unit   # or whatever the project's test target is\n\n# Or run just the webhook tests:\ncd internal/controller/podplacement\ngo test -v -tags integration -run \"scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook\" ./...\n```\n\nTo check what test targets are available:\n```bash\ngrep -E \"^[a-zA-Z_-]+:\" Makefile | head -20\n```\n\n## Summary\n\nThree files need changes:\n1. **`internal/controller/podplacement/scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook_test.go`** — add a new `Context` block with an `It` that creates a pod in `openshift-kube-apiserver` and asserts the scheduling gate is not present\n2. **`internal/controller/podplacement/suite_test.go`** line 306 — add `\"openshift-kube-apiserver\"` to the `EnsureNamespaces` call\n3. **`internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go`** line 438 — add `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"openshift-\")` to the `shouldIgnorePod` exclusion conditions (needed for the test to pass)"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":520,"cache_read_input_tokens":68592,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":520,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":3},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753","uuid":"040845ef-cf54-4630-ae4a-31b09c4be4e7","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:34:58.701Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDPwMqvR9dg4fhwFHgsi"}

in it.\n\n**Change at line 306:**\n\n```go\n// Before:\ntestingutils.EnsureNamespaces(ctx, k8sClient, \"openshift-config\", \"test-namespace\")\n\n// After:\ntestingutils.EnsureNamespaces(ctx, k8sClient, \"openshift-config\", \"test-namespace\", \"openshift-kube-apiserver\")\n```\n\n## Important note on code behavior\n\nThe `shouldIgnorePod` function in `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go:437` currently only excludes namespaces matching `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\")`. The namespace `openshift-kube-apiserver` starts with `openshift-`, not `kube-`, so it is **not** excluded by the current code.\n\nFor this test to pass, you would also need to update `shouldIgnorePod` at line 438 to exclude `openshift-` prefixed namespaces. The change would be:\n\n**File: `internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go`, line 438:**\n\n```go\n// Before:\nreturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") ||\n\n// After:\nreturn utils.Namespace() == pod.Namespace || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"kube-\") || strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"openshift-\") ||\n```\n\n## Commands to run\n\n```bash\n# Run the specific integration test\nmake test-unit   # or whatever the project's test target is\n\n# Or run just the webhook tests:\ncd internal/controller/podplacement\ngo test -v -tags integration -run \"scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook\" ./...\n```\n\nTo check what test targets are available:\n```bash\ngrep -E \"^[a-zA-Z_-]+:\" Makefile | head -20\n```\n\n## Summary\n\nThree files need changes:\n1. **`internal/controller/podplacement/scheduling_gate_mutating_webhook_test.go`** \u2014 add a new `Context` block with an `It` that creates a pod in `openshift-kube-apiserver` and asserts the scheduling gate is not present\n2. **`internal/controller/podplacement/suite_test.go`** line 306 \u2014 add `\"openshift-kube-apiserver\"` to the `EnsureNamespaces` call\n3. **`internal/controller/podplacement/pod_model.go`** line 438 \u2014 add `strings.HasPrefix(pod.Namespace, \"openshift-\")` to the `shouldIgnorePod` exclusion conditions (needed for the test to pass)", "stop_reason": "end_turn", "session_id": "08825ee3-2822-4cb7-9a97-2ce6ebb55753", "total_cost_usd": 1.3985927499999997, "usage": {"input_tokens": 466, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 46085, "cache_read_input_tokens": 1781213, "output_tokens": 8705, "server_tool_use": {"web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0}, "service_tier": "standard", "cache_creation": {"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 0, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 46085}, "inference_geo": "", "iterations": [], "speed": "standard"}, "modelUsage": {"claude-opus-4-6": {"inputTokens": 466, "outputTokens": 8705, "cacheReadInputTokens": 1781213, "cacheCreationInputTokens": 46085, "webSearchRequests": 0, "costUSD": 1.3985927499999997, "contextWindow": 200000, "maxOutputTokens": 64000}}, "permission_denials": [], "terminal_reason": "completed", "fast_mode_state": "off", "uuid": "cc07daf0-0e4c-4e9b-b142-8da0ff944c4f", "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:34:58.771Z"}
mto-003-add-metric (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness5

The implementation is technically correct and follows all the required conventions:

  1. Metric name: Uses the correct mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total name with the proper controller prefix.
  2. Metric type: Correctly uses *prometheus.CounterVec (not Counter or Gauge) with the image_ref label.
  3. Registration: Follows the existing pattern in the codebase - the metric is created in initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() and registered via metrics2.Registry.MustRegister(), consistent with the existing metrics in that file.
  4. Instrumentation location: Correctly placed in the cache-hit path in cache.go (inside the if architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache block), not in the registry-fetch path.
  5. Import paths: The import alias ctrlmetrics for github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics is correctly structured for this module.
  6. Go types: All types are correct - *prometheus.CounterVec as a package-level var, proper WithLabelValues(imageReference).Inc() call.
  7. Nil guard: The nil check on ImageCacheHitsCounter is a reasonable safety measure for test scenarios where metrics aren't initialized, showing good engineering judgment.
  8. Existing API usage: All referenced methods (NewCounterVec, WithLabelValues, Inc, MustRegister) exist in the prometheus client library. The code fits naturally into the existing file structure.
consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/1 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence5

The implementation follows all applicable conventions exactly:

  1. Metric naming: Uses the correct mto_ppo_ctrl_ prefix (mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total), matching the existing controller metrics pattern.
  2. Metric type: Uses prometheus.NewCounterVec with the image_ref label, which is the correct type for a monotonically increasing count metric.
  3. Registration pattern: The metric is declared as a package-level variable in the existing internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go file, created inside the initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() function (following the sync.Once pattern), and registered via metrics2.Registry.MustRegister — exactly matching the existing pattern for other metrics in that file.
  4. Instrumentation location: The counter is incremented in the cache-hit branch of GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet in pkg/image/cache.go, which is the correct cache return path (not the registry fetch path).
  5. Nil guard: The if ctrlmetrics.ImageCacheHitsCounter != nil guard is a thoughtful addition that prevents panics when the cache is used in test contexts where InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics() hasn't been called — this shows awareness of the lazy initialization pattern used in the codebase.
  6. Import aliasing: Uses ctrlmetrics alias to avoid collision with the existing metrics import, which is clean and idiomatic.

No convention violations were identified. A senior maintainer would not leave convention-related comments in code review.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check4

The implementation is well-grounded and follows existing patterns correctly. Let me analyze each aspect:

  1. Packages: The import paths used (github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics, github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics, github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils, sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics, github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus, github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable) are all consistent with what would exist in this repository based on the reference implementation context.
  2. Metric naming: Uses mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total which follows the correct mto_ppo_ctrl_ prefix convention.
  3. Metric type: Correctly uses prometheus.NewCounterVec with []string{"image_ref"} label, matching the reference specification.
  4. Registration pattern: Uses metrics2.Registry.MustRegister() which follows the existing pattern shown in the file (using sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics aliased as metrics2).
  5. File paths: Both internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go and pkg/image/cache.go are referenced as existing files that are being modified, consistent with the reference implementation.
  6. Existing variables and methods: The existing variables (TimeToProcessPod, TimeToProcessGatedPod, etc.), the initPodPlacementControllerMetrics function, the cacheProxy struct and its methods, metrics.InspectionGauge, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss, utils.HistogramObserve, marshaledImagePullSecrets, newRegistryInspector — all appear to be consistent with the existing codebase patterns.
  7. Cache instrumentation location: Correctly placed in the cache hit path (inside the if architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache block), not in the registry fetch path.
  8. Nil guard: The nil check on ctrlmetrics.ImageCacheHitsCounter is a reasonable safety measure since metrics are initialized lazily via sync.Once.

The only minor concern is whether all the existing metric variables and helper functions referenced (like metrics.InspectionGauge, metrics.InitCommonMetrics(), initCommonMetrics(), utils.Buckets()) exactly match the real codebase, but these are presented as part of existing code being preserved, and they follow consistent naming patterns. The implementation does not invent non-existent references — it creates new additions (the counter variable and its increment) while preserving existing code structure.

task_success5

The agent's implementation matches the reference in all key criteria: (1) Correct metric name mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total with the proper mto_ppo_ctrl_ prefix. (2) Uses prometheus.NewCounterVec (not Counter or Gauge) with the image_ref label. (3) Registration follows the existing pattern in internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go using metrics2.Registry.MustRegister, consistent with how other metrics in that file are registered. (4) Instrumented in the cache hit return path in pkg/image/cache.go, not in the registry fetch path. The nil guard is a sensible defensive addition. The only minor stylistic difference from the reference is using init() vs the existing initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() pattern, but the agent correctly followed the actual codebase convention of lazy initialization via sync.Once, which is arguably more correct than the reference's suggestion of using init().

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs are very similar and produce essentially the same implementation. Let me compare them against the reference implementation:

File 1: internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go

  • Both A and B add ImageCacheHitsCounter/ImageCacheHitsTotal as *prometheus.CounterVec with the correct metric name mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total and label image_ref.
  • Both create it with prometheus.NewCounterVec and add it to MustRegister.
  • Both follow the existing pattern correctly.
  • Minor naming difference: A uses ImageCacheHitsCounter, B uses ImageCacheHitsTotal. Neither name matters for correctness since both are valid exported names.

File 2: pkg/image/cache.go

  • Both import the controller metrics package with alias ctrlmetrics.
  • Both increment the counter in the cache-hit branch.
  • Key difference: Output A uses a nil guard (if ctrlmetrics.ImageCacheHitsCounter != nil) before incrementing, while Output B calls ctrlmetrics.ImageCacheHitsTotal.With(prometheus.Labels{...}).Inc() directly without a nil guard.
  • Output A uses .WithLabelValues(imageReference).Inc() while Output B uses .With(prometheus.Labels{"image_ref": imageReference}).Inc(). Both are functionally equivalent.
  • Output B additionally imports "github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus" to use prometheus.Labels, while Output A avoids this extra import by using WithLabelValues.

Nil guard consideration: Output A adds a nil guard which is a defensive programming practice but adds unnecessary complexity. The controller metrics initialization happens via InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics() which is called during controller setup (pod_reconciler.go:72), and the cache is only used through the controller flow. However, in tests that directly use pkg/image without initializing controller metrics, this nil guard would prevent a panic. This is a minor stylistic trade-off.

Correctness: Output B's approach without the nil guard is more aligned with the existing codebase pattern - none of the existing metric usages in cache.go use nil guards (e.g., metrics.InspectionGauge.Set(...) is called without checking for nil). If the metric is nil, the code should fail fast rather than silently skip instrumentation.

Did the agents actually write the files?: Output A only described the changes but did NOT actually write the files to disk (no Write/Edit tool calls). Output B also only described changes without writing them. Both outputs are equivalent in this regard - they produced the implementation as text in the conversation but neither applied the changes to the actual files.

Both implementations are functionally correct and very close. Output B is slightly preferred because:

  1. It doesn't add an unnecessary nil guard that breaks from the existing codebase pattern
  2. It uses prometheus.Labels{"image_ref": imageReference} which is slightly more explicit/self-documenting (though WithLabelValues is equally valid)
  3. Its explanation and summary are clearer and more concise

The difference is minor - both would score the same (4-5) against the reference. I'll give a slight edge to B for consistency with existing patterns.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment
task: |
  Add a new Prometheus counter metric named mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total with a
  label image_ref to internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/. Register it following
  the pattern of existing metrics in that package.

  Then instrument it in the image cache layer (pkg/image/cache.go or pkg/image/facade.go)
  so it increments each time an architecture result is returned from cache rather than
  fetched from the registry.
task_id: mto-003-add-metric
repo: multiarch-tuning-operator
category: observability
complexity: medium

    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage image\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n22\t\t\"encoding/hex\"\n23\t\t\"time\"\n24\t\n25\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics\"\n26\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n27\t\n28\t\t\"github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable\"\n29\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n30\t\n31\t\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n32\t)\n33\t\n34\ttype cacheProxy struct {\n35\t\tregistryInspector IRegistryInspector\n36\t\timageRefsCache    *expirable.LRU[string, sets.Set[string]] // LRU cache with expirable keys\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string,\n40\t\tskipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (sets.Set[string], error) {\n41\t\tmetrics.InitCommonMetrics()\n42\t\tmetrics.InspectionGauge.Set(float64(c.imageRefsCache.Len()))\n43\t\tnow := time.Now()\n44\t\tauthJSON, err := marshaledImagePullSecrets(imageReference, secrets)\n45\t\tif err != nil {\n46\t\t\treturn nil, err\n47\t\t}\n48\t\n49\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues(\"imageReference\", imageReference)\n50\t\thash := computeHash(imageReference, authJSON)\n51\t\tif architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache {\n52\t\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache hit\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n53\t\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit)\n54\t\t\treturn architectures, nil\n55\t\t}\n56\t\tarchitectures, err := c.registryInspector.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, true, secrets)\n57\t\tif err != nil {\n58\t\t\treturn nil, err\n59\t\t}\n60\t\n61\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache miss...adding to cache\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n62\t\tif !skipCache {\n63\t\t\tc.imageRefsCache.Add(hash, architectures)\n64\t\t}\n65\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss)\n66\t\treturn architectures, nil\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetRegistryInspector() IRegistryInspector {\n70\t\treturn c.registryInspector\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t// clearCache purges the image metadata cache\n74\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) clearCache() {\n75\t\tc.imageRefsCache.Purge()\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\tfunc newCacheProxy() *cacheProxy {\n79\t\treturn &cacheProxy{\n80\t\t\tregistryInspector: newRegistryInspector(),\n81\t\t\timageRefsCache:    expirable.NewLRU[string, sets.Set[string]](256, nil, time.Hour*6),\n82\t\t}\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\tfunc computeHash(imageReference string, secrets []byte) string {\n86\t\th := sha256.New()\n87\t\th.Write([]byte(imageReference))\n88\t\th.Write([]byte{0})\n89\t\th.Write(secrets)\n90\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n91\t}\n92\t",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:32:56.823Z"
  },

    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage image\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"sync\"\n22\t\n23\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n24\t)\n25\t\n26\tvar (\n27\t\tsingletonImageFacade *Facade\n28\t\t// once is used for lazy initialization of the singletonImageFacade\n29\t\tonce sync.Once\n30\t)\n31\t\n32\ttype Facade struct {\n33\t\tinspectionCache       ICache\n34\t\tstoreGlobalPullSecret func(pullSecret []byte)\n35\t\tclearCache            func()\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\tfunc (i *Facade) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string, skipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (architectures sets.Set[string], err error) {\n39\t\treturn i.inspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, skipCache, secrets)\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\tfunc (i *Facade) StoreGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret []byte) {\n43\t\ti.storeGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret)\n44\t\ti.clearCache()\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\tfunc newImageFacade() *Facade {\n48\t\tinspectionCache := newCacheProxy()\n49\t\treturn &Facade{\n50\t\t\tinspectionCache:       inspectionCache,\n51\t\t\tstoreGlobalPullSecret: inspectionCache.registryInspector.storeGlobalPullSecret,\n52\t\t\tclearCache:            inspectionCache.clearCache,\n53\t\t}\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\tfunc FacadeSingleton() *Facade {\n57\t\tonce.Do(func() {\n58\t\t\tsingletonImageFacade = newImageFacade()\n59\t\t})\n60\t\treturn singletonImageFacade\n61\t}\n62\t",

License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage image\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n22\t\t\"encoding/hex\"\n23\t\t\"time\"\n24\t\n25\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics\"\n26\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n27\t\n28\t\t\"github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable\"\n29\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n30\t\n31\t\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n32\t)\n33\t\n34\ttype cacheProxy struct {\n35\t\tregistryInspector IRegistryInspector\n36\t\timageRefsCache    *expirable.LRU[string, sets.Set[string]] // LRU cache with expirable keys\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string,\n40\t\tskipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (sets.Set[string], error) {\n41\t\tmetrics.InitCommonMetrics()\n42\t\tmetrics.InspectionGauge.Set(float64(c.imageRefsCache.Len()))\n43\t\tnow := time.Now()\n44\t\tauthJSON, err := marshaledImagePullSecrets(imageReference, secrets)\n45\t\tif err != nil {\n46\t\t\treturn nil, err\n47\t\t}\n48\t\n49\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues(\"imageReference\", imageReference)\n50\t\thash := computeHash(imageReference, authJSON)\n51\t\tif architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache {\n52\t\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache hit\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n53\t\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit)\n54\t\t\treturn architectures, nil\n55\t\t}\n56\t\tarchitectures, err := c.registryInspector.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, true, secrets)\n57\t\tif err != nil {\n58\t\t\treturn nil, err\n59\t\t}\n60\t\n61\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache miss...adding to cache\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n62\t\tif !skipCache {\n63\t\t\tc.imageRefsCache.Add(hash, architectures)\n64\t\t}\n65\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss)\n66\t\treturn architectures, nil\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetRegistryInspector() IRegistryInspector {\n70\t\treturn c.registryInspector\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t// clearCache purges the image metadata cache\n74\tfunc (c *cacheProxy) clearCache() {\n75\t\tc.imageRefsCache.Purge()\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\tfunc newCacheProxy() *cacheProxy {\n79\t\treturn &cacheProxy{\n80\t\t\tregistryInspector: newRegistryInspector(),\n81\t\t\timageRefsCache:    expirable.NewLRU[string, sets.Set[string]](256, nil, time.Hour*6),\n82\t\t}\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\tfunc computeHash(imageReference string, secrets []byte) string {\n86\t\th := sha256.New()\n87\t\th.Write([]byte(imageReference))\n88\t\th.Write([]byte{0})\n89\t\th.Write(secrets)\n90\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n91\t}\n92\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d0100e9-dc56-4f79-bfa8-c77a29b35692","uuid":"2a307e13-181e-4667-9206-31c0c033ede5","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:32:56.823Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/image/cache.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage image\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/image/metrics\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/golang-lru/v2/expirable\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\n\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n)\n\ntype cacheProxy struct {\n\tregistryInspector IRegistryInspector\n\timageRefsCache    *expirable.LRU[string, sets.Set[string]] // LRU cache with expirable keys\n}\n\nfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string,\n\tskipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (sets.Set[string], error) {\n\tmetrics.InitCommonMetrics()\n\tmetrics.InspectionGauge.Set(float64(c.imageRefsCache.Len()))\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\tauthJSON, err := marshaledImagePullSecrets(imageReference, secrets)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithValues(\"imageReference\", imageReference)\n\thash := computeHash(imageReference, authJSON)\n\tif architectures, ok := c.imageRefsCache.Get(hash); ok && !skipCache {\n\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache hit\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenHit)\n\t\treturn architectures, nil\n\t}\n\tarchitectures, err := c.registryInspector.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, true, secrets)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tlog.V(3).Info(\"Cache miss...adding to cache\", \"architectures\", architectures, \"hash\", hash)\n\tif !skipCache {\n\t\tc.imageRefsCache.Add(hash, architectures)\n\t}\n\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToInspectImageGivenMiss)\n\treturn architectures, nil\n}\n\nfunc (c *cacheProxy) GetRegistryInspector() IRegistryInspector {\n\treturn c.registryInspector\n}\n\n// clearCache purges the image metadata cache\nfunc (c *cacheProxy) clearCache() {\n\tc.imageRefsCache.Purge()\n}\n\nfunc newCacheProxy() *cacheProxy {\n\treturn &cacheProxy{\n\t\tregistryInspector: newRegistryInspector(),\n\t\timageRefsCache:    expirable.NewLRU[string, sets.Set[string]](256, nil, time.Hour*6),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc computeHash(imageReference string, secrets []byte) string {\n\th := sha256.New()\n\th.Write([]byte(imageReference))\n\th.Write([]byte{0})\n\th.Write(secrets)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n}\n","numLines":92,"startLine":1,"totalLines":92}}}

License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage image\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"sync\"\n22\t\n23\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n24\t)\n25\t\n26\tvar (\n27\t\tsingletonImageFacade *Facade\n28\t\t// once is used for lazy initialization of the singletonImageFacade\n29\t\tonce sync.Once\n30\t)\n31\t\n32\ttype Facade struct {\n33\t\tinspectionCache       ICache\n34\t\tstoreGlobalPullSecret func(pullSecret []byte)\n35\t\tclearCache            func()\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\tfunc (i *Facade) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string, skipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (architectures sets.Set[string], err error) {\n39\t\treturn i.inspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, skipCache, secrets)\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\tfunc (i *Facade) StoreGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret []byte) {\n43\t\ti.storeGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret)\n44\t\ti.clearCache()\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\tfunc newImageFacade() *Facade {\n48\t\tinspectionCache := newCacheProxy()\n49\t\treturn &Facade{\n50\t\t\tinspectionCache:       inspectionCache,\n51\t\t\tstoreGlobalPullSecret: inspectionCache.registryInspector.storeGlobalPullSecret,\n52\t\t\tclearCache:            inspectionCache.clearCache,\n53\t\t}\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\tfunc FacadeSingleton() *Facade {\n57\t\tonce.Do(func() {\n58\t\t\tsingletonImageFacade = newImageFacade()\n59\t\t})\n60\t\treturn singletonImageFacade\n61\t}\n62\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d0100e9-dc56-4f79-bfa8-c77a29b35692","uuid":"d392dd8c-34eb-40c6-b08a-fb26cf39598f","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:32:56.909Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/image/facade.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage image\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tsingletonImageFacade *Facade\n\t// once is used for lazy initialization of the singletonImageFacade\n\tonce sync.Once\n)\n\ntype Facade struct {\n\tinspectionCache       ICache\n\tstoreGlobalPullSecret func(pullSecret []byte)\n\tclearCache            func()\n}\n\nfunc (i *Facade) GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx context.Context, imageReference string, skipCache bool, secrets [][]byte) (architectures sets.Set[string], err error) {\n\treturn i.inspectionCache.GetCompatibleArchitecturesSet(ctx, imageReference, skipCache, secrets)\n}\n\nfunc (i *Facade) StoreGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret []byte) {\n\ti.storeGlobalPullSecret(pullSecret)\n\ti.clearCache()\n}\n\nfunc newImageFacade() *Facade {\n\tinspectionCache := newCacheProxy()\n\treturn &Facade{\n\t\tinspectionCache:       inspectionCache,\n\t\tstoreGlobalPullSecret: inspectionCache.registryInspector.storeGlobalPullSecret,\n\t\tclearCache:            inspectionCache.clearCache,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc FacadeSingleton() *Facade {\n\tonce.Do(func() {\n\t\tsingletonImageFacade = newImageFacade()\n\t})\n\treturn singletonImageFacade\n}\n","numLines":62,"startLine":1,"totalLines":62}}}
