{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01QF8vz1uy6PrdSQX5qxzZbR","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage metrics\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"sync\"\n5\t\n6\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n7\t\n8\t\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n9\t\tmetrics2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics\"\n10\t)\n11\t\n12\tvar onceCommon sync.Once\n13\t\n14\tvar (\n15\t\tInspectionGauge             prometheus.Gauge\n16\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit  prometheus.Histogram\n17\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss prometheus.Histogram\n18\t)\n19\t\n20\tfunc InitCommonMetrics() {\n21\t\tonceCommon.Do(func() {\n22\t\t\tInspectionGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(\n23\t\t\t\tprometheus.GaugeOpts{\n24\t\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_inspection_cache_size\",\n25\t\t\t\t\tHelp: \"Current size of the MTO inspection cache\",\n26\t\t\t\t},\n27\t\t\t)\n28\t\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n29\t\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n30\t\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_hit_processing_duration_seconds\",\n31\t\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache hit for MTO inspection\",\n32\t\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n33\t\t\t\t})\n34\t\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n35\t\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n36\t\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_miss_processing_duration_seconds\",\n37\t\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache miss for MTO inspection\",\n38\t\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n39\t\t\t\t})\n40\t\n41\t\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(InspectionGauge)\n42\t\t})\n43\t}\n44\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8d0100e9-dc56-4f79-bfa8-c77a29b35692","uuid":"0207a960-b636-4dae-b717-485804681b07","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:32:57.080Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/pkg/image/metrics/common_metrics.go","content":"package metrics\n\nimport (\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n\tmetrics2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics\"\n)\n\nvar onceCommon sync.Once\n\nvar (\n\tInspectionGauge             prometheus.Gauge\n\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit  prometheus.Histogram\n\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss prometheus.Histogram\n)\n\nfunc InitCommonMetrics() {\n\tonceCommon.Do(func() {\n\t\tInspectionGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(\n\t\t\tprometheus.GaugeOpts{\n\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_inspection_cache_size\",\n\t\t\t\tHelp: \"Current size of the MTO inspection cache\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_hit_processing_duration_seconds\",\n\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache hit for MTO inspection\",\n\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_miss_processing_duration_seconds\",\n\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache miss for MTO inspection\",\n\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t\t})\n\n\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(InspectionGauge)\n\t})\n}\n","numLines":44,"startLine":1,"totalLines":44}}}

{"type": "system", "subtype": "thinking_tokens", "estimated_tokens": 537, "estimated_tokens_delta": 209, "uuid": "e8d21618-2324-42dc-b709-a95f6ed10afa", "session_id": "8d0100e9-dc56-4f79-bfa8-c77a29b35692", "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:33:13.484Z"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_014oJ4nJskKZx4LzCA14tMsX","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I have a clear picture. Let me check where the metric should be defined. The task says to add it to `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/`, but looking at the existing pattern:\n\n1. `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go` - defines controller-specific metrics with `mto_ppo_ctrl_` prefix\n2. `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/common_metrics.go` - defines common metrics (GatedPodsGauge)\n3. `pkg/image/metrics/common_metrics.go` - defines image-level metrics (InspectionGauge, cache hit/miss histograms)\nThe new metric `mto_ppo_ctrl_image_cache_hits_total` has the controller prefix, so it belongs in `controller.go`, but there's a problem—it needs to be incremented in `pkg/image/cache.go` which imports `pkg/image/metrics`, not the controller metrics package. I need to figure out how to bridge this dependency issue while following the task's requirement to add the metric to the controller metrics directory. I need to check the module structure in go.mod to understand whether importing from `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics` into `pkg/image` would violate Go's internal package visibility rules.","signature":"Et8QCnMIDxACGAIqQF8RZJjP8qwwUx80hOb9RXyr7rAoJ0KXBbkFR4bUvyL/VSnC2SEomardbD95bLMxwa3HoOjyxkwlDiz8WTmQee8yD2NsYXVkZS1vcHVzLTQtNjgAQgh0aGlua2luZ1oMODM1MTY4MDcxMTUyEgyi70SELcTloDsK1DoaDLarlwXWR+d7JjyuHSIwSELHBl7EH6mjP6Lvw3Gvwp/i8Y0dROmKhu2JSjk/W1ZhT9fVHLC3ZKYdZbZRdZIJKpkPSQXwSDiI3VqtCTlTbmkKN0P+2/UYIxdaEScYGC8WX10RYrhcqAkNwJOAjSBihB5hOU06hjIdixfdJYKg5d9CIgnakC5P2qKnRWjdU8IdN5qkcT7ga5qL1ECv8xtpL0Nt4eQU6U1UF1+RT56mkhBFsiqVRpAbhkMz0mMlGZcjygX31xPEkXIsmws862LV3qFGHStPl+u2cHXp+OUCylqi5vWMLVSRZUy9VWI4URdTiqWCnD0VtuerM3nXwkEExG2VMQL/eOmGeID2TrllUZhEXc+0Y6pvWuLBCJGIabQeRqR5HRNKV4KTIG0v3AeTvcgpus9S1lzNM2VmVSqUMiM5Wm+6CA2Td6nKFyWME7TBJ/n6jlW0jjF6fVtCpUZlOpqLlRYYEw2+Tv2P14yjsvqslE1peFg5k/hIuwWwU0yELFU9aW6TX1QLwZqlXYeWyqL61SPGrxoMSzAvYPrTnRg91dZ1w8hS6Na1BHaGXcc1JuCRMBoZEwrl1cxeyrlMTSJi7/wxijxagu323ibWGVZgPe77vVa8QzzFye7LYojbxIbZ3O9z0vtThpZGzOxMzaBV0vIpY+kfVHC2ELkQSbltXTq4Ii3OhP+xdG3GHTMC8saj+F6hsF/HMGMY4cgxJoXw1CbJYlA/Bgj8j+XNOKTQuvkAqyCBgjyFIWOrJ/FQJ+yvzuXoSnrL6AX1Ah4v1V7YopNNj8jiz7c6a8v5hllJvu4F6BniWq2okTFXsicY/dXuRzlYiGlvYKzuQVrZXrnkRXC4J+guqB0Y3noIsG5uFyaCWxb4jggRxZ+lJDMIIc5ODDm9yhg+t5CJtJE07S5hv11NQiiH8Vuw08DzO+EJ8Rhdiu5AU0i+6K9hURSW/QzvT1pfH/f4r00PWxinHWKY/R6uq95BRP0LXIFfIAdzn71HMAmHt+C6fAL5Sri6bgZf74qmx1/EGC2eFk03ilCD5heu585RS3VivQBLxsord61ygHdcElx7/TOu3y7+eYyQgUMYlxC68AaNKYSle8wwQaf0lWcoPCAfSXUuSyPM02V98PdjQswiqu2wiu3hCNHXW17bZDq2/t8dR4ROVtHhC2az9SHEjQ2JFY21lSn2+qIIxNbUDwJTwujdlYRQgynqCFppcI6H3JG8OtJ7HjNH9rC4SlBFJISiUl01MtOX1dDUQPN5mWrHsQU0pnAMm3OT2zev2j0YPKxZpz+0PxW9wEOacvdkAVzS1oFRTMMD+7fVpMXANwtFXQYAG3nA36RXd/U6v1OjirA2JTvxhnaHhvNw5Hwcv+SG32TN8oFr+1yFYkvOOKiRNMrTC7qIGf4HRQkBd0Kw7HOOj/QHVfy1Q07vpIp6iKESyiEaoCgBR/WQBCxTCLoaJirC6qWCfSsdfbN6TVWgkZ1FM4LACNMSK2i0kX2hXlAVS7a+RPsTK8ScM3oWvG2kzI5AU+aORS4qf2EGsmzBzugD9NFOl/eCOEvznnF5/NsKdn7PUu30ZsD1rSR7yZLcMcvXQ1TFE1zcx6pDshluiQsxiXUdf2A1AgEMAO2MWjFUKNTUh+cWvzpCBxz0IUWM303TmMUXGwQxb5VgjSGldg/kXp4e76OBpWJS6JnW0tmsIETGIYhVCrzyWXshFPBHcFVEw1gM8CcsflfFRyBeFFUPWKr3Jxx8xNiANQpisuMwfhQr6V5HAGR6yiPBJgh3bV4QTHC590+ajr+ mto-004-webhook-validation (3/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness3

The agent's implementation has several notable deviations from the reference and potential correctness issues:

  1. Does not use field.Invalid: The reference explicitly requires using field.Invalid for structured Kubernetes API errors with field.NewPath(...). The agent's implementation just returns a raw error from ValidateArchitecturesSet(), which is a plain error, not a structured field error. This is explicitly listed as a Score 5 criterion.
  2. Wrong ValidateArchitecturesSet() signature: The agent calls cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.ValidateArchitecturesSet() as if it returns (something, error) with two return values (if _, err := ...), but the reference shows it returns just error. This would cause a compilation error if the method only returns one value.
  3. No Ginkgo/builder-based tests: The reference specifically requires "unit test using builder API and Ginkgo structure" (e.g., Describe/Context/It blocks, builder.NewClusterPodPlacementConfig().WithNodeAffinityScoring(...).Build()). The agent uses standard Go testing.T table-driven tests instead, which is a valid testing approach but doesn't match the required pattern.
  4. Incorrect type references: The agent uses plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm in tests, but the reference uses commonv1alpha1.Platform with an Architecture field. These may or may not be the same type depending on the codebase structure, but the naming discrepancy suggests potential type mismatch.
  5. Positive aspects: The nil-guards are correct, validation is applied in both Create and Update paths, and the overall approach of calling ValidateArchitecturesSet() rather than reimplementing it is correct. The test coverage is thorough with good edge cases.

The lack of field.Invalid structured errors and the potentially wrong method signature are significant issues that would either fail to compile or fail to meet the API conventions required.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/2 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence2

The agent's implementation deviates from multiple key repository conventions:

  1. No Ginkgo test structure: The repo convention explicitly requires Describe/Context/It BDD hierarchy with Ginkgo, but the agent wrote standard Go table-driven tests using func Test* and t.Run. This is a major convention violation.
  2. No builder API usage: The repo convention requires test code to use fluent builders from pkg/testing/builder (e.g., builder.NewClusterPodPlacementConfig().WithNodeAffinityScoring(...).Build()), but the agent constructed raw struct literals throughout all tests.
  3. No field.Invalid structured error: The reference implementation specifies using field.Invalid with field.NewPath for structured Kubernetes API errors and accumulating into field.ErrorList. The agent's implementation simply delegates to ValidateArchitecturesSet() and returns its raw error, missing the structured error pattern with field.Invalid.
  4. Different validation approach: While the agent does call ValidateArchitecturesSet() (good) and has nil-guards (good), and applies validation in both create and update paths (good), the overall approach of refactoring an existing validate method rather than adding to ValidateCreate/ValidateUpdate with field.Invalid differs from the reference. However, the agent's note about replacing existing inline code suggests the codebase may already have had some validation logic.

The agent correctly reuses ValidateArchitecturesSet() and has proper nil-guards, which are positives, but the test conventions (no Ginkgo, no builder API) and missing field.Invalid structured errors represent significant departures from repo conventions.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check2

The agent's implementation has several potential hallucination issues:

  1. ValidateArchitecturesSet() return signature: The agent calls if _, err := cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.ValidateArchitecturesSet(); err != nil with two return values, but the reference implementation shows ValidateArchitecturesSet() returning only an error (single return value). The agent invented a two-return-value signature for this method.
  2. Package path github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins: The reference implementation uses commonv1alpha1.Platform type with an Architecture field, suggesting the types are in a common/v1alpha1 package. The agent uses plugins.Plugins, plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, and plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm from a api/common/plugins package. This package path and these type names may be invented.
  3. Type names: The reference uses commonv1alpha1.Platform with an Architecture field, while the agent uses plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm with Architecture and Weight fields. The type name NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm appears to be invented.
  4. The agent claims there is an existing inline duplicate-architecture loop in the validate method that it's replacing. The reference implementation describes adding NEW validation code to ValidateCreate and ValidateUpdate, not replacing existing code. The agent appears to have invented the existing code it claims to be replacing.
  5. Missing field.Invalid structured error: The reference implementation specifically requires using field.Invalid for structured errors, but the agent simply returns the raw error from ValidateArchitecturesSet() without wrapping it in field.Invalid.
  6. Missing builder API and Ginkgo tests: The reference specifies using the builder API pattern (builder.NewClusterPodPlacementConfig()) and Ginkgo Describe/Context/It structure, but the agent uses standard Go table-driven tests instead.

While some of these are quality issues rather than hallucinations, the invented return signature for ValidateArchitecturesSet(), the potentially wrong package path and type names, and the claim about replacing existing code are concerning hallucination indicators.

task_success3

The agent correctly identifies the webhook file and adds nil-guards for Spec.Plugins and Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, and calls ValidateArchitecturesSet() rather than reimplementing duplicate detection. It covers both ValidateCreate and ValidateUpdate paths (via the shared validate method). Tests cover both create and update with multiple cases. However, there are significant deviations from the reference: (1) It does not use field.Invalid for structured Kubernetes API errors with field.NewPath(...) and field.ErrorList, instead returning a raw error from ValidateArchitecturesSet(). The reference explicitly requires structured field.Invalid errors. (2) The ValidateArchitecturesSet() is called with two return values (_, err), suggesting the agent may have the wrong function signature (the reference shows it returns only error). (3) Tests use standard Go testing package table-driven tests rather than the Ginkgo/builder pattern specified in the reference. (4) The agent incorrectly references plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm and plugins.Plugins types rather than commonv1alpha1.Platform types shown in the reference. These issues mean the code likely wouldn't compile as-is and misses key quality criteria (structured errors, builder-based Ginkgo tests).

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs are essentially identical in their implementation approach and quality. Both correctly:

  1. Webhook modification: Replace the inline duplicate-architecture checking loop in validate() with a call to cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.ValidateArchitecturesSet(), and remove the unused "errors" import.
  2. Test file: Create api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_webhook_test.go with table-driven tests for both ValidateCreate and ValidateUpdate, covering nil plugins, nil nodeAffinityScoring, unique architectures, duplicate architectures, and single architecture cases.
  3. Commands: Both specify go test ./api/v1beta1/... commands to verify the implementation.

The only minor differences are cosmetic:

  • Output B includes one additional test case ("duplicate among multiple architectures fails validation" with 3 entries) and includes oldCppc in the ValidateUpdate test struct (slightly more explicit), while Output A passes an empty &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{} directly.
  • Output A includes a copyright header in the test file; Output B does not.
  • Both match the reference implementation's core requirements equally well.

These differences are negligible and neither output is meaningfully better than the other. Both would receive a score of 5 (fully correct).

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment
task: |
  In api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_webhook.go, add validation logic that rejects
  a ClusterPodPlacementConfig if spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms contains
  duplicate architecture values.

  The method ValidateArchitecturesSet() already exists on NodeAffinityScoring in
  api/common/plugins/nodeaffinityscoring_plugin.go — call it from ValidateCreate and
  ValidateUpdate. Write a corresponding unit test.
task_id: mto-004-webhook-validation
repo: multiarch-tuning-operator
category: security
complexity: medium

\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n25\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n26\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n27\t)\n28\t\n29\t// +kubebuilder:webhook:path=/validate-multiarch-openshift-io-v1beta1-clusterpodplacementconfig,mutating=false,failurePolicy=fail,sideEffects=None,groups=multiarch.openshift.io,resources=clusterpodplacementconfigs,verbs=create;update;delete,versions=v1beta1,name=validate-clusterpodplacementconfig.multiarch.openshift.io,admissionReviewVersions=v1\n30\t\n31\tfunc (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n32\t\treturn ctrl.NewWebhookManagedBy(mgr, &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).\n33\t\t\tWithValidator(&ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator{\n34\t\t\t\tClient: mgr.GetClient(),\n35\t\t\t}).\n36\t\t\tComplete()\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator validates ClusterPodPlacementConfig resources\n40\t// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false\n41\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator struct {\n42\t\tClient client.Client\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateCreate(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n46\t\treturn v.validate(obj)\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateUpdate(ctx context.Context, oldObj, newObj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n50\t\treturn v.validate(newObj)\n51\t}\n52\t\n53\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateDelete(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n54\t\t// Check if any local PodPlacementConfig exists. If exists, deny deletion of ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n55\t\texistingPPCs := &PodPlacementConfigList{}\n56\t\tif err := v.Client.List(ctx, existingPPCs); err != nil {\n57\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs: %w\", err)\n58\t\t}\n59\t\tif len(existingPPCs.Items) != 0 {\n60\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot delete ClusterPodPlacementConfig while local PodPlacementConfigs still exist\")\n61\t\t}\n62\t\treturn nil, nil\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n66\t\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n67\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n68\t\t}\n69\t\t// Verify unique Architecture terms\n70\t\tplatforms := make(map[string]struct{})\n71\t\tfor _, term := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n72\t\t\tif _, ok := platforms[term.Architecture]; ok {\n73\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"duplicate architecture in the .spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms list\")\n74\t\t\t}\n75\t\t\tplatforms[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n76\t\t}\n77\t\treturn nil, nil\n78\t}\n79\t",

    "content": "1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2025 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage plugins\n18\t\n19\timport \"fmt\"\n20\t\n21\tconst (\n22\t\t// PluginName for NodeAffinityScoring.\n23\t\tNodeAffinityScoringPluginName = \"NodeAffinityScoring\"\n24\t)\n25\t\n26\t// NodeAffinityScoring is the plugin that implements the ScorePlugin interface.\n27\ttype NodeAffinityScoring struct {\n28\t\tBasePlugin `json:\",inline\"`\n29\t\n30\t\t// Platforms is a required field and must contain at least one entry.\n31\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:MinItems=1\n32\t\tPlatforms []NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm `json:\"platforms\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=platforms\"`\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t// ValidateArchitecturesSet checks whether duplicate architectures are set in NodeAffinityScoring\n36\tfunc (n *NodeAffinityScoring) ValidateArchitecturesSet() (bool, error) {\n37\t\tseen := make(map[string]struct{})\n38\t\tfor _, term := range n.Platforms {\n39\t\t\tif _, exists := seen[term.Architecture]; exists {\n40\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate architecture %q found in nodeAffinityScoring.platforms\", term.Architecture)\n41\t\t\t}\n42\t\n43\t\t\tseen[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n44\t\t}\n45\t\treturn true, nil\n46\t}\n47\t\n48\t// NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm holds configuration for specific platforms, with required fields validated.\n49\ttype NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm struct {\n50\t\t// Architecture must be a list of non-empty string of arch names.\n51\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x\n52\t\tArchitecture string `json:\"architecture\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=architecture\"`\n53\t\n54\t\t// weight associated with matching the corresponding NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm,\n55\t\t// in the range 1-100.\n56\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Minimum:=1\n57\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Maximum:=100\n58\t\tWeight int32 `json:\"weight\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=weight\"`\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t// Name returns the name of the NodeAffinityScoringPluginName.\n62\tfunc (b *NodeAffinityScoring) Name() string {\n63\t\treturn NodeAffinityScoringPluginName\n64\t}\n65\t",

License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage v1beta1\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"errors\"\n22\t\t\"fmt\"\n23\t\n24\t\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n25\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n26\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n27\t)\n28\t\n29\t// (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n32\t\treturn ctrl.NewWebhookManagedBy(mgr, &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).\n33\t\t\tWithValidator(&ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator{\n34\t\t\t\tClient: mgr.GetClient(),\n35\t\t\t}).\n36\t\t\tComplete()\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator validates ClusterPodPlacementConfig resources\n40\t// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false\n41\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator struct {\n42\t\tClient client.Client\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateCreate(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n46\t\treturn v.validate(obj)\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateUpdate(ctx context.Context, oldObj, newObj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n50\t\treturn v.validate(newObj)\n51\t}\n52\t\n53\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateDelete(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n54\t\t// Check if any local PodPlacementConfig exists. If exists, deny deletion of ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n55\t\texistingPPCs := &PodPlacementConfigList{}\n56\t\tif err := v.Client.List(ctx, existingPPCs); err != nil {\n57\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs: %w\", err)\n58\t\t}\n59\t\tif len(existingPPCs.Items) != 0 {\n60\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot delete ClusterPodPlacementConfig while local PodPlacementConfigs still exist\")\n61\t\t}\n62\t\treturn nil, nil\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\tfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n66\t\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n67\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n68\t\t}\n69\t\t// Verify unique Architecture terms\n70\t\tplatforms := make(map[string]struct{})\n71\t\tfor _, term := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n72\t\t\tif _, ok := platforms[term.Architecture]; ok {\n73\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"duplicate architecture in the .spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms list\")\n74\t\t\t}\n75\t\t\tplatforms[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n76\t\t}\n77\t\treturn nil, WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage v1beta1\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n)\n\n// +kubebuilder:webhook:path=/validate-multiarch-openshift-io-v1beta1-clusterpodplacementconfig,mutating=false,failurePolicy=fail,sideEffects=None,groups=multiarch.openshift.io,resources=clusterpodplacementconfigs,verbs=create;update;delete,versions=v1beta1,name=validate-clusterpodplacementconfig.multiarch.openshift.io,admissionReviewVersions=v1\n\nfunc (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\treturn ctrl.NewWebhookManagedBy(mgr, &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).\n\t\tWithValidator(&ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator{\n\t\t\tClient: mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\t}).\n\t\tComplete()\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator validates ClusterPodPlacementConfig resources\n// +kubebuilder:object:generate=false\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator struct {\n\tClient client.Client\n}\n\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateCreate(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\treturn v.validate(obj)\n}\n\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateUpdate(ctx context.Context, oldObj, newObj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\treturn v.validate(newObj)\n}\n\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) ValidateDelete(ctx context.Context, obj *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\t// Check if any local PodPlacementConfig exists. If exists, deny deletion of ClusterPodPlacementConfig.\n\texistingPPCs := &PodPlacementConfigList{}\n\tif err := v.Client.List(ctx, existingPPCs); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif len(existingPPCs.Items) != 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot delete ClusterPodPlacementConfig while local PodPlacementConfigs still exist\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\t// Verify unique Architecture terms\n\tplatforms := make(map[string]struct{})\n\tfor _, term := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n\t\tif _, ok := platforms[term.Architecture]; ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"duplicate architecture in the .spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms list\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tplatforms[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n","numLines":79,"startLine":1,"totalLines":79}}}

duplicate architectures are set in NodeAffinityScoring\nfunc (n *NodeAffinityScoring) ValidateArchitecturesSet() (bool, error) {\n\tseen := make(map[string]struct{})\n\tfor _, term := range n.Platforms {\n\t\tif _, exists := seen[term.Architecture]; exists {\n\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate architecture %q found in nodeAffinityScoring.platforms\", term.Architecture)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tseen[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n\t}\n\treturn true, nil\n}\n\n// NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm holds configuration for specific platforms, with required fields {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01LoKYYG2J7TZGc2CFsXuduA","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage v1beta1\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"fmt\"\n21\t\t\"strings\"\n22\t\n23\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n24\t\n25\t\t\"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/v1helpers\"\n26\t\n27\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n28\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n29\t)\n30\t\n31\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n32\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n33\t\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n34\t\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n35\t\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n36\t\t// +optional\n37\t\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n38\t\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n39\t\n40\t\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n41\t\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n42\t\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n43\t\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n44\t\t// +optional\n45\t\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n46\t\n47\t\t// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.\n48\t\t// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.\n49\t\t// +optional\n50\t\tPlugins *plugins.Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n51\t\n52\t\t// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n53\t\t// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture\n54\t\t// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n55\t\t// +optional\n56\t\t// +kubebuilder:default=\"\"\n57\t\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;\"\"\n58\t\tFallbackArchitecture string `json:\"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty\"`\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n62\ttype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n63\t\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n64\t\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n65\t\n66\t\t// The following fields are used to derive the conditions. They are not exposed to the user.\n67\t\t//nolint:revive // controller-gen requires json tags on all fields, even unexported ones\n68\t\tavailable                                bool `json:\"-\"`\n69\t\tprogressing                              bool `json:\"-\"`\n70\t\tdegraded                                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n71\t\tdeprovisioning                           bool `json:\"-\"`\n72\t\tpodPlacementControllerNotReady           bool `json:\"-\"`\n73\t\tpodPlacementWebhookNotReady              bool `json:\"-\"`\n74\t\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable bool `json:\"-\"`\n75\t\tcanDeployMutatingWebhook                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsReady() bool {\n79\t\treturn s.available\n80\t}\n81\t\n82\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsProgressing() bool {\n83\t\treturn s.progressing\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDegraded() bool {\n87\t\treturn s.degraded\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDeprovisioning() bool {\n91\t\treturn s.deprovisioning\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementControllerNotReady() bool {\n95\t\treturn s.podPlacementControllerNotReady\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {\n99\t\treturn s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {\n103\t\treturn s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {\n107\t\treturn s.canDeployMutatingWebhook\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.\n111\t// The build Conditions are:\n112\t//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning\n113\t//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted\n114\t//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist\n115\t//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date\n116\t//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date\n117\t//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.\n118\t//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).\n119\tfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(\n120\t\tpodPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,\n121\t\tpodPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,\n122\t\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,\n123\t\tdeprovisioning bool) {\n124\t\ts.deprovisioning = deprovisioning\n125\t\t// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration\n126\t\ts.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable\n127\t\t// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date\n128\t\ts.podPlacementControllerNotReady = components exist and have at least one replica ready\n131\t\ts.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable\n132\t\t// if some configuration if the pod placement controller and webhook are available\n135\t\t// (at least one replica)\n136\t\ts.canDeployMutatingWebhook = podPlacementWebhookAvailable && notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady)),\n191\t\t})\n192\t\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n193\t\t\tType:    PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType,\n194\t\t\tStatus: (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/v1helpers\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n)\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec defines the desired state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec struct {\n\t// LogVerbosity is the log level for the pod placement components.\n\t// Valid values are: \"Normal\", \"Debug\", \"Trace\", \"TraceAll\".\n\t// Defaults to \"Normal\".\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=Normal\n\tLogVerbosity common.LogVerbosityLevel `json:\"logVerbosity,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// NamespaceSelector selects the namespaces where the pod placement operand can process the nodeAffinity\n\t// of the pods. If left empty, all the namespaces are considered.\n\t// The default sample allows to exclude all the namespaces where the\n\t// label \"multiarch.openshift.io/exclude-pod-placement\" exists.\n\t// +optional\n\tNamespaceSelector *metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"namespaceSelector,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Plugins defines the configurable plugins for this component.\n\t// This field is optional and will be omitted from the output if not set.\n\t// +optional\n\tPlugins *plugins.Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FallbackArchitecture defines the architecture to use if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// If configured, the PodPlacementController will set the node affinity of the pod to the fallback architecture\n\t// if the image inspector cannot determine the image's architecture.\n\t// +optional\n\t// +kubebuilder:default=\"\"\n\t// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=arm64;amd64;ppc64le;s390x;\"\"\n\tFallbackArchitecture string `json:\"fallbackArchitecture,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus defines the observed state of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus struct {\n\t// Conditions represents the latest available observations of a ClusterPodPlacementConfig's current state.\n\tConditions []metav1.Condition `json:\"conditions,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// The following fields are used to derive the conditions. They are not exposed to the user.\n\t//nolint:revive // controller-gen requires json tags on all fields, even unexported ones\n\tavailable                                bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tprogressing                              bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tdegraded                                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tdeprovisioning                           bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tpodPlacementControllerNotReady           bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tpodPlacementWebhookNotReady              bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable bool `json:\"-\"`\n\tcanDeployMutatingWebhook                 bool `json:\"-\"`\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsReady() bool {\n\treturn s.available\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsProgressing() bool {\n\treturn s.progressing\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDegraded() bool {\n\treturn s.degraded\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsDeprovisioning() bool {\n\treturn s.deprovisioning\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementControllerNotReady() bool {\n\treturn s.podPlacementControllerNotReady\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsPodPlacementWebhookNotReady() bool {\n\treturn s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) IsMutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable() bool {\n\treturn s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) CanDeployMutatingWebhook() bool {\n\treturn s.canDeployMutatingWebhook\n}\n\n// Build sets the conditions in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig object.\n// The build Conditions are:\n//   - Degraded: if some components are not available (no replicas) and the object is not deprovisioning\n//   - Deprovisioning: if the object is being deleted\n//   - MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable: if the mutating webhook configuration does not exist\n//   - PodPlacementControllerNotReady: if the pod placement controller is not available or up-to-date\n//   - PodPlacementWebhookNotReady: if the pod placement webhook is not available or up-to-date\n//   - Progressing: if the object is not deprovisioning and some of the components are not up-to-date.\n//   - Available: if all the components are available to serve the requests and reconcile node affinities (at least one replica).\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) Build(\n\tpodPlacementControllerAvailable, podPlacementWebhookAvailable,\n\tpodPlacementControllerUpToDate, podPlacementWebhookUpToDate,\n\tmutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable,\n\tdeprovisioning bool) {\n\ts.deprovisioning = deprovisioning\n\t// tracks existence of the mutating webhook configuration\n\ts.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable = !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable\n\t// tracks the availability of the pod placement controller and webhook and if they are up to date\n\ts.podPlacementControllerNotReady = !podPlacementControllerAvailable || !podPlacementControllerUpToDate\n\ts.podPlacementWebhookNotReady = !podPlacementWebhookAvailable || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate\n\t// if all the components exist and have at least one replica ready\n\ts.available = mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable && podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable\n\t// if some components are not available (no replicas)\n\ts.degraded = !s.available && !s.deprovisioning // degraded will not track deprovisioning\n\t// allow the deployment of the mutating webhook configuration if the pod placement controller and webhook are available\n\t// (at least one replica)\n\ts.canDeployMutatingWebhook = podPlacementWebhookAvailable && podPlacementControllerAvailable && !s.deprovisioning\n\ts.progressing = (!podPlacementControllerUpToDate || !podPlacementWebhookUpToDate || !mutatingWebhookConfigurationAvailable) && !s.deprovisioning\n\ts.buildConditions()\n}\n\nfunc (s *ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus) buildConditions() {\n\tif s.Conditions == nil {\n\t\ts.Conditions = []metav1.Condition{}\n\t}\n\treason := \"\"\n\tif s.podPlacementControllerNotReady {\n\t\treason += PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType\n\t}\n\tif s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady {\n\t\treason += PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType\n\t}\n\tif s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable {\n\t\treason += MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable\n\t}\n\tif reason == \"\" {\n\t\treason = AllComponentsReady\n\t}\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    AvailableType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.available),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ReadyMsg, notFromBool(s.available), strings.TrimSpace(notFromBool(s.available))),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    ProgressingType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.progressing),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(ProgressingMsg, notFromBool(s.progressing)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    DegradedType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.degraded),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.degraded)), DegradedType),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DegradedMsg, notFromBool(s.degraded)),\n\t})\n\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix := \"\"\n\tif s.deprovisioning {\n\t\tdeprovisinoingMessagePostfix = PendingDeprovisioningMsg\n\t}\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    DeprovisioningType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.deprovisioning),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s%s\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(s.deprovisioning)), DeprovisioningType),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(DeprovisioningMsg, notFromBool(s.deprovisioning), deprovisinoingMessagePostfix),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    PodPlacementControllerNotRolledOutType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementControllerNotReady),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementController%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady))),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementControllerRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementControllerNotReady)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    PodPlacementWebhookNotRolledOutType,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady),\n\t\tReason:  fmt.Sprintf(\"PodPlacementWebhook%sReady\", trimAndCapitalize(notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady))),\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(PodPlacementWebhookRolledOutMsg, notFromBool(!s.podPlacementWebhookNotReady)),\n\t})\n\tv1helpers.SetCondition(&s.Conditions, metav1.Condition{\n\t\tType:    MutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable,\n\t\tStatus:  conditionFromBool(s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable),\n\t\tReason:  reason,\n\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(MutatingWebhookConfigurationReadyMsg, notFromBool(!s.mutatingWebhookConfigurationNotAvailable)),\n\t})\n}\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfig defines the configuration for the architecture aware pod placement operand.\n// Users can only deploy a single object named \"cluster\".\n// Creating the object enables the operand.\n// +kubebuilder:object:root=true\n// +kubebuilder:storageversion\n// +kubebuilder:subresource:status\n// +kubebuilder:resource:path=clusterpodplacementconfigs,scope=Cluster\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Available,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Progressing,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Degraded,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Degraded\")].status,type=string\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Since,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Progressing\")].lastTransitionTime,type=date\n// +kubebuilder:printcolumn:name=Status,JSONPath=.status.conditions[?(@.type==\"Available\")].reason,type=string\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfig struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta   `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ObjectMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\n\tSpec   ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec   `json:\"spec,omitempty\"`\n\tStatus ClusterPodPlacementConfigStatus `json:\"status,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc (c *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) PluginsEnabled(plugin common.Plugin) bool {\n\tif c.Spec.Plugins != nil {\n\t\treturn c.Spec.Plugins.PluginEnabled(plugin)\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n//+kubebuilder:object:root=true\n\n// ClusterPodPlacementConfigList contains a list of ClusterPodPlacementConfig\ntype ClusterPodPlacementConfigList struct {\n\tmetav1.TypeMeta `json:\",inline\"`\n\tmetav1.ListMeta `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n\tItems           []ClusterPodPlacementConfig `json:\"items\"`\n}\n\nfunc conditionFromBool(b bool) metav1.ConditionStatus {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn metav1.ConditionTrue\n\t}\n\treturn metav1.ConditionFalse\n}\n\nfunc notFromBool(b bool) string {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"not \"\n}\n\nfunc trimAndCapitalize(s string) string {\n\ttrimmed := strings.TrimSpace(s)\n\tif len(trimmed) == 0 {\n\t\treturn trimmed\n\t}\n\treturn strings.ToUpper(string(trimmed[0])) + trimmed[1:]\n}\n","numLines":263,"startLine":1,"totalLines":263}}}

{\n40\t\t\tb bool\n41\t\t}\n42\t\ttests := []struct {\n43\t\t\tname string\n44\t\t\targs args\n45\t\t\twant string\n46\t\t}{\n47\t\t\t{\n48\t\t\t\tname: \"Test notFromBool with {\n61\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"notFromBool() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n62\t\t\t\t}\n63\t\t\t})\n64\t\t}\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\tfunc Test_trimAndCapitalize(t *testing.T) {\n68\t\ttype args struct Here's the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## File 1: `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_webhook.go`\n\nThe `validate` method currently has inline duplicate-architecture checking. Replace it with a call to `ValidateArchitecturesSet()`.\n\n**Change the `validate` method** (lines 65–78) from:\n\n```go\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\t// Verify unique Architecture terms\n\tplatforms := make(map[string]struct{})\n\tfor _, term := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n\t\tif _, ok := platforms[term.Architecture]; ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"duplicate architecture in the .spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms list\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tplatforms[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n```\n\n**to:**\n\n```go\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\tif _, err := cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.ValidateArchitecturesSet(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n```\n\nAlso update the import block — `\"errors\"` is no longer needed. Change:\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n)\n```\n\n**to:**\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n## File 2: `api/v1beta1/clusterpodplacementconfig_webhook_test.go` (new file)\n\n```go\n/*\nCopyright 2025 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage v1beta1\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common/plugins\"\n)\n\nfunc TestClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator_ValidateCreate(t *testing.T) {\n\tv := &ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator{}\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname    string\n\t\tcppc    *ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n\t\twantErr bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"nil plugins\",\n\t\t\tcppc: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"nil nodeAffinityScoring\",\n\t\t\tcppc: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{\n\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &plugins.Plugins{},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"unique architectures\",\n\t\t\tcppc: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{\n\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &plugins.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNodeAffinityScoring: &plugins.NodeAffinityScoring{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPlatforms: []plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"amd64\", Weight: 50},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"arm64\", Weight: 50},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"duplicate architectures\",\n\t\t\tcppc: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{\n\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &plugins.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNodeAffinityScoring: &plugins.NodeAffinityScoring{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPlatforms: []plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"amd64\", Weight: 50},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"amd64\", Weight: 80},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"single architecture\",\n\t\t\tcppc: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{\n\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &plugins.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNodeAffinityScoring: []plugins.NodeAffinityScoringPlatformTerm{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"amd64\", Weight: 50},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Architecture: \"arm64\", Weight: 50},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"duplicate architectures on update\",\n\t\t\tnewObj: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{\n\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &plugins.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tNodeAffinityScoring: err := v.ValidateUpdate(context.Background(), &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}, tt.newObj)\n\t\t\tif (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ValidateUpdate() error = %v, wantErr %v\", err, tt.wantErr)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Commands to run\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment\n\n# Verify the webhook compiles Replace it with a call to `ValidateArchitecturesSet()`.\n\n**Change the `validate` method** (lines 65\u201378) from:\n\n```go\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\t// Verify unique Architecture terms\n\tplatforms := make(map[string]struct{})\n\tfor _, term := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n\t\tif _, ok := platforms[term.Architecture]; ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"duplicate architecture in the .spec.plugins.nodeAffinityScoring.platforms list\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tplatforms[term.Architecture] = struct{}{}\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n```\n\n**to:**\n\n```go\nfunc (v *ClusterPodPlacementConfigValidator) validate(cppc *ClusterPodPlacementConfig) (warnings admission.Warnings, err error) {\n\tif cppc.Spec.Plugins == nil || cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\tif _, err := cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.ValidateArchitecturesSet(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn nil, nil\n}\n```\n\nAlso update the import block \u2014 `\"errors\"` is no longer needed. Change:\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n)\n```\n\n**to:**\n\n```go\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook/admission\"\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n## File 2: &ClusterPodPlacementConfig{\n\t\t\t\tSpec: ClusterPodPlacementConfigSpec{},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"nil nodeAffinityScoring\",\n\t\t\tcppc: mto-005-promql-alert (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness3

The agent correctly identifies the metric names (mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total and mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total) and uses the correct rate() function on counters, which are important correctness criteria. The PrometheusRule YAML structure is valid and includes appropriate annotations with humanizePercentage. However, there are several notable deviations from the reference:

  1. Expression formula differs: The agent uses failures / (failures + processed) instead of the reference's failures / processed. The agent's interpretation treats processed_pods_total as only successful pods, while the reference treats it as total pods (including failures). This is a semantic difference that changes the alert behavior, though the agent's interpretation could be defensible depending on the actual metric semantics.
  2. Window size: Uses rate(...[30m]) instead of the reference's rate(...[5m]). The reference specifies a 5-minute rate window as part of the SLO parameters.
  3. for duration: Uses for: 15m instead of the reference's for: 5m. This significantly delays alert firing compared to the specification.
  4. Namespace: Uses openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator instead of multiarch-tuning-operator. While this might be valid in an OpenShift context, it deviates from the reference.

The correct metric names and valid YAML structure prevent this from scoring lower, but the deviations in the expression formula, window size, and for duration represent meaningful departures from the documented SLO parameters that would affect operational behavior.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/1 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence3

The agent correctly identified and used the proper metric names with the mto_ppo_ctrl_ prefix (mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total and mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total), which follows the repository's metric naming convention. The PrometheusRule YAML structure is valid and the agent correctly used rate() on counters. However, there are several deviations from the reference implementation: (1) The expression uses a 30-minute window ([30m]) instead of the documented 5-minute window ([5m]), (2) the for duration is 15m instead of the specified 5m, (3) the expression formula is failures / (failures + successes) instead of the simpler failures / total as specified in the reference (though there's a semantic argument here about what "processed" means), (4) the namespace is openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator instead of multiarch-tuning-operator, and (5) the labels on the metadata differ from the reference (using app.kubernetes.io labels instead of prometheus: k8s and role: alert-rules). The agent did follow some repo conventions like placing files in the config/monitoring/ directory with proper kustomization and using appropriate Kubernetes labeling patterns, but the core SLO parameters deviate from the documented requirements in RELIABILITY.md. The correct metric names and overall structure show partial awareness of conventions, but the multiple parameter deviations prevent a higher score.

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check3

The agent correctly identifies and uses the documented metric names (mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total and mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total), which is the most critical correctness criterion. The PrometheusRule YAML structure is valid, the threshold is correctly 0.05 (5%), and rate() is correctly applied to counters.

However, there are several deviations from the reference implementation:

  1. Expression formula differs: The agent uses failed / (failed + processed) instead of the reference's failed / processed. The agent's design notes claim that mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total counts "successes" rather than "total processed pods," which contradicts the metric name itself ("processed_pods_total" implies all pods processed, not just successes). This is a potential misinterpretation of the metric semantics, though the metric names themselves are correct.
  2. Window size: Uses rate(...[30m]) instead of the reference's rate(...[5m]). The reference specifies a 5-minute rate window.
  3. for duration: Uses for: 15m instead of the reference's for: 5m.
  4. Namespace: Uses openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator instead of multiarch-tuning-operator. This could be correct for an OpenShift deployment but differs from reference.
  5. References to existing files: The agent references internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go:64-69, docs/metrics.md, config/prometheus/monitor.yaml, and docs/alerts/enoexec-alert.md. These appear to be references to files that supposedly exist in the repository. If any of these don't exist, they would be hallucinations. However, given the task involves a real codebase with metrics definitions, these references are plausible.

The metric names are correct and grounded. The structural deviations (window size, for duration, formula) represent design choices rather than hallucinations of non-existent entities. The file path references to existing code are reasonable claims about the codebase structure.

task_success3

The agent correctly identified the metric names (mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total and mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total), used rate() on counters, set the threshold at 0.05 (5%), used severity: critical, and provided a human-readable annotation message — all matching core correctness criteria. However, there are significant deviations from the reference: the expression uses rate(...[30m]) instead of rate(...[5m]), uses for: 15m instead of for: 5m, and the denominator is failed + processed instead of just processed (which changes the semantics of the ratio, since the reference treats processed_pods_total as the total, not just successes). The namespace is also different (openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator vs multiarch-tuning-operator). These differences in the expression logic and timing parameters are non-trivial and would require fixes before matching the intended SLO definition.

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs accomplish the core task equivalently well. Let me compare against the reference implementation:

Reference implementation key elements:

  1. New file config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml with a PrometheusRule CR
  2. Alert named MultiarchImageInspectionFailureRateCritical
  3. Uses mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total and mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total counters
  4. PromQL expr computing failure rate as rate(failed[5m]) / rate(processed[5m]) > 0.05
  5. severity: critical label
  6. Human-readable annotations.message
  7. New config/monitoring/kustomization.yaml
  8. No special make targets needed

Output A:

  • Creates config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml with correct PrometheusRule CR ✓
  • Correct alert name ✓
  • Uses correct metrics ✓
  • PromQL uses rate(...[30m]) window (differs from reference's 5m but is reasonable) and computes failed / (failed + processed) rather than failed / processed — a slightly different formula but arguably more mathematically correct for computing a failure rate as a fraction of total attempts
  • Has severity: critical ✓
  • Has annotations.message ✓
  • Creates config/monitoring/kustomization.yaml ✓
  • Modifies config/prometheus/kustomization.yaml to include ../monitoring (different from reference which modifies config/default/kustomization.yaml)
  • Includes for: 15m (reference has for: 5m)

Output B:

  • Creates config/monitoring/prometheus-rule.yaml with correct PrometheusRule CR ✓
  • Correct alert name ✓
  • Uses correct metrics ✓
  • PromQL uses rate(...[5m]) window matching reference, computes failed / processed matching reference formula
  • Threshold > 0.05 ✓
  • Has severity: critical ✓
  • Has annotations.message ✓
  • Creates config/monitoring/kustomization.yaml ✓
  • Modifies config/default/kustomization.yaml to add ../monitoring — matches reference approach

Output B more closely matches the reference in terms of the PromQL expression (same window, same formula) and the kustomization wiring (modifying config/default rather than config/prometheus). Output A uses a different 30m window and a different denominator formula, plus wires through config/prometheus instead of config/default.

However, Output A's formula failed / (failed + processed) is arguably more robust (since processed_pods_total counts successful processing while failed counts failures, the true total is their sum), and the 30m window is a reasonable engineering choice. Output A also provides more detailed design notes.

Output B more closely matches the reference but both are functionally correct implementations. The differences are minor: window size, exact formula variant, and where the kustomization wiring goes. Both would work and pass code review with minor comments.

Overall these are very close in quality — both get the core task right with minor variations from the reference. I'll call this a tie since neither is clearly superior: B matches the reference more precisely but A provides better documentation and arguably a more robust formula.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment
task: |
  Write a PrometheusRule Kubernetes custom resource YAML file to be placed under
  config/monitoring/. Define an alert named MultiarchImageInspectionFailureRateCritical
  that fires when the image inspection failure rate exceeds the SLO threshold over the
  defined measurement window. Use the existing Prometheus metric counters in this
  repository. Include the expr, a severity label, and a human-readable annotations.message.
task_id: mto-005-promql-alert
repo: multiarch-tuning-operator
category: observability
complexity: low

    "type": "tool_result",
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_vrtx_016uK7Pv2cCUCHvRNUftQvt6",
    "tool_name": "Read",
    "content": "1\tpackage metrics\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"sync\"\n5\t\n6\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n7\t\n8\t\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n9\t\tmetrics2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics\"\n10\t)\n11\t\n12\tvar onceCommon sync.Once\n13\t\n14\tvar (\n15\t\tInspectionGauge             prometheus.Gauge\n16\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit  prometheus.Histogram\n17\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss prometheus.Histogram\n18\t)\n19\t\n20\tfunc InitCommonMetrics() {\n21\t\tonceCommon.Do(func() {\n22\t\t\tInspectionGauge = prometheus.NewGauge(\n23\t\t\t\tprometheus.GaugeOpts{\n24\t\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_inspection_cache_size\",\n25\t\t\t\t\tHelp: \"Current size of the MTO inspection cache\",\n26\t\t\t\t},\n27\t\t\t)\n28\t\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenHit = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n29\t\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n30\t\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_hit_processing_duration_seconds\",\n31\t\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache hit for MTO inspection\",\n32\t\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n33\t\t\t\t})\n34\t\t\tTimeToInspectImageGivenMiss = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n35\t\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n36\t\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_cache_miss_processing_duration_seconds\",\n37\t\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Duration to process a cache miss for MTO inspection\",\n38\t\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n39\t\t\t\t})\n40\t\n41\t\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(InspectionGauge)\n42\t\t})\n43\t}\n44\t",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:35:15.226Z"
  },

prometheus.NewCounter(\n\t\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_enoexecevents_total\",\n\t\t\t\tHelp: \"The counter for exec format errors detected and reported\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t\tEnoexecCounterInvalid = prometheus.NewCounter(\n\t\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_enoexecevents_invalid_total\",\n\t\t\t\tHelp: \"The counter for ENoExecEvents objects that faled the reconciliation and report as pod events\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(EnoexecCounter)\n\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(EnoexecCounterInvalid)\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc InitMetrics() {\n\tinitMetrics()\n}\n","numLines":37,"startLine":1,"totalLines":37}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01PQVnmKcAMschfQkqNDKE2V","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_019W3nWx3rm95BaASnPfqLFp","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/docs/metrics.md"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1473,"cache_read_input_tokens":33966,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1473,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":21},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"814662fc-ec47-4447-9c91-2318794d2590","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:25.116Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ1RtrvdyYqomoCbYVM"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_019W3nWx3rm95BaASnPfqLFp","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Metrics\n2\t\n3\tAll the components expose the default metrics of the controller-runtime project.\n4\t\n5\tRefer to https://book.kubebuilder.io/reference/metrics-reference.\n6\t\n7\tIt is responsibility of the user to install the monitoring operator and configure the monitoring stack to be able to scrape the metrics \n8\tfrom the multi-arch tuning operator components.\n9\t\n10\tIn Openshift, when deploying through the OperatorHub or the Operator Lifecycle Manager, the monitoring stack is automatically installed and configured.\n11\tUsers can enable the monitoring of the multi-arch tuning operator by labeling the namespace where the operator is deployed with `openshift.io/cluster-monitoring: \"true\"`.\n12\tIf installing from the UI, the users can opt-in to enable monitoring in the recommended namespace `openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator` via the available checkbox at installation time.\n13\t\n14\t## Pod Placement Operand\n15\t\n16\tThe following metrics are exposed by the Pod Placement Operand:\n17\t\n18\t| Metric                                            | Type      | Controller               | Description                                                                                                     |\n19\t|---------------------------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n20\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds`        | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to process any pod.                                                                              |\n21\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds`  | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to process a pod that is gated (includes inspection).                                            |\n22\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds`      | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to inspect an image (it may include the time to retrieve the info from a cache).                 |\n23\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds` | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to inspect all the images in a pod (it may include the time to retrieve this info from a cache). |\n24\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total`               | Counter   | pod placement controller | The total number of pods processed by the pod placement controller that had a scheduling gate                   |\n25\t| `mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total`      | Counter   | pod placement controller | The total number of image inspections that failed.                                                              |\n26\t| `mto_ppo_pods_gated`                              | Gauge     | controller and webhook   | The current number of gated pods (this metric is not considered reliable yet). It should converge to 0.         |\n27\t| `mto_ppo_wh_pods_processed_total`                 | Counter   | mutating webhook         | The total number of pods processed by the webhook.                                                              |\n28\t| `mto_ppo_wh_pods_gated_total`                     | Counter   | mutating webhook         | The total number of pods gated by the webhook.                                                                  |\n29\t| `mto_ppo_wh_response_time_seconds`                | Histogram | mutating webhook         | The response time of the webhook.                                                                               |\n30\t\n31\t## Exec Format Error Operand\n32\t\n33\tThe following metrics are exposed by the Exec Format Error Operand:\n34\t\n35\t| Metric                      | Type    | Controller      | Description                                                                                  |\n36\t|-----------------------------|---------|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n37\t| `mto_enoexecevents`         | Counter | enoexec handler | The total number of exec format error detected and reported                                  |\n38\t| `mto_enoexecevents_invalid` | Counter | enoexec handler | The counter for ENoExecEvents objects that faled the reconciliation and report as pod events |\n39\t\n40\t\n41\t## Example queries\n42\t\n43\tThe following queries can be used to monitor the pod placement operand:\n44\t\n45\t```sql\n46\t-- Memory usage\n47\tsum(container_memory_rss{namespace='openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator', container=\"\"}) BY (pod)\n48\t\n49\t-- CPU Usage\n50\tpod:container_cpu_usage:sum{namespace='openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator'}\n51\t\n52\t\n53\t-- 50th percentile time to process pods\n54\t-- Gated pods\n55\thistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n56\t-- Any pods in the controller\n57\thistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n58\t-- Image inspection\n59\thistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n60\t-- All images in a pod insection\n61\thistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n62\t-- Time to schedule a pod (kubernetes metrics)\n63\thistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(scheduler_pod_scheduling_sli_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n64\t\n65\t\n66\t-- Total pods processed by the webhooks\n67\tsum(mto_ppo_wh_pods_processed_total)\n68\t-- Total pods gated by the webhooks\n69\tsum(mto_ppo_wh_pods_gated_total)\n70\t-- Total gated pods processed by the controller\n71\tsum(mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total)\n72\t-- Failed image inspection\n73\tsum(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total)\n74\t\n75\t-- Current number of gated pods (with the multiarch tuning operator scheduling gate)\n76\tsum(mto_ppo_pods_gated)\n77\t-- Current number of gated pods (with any scheduling gate)\n78\tsum(scheduler_pending_pods{queue=\"gated\"})\n79\tsum(scheduler_pending_pods) by (queue)\n80\t\n81\t-- Distribution of the time to inspect an image\n82\tsum by(le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds_bucket[5m]))\n83\t\n84\t-- Rate of increase of exec format errors in the last 6h\n85\tirate(mto_enoexecevents_total[6h])\n86\t\n87\t```\n88\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"2316da68-2a7f-4844-be5b-263e308b6a70","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:25.124Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/docs/metrics.md","content":"# Metrics\n\nAll the components expose the default metrics of the controller-runtime project.\n\nRefer to https://book.kubebuilder.io/reference/metrics-reference.\n\nIt is responsibility of the user to install the monitoring operator and configure the monitoring stack to be able to scrape the metrics \nfrom the multi-arch tuning operator components.\n\nIn Openshift, when deploying through the OperatorHub or the Operator Lifecycle Manager, the monitoring stack is automatically installed and configured.\nUsers can enable the monitoring of the multi-arch tuning operator by labeling the namespace where the operator is deployed with `openshift.io/cluster-monitoring: \"true\"`.\nIf installing from the UI, the users can opt-in to enable monitoring in the recommended namespace `openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator` via the available checkbox at installation time.\n\n## Pod Placement Operand\n\nThe following metrics are exposed by the Pod Placement Operand:\n\n| Metric                                            | Type      | Controller               | Description                                                                                                     |\n|---------------------------------------------------|-----------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds`        | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to process any pod.                                                                              |\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds`  | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to process a pod that is gated (includes inspection).                                            |\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds`      | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to inspect an image (it may include the time to retrieve the info from a cache).                 |\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds` | Histogram | pod placement controller | The time taken to inspect all the images in a pod (it may include the time to retrieve this info from a cache). |\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total`               | Counter   | pod placement controller | The total number of pods processed by the pod placement controller that had a scheduling gate                   |\n| `mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total`      | Counter   | pod placement controller | The total number of image inspections that failed.                                                              |\n| `mto_ppo_pods_gated`                              | Gauge     | controller and webhook   | The current number of gated pods (this metric is not considered reliable yet). It should converge to 0.         |\n| `mto_ppo_wh_pods_processed_total`                 | Counter   | mutating webhook         | The total number of pods processed by the webhook.                                                              |\n| `mto_ppo_wh_pods_gated_total`                     | Counter   | mutating webhook         | The total number of pods gated by the webhook.                                                                  |\n| `mto_ppo_wh_response_time_seconds`                | Histogram | mutating webhook         | The response time of the webhook.                                                                               |\n\n## Exec Format Error Operand\n\nThe following metrics are exposed by the Exec Format Error Operand:\n\n| Metric                      | Type    | Controller      | Description                                                                                  |\n|-----------------------------|---------|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n| `mto_enoexecevents`         | Counter | enoexec handler | The total number of exec format error detected and reported                                  |\n| `mto_enoexecevents_invalid` | Counter | enoexec handler | The counter for ENoExecEvents objects that faled the reconciliation and report as pod events |\n\n\n## Example queries\n\nThe following queries can be used to monitor the pod placement operand:\n\n```sql\n-- Memory usage\nsum(container_memory_rss{namespace='openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator', container=\"\"}) BY (pod)\n\n-- CPU Usage\npod:container_cpu_usage:sum{namespace='openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator'}\n\n\n-- 50th percentile time to process pods\n-- Gated pods\nhistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n-- Any pods in the controller\nhistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n-- Image inspection\nhistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n-- All images in a pod insection\nhistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n-- Time to schedule a pod (kubernetes metrics)\nhistogram_quantile(0.5, sum by (le) (rate(scheduler_pod_scheduling_sli_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))\n\n\n-- Total pods processed by the webhooks\nsum(mto_ppo_wh_pods_processed_total)\n-- Total pods gated by the webhooks\nsum(mto_ppo_wh_pods_gated_total)\n-- Total gated pods processed by the controller\nsum(mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total)\n-- Failed image inspection\nsum(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total)\n\n-- Current number of gated pods (with the multiarch tuning operator scheduling gate)\nsum(mto_ppo_pods_gated)\n-- Current number of gated pods (with any scheduling gate)\nsum(scheduler_pending_pods{queue=\"gated\"})\nsum(scheduler_pending_pods) by (queue)\n\n-- Distribution of the time to inspect an image\nsum by(le) (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds_bucket[5m]))\n\n-- Rate of increase of exec format errors in the last 6h\nirate(mto_enoexecevents_total[6h])\n\n```\n","numLines":88,"startLine":1,"totalLines":88}}}
