{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01Tp9VdDu8QPrsmJjXLJkjeE","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\n2\t# Prometheus Monitor Service (Metrics)\n3\tapiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1\n4\tkind: ServiceMonitor\n5\tmetadata:\n6\t  labels:\n7\t    control-plane: controller-manager\n8\t    app.kubernetes.io/name: servicemonitor\n9\t    app.kubernetes.io/instance: controller-tuning-metrics-monitor\n10\t    app.kubernetes.io/component: metrics\n11\t    app.kubernetes.io/created-by: multiarch-tuning-operator\n12\t    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: multiarch-tuning-operator\n13\t    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: kustomize\n14\t  name: controller-manager-metrics-monitor\n15\t  namespace: system\n16\tspec:\n17\t  endpoints:\n18\t    - path: /metrics\n19\t      port: https\n20\t      scheme: https\n21\t      bearerTokenFile: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token\n22\t      tlsConfig:\n23\t        insecureSkipVerify: true\n24\t  selector:\n25\t    matchLabels:\n26\t      control-plane: controller-manager\n27\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"8fe07807-00f6-4de5-baf3-13d35cd077ee","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:30.175Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/config/prometheus/monitor.yaml","content":"\n# Prometheus Monitor Service (Metrics)\napiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1\nkind: ServiceMonitor\nmetadata:\n  labels:\n    control-plane: controller-manager\n    app.kubernetes.io/name: servicemonitor\n    app.kubernetes.io/instance: controller-tuning-metrics-monitor\n    app.kubernetes.io/component: metrics\n    app.kubernetes.io/created-by: multiarch-tuning-operator\n    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: multiarch-tuning-operator\n    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: kustomize\n  name: controller-manager-metrics-monitor\n  namespace: system\nspec:\n  endpoints:\n    - path: /metrics\n      port: https\n      scheme: https\n      bearerTokenFile: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token\n      tlsConfig:\n        insecureSkipVerify: true\n  selector:\n    matchLabels:\n      control-plane: controller-manager\n","numLines":27,"startLine":1,"totalLines":27}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01DaxeNstT4rgHYAoN2hU5bT","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage metrics\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"sync\"\n5\t\n6\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n7\t\n8\t\tmetrics2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics\"\n9\t\n10\t\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n11\t)\n12\t\n13\tvar (\n14\t\tTimeToProcessPod        prometheus.Histogram\n15\t\tTimeToProcessGatedPod   prometheus.Histogram\n16\t\tTimeToInspectImage      prometheus.Histogram\n17\t\tTimeToInspectPodImages  prometheus.Histogram\n18\t\tProcessedPodsCtrl       prometheus.Counter\n19\t\tFailedInspectionCounter prometheus.Counter\n20\t)\n21\t\n22\tvar onceController sync.Once\n23\t\n24\tfunc InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {\n25\t\tonceController.Do(initPodPlacementControllerMetrics)\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\tfunc initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {\n29\t\tinitCommonMetrics()\n30\t\tTimeToProcessPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n31\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n32\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds\",\n33\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to process any pod\",\n34\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n35\t\t\t},\n36\t\t)\n37\t\tTimeToProcessGatedPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n38\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n39\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds\",\n40\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to process a pod that is gated (includes inspection)\",\n41\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n42\t\t\t},\n43\t\t)\n44\t\tTimeToInspectImage = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n45\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n46\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds\",\n47\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to inspect an image\",\n48\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n49\t\t\t},\n50\t\t)\n51\t\tTimeToInspectPodImages = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n52\t\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n53\t\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds\",\n54\t\t\t\tHelp:    \"The time taken to inspect all the images in a pod (all containers)\",\n55\t\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n56\t\t\t},\n57\t\t)\n58\t\tProcessedPodsCtrl = prometheus.NewCounter(\n59\t\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n60\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total\",\n61\t\t\t\tHelp: \"The total number of pods processed by the pod placement controller that had a scheduling gate\",\n62\t\t\t},\n63\t\t)\n64\t\tFailedInspectionCounter = prometheus.NewCounter(\n65\t\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n66\t\t\t\tName: \"mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total\",\n67\t\t\t\tHelp: \"The total number of image inspections that failed\",\n68\t\t\t},\n69\t\t)\n70\t\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(TimeToProcessPod, TimeToProcessGatedPod, TimeToInspectImage,\n71\t\t\tTimeToInspectPodImages, ProcessedPodsCtrl, FailedInspectionCounter)\n72\t}\n73\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"a7f52b61-2aee-40f5-8abb-03f212de2db5","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:36.600Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go","content":"package metrics\n\nimport (\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n\n\tmetrics2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics\"\n\n\t\"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tTimeToProcessPod        prometheus.Histogram\n\tTimeToProcessGatedPod   prometheus.Histogram\n\tTimeToInspectImage      prometheus.Histogram\n\tTimeToInspectPodImages  prometheus.Histogram\n\tProcessedPodsCtrl       prometheus.Counter\n\tFailedInspectionCounter prometheus.Counter\n)\n\nvar onceController sync.Once\n\nfunc InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {\n\tonceController.Do(initPodPlacementControllerMetrics)\n}\n\nfunc initPodPlacementControllerMetrics() {\n\tinitCommonMetrics()\n\tTimeToProcessPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_pod_seconds\",\n\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to process any pod\",\n\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tTimeToProcessGatedPod = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_process_gated_pod_seconds\",\n\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to process a pod that is gated (includes inspection)\",\n\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tTimeToInspectImage = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_image_seconds\",\n\t\t\tHelp:    \"Time taken to inspect an image\",\n\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tTimeToInspectPodImages = prometheus.NewHistogram(\n\t\tprometheus.HistogramOpts{\n\t\t\tName:    \"mto_ppo_ctrl_time_to_inspect_pod_images_seconds\",\n\t\t\tHelp:    \"The time taken to inspect all the images in a pod (all containers)\",\n\t\t\tBuckets: utils.Buckets(),\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tProcessedPodsCtrl = prometheus.NewCounter(\n\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\t\tName: \"mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total\",\n\t\t\tHelp: \"The total number of pods processed by the pod placement controller that had a scheduling gate\",\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tFailedInspectionCounter = prometheus.NewCounter(\n\t\tprometheus.CounterOpts{\n\t\t\tName: \"mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total\",\n\t\t\tHelp: \"The total number of image inspections that failed\",\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tmetrics2.Registry.MustRegister(TimeToProcessPod, TimeToProcessGatedPod, TimeToInspectImage,\n\t\tTimeToInspectPodImages, ProcessedPodsCtrl, FailedInspectionCounter)\n}\n","numLines":73,"startLine":1,"totalLines":73}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_017uP3MeigU5h6M4tQRhaem8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Adds namespace to all resources.\n2\t# This must match the namespace defined in ../static-namespace/namespace.transformer.yaml\n3\tnamespace: openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator\n4\t\n5\t# Value of this field is prepended to the\n6\t# names of all resources, e.g. a deployment named\n7\t# \"wordpress\" becomes \"alices-wordpress\".\n8\t# Note that it should also match with the prefix (text before '-') of the namespace\n9\t# field above.\n10\tnamePrefix: multiarch-tuning-operator-\n11\t\n12\t# Labels to add to all resources and selectors.\n13\t#commonLabels:\n14\t#  someName: someValue\n15\t\n16\t# [WEBHOOK] To enable webhook, uncomment all the sections with [WEBHOOK] prefix including the one in\n17\t# crd/kustomization.yaml\n18\t# [CERTMANAGER] To enable cert-manager, uncomment all sections with 'CERTMANAGER'. 'WEBHOOK' components are required.\n19\t#- ../certmanager\n20\t# [PROMETHEUS] To enable prometheus monitor, uncomment all sections with 'PROMETHEUS'.\n21\t#- ../prometheus\n22\t\n23\t# [WEBHOOK] To enable webhook, uncomment all the sections with [WEBHOOK] prefix including the one in\n24\t# crd/kustomization.yaml\n25\t#- manager_webhook_patch.yaml\n26\t\n27\t# [CERTMANAGER] To enable cert-manager, uncomment all sections with 'CERTMANAGER'.\n28\t# Uncomment 'CERTMANAGER' sections in crd/kustomization.yaml to enable the CA injection in the admission webhooks.\n29\t# 'CERTMANAGER' needs to be enabled to use ca injection\n30\t\n31\t# the following config is for teaching kustomize how to do var substitution\n32\tapiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1\n33\tkind: Kustomization\n34\tresources:\n35\t- ../crd\n36\t- ../rbac\n37\t- ../manager\n38\t- ../webhook\n39\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"eb51ad4a-b1be-4f1e-8769-513e867f7b64","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:42.344Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/config/default/kustomization.yaml","content":"# Adds namespace to all resources.\n# This must match the namespace defined in ../static-namespace/namespace.transformer.yaml\nnamespace: openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator\n\n# Value of this field is prepended to the\n# names of all resources, e.g. a deployment named\n# \"wordpress\" becomes \"alices-wordpress\".\n# Note that it should also match with the prefix (text before '-') of the namespace\n# field above.\nnamePrefix: multiarch-tuning-operator-\n\n# Labels to add to all resources and selectors.\n#commonLabels:\n#  someName: someValue\n\n# [WEBHOOK] To enable webhook, uncomment all the sections with [WEBHOOK] prefix including the one in\n# crd/kustomization.yaml\n# [CERTMANAGER] To enable cert-manager, uncomment all sections with 'CERTMANAGER'. 'WEBHOOK' components are required.\n#- ../certmanager\n# [PROMETHEUS] To enable prometheus monitor, uncomment all sections with 'PROMETHEUS'.\n#- ../prometheus\n\n# [WEBHOOK] To enable webhook, uncomment all the sections with [WEBHOOK] prefix including the one in\n# crd/kustomization.yaml\n#- manager_webhook_patch.yaml\n\n# [CERTMANAGER] To enable cert-manager, uncomment all sections with 'CERTMANAGER'.\n# Uncomment 'CERTMANAGER' sections in crd/kustomization.yaml to enable the CA injection in the admission webhooks.\n# 'CERTMANAGER' needs to be enabled to use ca injection\n\n# the following config is for teaching kustomize how to do var substitution\napiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1\nkind: Kustomization\nresources:\n- ../crd\n- ../rbac\n- ../manager\n- ../webhook\n","numLines":39,"startLine":1,"totalLines":39}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_018igKDAxRWyVonDa57H4u4q","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_01MCZ1x8QRVWD31ZjHQFSsco","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"find . -name 'PrometheusRule*' -o -name 'prometheusrule*' -o -name '*alert*' | grep -v vendor | grep -v .git"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1216,"cache_read_input_tokens":38909,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1216,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"fed2e73d-a1a9-447d-bf9f-59d5ffc5a791","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:43.264Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ2mg2QcLNT2hrv1iAe"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01QJ4Q9iEj8pq7osESyBk59R","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_01UnavPzppq4xW1BqKjLWWzh","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/docs/alerts/enoexec-alert.md"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":910,"cache_read_input_tokens":40125,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":910,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":24},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"38447fb5-82cd-4ca9-aab2-25f21957157c","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:49.136Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ3F6nR1q9PjfWfSVDU"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01UnavPzppq4xW1BqKjLWWzh","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ExecFormatErrorsDetected\n2\t\n3\t## Meaning\n4\t\n5\tThe `ExecFormatErrorsDetected` alert fires when the Exec Format Error plugin is enabled and has detected **exec format errors** in one or more pods during the last 6 hours.\n6\t\n7\t## Impact\n8\t\n9\tAffected pods are trying to execute a binary that is not built for the host node’s CPU architecture.\n10\t\n11\t**Possible causes include:**\n12\t\n13\t1. **Incorrect scheduling** – The pod lacks the right node affinity and is scheduled onto an unsupported architecture. In this case, the entrypoint often fails and the pod enters `CrashLoopBackOff`.\n14\t2. **Wrong image metadata** – The image manifest reports an incorrect architecture (e.g., published as `arm64` but actually built for `amd64`).\n15\t3. **Incorrect binary in the image** – A build step (e.g., a `RUN` layer in the Dockerfile) downloads or installs a binary for the wrong architecture, regardless of the target.\n16\t4. **Binary injected at runtime** – A binary is introduced dynamically, for example through a `PersistentVolume`, `ImageVolume`, or by being downloaded into ephemeral storage by the entrypoint or application logic.\n17\t\n18\t## Diagnosis\n19\t\n20\t1. **List recent events** reported by the Multiarch Tuning Operator:\n21\t   \n22\t```bash\n23\tkubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.kind=Pod,reportingController=multiarch-tuning-operator\n24\t```\n25\t\n26\t2. **Inspect and check the logs of the affected pods**:\n27\t\t* Verify if node affinity is set.\n28\t\t\t* If not, check whether the pod should have been patched automatically by the Pod Placement Operand, or whether it is excluded via `ClusterPodPlacementConfig`.\n29\t\t\t* If exclusion is intentional, manually configure node affinity to restrict scheduling to supported architectures.\n30\t\t\t* If node affinity should have been added automatically, review Pod Placement Operand documentation to confirm cluster and registry configurations are correct.\n31\t\t* If the pod is on a supported architecture but still fails (e.g., in `CrashLoopBackOff`), check container logs to identify which binary is causing the error.\n32\t\t* If the issue comes from a binary bundled at runtime, update the pod or its owning resource to avoid introducing incompatible binaries.\n33\t\t* If the issue originates from the image itself, contact the image provider and request a corrected build.\n34\t\n35\t## Mitigation\n36\t\n37\tWhile working on a permanent fix, you may mitigate by **forcing the pod to run on a node with a CPU architecture compatible with both the problematic binary and the rest of the containers**.\n38\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"0188b1e8-4ed8-417e-93fa-a56cbd0fef97","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:35:49.142Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/docs/alerts/enoexec-alert.md","content":"# ExecFormatErrorsDetected\n\n## Meaning\n\nThe `ExecFormatErrorsDetected` alert fires when the Exec Format Error plugin is enabled and has detected **exec format errors** in one or more pods during the last 6 hours.\n\n## Impact\n\nAffected pods are trying to execute a binary that is not built for the host node’s CPU architecture.\n\n**Possible causes include:**\n\n1. **Incorrect scheduling** – The pod lacks the right node affinity and is scheduled onto an unsupported architecture. In this case, the entrypoint often fails and the pod enters `CrashLoopBackOff`.\n2. **Wrong image metadata** – The image manifest reports an incorrect architecture (e.g., published as `arm64` but actually built for `amd64`).\n3. **Incorrect binary in the image** – A build step (e.g., a `RUN` layer in the Dockerfile) downloads or installs a binary for the wrong architecture, regardless of the target.\n4. **Binary injected at runtime** – A binary is introduced dynamically, for example through a `PersistentVolume`, `ImageVolume`, or by being downloaded into ephemeral storage by the entrypoint or application logic.\n\n## Diagnosis\n\n1. **List recent events** reported by the Multiarch Tuning Operator:\n   \n```bash\nkubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.kind=Pod,reportingController=multiarch-tuning-operator\n```\n\n2. **Inspect and check the logs of the affected pods**:\n\t* Verify if node affinity is set.\n\t\t* If not, check whether the pod should have been patched automatically by the Pod Placement Operand, or whether it is excluded via `ClusterPodPlacementConfig`.\n\t\t* If exclusion is intentional, manually configure node affinity to restrict scheduling to supported architectures.\n\t\t* If node affinity should have been added automatically, review Pod Placement Operand documentation to confirm cluster and registry configurations are correct.\n\t* If the pod is on a supported architecture but still fails (e.g., in `CrashLoopBackOff`), check container logs to identify which binary is causing the error.\n\t* If the issue comes from a binary bundled at runtime, update the pod or its owning resource to avoid introducing incompatible binaries.\n\t* If the issue originates from the image itself, contact the image provider and request a corrected build.\n\n## Mitigation\n\nWhile working on a permanent fix, you may mitigate by **forcing the pod to run on a node with a CPU architecture compatible with both the problematic binary and the rest of the containers**.\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

Let me check the Makefile for any relevant targets."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":502,"cache_read_input_tokens":42103,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":502,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"c84d5a1b-0733-4329-8fa2-00c017ac2daf","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:02.805Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ4AGbk8nToz6Mv7kEo"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_018tLdS1jSfkAyGyNKzzXqxW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_01D6VGejQ9Cx5fr51QnnBVcM","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"grep -n 'monitor\\|prometheus\\|alert' Makefile | head -20"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":502,"cache_read_input_tokens":42103,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":502,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"0eb7b624-35a6-4184-8d3f-f180e7ee0ed3","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:03.091Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ4AGbk8nToz6Mv7kEo"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01D6VGejQ9Cx5fr51QnnBVcM","type":"tool_result","content":"(Bash completed with no output)","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"4d1955f9-e538-4edf-819d-6a6db52212f3","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:03.870Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_018Fw8Y8mQsYE9ecQ4g5JVp9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have all the information needed. Here's the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n### Key metrics identified\n\nFrom `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go`:\n- **`mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total`** — Counter for failed image inspections\n- **`mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total`** — Counter for total pods processed (which required inspection)\n\nThe failure rate is: `failed / (failed + processed)` over a measurement window. The `rate()` function over both counters gives us the per-second rates, and dividing them yields the failure ratio.\n\n---\n\n### New file: `config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml`\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1\nkind: PrometheusRule\nmetadata:\n  name: multiarch-tuning-operator-alerts\n  namespace: openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator\n  labels:\n    app.kubernetes.io/name: prometheusrule\n    app.kubernetes.io/instance: multiarch-tuning-operator-alerts\n    app.kubernetes.io/component: monitoring\n    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: multiarch-tuning-operator\n    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: kustomize\nspec:\n  groups:\n    - name: multiarch-tuning-operator.rules\n      rules:\n        - alert: MultiarchImageInspectionFailureRateCritical\n          expr: |\n            (\n              rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total[30m])\n              /\n              (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total[30m]) + rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total[30m]))\n            ) > 0.05\n          for: 15m\n          labels:\n            severity: critical\n          annotations:\n            summary: Image inspection failure rate exceeds SLO threshold.\n            message: >-\n              The multiarch tuning operator image inspection failure rate has exceeded\n              5% over the last 30 minutes. Current failure ratio is\n              {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}. This may indicate registry\n              connectivity issues, authentication failures, or misconfigured image\n              references. Investigate the pod placement controller logs and verify\n              registry access.\n```\n\n---\n\n### New file: `config/monitoring/kustomization.yaml`\n\n```yaml\nresources:\n  - prometheus-alert.yaml\n```\n\n---\n\n### Modified file: `config/prometheus/kustomization.yaml`\n\nAdd the monitoring resources:\n\n```yaml\nresources:\n- monitor.yaml\n- ../monitoring\n```\n\n---\n\n### Commands to run\n\nNo make targets or special commands are required — this is a pure YAML addition. To verify it applies cleanly with kustomize:\n\n```bash\ncd config/default\n# Uncomment the prometheus line in config/default/kustomization.yaml if not already done, then:\nkustomize build ../prometheus\n```\n\nOr to apply directly to a cluster:\n\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml\n```\n\n---\n\n### Design notes\n\n1. **Metric choice**: Uses `mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total` (failures) and `mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total` (successes) — both are Counters defined in `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go:64-69`. The ratio `failures / (failures + successes)` gives the error rate as a fraction of all inspection attempts.\n\n2. **Measurement window**: `rate(...[30m])` provides a 30-minute sliding window, consistent with the existing example queries in `docs/metrics.md` that use 5m windows for latency but appropriate for an SLO-style failure rate that needs smoothing.\n\n3. **Threshold**: `> 0.05` (5%) is a standard SLO error budget threshold for infrastructure operators. The `for: 15m` clause prevents flapping by requiring the condition to hold for 15 minutes before firing.\n\n4. **Division safety**: When `rate()` of both counters is 0 (no traffic), the expression evaluates to `NaN`, which does not satisfy `> 0.05`, so the alert won't fire during idle periods — no explicit guard needed.\n\n5. **Labels/annotations**: Follow the same conventions as the existing `ServiceMonitor` in `config/prometheus/monitor.yaml` and the alert documentation pattern in `docs/alerts/enoexec-alert.md`. The `severity: critical` label follows Kubernetes alerting conventions."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":142,"cache_read_input_tokens":42605,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":142,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d","uuid":"fcca2feb-9f1b-43e3-b8b0-0dfb140772c5","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:26.641Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ4iHgSM55RcZdvqCxs"}
{"type": "result", "subtype": "success", "is_error": false, "api_error_status": null, "duration_ms": 84419, "duration_api_ms": 78356, "ttft_ms": 5061, "ttft_stream_ms": 4093, "time_to_request_ms": 19, "num_turns": 23, "result": "I now have all the information needed. Here's the complete implementation:\n\n---\n\n## Implementation\n\n### Key metrics identified\n\nFrom `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go`:\n- **`mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total`** \u2014 Counter for failed image inspections\n- **`mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total`** \u2014 Counter for total pods processed (which required inspection)\n\nThe failure rate is: `failed / (failed + processed)` over a measurement window. The `rate()` function over both counters gives us the per-second rates, and dividing them yields the failure ratio.\n\n---\n\n### New file: `config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml`\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1\nkind: PrometheusRule\nmetadata:\n  name: multiarch-tuning-operator-alerts\n  namespace: openshift-multiarch-tuning-operator\n  labels:\n    app.kubernetes.io/name: prometheusrule\n    app.kubernetes.io/instance: multiarch-tuning-operator-alerts\n    app.kubernetes.io/component: monitoring\n    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: multiarch-tuning-operator\n    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: kustomize\nspec:\n  groups:\n    - name: multiarch-tuning-operator.rules\n      rules:\n        - alert: MultiarchImageInspectionFailureRateCritical\n          expr: |\n            (\n              rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total[30m])\n              /\n              (rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total[30m]) + rate(mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total[30m]))\n            ) > 0.05\n          for: 15m\n          labels:\n            severity: critical\n          annotations:\n            summary: Image inspection failure rate exceeds SLO threshold.\n            message: >-\n              The multiarch tuning operator image inspection failure rate has exceeded\n              5% over the last 30 minutes. Current failure ratio is\n              {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}. This may indicate registry\n              connectivity issues, authentication failures, or misconfigured image\n              references. Investigate the pod placement controller logs and verify\n              registry access.\n```\n\n---\n\n### New file: `config/monitoring/kustomization.yaml`\n\n```yaml\nresources:\n  - prometheus-alert.yaml\n```\n\n---\n\n### Modified file: `config/prometheus/kustomization.yaml`\n\nAdd the monitoring resources:\n\n```yaml\nresources:\n- monitor.yaml\n- ../monitoring\n```\n\n---\n\n### Commands to run\n\nNo make targets or special commands are required \u2014 this is a pure YAML addition. To verify it applies cleanly with kustomize:\n\n```bash\ncd config/default\n# Uncomment the prometheus line in config/default/kustomization.yaml if not already done, then:\nkustomize build ../prometheus\n```\n\nOr to apply directly to a cluster:\n\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f config/monitoring/prometheus-alert.yaml\n```\n\n---\n\n### Design notes\n\n1. **Metric choice**: Uses `mto_ppo_ctrl_failed_image_inspection_total` (failures) and `mto_ppo_ctrl_processed_pods_total` (successes) \u2014 both are Counters defined in `internal/controller/podplacement/metrics/controller.go:64-69`. The ratio `failures / (failures + successes)` gives the error rate as a fraction of all inspection attempts.\n\n2. **Measurement window**: `rate(...[30m])` provides a 30-minute sliding window, consistent with the existing example queries in `docs/metrics.md` that use 5m windows for latency but appropriate for an SLO-style failure rate that needs smoothing.\n\n3. **Threshold**: `> 0.05` (5%) is a standard SLO error budget threshold for infrastructure operators. The `for: 15m` clause prevents flapping by requiring the condition to hold for 15 minutes before firing.\n\n4. **Division safety**: When `rate()` of both counters is 0 (no traffic), the expression evaluates to `NaN`, which does not satisfy `> 0.05`, so the alert won't fire during idle periods \u2014 no explicit guard needed.\n\n5. **Labels/annotations**: Follow the same conventions as the existing `ServiceMonitor` in `config/prometheus/monitor.yaml` and the alert documentation pattern in `docs/alerts/enoexec-alert.md`. The `severity: critical` label follows Kubernetes alerting conventions.", "stop_reason": "end_turn", "session_id": "58a3cc7a-250c-40c8-a385-7a795eebce6d", "total_cost_usd": 0.38560849999999997, "usage": {"input_tokens": 46, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 19720, "cache_read_input_tokens": 368257, "output_tokens": 3120, "server_tool_use": {"web_search_requests": 0, "web_fetch_requests": 0}, "service_tier": "standard", "cache_creation": {"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 0, "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 19720}, "inference_geo": "", "iterations": [], "speed": "standard"}, "modelUsage": {"claude-opus-4-6": {"inputTokens": 46, "outputTokens": 3120, "cacheReadInputTokens": 368257, "cacheCreationInputTokens": 19720, "webSearchRequests": 0, "costUSD": 0.38560849999999997, "contextWindow": 200000, "maxOutputTokens": 64000}}, "permission_denials": [], "terminal_reason": "completed", "fast_mode_state": "off", "uuid": "4f4d8255-2979-4ac8-8d0f-ff65de3e09ab", "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:36:26.676Z"}
mto-006-new-execution-mode (4/6 pass) TIE
JudgeValueRationale
code_correctness4

The agent's implementation is largely correct and follows the existing codebase conventions well. The key elements are all present:

  1. New package created at internal/controller/metricsaggregator/ with a proper reconciler — correct.
  2. Mutual exclusion updated in validateFlags() to include the new flag — correct.
  3. Leader election ID uses the prefix-based pattern matching the existing codebase convention (fmt.Sprintf("metrics-aggregator-%s", leaderID)) — correct.
  4. Flag registration in bindFlags() — correct.
  5. RunMetricsAggregator function follows the same pattern as other Run* functions — correct.
  6. Import paths are correct for this repository's module structure.
  7. Types and API usage are correct — client.Client, kubernetes.Clientset, runtime.Scheme match existing patterns.

Minor issues:

  • The reference implementation specifies a unique leader election ID constant (e.g., const metricsAggregatorLeaderElectionID = "multiarch-tuning-operator-metrics-aggregator.multiarch.openshift.io"), but the agent uses the prefix-based pattern (fmt.Sprintf("metrics-aggregator-%s", leaderID)). While this follows the existing codebase pattern and produces a unique ID, it doesn't match the reference's explicit mention of a dedicated constant with the specific naming format <operator-name>-<mode>.<domain>.
  • The reconciler's SetupWithManager uses For(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}) which watches CPPC objects, while the reference uses Named("metrics-aggregator") without a For() clause. The agent's approach is reasonable but differs from the reference which uses a simpler stub without watching a specific resource.
  • The reference explicitly calls out starting the CPPC informer in the new mode's setup block, while the agent defers this to the existing enableCPPCInformer flag mechanism. This is a valid architectural choice but doesn't match the reference's explicit requirement to start the CPPC informer in the mode block.

Overall, the code would compile and function correctly, with the differences being more about design choices than correctness errors.

consulted_docsFAIL

Read 0/3 expected docs (coverage 0%, threshold 50%)

convention_adherence4

The implementation follows most of the key conventions correctly:

  1. Execution mode conventions: The new mode is properly added with mutual exclusion via btoi() counting in validateFlags(), a unique leader election ID prefix (metrics-aggregator-), and a dedicated Run function following the exact same pattern as RunENoExecEventControllers and others. The flag is registered in bindFlags() correctly.
  2. New package structure: Created at internal/controller/metricsaggregator/ following the existing package organization pattern.
  3. CPPC access pattern: The agent correctly explains that CPPC access uses the informer package (clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()), not direct API calls, and that --enable-cppc-informer should be paired with the new flag. This follows the documented convention.
  4. SetupWithManager pattern: Follows the same must() wrapper pattern used by other controllers.

The one notable deviation from the reference implementation is the leader election ID format. The reference specifies a dedicated constant like multiarch-tuning-operator-metrics-aggregator.multiarch.openshift.io (each mode has its own unique constant), while the agent uses the existing prefix-concatenation pattern (metrics-aggregator-208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io). While this technically produces a unique ID and follows the pattern already in the codebase (the existing code uses the prefix approach), the reference explicitly calls for a dedicated constant following the format <operator-name>-<mode>.<domain>. The agent's approach is consistent with the existing codebase code, so this is arguably following the actual repo convention rather than the idealized reference.

The reconciler struct includes client.Client, ClientSet, and Scheme fields mirroring the pattern of other reconcilers like PodReconciler, and watches ClusterPodPlacementConfig via For(). The reference has a more minimal stub (no fields, Named() instead of For()), but the agent's approach is actually more aligned with existing reconciler conventions in the codebase. This is a minor difference that doesn't violate conventions.

No tests were written, but neither the reference nor the task description explicitly required tests. The copyright header follows repo convention. Overall, this is a strong implementation with at most one minor deviation (leader election ID as prefix vs. constant).

efficiencyFAIL

Excessive: 119 turns, $7.92

hallucination_check3

The implementation is largely grounded in the existing codebase patterns, but there are some concerns:

Grounded elements:

  1. The new package internal/controller/metricsaggregator/ follows the existing pattern of other controller packages like internal/controller/podplacement/, internal/controller/operator/, etc.
  2. The import paths reference real existing packages in the repository (e.g., github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1, github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/enoexecevent/handler).
  3. The RunMetricsAggregator function follows the exact same pattern as RunENoExecEventControllers and other Run* functions.
  4. The must(), btoi(), validateFlags(), bindFlags() functions are referenced correctly based on the existing code patterns.
  5. The leader election ID construction using fmt.Sprintf("metrics-aggregator-%s", leaderID) follows the same pattern as other modes.
  6. The flag registration pattern matches existing flags.

Potential concerns but acceptable:

  1. The reconciler struct includes client.Client, kubernetes.Clientset, and runtime.Scheme fields - this follows the pattern of existing reconcilers like ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler and PodReconciler.
  2. The SetupWithManager watches ClusterPodPlacementConfig using .For() - this is a reasonable choice for a new controller, though the reference implementation uses .Named() instead. This is a design difference rather than a hallucination.
  3. The multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig type is referenced correctly as it exists in the API package.

Key difference from reference:

  • The reference implementation uses a simpler reconciler without client.Client, ClientSet, and Scheme fields, and uses Named("metrics-aggregator") instead of For(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}) in SetupWithManager. The agent's approach adds more fields and watches CPPC objects, which is a design choice but not a hallucination.
  • The reference calls for a unique leader election ID constant (e.g., metricsAggregatorLeaderElectionID), while the agent uses the existing prefix pattern. This follows the codebase's actual convention, so it's actually more grounded than the reference.

No clear hallucinations found:

  • All imported packages appear to exist in the repository
  • All referenced types, methods, and fields appear to be real
  • File paths are correct
  • The pattern of code follows existing conventions in the codebase

The implementation does not appear to contain hallucinated references to non-existent code. All references to existing packages, types, methods, and patterns are consistent with what would exist in the codebase. The agent creates new files and functions following established patterns.

task_success4

The agent successfully completed nearly all required sub-tasks: created the new internal/controller/metricsaggregator/reconciler.go package with a proper reconciler struct and SetupWithManager method, added the enableMetricsAggregator flag variable and registration, updated mutual exclusion validation in validateFlags(), added the leader election ID prefix, created the RunMetricsAggregator function, and wired it into the main dispatch block. The two notable deviations from the reference are: (1) the leader election ID uses a prefix-based approach (metrics-aggregator-208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io) rather than a dedicated constant with the reference's naming format (multiarch-tuning-operator-metrics-aggregator.multiarch.openshift.io), though this follows the existing codebase convention for other modes; and (2) the reconciler watches ClusterPodPlacementConfig via .For() rather than using a Named("metrics-aggregator") controller without a watched resource, and includes extra struct fields (Client, ClientSet, Scheme) beyond the minimal stub. The CPPC informer access is explained but not directly started in the new mode's block — instead, the agent correctly notes the existing --enable-cppc-informer flag pattern. These are minor differences that follow existing codebase conventions and would likely pass review with minor comments.

pairwiseTIE

Both outputs produce virtually identical implementations that correctly address all required sub-tasks:

  1. New reconciler file: Both create internal/controller/metricsaggregator/reconciler.go with a MetricsAggregatorReconciler struct embedding client.Client, having Scheme and ClientSet fields, a stub Reconcile method, and a SetupWithManager that watches ClusterPodPlacementConfig. Output B adds a Recorder field and a NewReconciler constructor function (closer to the enoexecevent handler pattern), while Output A uses direct struct initialization (closer to the PodReconciler pattern). Both approaches are valid.
  2. Flag variable: Both add enableMetricsAggregator bool to the var block.
  3. Leader election ID: Both add metrics-aggregator- prefix via fmt.Sprintf("metrics-aggregator-%s", leaderID).
  4. Flag binding: Both register --enable-metrics-aggregator in bindFlags().
  5. Mutual exclusion: Both update validateFlags() to include enableMetricsAggregator in both the "at least one" and "no more than one" checks.
  6. Run function: Both add a RunMetricsAggregator function and call it guarded by enableMetricsAggregator. Output A uses direct struct initialization (&metricsaggregator.MetricsAggregatorReconciler{...}), while Output B uses metricsaggregator.NewReconciler(...). Both are valid.
  7. CPPC informer access: Both correctly explain that the CPPC informer is accessed via the independent --enable-cppc-informer flag and clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig().
  8. Import: Both add the metricsaggregator import.
  9. Build commands: Both mention go build ./... to verify compilation.

The differences are stylistic (constructor function vs direct initialization, extra Recorder field), and both are consistent with patterns found in the existing codebase. Neither output has significant errors or omissions compared to the reference. Both would score 4-5 on the rubric.

Input
repo_path: /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment
task: |
  Add a fifth execution mode flag --enable-metrics-aggregator to cmd/main.go.
  Create a stub reconciler MetricsAggregatorReconciler in
  internal/controller/metricsaggregator/reconciler.go.

  The new mode must follow all conventions established by the existing four modes:
  - Its own unique leader election ID
  - Its own SetupWithManager call
  - Guarded by the same mutual exclusion as other modes in bindFlags
  - Access to the CPPC informer

  Show the complete changes required to cmd/main.go.
task_id: mto-006-new-execution-mode
repo: multiarch-tuning-operator
category: architecture
complexity: high

{\n121\t\t\tleaderID = fmt.Sprintf(\"ppc-controllers-%s\", leaderID)\n122\t\t\t// We need to watch the pods with the status.phase equal to Pending to be able to update the nodeAffinity.\n123\t\t\t// Reset CVEs. For more information see:\n138\t\t// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qppj-fm5r-hxr3\n139\t\t// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4374-p667-p6c8\n140\t\t// - https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubebuilder/blob/33a2f3dc556a9e49e06e6f19e0ae737d82d402db/testdata/project-v4/cmd/main.go#L78-L89\n141\t\tvar tlsOpts []func(*tls.Config)\n142\t\tdisableHTTP2 := func(c *tls.Config) {\n143\t\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"disabling http/2\")\n144\t\t\tc.NextProtos = []string{\"http/1.1\"}\n145\t\t}\n146\t\ttlsOpts = append(tlsOpts, disableHTTP2)\n147\t\n148\t\twebhookServer := webhook.NewServer(webhook.Options{\n149\t\t\tPort:    9443,\n150\t\t\tCertDir: certDir,\n151\t\t\tTLSOpts: tlsOpts,\n152\t\t})\n153\t\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{\n154\t\t\tScheme: scheme,\n155\t\t\tMetrics: metricsserver.Options{\n156\t\t\t\tBindAddress:    metricsAddr,\n157\t\t\t\tCertDir:        certDir,\n158\t\t\t\tFilterProvider: filters.WithAuthenticationAndAuthorization,\n159\t\t\t\tSecureServing:  true,\n160\t\t\t},\n161\t\t\tWebhookServer:          webhookServer,\n162\t\t\tHealthProbeBindAddress: probeAddr,\n163\t\t\tLeaderElection:         enableLeaderElection,\n164\t\t\tLeaderElectionID:       leaderID,\n165\t\t\tCache:                  cacheOpts,\n166\t\t\tLogger:                 ctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).WithName(\"manager\"),\n167\t\t\t// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel defines if the leader should step down voluntarily\n168\t\t\t// when the Manager ends. This requires the binary to immediately end when the\n169\t\t\t// Manager is stopped, otherwise, this setting is unsafe. Setting this significantly\n170\t\t\t// speeds up voluntary leader transitions as the new leader don't have to wait\n171\t\t\t// LeaseDuration time first.\n172\t\t\t//\n173\t\t\t// In the default scaffold provided, the program ends immediately after\n174\t\t\t// the manager stops, so would be fine to enable this option. However,\n175\t\t\t// if you are doing or is intended to do any operation such as perform cleanups\n176\t\t\t// after the manager stops then its usage might be unsafe.\n177\t\t\t// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel: true,\n178\t\t})\n179\t\tmust(err, \"unable to create manager\")\n180\t\n181\t\t//+kubebuilder:scaffold:builder\n182\t\tmust(mgr.AddHealthzCheck(\"healthz\", healthz.Ping), \"unable to set up health check\")\n183\t\tmust(mgr.AddReadyzCheck(\"readyz\", healthz.Ping), \"unable to set up ready check\")\n184\t\n185\t\tif enableCPPCInformer {\n186\t\t\tmust(mgr.Add(clusterpodplacementconfig.NewCPPCSyncer(mgr)), \"unable to instantiate CPPCSyncer\")\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\tif enableOperator {\n190\t\t\tRunOperator(mgr)\n191\t\t\tRunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr)\n192\t\t}\n193\t\tif enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers {\n194\t\t\tRunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr)\n195\t\t}\n196\t\tif enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook {\n197\t\t\tRunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr)\n198\t\t}\n199\t\tif enableENoExecEventControllers {\n200\t\t\tRunENoExecEventControllers(mgr)\n201\t\t}\n202\t\n203\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"starting manager\")\n204\t\tmust(mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler()), \"unable to start the manager\")\n205\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"the manager has stopped\")\n206\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"running post functions\")\n207\t\tfor _, f := range postFuncs {\n208\t\t\tf()\n209\t\t}\n210\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"exiting\")\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\tfunc RunOperator(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n214\t\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n215\t\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n216\t\n217\t\t// Get GVK for ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n218\t\tgvk, _ := apiutil.GVKForObject(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}, mgr.GetScheme())\n219\t\n220\t\t// Set up the reconciler\n221\t\tmust((&operator.ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler{\n222\t\t\tClient:        mgr.GetClient(),\n223\t\t\tScheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),\n224\t\t\tClientSet:     clientset,\n225\t\t\tDynamicClient: dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(config),\n226\t\t\tRecorder: events.NewKubeRecorder(\n227\t\t\t\tclientset.CoreV1().Events(utils.Namespace()),\n228\t\t\t\tutils.OperatorName,\n229\t\t\t\t&corev1.ObjectReference{\n230\t\t\t\t\tKind:       gvk.Kind,\n231\t\t\t\t\tName:       common.SingletonResourceObjectName,\n232\t\t\t\t\tNamespace:  utils.Namespace(),\n233\t\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: gvk.GroupVersion().String(),\n234\t\t\t\t},\n235\t\t\t\tclock.RealClock{},\n236\t\t\t),\n237\t\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n238\t\tmust((&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController,\n239\t\t\tcontrollerKey, \"ClusterPodPlacementConfigConversionWebhook\")\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\tfunc RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n243\t\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n244\t\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n245\t\n246\t\tmust((&podplacement.PodReconciler{\n247\t\t\tClient:    mgr.GetClient(),\n248\t\t\tAPIReader: mgr.GetAPIReader(),\n249\t\t\tScheme:    mgr.GetScheme(),\n250\t\t\tClientSet: clientset,\n251\t\t\tRecorder:  mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n252\t\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr),\n253\t\t\tunableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"PodReconciler\")\n254\t\n255\t\tmust(mgr.Add(podplacement.NewGlobalPullSecretSyncer(clientset, globalPullSecretNamespace, globalPullSecretName)),\n256\t\t\tunableToAddRunnable, runnableKey, \"GlobalPullSecretSyncer\")\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\tfunc RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n260\t\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n261\t\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n262\t\tpool, err := ants.NewMultiPool(16, 16, ants.LeastTasks,\n263\t\t\tants.WithPreAlloc(true))\n264\t\tmust(err, \"unable to create multi pool for the webhook's event messages\")\n265\t\tpostFuncs = append(postFuncs, func() {\n266\t\t\terr = pool.ReleaseTimeout(30 * time.Second)\n267\t\t\tif err != nil {\n268\t\t\t\tsetupLog.Error(err, \"failed to release the worker pool\")\n269\t\t\t}\n270\t\t\tants.Release()\n271\t\t})\n272\t\thandler := podplacement.NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(mgr.GetClient(), clientset, mgr.GetScheme(),\n273\t\t\tmgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), pool) //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n274\t\tmgr.GetWebhookServer().Register(\"/add-pod-scheduling-gate\", &webhook.Admission{Handler: handler})\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\tfunc RunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n278\t\tmgr.GetWebhookServer().Register(\"/validate-multiarch-openshift-io-v1beta1-podplacementconfig\",\n279\t\t\t&webhook.Admission{Handler: podplacementconfig.NewPodPlacementConfigWebhook(mgr.GetAPIReader(), mgr.GetScheme())})\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\tfunc RunENoExecEventControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n283\t\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n284\t\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n285\t\tmust(enoexeceventhandler.NewReconciler(\n286\t\t\tmgr.GetClient(),\n287\t\t\tclientset,\n288\t\t\tmgr.GetScheme(),\n289\t\t\tmgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.EnoexecControllerName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n290\t\t).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"ENoExecEventController\")\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\tfunc validateFlags() error {\n294\t\tif !enableOperator && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook && !enableENoExecEventControllers {\n295\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"at least one of the following flags must be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers\")\n296\t\t}\n297\t\t// no more than one of the flags can be set\n298\t\tif btoi(enableOperator)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook)+btoi(enableENoExecEventControllers) > 1 {\n299\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"only one of the following flags can be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers\")\n300\t\t}\n301\t\treturn nil\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\tfunc bindFlags() {\n305\t\tflag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, \"metrics-bind-address\", \":8080\", \"The address the metric endpoint binds to.\")\n306\t\tflag.StringVar(&probeAddr, \"health-probe-bind-address\", \":8081\", \"The address the probe endpoint binds to.\")\n307\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, \"leader-elect\", false,\n308\t\t\t\"Enable leader election for controller manager. \"+\n309\t\t\t\t\"Enabling this will ensure there is only one active controller manager.\")\n310\t\tflag.StringVar(&certDir, \"cert-dir\", \"/var/run/manager/tls\", \"The directory where the TLS certs are stored\")\n311\t\t// TODO: Change the defaults to match a local secret; the OCP specific settings will be provided by the operator\n312\t\tflag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretNamespace, \"global-pull-secret-namespace\", \"openshift-config\", \"The namespace where the global pull secret is stored\")\n313\t\tflag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretName, \"global-pull-secret-name\", \"pull-secret\", \"The name of the global pull secret\")\n314\t\tflag.StringVar(&registryCertificatesConfigMapName, \"registry-certificates-configmap-name\", \"image-registry-certificates\", \"The name of the configmap that contains the registry certificates\")\n315\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook, \"enable-ppc-webhook\", false, \"Enable the pod placement config operand webhook\")\n316\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers, \"enable-ppc-controllers\", false, \"Enable the pod placement config operand controllers\")\n317\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableOperator, \"enable-operator\", false, \"Enable the operator\")\n318\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableCPPCInformer, \"enable-cppc-informer\", false, \"Enable informer for ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n319\t\tflag.BoolVar(&enableENoExecEventControllers, \"enable-enoexec-event-controllers\", false, \"Enable the ENoExecEvent controllers\")\n320\t\t// This may be deprecated in the future. It is used to support the current way of setting the log level for operands\n321\t\t// If operands will start to support a controller that watches the ClusterPodPlacementConfig, this flag may be removed\n322\t\t// and the log level will be set in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig at runtime (with no need for reconciliation)\n323\t\tflag.IntVar(&initialLogLevel, \"initial-log-level\", common.LogVerbosityLevelNormal.ToZapLevelInt(), \"Initial log level. Converted to zap\")\n324\t\tklog.InitFlags(nil)\n325\t\tflag.Parse()\n326\t\t// Set the Log Level as AtomicLevel to allow runtime changes\n327\t\tutils.AtomicLevel = zapuber.NewAtomicLevelAt(zapcore.Level(int8(-initialLogLevel))) // #nosec G115 -- initialLogLevel is constrained to 0-3 range\n328\t\tzapLogger := zap.New(zap.Level(utils.AtomicLevel), zap.UseDevMode(false))\n329\t\tklog.SetLogger(zapLogger)\n330\t\tctrllog.SetLogger(zapLogger)\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\tfunc must(err error, msg string, keysAndValues ...interface{}) {\n334\t\tif err != nil {\n335\t\t\tsetupLog.Error(err, msg, keysAndValues...)\n336\t\t\tos.Exit(1)\n337\t\t}\n338\t}\n339\t\n340\tfunc btoi(b bool) int {\n341\t\tif b {\n342\t\t\treturn 1\n343\t\t}\n344\t\treturn 0\n345\t}\n346\t",
    "is_error": false,
    "timestamp": "2026-07-20T13:36:19.143Z"
