utils.Namespace() {\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"ENoExecEvent is not in the operator namespace, skipping reconciliation\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name, \"namespace\", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonWrongNamespace)\n\t\t// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName == \"\" {\n\t\t// If the ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, we ignore it\n\t\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"ENoExecEvent does not have a pod name, skipping reconciliation\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name, \"namespace\", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)\n\t\t// Return nil to avoid requeuing - this event will be cleaned up by deleteErroredENoExecEvents\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Log the ENoExecEvent instance\n\tlogger.Info(\"Reconciling ENoExecEvent\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name, \"namespace\", eNoExecEvent.Namespace)\n\tret, err := r.reconcile(ctx, eNoExecEvent)\n\t// If the reconciliation was successful, or one of the objects was not found, we delete the ENoExecEvent resource.\n\tif client.IgnoreNotFound(err) == nil {\n\t\tif err := r.Delete(ctx, &eNoExecEvent.ENoExecEvent); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to delete ENoExecEvent resource after reconciliation\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name)\n\t\t\t// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works\n\t\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)\n\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tmetrics.EnoexecCounter.Inc()\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"Deleted ENoExecEvent resource after successful reconciliation\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name)\n\t\treturn ret, nil\n\t}\n\tmetrics.EnoexecCounterInvalid.Inc()\n\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to reconcile ENoExecEvent\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name)\n\t// Mark as error - this is a real reconciliation error that will be retried\n\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonReconciliation)\n\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n}\n\nfunc (r *Reconciler) reconcile(ctx context.Context, eNoExecEvent *ENoExecEvent) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\tlogger := log.FromContext(ctx)\n\t// Retrieve the pod\n\tpodObj, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace).Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, metav1.GetOptions{})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// If the pod is not found, the error will be ignored and the ENoExecEvent will be deleted by the caller.\n\t\t// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to get pod for ENoExecEvent\", \"podName\",\n\t\t\teNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, \"namespace\", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)\n\t\t// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t}\n\n\tpod := models.NewPod(podObj, ctx, r.recorder)\n\tif pod.PodObject().Spec.NodeName != eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName {\n\t\t// If the pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated, we skip the reconciliation.\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"Pod is not scheduled on the node where the ENoExecEvent was generated\", \"podName\",\n\t\t\tpod.Name, \"namespace\", pod.Namespace, \"nodeName\", eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName)\n\t\t// This is not an error case - mark it and return nil so it gets deleted\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Retrieve the node\n\tnode, err := r.clientSet.CoreV1().Nodes().Get(ctx, eNoExecEvent.Status.NodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// When the error is \"not found\", the ENoExecEvent may refer a new pod with the same name scheduled on a different node.\n\t\t// This ENoExecEvent is then not relevant anymore and will be deleted by the caller.\n\t\t// Other errors will be returned to the caller, which will retry the reconciliation.\n\t\t// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures and missed ENoExecEvent reconciliations\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to get node for ENoExecEvent\", \"nodeName\", podObj.Spec.NodeName,\n\t\t\t\"podName\", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodName, \"namespace\", eNoExecEvent.Status.PodNamespace)\n\t\t// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonNodeNotFound)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t}\n\n\tcontainerName, err := pod.ContainerNameFor(eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Container ID not found in pod status\", \"containerID\", eNoExecEvent.Status.ContainerID)\n\t\tcontainerName = utils.UnknownContainer\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.Info(\"Publishing event for ENoExecEvent\", \"podName\", pod.Name, \"namespace\", pod.Namespace)\n\tpod.PublishEvent(v1.EventTypeWarning, utils.ExecFormatErrorEventReason,\n\t\tutils.ExecFormatErrorEventMessage(containerName, node.Labels[utils.ArchLabel]))\n\n\t// Label the pod with the ENoExecEvent label.\n\tpod.EnsureLabel(utils.ExecFormatErrorLabelKey, utils.True)\n\t// TODO: increment counter for exec format error failures\n\t// Update the pod with the new label\n\tif _, err = r.clientSet.CoreV1().Pods(pod.Namespace).Update(ctx, pod.PodObject(), metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to label the pod\", \"podName\", pod.Name, \"namespace\", pod.Namespace)\n\t\t// if the error is \"not found\", it means the pod has been deleted, the caller will handle this case.\n\t\t// Mark as error but return the original error so client.IgnoreNotFound works\n\t\tr.markAsError(ctx, eNoExecEvent, ErrorReasonPodNotFound)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t}\n\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n}\n\n// SetupWithManager sets up the controller with the Manager.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\t// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent\n\t// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.\n\tmaxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4\n\tlog.FromContext(context.Background()).Info(\"Setting up the ENoExecEventReconciler with the manager with max\"+\n\t\t\" concurrent reconciles\", \"maxConcurrentReconciles\", maxConcurrentReconciles)\n\n\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n\t\tFor(&multiarchv1beta1.ENoExecEvent{}).\n\t\tWithOptions(ctrl2.Options{\n\t\t\tMaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,\n\t\t}).\n\t\tComplete(r)\n}\n\n// markAsError marks the ENoExecEvent with an error label and persists it.\n// The errored events are kept in the cluster and cleaned up later by the operator controller's\n// deleteErroredENoExecEvents function during CPPC deletion or plugin disable.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) markAsError(ctx context.Context, eNoExecEvent *ENoExecEvent, errorReason string) {\n\tlogger := log.FromContext(ctx)\n\n\t// Set the error label\n\teNoExecEvent.EnsureLabel(ENoExecEventErrorLabel, errorReason)\n\n\t// Update the ENoExecEvent to persist the label.\n\t// Ignore NotFound errors - the object may have already been deleted.\n\tif err := r.Update(ctx, &eNoExecEvent.ENoExecEvent); client.IgnoreNotFound(err) != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Failed to update ENoExecEvent with error label\", \"name\", eNoExecEvent.Name, \"reason\", errorReason)\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":209,"startLine":1,"totalLines":209}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_vrtx_01AGh6mrkF2thGeugf7RdBsK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_vrtx_01RDHjyovMLqFATvZpoBMPWM","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_reconciler.go"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":85,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1065,"cache_read_input_tokens":38785,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1065,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":21},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"376154b9-51e0-4de1-b074-06817c71730c","uuid":"6a074043-bf09-42f9-a7a2-f70454cead5a","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:32.391Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ6NVcbbeokx9Hft4RB"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01RDHjyovMLqFATvZpoBMPWM","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\tCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n3\t\n4\tLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5\tyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6\tYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n7\t\n8\t    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9\t\n10\tUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11\tdistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12\tWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13\tSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14\tlimitations under the License.\n15\t*/\n16\t\n17\tpackage podplacement\n18\t\n19\timport (\n20\t\t\"context\"\n21\t\t\"fmt\"\n22\t\truntime2 \"runtime\"\n23\t\t\"sort\"\n24\t\t\"time\"\n25\t\n26\t\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n27\t\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n28\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n29\t\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n30\t\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n31\t\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n32\t\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n33\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n34\t\tctrl2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/controller\"\n35\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler\"\n36\t\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n37\t\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile\"\n38\t\n39\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n40\t\tmultiarchv1beta1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n41\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics\"\n42\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig\"\n43\t\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n44\t)\n45\t\n46\t// PodReconciler reconciles a Pod object\n47\ttype PodReconciler struct {\n48\t\tclient.Client\n49\t\tAPIReader client.Reader\n50\t\tScheme    *runtime.Scheme\n51\t\tClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset\n52\t\tRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\t// Note: The operand (pod-placement-controller) RBAC is defined programmatically in\n56\t// podplacement_objects.go buildClusterRoleController(). Do NOT add operand-specific\n57\t// RBAC markers here — kubebuilder merges all markers into the manager's ClusterRole,\n58\t// which would over-provision the operator SA with permissions only the operand needs.\n59\t\n60\t// Reconcile is part of the main kubernetes reconciliation loop which aims to\n61\t// move the current state of the cluster closer to the desired state.\n62\t// the Pod object against the actual cluster state, and then\n63\t// perform operations to make the cluster state reflect the state specified by\n64\t// the user.\n65\t//\n66\t// For more details, check Reconcile and its Result here:\n67\t// - https://pkg.go.dev/sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime@v0.14.1/pkg/reconcile\n68\t// Reconcile has to watch the pod object if it has the scheduling gate with name SchedulingGateName,\n69\t// inspect the images in the pod spec, update the nodeAffinity accordingly and remove the scheduling gate.\n70\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n71\t\t// Lazy initialization of the metrics to support concurrent reconciles\n72\t\tmetrics.InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics()\n73\t\tnow := time.Now()\n74\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessPod)\n75\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n76\t\n77\t\tpod := newPod(&corev1.Pod{}, ctx, r.Recorder)\n78\t\n79\t\tif err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, pod.PodObject()); err != nil {\n80\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Unable to fetch pod\", \"error\", err)\n81\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)\n82\t\t}\n83\t\t// Pods without the scheduling gate should be ignored.\n84\t\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n85\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod does not have the scheduling gate. Ignoring...\")\n86\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n87\t\t}\n88\t\tmetrics.ProcessedPodsCtrl.Inc()\n89\t\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessGatedPod)\n90\t\tr.processPod(ctx, pod)\n91\t\terr := r.Update(ctx, pod.PodObject())\n92\t\tif err != nil {\n93\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Unable to update the pod\")\n94\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalFailure, SchedulingGateRemovalFailureMsg)\n95\t\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n96\t\t}\n97\t\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n98\t\t\t// Only publish the event if the scheduling gate has been removed and the pod has been updated successfully.\n99\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalSuccess, SchedulingGateRemovalSuccessMsg)\n100\t\t\tmetrics.GatedPodsGauge.Dec()\n101\t\t}\n102\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) processPod(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) {\n106\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n107\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing pod\")\n108\t\n109\t\tcppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()\n110\t\t// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace\n111\t\tppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}\n112\t\tif err := r.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n113\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace\")\n114\t\t\treturn\n115\t\t}\n116\t\t// The informer cache can lag behind the API server. If the cache shows no PPCs,\n117\t\t// verify with a direct API read to avoid a race where a just-created PPC is missed\n118\t\t// and the pod is permanently ungated without its preferred affinity.\n119\t\tif len(ppcList.Items) == 0 {\n120\t\t\tif err := r.APIReader.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n121\t\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list PodPlacementConfigs from API server\")\n122\t\t\t\treturn\n123\t\t\t}\n124\t\t}\n125\t\n126\t\t// Filter to only PPCs that match this pod's labels - do this once for efficiency\n127\t\tmatchingPPCs := pod.filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList)\n128\t\n129\t\tif pod.shouldIgnorePod(cppc, matchingPPCs) {\n130\t\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"A pod with the scheduling gate should be ignored. Ignoring...\")\n131\t\t\t// We can reach this branch when:\n132\t\t\t// - The pod has been gated but not processed before the operator changed configuration such that the pod should be ignored.\n133\t\t\t// - The pod has got some other changes in the admission chain from another webhook that makes it not suitable for processing anymore\n134\t\t\t//\t(for example another actor set the nodeAffinity already for the kubernetes.io/arch label).\n135\t\t\t// In both cases, we should just remove the scheduling gate.\n136\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n137\t\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n138\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnored, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnoredMsg)\n139\t\t\treturn\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\t// Skip preferred affinity processing if the user has already configured architecture-related preferred affinity\n143\t\t// or if the reconcile loop has already applied the PPCs/CPPC (e.g., due to a retry or re-reconciliation)\n144\t\tif !pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n145\t\t\tr.applyMatchingPPCs(ctx, matchingPPCs, pod)\n146\t\n147\t\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n148\t\t\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind)\n149\t\t\t}\n150\t\t} else {\n151\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod already has architecture-related preferred affinity. This could be user-defined or from a previous reconcile loop. Skipping PPC/CPPC preferred affinity processing.\")\n152\t\t\t// Track that configs were skipped due to user-defined preferences\n153\t\t\tr.trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx, pod, cppc, matchingPPCs)\n154\t\t}\n155\t\n156\t\t// Prepare the requirement for the node affinity.\n157\t\tpsdl, err := r.pullSecretDataList(ctx, pod)\n158\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to retrieve the image pull secret data for the pod.\")\n159\t\t// If no error occurred when retrieving the image pull secret data, set the node affinity.\n160\t\tif err == nil {\n161\t\t\t_, err = pod.SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(psdl)\n162\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to set the node affinity for the pod.\")\n163\t\t}\n164\t\tif pod.maxRetries() && err != nil {\n165\t\t\t// the number of retries is incremented in the handleError function when the error is not nil.\n166\t\t\t// If we enter this branch, the retries counter has been incremented and reached the max retries.\n167\t\t\t// The counter starts at 1 when the first error occurs. Therefore, when the reconciler tries maxRetries times,\n168\t\t\t// the counter is equal to the maxRetries value and the pod should not be processed again.\n169\t\t\t// Publish this event and remove the scheduling gate.\n170\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Max retries Reached. The pod will not have the nodeAffinity set.\")\n171\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", ImageInspectionErrorMaxRetriesMsg, err.Error()))\n172\t\n173\t\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != \"\" {\n174\t\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Setting the nodeAffinity to the fallback architecture\", \"fallbackArchitecture\", cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n175\t\t\t\tpod.setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n176\t\t\t}\n177\t\t}\n178\t\t// If the pod has been processed successfully or the max retries have been reached, remove the scheduling gate.\n179\t\tif err == nil || pod.maxRetries() {\n180\t\t\t// If no preferred node affinity was set by any config, log and publish an event\n181\t\t\tif pod.Labels[utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel] == utils.LabelValueNotSet {\n182\t\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n183\t\t\t\t\tArchitecturePreferredPredicateSkippedMsg)\n184\t\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No preferred node affinity was set\")\n185\t\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n188\t\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n189\t\t}\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// applyMatchingPPCs applies the pre-filtered matching PodPlacementConfigs to the pod.\n193\t// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\n194\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) applyMatchingPPCs(ctx context.Context, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig, pod *Pod) {\n195\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithName(\"PodPlacementConfig\")\n196\t\n197\t\t// Sort the configurations by descending priority\n198\t\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n199\t\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority\n200\t\t})\n201\t\n202\t\t// For each matching namespace-scoped configuration, apply if plugin is enabled\n203\t\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n204\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing PodPlacementConfig\", \"namespace\", ppc.Namespace, \"name\", ppc.Name)\n205\t\n206\t\t\t// check if plugin is enabled\n207\t\t\tif !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n208\t\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Skipping PodPlacementConfig NodeAffinityScoring disabled\", \"namespace\", ppc.Namespace, \"name\", ppc.Name)\n209\t\t\t\tcontinue\n210\t\t\t}\n211\t\n212\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Applying namespace-scoped config\", \"PodPlacementConfig\", ppc.Name)\n213\t\t\tconfigSource := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-%s\", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)\n214\t\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource)\n215\t\t}\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// trackSkippedMatchingConfigs tracks in the annotation which PPC/CPPC configs would have been applied\n219\t// but were skipped due to user-defined architecture-related preferred affinity.\n220\t// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\n221\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod, cppc *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) {\n222\t\t// Sort by descending priority (same as applyMatchingPPCs)\n223\t\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n224\t\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority\n225\t\t})\n226\t\n227\t\t// Track skipped PodPlacementConfigs\n228\t\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n229\t\t\tif !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n230\t\t\t\tcontinue\n231\t\t\t}\n232\t\n233\t\t\tconfigSource := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-%s\", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)\n234\t\t\t// Track each platform term as skipped\n235\t\t\tfor _, platform := range ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n236\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, configSource, false)\n237\t\t\t}\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Track skipped ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n241\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n242\t\t\tfor _, platform := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n243\t\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind, false)\n244\t\t\t}\n245\t\t}\n246\t}\n247\t\n248\t// pullSecretDataList returns the list of secrets data for the given pod given its imagePullSecrets field\n249\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) pullSecretDataList(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) ([][]byte, error) {\n250\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n251\t\tsecretAuths := make([][]byte, 0)\n252\t\tsecretList := pod.getPodImagePullSecrets()\n253\t\tfor _, pullsecret := range secretList {\n254\t\t\tsecret, err := r.ClientSet.CoreV1().Secrets(pod.Namespace).Get(ctx, pullsecret, metav1.GetOptions{})\n255\t\t\tif err != nil {\n256\t\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Error getting secret\", \"secret\", pullsecret)\n257\t\t\t\tcontinue\n258\t\t\t}\n259\t\t\tif secretData, err := utils.ExtractAuthFromSecret(secret); err != nil {\n260\t\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Error extracting auth from secret\", \"secret\", pullsecret)\n261\t\t\t\tcontinue\n262\t\t\t} else {\n263\t\t\t\tsecretAuths = append(secretAuths, secretData)\n264\t\t\t}\n265\t\t}\n266\t\treturn secretAuths, nil\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t// SetupWithManager sets up the controller with the Manager.\n270\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n271\t\t// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent\n272\t\t// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.\n273\t\t// The main bottleneck is the image inspection.\n274\t\tmaxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4\n275\t\tctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).Info(\"Setting up the PodReconciler with the manager with max\"+\n276\t\t\t\" concurrent reconciles\", \"maxConcurrentReconciles\", maxConcurrentReconciles)\n277\t\n278\t\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n279\t\t\tFor(&corev1.Pod{}).\n280\t\t\t// Watch PodPlacementConfig to re-queue gated pods when a PPC is created, updated, or deleted.\n281\t\t\t// Without this, there is a race between a PPC appearing in the API server and the informer\n282\t\t\t// cache syncing it. If a pod is reconciled before the cache has the PPC, the pod is processed\n283\t\t\t// without the PPC's preferred affinity and the scheduling gate is removed — permanently missing\n284\t\t\t// the PPC configuration. By watching PPCs, we re-queue any still-gated pods in the namespace\n285\t\t\t// so they are reprocessed with the PPC now in the cache.\n286\t\t\t//\n287\t\t\t// Limitation: this only helps pods that are still gated (gate=gated) when the PPC watch event\n288\t\t\t// arrives. If a pod's image inspection completes instantly (e.g., cache hit) and the gate is\n289\t\t\t// removed before the PPC watch fires, the pod won't be re-queued. In practice this is rare\n290\t\t\t// because image inspection involves network I/O (hundreds of ms) while informer watches\n291\t\t\t// deliver events in ~10ms. Fully closing this gap would require re-processing ungated pods,\n292\t\t\t// which conflicts with the one-shot scheduling gate design.\n293\t\t\tWatches(\n294\t\t\t\t&multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig{},\n295\t\t\t\thandler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(r.mapPPCToPods),\n296\t\t\t).\n297\t\t\tWithOptions(ctrl2.Options{\n298\t\t\t\tMaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,\n299\t\t\t}).\n300\t\t\tComplete(r)\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// mapPPCToPods returns reconcile requests for all gated pods in the PPC's namespace.\n304\tfunc (r *PodReconciler) mapPPCToPods(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object) []reconcile.Request {\n305\t\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n306\t\tpodList := &corev1.PodList{}\n307\t\tif err := r.List(ctx, podList,\n308\t\t\tclient.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()),\n309\t\t\tclient.MatchingLabels{utils.SchedulingGateLabel: utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueGated},\n310\t\t); err != nil {\n311\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Failed to list gated pods for PodPlacementConfig change\", \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace())\n312\t\t\treturn nil\n313\t\t}\n314\t\trequests := make([]reconcile.Request, 0, len(podList.Items))\n315\t\tfor i := range podList.Items {\n316\t\t\trequests = append(requests, reconcile.Request{\n317\t\t\t\tNamespacedName: types.NamespacedName{\n318\t\t\t\t\tName:      podList.Items[i].Name,\n319\t\t\t\t\tNamespace: podList.Items[i].Namespace,\n320\t\t\t\t},\n321\t\t\t})\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(requests) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Re-queuing gated pods due to PodPlacementConfig change\",\n325\t\t\t\t\"ppc\", obj.GetName(), \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace(), \"podCount\", len(requests))\n326\t\t}\n327\t\treturn requests\n328\t}\n329\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"376154b9-51e0-4de1-b074-06817c71730c","uuid":"6a6c15a9-1d47-4dfa-a6c1-d83d988ad460","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:36:32.399Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment/internal/controller/podplacement/pod_reconciler.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage podplacement\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\truntime2 \"runtime\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\tctrl2 \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/controller\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/handler\"\n\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/reconcile\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\tmultiarchv1beta1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement/metrics\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n)\n\n// PodReconciler reconciles a Pod object\ntype PodReconciler struct {\n\tclient.Client\n\tAPIReader client.Reader\n\tScheme    *runtime.Scheme\n\tClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset\n\tRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n}\n\n// Note: The operand (pod-placement-controller) RBAC is defined programmatically in\n// podplacement_objects.go buildClusterRoleController(). Do NOT add operand-specific\n// RBAC markers here — kubebuilder merges all markers into the manager's ClusterRole,\n// which would over-provision the operator SA with permissions only the operand needs.\n\n// Reconcile is part of the main kubernetes reconciliation loop which aims to\n// move the current state of the cluster closer to the desired state.\n// the Pod object against the actual cluster state, and then\n// perform operations to make the cluster state reflect the state specified by\n// the user.\n//\n// For more details, check Reconcile and its Result here:\n// - https://pkg.go.dev/sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime@v0.14.1/pkg/reconcile\n// Reconcile has to watch the pod object if it has the scheduling gate with name SchedulingGateName,\n// inspect the images in the pod spec, update the nodeAffinity accordingly and remove the scheduling gate.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\t// Lazy initialization of the metrics to support concurrent reconciles\n\tmetrics.InitPodPlacementControllerMetrics()\n\tnow := time.Now()\n\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessPod)\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n\n\tpod := newPod(&corev1.Pod{}, ctx, r.Recorder)\n\n\tif err := r.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, pod.PodObject()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Unable to fetch pod\", \"error\", err)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)\n\t}\n\t// Pods without the scheduling gate should be ignored.\n\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod does not have the scheduling gate. Ignoring...\")\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n\t}\n\tmetrics.ProcessedPodsCtrl.Inc()\n\tdefer utils.HistogramObserve(now, metrics.TimeToProcessGatedPod)\n\tr.processPod(ctx, pod)\n\terr := r.Update(ctx, pod.PodObject())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Unable to update the pod\")\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalFailure, SchedulingGateRemovalFailureMsg)\n\t\treturn ctrl.Result{}, err\n\t}\n\tif !pod.HasSchedulingGate() {\n\t\t// Only publish the event if the scheduling gate has been removed and the pod has been updated successfully.\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareSchedulingGateRemovalSuccess, SchedulingGateRemovalSuccessMsg)\n\t\tmetrics.GatedPodsGauge.Dec()\n\t}\n\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) processPod(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing pod\")\n\n\tcppc := clusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()\n\t// List existing PodPlacementConfigs in the same namespace\n\tppcList := &multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigList{}\n\tif err := r.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list existing PodPlacementConfigs in namespace\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// The informer cache can lag behind the API server. If the cache shows no PPCs,\n\t// verify with a direct API read to avoid a race where a just-created PPC is missed\n\t// and the pod is permanently ungated without its preferred affinity.\n\tif len(ppcList.Items) == 0 {\n\t\tif err := r.APIReader.List(ctx, ppcList, client.InNamespace(pod.Namespace)); err != nil {\n\t\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"failed to list PodPlacementConfigs from API server\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter to only PPCs that match this pod's labels - do this once for efficiency\n\tmatchingPPCs := pod.filterMatchingPPCs(ppcList)\n\n\tif pod.shouldIgnorePod(cppc, matchingPPCs) {\n\t\tlog.V(3).Info(\"A pod with the scheduling gate should be ignored. Ignoring...\")\n\t\t// We can reach this branch when:\n\t\t// - The pod has been gated but not processed before the operator changed configuration such that the pod should be ignored.\n\t\t// - The pod has got some other changes in the admission chain from another webhook that makes it not suitable for processing anymore\n\t\t//\t(for example another actor set the nodeAffinity already for the kubernetes.io/arch label).\n\t\t// In both cases, we should just remove the scheduling gate.\n\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnored, ArchitectureAwareGatedPodIgnoredMsg)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Skip preferred affinity processing if the user has already configured architecture-related preferred affinity\n\t// or if the reconcile loop has already applied the PPCs/CPPC (e.g., due to a retry or re-reconciliation)\n\tif !pod.isPreferredAffinityConfiguredForArchitecture() {\n\t\tr.applyMatchingPPCs(ctx, matchingPPCs, pod)\n\n\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"Pod already has architecture-related preferred affinity. This could be user-defined or from a previous reconcile loop. Skipping PPC/CPPC preferred affinity processing.\")\n\t\t// Track that configs were skipped due to user-defined preferences\n\t\tr.trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx, pod, cppc, matchingPPCs)\n\t}\n\n\t// Prepare the requirement for the node affinity.\n\tpsdl, err := r.pullSecretDataList(ctx, pod)\n\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to retrieve the image pull secret data for the pod.\")\n\t// If no error occurred when retrieving the image pull secret data, set the node affinity.\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\t_, err = pod.SetNodeAffinityArchRequirement(psdl)\n\t\tpod.handleError(err, \"Unable to set the node affinity for the pod.\")\n\t}\n\tif pod.maxRetries() && err != nil {\n\t\t// the number of retries is incremented in the handleError function when the error is not nil.\n\t\t// If we enter this branch, the retries counter has been incremented and reached the max retries.\n\t\t// The counter starts at 1 when the first error occurs. Therefore, when the reconciler tries maxRetries times,\n\t\t// the counter is equal to the maxRetries value and the pod should not be processed again.\n\t\t// Publish this event and remove the scheduling gate.\n\t\tlog.Info(\"Max retries Reached. The pod will not have the nodeAffinity set.\")\n\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeWarning, ImageArchitectureInspectionError, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", ImageInspectionErrorMaxRetriesMsg, err.Error()))\n\n\t\tif cppc != nil && cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture != \"\" {\n\t\t\tlog.Info(\"Setting the nodeAffinity to the fallback architecture\", \"fallbackArchitecture\", cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n\t\t\tpod.setRequiredNodeAffinityToFallbackArchitecture(cppc.Spec.FallbackArchitecture)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// If the pod has been processed successfully or the max retries have been reached, remove the scheduling gate.\n\tif err == nil || pod.maxRetries() {\n\t\t// If no preferred node affinity was set by any config, log and publish an event\n\t\tif pod.Labels[utils.PreferredNodeAffinityLabel] == utils.LabelValueNotSet {\n\t\t\tpod.PublishEvent(corev1.EventTypeNormal, ArchitectureAwareNodeAffinitySet,\n\t\t\t\tArchitecturePreferredPredicateSkippedMsg)\n\t\t\tlog.V(2).Info(\"No preferred node affinity was set\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Removing the scheduling gate from pod.\")\n\t\tpod.RemoveSchedulingGate()\n\t}\n}\n\n// applyMatchingPPCs applies the pre-filtered matching PodPlacementConfigs to the pod.\n// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) applyMatchingPPCs(ctx context.Context, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig, pod *Pod) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx).WithName(\"PodPlacementConfig\")\n\n\t// Sort the configurations by descending priority\n\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority\n\t})\n\n\t// For each matching namespace-scoped configuration, apply if plugin is enabled\n\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Processing PodPlacementConfig\", \"namespace\", ppc.Namespace, \"name\", ppc.Name)\n\n\t\t// check if plugin is enabled\n\t\tif !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Skipping PodPlacementConfig NodeAffinityScoring disabled\", \"namespace\", ppc.Namespace, \"name\", ppc.Name)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlog.Info(\"Applying namespace-scoped config\", \"PodPlacementConfig\", ppc.Name)\n\t\tconfigSource := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-%s\", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)\n\t\tpod.SetPreferredArchNodeAffinity(ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring, configSource)\n\t}\n}\n\n// trackSkippedMatchingConfigs tracks in the annotation which PPC/CPPC configs would have been applied\n// but were skipped due to user-defined architecture-related preferred affinity.\n// The matchingPPCs slice should already be filtered to only include PPCs whose label selector matches the pod.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) trackSkippedMatchingConfigs(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod, cppc *multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig, matchingPPCs []multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig) {\n\t// Sort by descending priority (same as applyMatchingPPCs)\n\tsort.Slice(matchingPPCs, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn matchingPPCs[i].Spec.Priority > matchingPPCs[j].Spec.Priority\n\t})\n\n\t// Track skipped PodPlacementConfigs\n\tfor _, ppc := range matchingPPCs {\n\t\tif !ppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconfigSource := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-%s\", multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfigKind, ppc.Name)\n\t\t// Track each platform term as skipped\n\t\tfor _, platform := range ppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, configSource, false)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Track skipped ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n\tif cppc != nil && cppc.PluginsEnabled(common.NodeAffinityScoringPluginName) {\n\t\tfor _, platform := range cppc.Spec.Plugins.NodeAffinityScoring.Platforms {\n\t\t\tpod.trackAffinitySource(platform.Architecture, platform.Weight, multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfigKind, false)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// pullSecretDataList returns the list of secrets data for the given pod given its imagePullSecrets field\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) pullSecretDataList(ctx context.Context, pod *Pod) ([][]byte, error) {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tsecretAuths := make([][]byte, 0)\n\tsecretList := pod.getPodImagePullSecrets()\n\tfor _, pullsecret := range secretList {\n\t\tsecret, err := r.ClientSet.CoreV1().Secrets(pod.Namespace).Get(ctx, pullsecret, metav1.GetOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Error getting secret\", \"secret\", pullsecret)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif secretData, err := utils.ExtractAuthFromSecret(secret); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Error extracting auth from secret\", \"secret\", pullsecret)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsecretAuths = append(secretAuths, secretData)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn secretAuths, nil\n}\n\n// SetupWithManager sets up the controller with the Manager.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\t// This reconciler is mostly I/O bound due to the pod and node retrievals, so we can increase the number of concurrent\n\t// reconciles to the number of CPUs * 4.\n\t// The main bottleneck is the image inspection.\n\tmaxConcurrentReconciles := runtime2.NumCPU() * 4\n\tctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).Info(\"Setting up the PodReconciler with the manager with max\"+\n\t\t\" concurrent reconciles\", \"maxConcurrentReconciles\", maxConcurrentReconciles)\n\n\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n\t\tFor(&corev1.Pod{}).\n\t\t// Watch PodPlacementConfig to re-queue gated pods when a PPC is created, updated, or deleted.\n\t\t// Without this, there is a race between a PPC appearing in the API server and the informer\n\t\t// cache syncing it. If a pod is reconciled before the cache has the PPC, the pod is processed\n\t\t// without the PPC's preferred affinity and the scheduling gate is removed — permanently missing\n\t\t// the PPC configuration. By watching PPCs, we re-queue any still-gated pods in the namespace\n\t\t// so they are reprocessed with the PPC now in the cache.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// Limitation: this only helps pods that are still gated (gate=gated) when the PPC watch event\n\t\t// arrives. If a pod's image inspection completes instantly (e.g., cache hit) and the gate is\n\t\t// removed before the PPC watch fires, the pod won't be re-queued. In practice this is rare\n\t\t// because image inspection involves network I/O (hundreds of ms) while informer watches\n\t\t// deliver events in ~10ms. Fully closing this gap would require re-processing ungated pods,\n\t\t// which conflicts with the one-shot scheduling gate design.\n\t\tWatches(\n\t\t\t&multiarchv1beta1.PodPlacementConfig{},\n\t\t\thandler.EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(r.mapPPCToPods),\n\t\t).\n\t\tWithOptions(ctrl2.Options{\n\t\t\tMaxConcurrentReconciles: maxConcurrentReconciles,\n\t\t}).\n\t\tComplete(r)\n}\n\n// mapPPCToPods returns reconcile requests for all gated pods in the PPC's namespace.\nfunc (r *PodReconciler) mapPPCToPods(ctx context.Context, obj client.Object) []reconcile.Request {\n\tlog := ctrllog.FromContext(ctx)\n\tpodList := &corev1.PodList{}\n\tif err := r.List(ctx, podList,\n\t\tclient.InNamespace(obj.GetNamespace()),\n\t\tclient.MatchingLabels{utils.SchedulingGateLabel: utils.SchedulingGateLabelValueGated},\n\t); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Error(err, \"Failed to list gated pods for PodPlacementConfig change\", \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\trequests := make([]reconcile.Request, 0, len(podList.Items))\n\tfor i := range podList.Items {\n\t\trequests = append(requests, reconcile.Request{\n\t\t\tNamespacedName: types.NamespacedName{\n\t\t\t\tName:      podList.Items[i].Name,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace: podList.Items[i].Namespace,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tif len(requests) > 0 {\n\t\tlog.V(1).Info(\"Re-queuing gated pods due to PodPlacementConfig change\",\n\t\t\t\"ppc\", obj.GetName(), \"namespace\", obj.GetNamespace(), \"podCount\", len(requests))\n\t}\n\treturn requests\n}\n","numLines":329,"startLine":1,"totalLines":329}}}

metricsaggregator\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n\n\tmultiarchv1beta1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n)\n\ntype MetricsAggregatorReconciler struct {\n\tclient.Client\n\tClientSet *kubernetes.Clientset\n\tScheme    *runtime.Scheme\n}\n\nfunc (r *MetricsAggregatorReconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req ctrl.Request) (ctrl.Result, error) {\n\t_ = log.FromContext(ctx)\n\treturn ctrl.Result{}, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *MetricsAggregatorReconciler) SetupWithManager(mgr ctrl.Manager) error {\n\treturn ctrl.NewControllerManagedBy(mgr).\n\t\tFor(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).\n\t\tComplete(r)\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## File 2: Modified — `cmd/main.go`\n\nThe changes to `cmd/main.go` are in Change:\n\n```go\n\tenableENoExecEventControllers bool\n\tenableCPPCInformer bool\n```\n\nto:\n\n```go\n\tenableENoExecEventControllers bool\n\tenableMetricsAggregator       bool\n\tenableCPPCInformer bool\n```\n\n### Change 3: Add leader election ID prefix, in `main()`\n\nAfter the existing `enableENoExecEventControllers` leader ID block (around line 135), add:\n\n```go\n\tif after `RunENoExecEventControllers`):\n\n```go\nfunc RunMetricsAggregator(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\tmust((&metricsaggregator.MetricsAggregatorReconciler{\n\t\tClient:    mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tClientSet: clientset,\n\t\tScheme: !enableENoExecEventControllers && !enableMetricsAggregator {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"at least one of the following flags must be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers, --enable-metrics-aggregator\")\n\t}\n\t// no more than one of the flags can be set\n\tif btoi(enableOperator)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook)+btoi(enableENoExecEventControllers)+btoi(enableMetricsAggregator) > 1 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"only one of the following flags can be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers, aggregator\")\n```\n\n---\n\n## Complete `cmd/main.go` after all changes\n\nFor clarity, here is the full modified `cmd/main.go`:\n\n```go\n/*\nCopyright 2023 Red Hat, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/tls\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"flag\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t// Import all Kubernetes client auth plugins (e.g. Azure, GCP, OIDC, etc.)\n\t// to ensure that exec-entrypoint and run can make use of them.\n\t_ \"k8s.io/client-go/plugin/pkg/client/auth\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\tclientgoscheme \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n\tctrl \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime\"\n\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/cache\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client/apiutil\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/healthz\"\n\tctrllog \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log/zap\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/filters\"\n\tmetricsserver \"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics/server\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/webhook\"\n\n\t//+kubebuilder:scaffold:imports\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/operator/events\"\n\n\t\"github.com/panjf2000/ants/v2\"\n\tzapuber \"go.uber.org/zap\"\n\t\"go.uber.org/zap/zapcore\"\n\n\tmonitoringv1 \"github.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/pkg/apis/monitoring/v1\"\n\n\tmultiarchv1alpha1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1alpha1\"\n\tmultiarchv1beta1 \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/v1beta1\"\n\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/api/common\"\n\tenoexeceventhandler \"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/enoexecevent/handler\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/metricsaggregator\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/operator\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacement\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/internal/controller/podplacementconfig\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/informers/clusterpodplacementconfig\"\n\t\"github.com/openshift/multiarch-tuning-operator/pkg/utils\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tunableToCreateController = \"unable to create controller\"\n\tunableToAddRunnable      = \"unable to add runnable\"\n\tcontrollerKey            = \"controller\"\n\trunnableKey              = \"runnable\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tscheme   = runtime.NewScheme()\n\tsetupLog = ctrl.Log.WithName(\"setup\")\n\tmetricsAddr,\n\tprobeAddr,\n\tcertDir,\n\tglobalPullSecretNamespace,\n\tglobalPullSecretName,\n\tregistryCertificatesConfigMapName string\n\tenableLeaderElection,\n\tenableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook,\n\tenableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers,\n\tenableENoExecEventControllers bool\n\tenableMetricsAggregator bool\n\tenableCPPCInformer      bool\n\tenableOperator          bool\n\tinitialLogLevel         int\n\tpostFuncs               []func()\n)\n\nfunc init() {\n\tutilruntime.Must(clientgoscheme.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(multiarchv1alpha1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(multiarchv1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n\tutilruntime.Must(monitoringv1.AddToScheme(scheme))\n}\n\nfunc main() {\n\tbindFlags()\n\tmust(validateFlags(), \"invalid flags\")\n\tcacheOpts := cache.Options{\n\t\tDefaultTransform: cache.TransformStripManagedFields(),\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the leader election ID deterministically and based on the flags\n\tleaderID := \"208d7abd.multiarch.openshift.io\"\n\tif enableOperator {\n\t\tleaderID = fmt.Sprintf(\"operator-%s\", leaderID)\n\t}\n\tif enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers {\n\t\tleaderID = fmt.Sprintf(\"ppc-controllers-%s\", leaderID)\n\t\t// We need to watch the pods with the status.phase equal to Pending to be able to update the nodeAffinity.\n\t\t// We can discard the other pods because they are already scheduled.\n\t\tcacheOpts.ByObject = map[client.Object]cache.ByObject{\n\t\t\t&corev1.Pod{}: {\n\t\t\t\tField: fields.OneTermEqualSelector(\"status.phase\", \"Pending\"),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif enableENoExecEventControllers {\n\t\tleaderID = fmt.Sprintf(\"enoexecevent-controllers-%s\", leaderID)\n\t\tcacheOpts.DefaultNamespaces = map[string]cache.Config{\n\t\t\tutils.Namespace(): {},\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif enableMetricsAggregator {\n\t\tleaderID = fmt.Sprintf(\"metrics-aggregator-%s\", leaderID)\n\t}\n\n\t// Rapid Reset CVEs. For more information see:\n\t// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qppj-fm5r-hxr3\n\t// - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4374-p667-p6c8\n\t// - https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubebuilder/blob/33a2f3dc556a9e49e06e6f19e0ae737d82d402db/testdata/project-v4/cmd/main.go#L78-L89\n\tvar tlsOpts []func(*tls.Config)\n\tdisableHTTP2 := func(c *tls.Config) {\n\t\tsetupLog.Info(\"disabling http/2\")\n\t\tc.NextProtos = []string{\"http/1.1\"}\n\t}\n\ttlsOpts = append(tlsOpts, disableHTTP2)\n\n\twebhookServer := webhook.NewServer(webhook.Options{\n\t\tPort:    9443,\n\t\tCertDir: certDir,\n\t\tTLSOpts: tlsOpts,\n\t})\n\tmgr, err := ctrl.NewManager(ctrl.GetConfigOrDie(), ctrl.Options{\n\t\tScheme: scheme,\n\t\tMetrics: metricsserver.Options{\n\t\t\tBindAddress:    metricsAddr,\n\t\t\tCertDir:        certDir,\n\t\t\tFilterProvider: filters.WithAuthenticationAndAuthorization,\n\t\t\tSecureServing:  true,\n\t\t},\n\t\tWebhookServer:          webhookServer,\n\t\tHealthProbeBindAddress: probeAddr,\n\t\tLeaderElection:         enableLeaderElection,\n\t\tLeaderElectionID:       leaderID,\n\t\tCache:                  cacheOpts,\n\t\tLogger:                 ctrllog.FromContext(context.Background()).WithName(\"manager\"),\n\t\t// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel defines if the leader should step down voluntarily\n\t\t// when the Manager ends. This requires the binary to immediately end when the\n\t\t// Manager is stopped, otherwise, this setting is unsafe. Setting this significantly\n\t\t// speeds up voluntary leader transitions as the new leader don't have to wait\n\t\t// LeaseDuration time first.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// In the default scaffold provided, the program ends immediately after\n\t\t// the manager stops, so would be fine to enable this option. However,\n\t\t// if you are doing or is intended to do any operation such as perform cleanups\n\t\t// after the manager stops then its usage might be unsafe.\n\t\t// LeaderElectionReleaseOnCancel: true,\n\t})\n\tmust(err, \"unable to create manager\")\n\n\t//+kubebuilder:scaffold:builder\n\tmust(mgr.AddHealthzCheck(\"healthz\", healthz.Ping), \"unable to set up health check\")\n\tmust(mgr.AddReadyzCheck(\"readyz\", healthz.Ping), \"unable to set up ready check\")\n\n\tif enableCPPCInformer {\n\t\tmust(mgr.Add(clusterpodplacementconfig.NewCPPCSyncer(mgr)), \"unable to instantiate CPPCSyncer\")\n\t}\n\n\tif enableOperator {\n\t\tRunOperator(mgr)\n\t\tRunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr)\n\t}\n\tif enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers {\n\t\tRunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr)\n\t}\n\tif enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook {\n\t\tRunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr)\n\t}\n\tif enableENoExecEventControllers {\n\t\tRunENoExecEventControllers(mgr)\n\t}\n\tif enableMetricsAggregator {\n\t\tRunMetricsAggregator(mgr)\n\t}\n\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"starting manager\")\n\tmust(mgr.Start(ctrl.SetupSignalHandler()), \"unable to start the manager\")\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"the manager has stopped\")\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"running post functions\")\n\tfor _, f := range postFuncs {\n\t\tf()\n\t}\n\tsetupLog.Info(\"exiting\")\n}\n\nfunc RunOperator(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\n\t// Get GVK for ClusterPodPlacementConfig\n\tgvk, _ := apiutil.GVKForObject(&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}, mgr.GetScheme())\n\n\t// Set up the reconciler\n\tmust((&operator.ClusterPodPlacementConfigReconciler{\n\t\tClient:        mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tScheme:        mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tClientSet:     clientset,\n\t\tDynamicClient: dynamic.NewForConfigOrDie(config),\n\t\tRecorder: events.NewKubeRecorder(\n\t\t\tclientset.CoreV1().Events(utils.Namespace()),\n\t\t\tutils.OperatorName,\n\t\t\t&corev1.ObjectReference{\n\t\t\t\tKind:       gvk.Kind,\n\t\t\t\tName:       common.SingletonResourceObjectName,\n\t\t\t\tNamespace:  utils.Namespace(),\n\t\t\t\tAPIVersion: gvk.GroupVersion().String(),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tclock.RealClock{},\n\t\t),\n\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tmust((&multiarchv1beta1.ClusterPodPlacementConfig{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController,\n\t\tcontrollerKey, \"ClusterPodPlacementConfigConversionWebhook\")\n}\n\nfunc RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\n\tmust((&podplacement.PodReconciler{\n\t\tClient:    mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tAPIReader: mgr.GetAPIReader(),\n\t\tScheme:    mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tClientSet: clientset,\n\t\tRecorder:  mgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr),\n\t\tunableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"PodReconciler\")\n\n\tmust(mgr.Add(podplacement.NewGlobalPullSecretSyncer(clientset, globalPullSecretNamespace, globalPullSecretName)),\n\t\tunableToAddRunnable, runnableKey, \"GlobalPullSecretSyncer\")\n}\n\nfunc RunClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\tpool, err := ants.NewMultiPool(16, 16, ants.LeastTasks,\n\t\tants.WithPreAlloc(true))\n\tmust(err, \"unable to create multi pool for the webhook's event messages\")\n\tpostFuncs = append(postFuncs, func() {\n\t\terr = pool.ReleaseTimeout(30 * time.Second)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsetupLog.Error(err, \"failed to release the worker pool\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tants.Release()\n\t})\n\thandler := podplacement.NewPodSchedulingGateMutatingWebHook(mgr.GetClient(), clientset, mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tmgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.OperatorName), pool) //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n\tmgr.GetWebhookServer().Register(\"/add-pod-scheduling-gate\", &webhook.Admission{Handler: handler})\n}\n\nfunc RunPodPlacementConfigWebHook(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tmgr.GetWebhookServer().Register(\"/validate-multiarch-openshift-io-v1beta1-podplacementconfig\",\n\t\t&webhook.Admission{Handler: podplacementconfig.NewPodPlacementConfigWebhook(mgr.GetAPIReader(), mgr.GetScheme())})\n}\n\nfunc RunENoExecEventControllers(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\tmust(enoexeceventhandler.NewReconciler(\n\t\tmgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tclientset,\n\t\tmgr.GetScheme(),\n\t\tmgr.GetEventRecorderFor(utils.EnoexecControllerName), //nolint:staticcheck // MULTIARCH-6087: will be fixed with events API migration\n\t).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"ENoExecEventController\")\n}\n\nfunc RunMetricsAggregator(mgr ctrl.Manager) {\n\tconfig := ctrl.GetConfigOrDie()\n\tclientset := kubernetes.NewForConfigOrDie(config)\n\tmust((&metricsaggregator.MetricsAggregatorReconciler{\n\t\tClient:    mgr.GetClient(),\n\t\tClientSet: clientset,\n\t\tScheme:    mgr.GetScheme(),\n\t}).SetupWithManager(mgr), unableToCreateController, controllerKey, \"MetricsAggregatorReconciler\")\n}\n\nfunc validateFlags() error {\n\tif !enableOperator && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers && !enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook && !enableENoExecEventControllers && !enableMetricsAggregator {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"at least one of the following flags must be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers, --enable-metrics-aggregator\")\n\t}\n\t// no more than one of the flags can be set\n\tif btoi(enableOperator)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers)+btoi(enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook)+btoi(enableENoExecEventControllers)+btoi(enableMetricsAggregator) > 1 {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"only one of the following flags can be set: --enable-operator, --enable-ppc-controllers, --enable-ppc-webhook, --enable-enoexec-event-controllers, --enable-metrics-aggregator\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc bindFlags() {\n\tflag.StringVar(&metricsAddr, \"metrics-bind-address\", \":8080\", \"The address the metric endpoint binds to.\")\n\tflag.StringVar(&probeAddr, \"health-probe-bind-address\", \":8081\", \"The address the probe endpoint binds to.\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableLeaderElection, \"leader-elect\", false,\n\t\t\"Enable leader election for controller manager. \"+\n\t\t\t\"Enabling this will ensure there is only one active controller manager.\")\n\tflag.StringVar(&certDir, \"cert-dir\", \"/var/run/manager/tls\", \"The directory where the TLS certs are stored\")\n\t// TODO: Change the defaults to match a local secret; the OCP specific settings will be provided by the operator\n\tflag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretNamespace, \"global-pull-secret-namespace\", \"openshift-config\", \"The namespace where the global pull secret is stored\")\n\tflag.StringVar(&globalPullSecretName, \"global-pull-secret-name\", \"pull-secret\", \"The name of the global pull secret\")\n\tflag.StringVar(&registryCertificatesConfigMapName, \"registry-certificates-configmap-name\", \"image-registry-certificates\", \"The name of the configmap that contains the registry certificates\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandWebHook, \"enable-ppc-webhook\", false, \"Enable the pod placement config operand webhook\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableClusterPodPlacementConfigOperandControllers, \"enable-ppc-controllers\", false, \"Enable the pod placement config operand controllers\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableOperator, \"enable-operator\", false, \"Enable the operator\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableCPPCInformer, \"enable-cppc-informer\", false, \"Enable informer for ClusterPodPlacementConfig\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableENoExecEventControllers, \"enable-enoexec-event-controllers\", false, \"Enable the ENoExecEvent controllers\")\n\tflag.BoolVar(&enableMetricsAggregator, \"enable-metrics-aggregator\", false, \"Enable the metrics aggregator\")\n\t// This may be deprecated in the future. It is used to support the current way of setting the log level for operands\n\t// If operands will start to support a controller that watches the ClusterPodPlacementConfig, this flag may be removed\n\t// and the log level will be set in the ClusterPodPlacementConfig at runtime (with no need for reconciliation)\n\tflag.IntVar(&initialLogLevel, \"initial-log-level\", common.LogVerbosityLevelNormal.ToZapLevelInt(), \"Initial log level. Converted to zap\")\n\tklog.InitFlags(nil)\n\tflag.Parse()\n\t// Set the Log Level as AtomicLevel to allow runtime changes\n\tutils.AtomicLevel = zapuber.NewAtomicLevelAt(zapcore.Level(int8(-initialLogLevel))) // #nosec G115 -- initialLogLevel is constrained to 0-3 range\n\tzapLogger := zap.New(zap.Level(utils.AtomicLevel), zap.UseDevMode(false))\n\tklog.SetLogger(zapLogger)\n\tctrllog.SetLogger(zapLogger)\n}\n\nfunc must(err error, msg string, keysAndValues ...interface{}) {\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tsetupLog.Error(err, msg, keysAndValues...)\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc btoi(b bool) int {\n\tif b {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t}\n\treturn 0\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## CPPC Informer access\n\nThe task requires \"access to the CPPC informer.\" The CPPC informer is enabled via the `--enable-cppc-informer` flag, which is independent of mode flags and is already passed to deployments that need it. The `MetricsAggregatorReconciler` can access the CPPC singleton at runtime via:\n\n```go\nclusterpodplacementconfig.GetClusterPodPlacementConfig()\n```\n\nThis is the same pattern used by `PodReconciler` (see `pod_reconciler.go:109`). The informer itself is started in `main()` when `enableCPPCInformer` is true — the operator deployment should pass both `--enable-metrics-aggregator` and `--enable-cppc-informer` together, just as `--enable-ppc-controllers` is paired with `--enable-cppc-informer`.\n\n---\n\n## Commands to run\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/a_b_evals/repos/multiarch-tuning-operator-treatment\n\n# Create the new directory\nmkdir -p internal/controller/metricsaggregator\n\n# After writing both files, verify it compiles:\ngo build ./...\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary of changes\n\n1. **New file `internal/controller/metricsaggregator/reconciler.go`** — Stub `MetricsAggregatorReconciler` with `Reconcile()` and `SetupWithManager()` methods, watching `ClusterPodPlacementConfig`.\n\n2. **Modified `cmd/main.go`** — Seven precise changes:\n   - Import the new `metricsaggregator` package\n   - Add `enableMetricsAggregator` boolean variable\n   - Add `metrics-aggregator-` leader election ID prefix\n   - Add `RunMetricsAggregator(mgr)` call guarded by the flag\n   - Add `RunMetricsAggregator` function\n   - Add `enableMetricsAggregator` to both `validateFlags()` checks (at-least-one and mutual exclusion)\n   - Register `--enable-metrics-aggregator` flag in `bindFlags()`"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":198,"cache_read_input_tokens":49471,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":198,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"376154b9-51e0-4de1-b074-06817c71730c","uuid":"b135ee21-6040-4f46-a396-d94c61ca559d","timestamp":"2026-07-20T13:38:18.686Z","request_id":"req_vrtx_011CdDQ7ShtjqUppp1znc7NC"}
